AADAccessReviewDefinition.d: This resource configures an Azure AD Access Review Definition.

AdditionalNotificationRecipients.d: Defines the list of additional users or group members to be notified of the access review progress.

DescriptionForAdmins.d: Description provided by review creators to provide more context of the review to admins. Supports $select.

DescriptionForReviewers.d: Description provided by review creators to provide more context of the review to reviewers. Reviewers see this description in the email sent to them requesting their review. Email notifications support up to 256 characters. Supports $select.

DisplayName.d: Name of the access review series. Supports $select and $orderby. Required on create.

FallbackReviewers.d: The fallback reviewers of the access review.

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

InstanceEnumerationScope.d: In the case of an all groups review, this determines the scope of which groups will be reviewed.

Reviewers.d: The reviewers of the access review.

ScopeValue.d: Defines the entities whose access is reviewed. For supported scopes, see accessReviewScope. Required on create. Supports $select and $filter (contains only). For examples of options for configuring scope, see Configure the scope of your access review definition using the Microsoft Graph API.

SettingsValue.d: The settings for an access review series, see type definition below. Supports $select. Required on create.

StageSettings.d: Required only for a multi-stage access review to define the stages and their settings. You can break down each review instance into up to three sequential stages, where each stage can have a different set of reviewers, fallback reviewers, and settings. Stages are created sequentially based on the dependsOn property. Optional. When this property is defined, its settings are used instead of the corresponding settings in the accessReviewScheduleDefinition object and its settings, reviewers, and fallbackReviewers properties.

AADAccessReviewPolicy.d: Use this resource to monitor the access review policy object.

AADActivityBasedTimeoutPolicy.d: This resource configures an Azure AD Activity Based Timeout Policy.

AzurePortalTimeOut.d: Timeout value in hh:mm:ss for c44b4083-3bb0-49c1-b47d-974e53cbdf3c: applies the policy to the Azure portal.

DefaultTimeOut.d: Timeout value in hh:mm:ss for default: applies the policy to all applications that support activity-based timeout functionality but don't have application-specific override.

DisplayName.d: Display name for this policy. Required.

Id.d: Id of the policy

AADAdminConsentRequestPolicy.d: Configures the Admin Consent Request Policy in Entra Id.

IsEnabled.d: Determines if the policy is enabled or not.

NotifyReviewers.d: Specifies whether reviewers will receive notifications.

RemindersEnabled.d: Specifies whether reviewers will receive reminder emails.

RequestDurationInDays.d: Specifies the duration the request is active before it automatically expires if no decision is applied.

Reviewers.d: The list of reviewers for the admin consent.

AADAdministrativeUnit.d: This resource configures an Azure Active Directory administrative unit.

Description.d: Description of the Administrative Unit

DisplayName.d: DisplayName of the Administrative Unit

Id.d: Object-Id of the Administrative Unit

IsMemberManagementRestricted.d: Indicates whether the management rights on resources in the administrative units should be restricted to ONLY the administrators scoped on the administrative unit object.

Members.d: Specify members. Only specify if MembershipType is NOT set to Dynamic

MembershipRule.d: Specify membership rule. Requires that MembershipType is set to Dynamic. Note that the functionality is currently in preview.

MembershipRuleProcessingState.d: Specify dynamic membership-rule processing-state. Valid values are 'On' and 'Paused'. Requires that MembershipType is set to Dynamic. Note that the functionality is currently in preview.

MembershipType.d: Specify membership type. Possible values are Assigned and Dynamic. Note that the functionality is currently in preview.

ScopedRoleMembers.d: Specify Scoped Role Membership. Note: Any groups must be role-enabled

Visibility.d: Visibility of the Administrative Unit. Specify HiddenMembership if members of the AU are hidden

AADAgreement.d: This resource configures Azure AD Terms of Use Agreements in Entra ID.

AcceptanceStatement.d: The acceptance statement included in the agreement.

DisplayName.d: The display name of the agreement.

FileData.d: The content of the agreement file.

FileName.d: The name of the agreement file.

Id.d: The unique identifier of the agreement.

IsPerDeviceAcceptanceRequired.d: Whether the agreement is per device or per user.

IsViewingBeforeAcceptanceRequired.d: Whether the user is required to view the agreement document before accepting.

Language.d: The language of the agreement file.

UserReacceptRequiredFrequency.d: Duration after which the user must re-accept the terms of use. Must be in ISO 8601 duration format.

AADAppManagementPolicy.d: Manages an app management policy that can be assigned to an application or service principal object.

Description.d: The description of the policy.

DisplayName.d: The display name of the policy.

Id.d: Id of the policy.

IsEnabled.d: Denotes whether the policy is enabled.

Restrictions.d: Restrictions that apply to an application or service principal object.

AADApplication.d: This resource configures an Azure Active Directory Application.

Api.d: Specifies settings for an application that implements a web API.

AppRoles.d: The collection of roles defined for the application. With app role assignments, these roles can be assigned to users, groups, or service principals associated with other applications. Not nullable.

ApplicationTemplateId.d: Identifier of the associated Application Template.

AuthenticationBehaviors.d: The collection of breaking change behaviors related to token issuance that are configured for the application. Authentication behaviors are unset by default (null) and must be explicitly enabled or disabled. Nullable. Returned only on $select. For more information about authentication behaviors, see Manage application authenticationBehaviors to avoid unverified use of email claims for user identification or authorization.

Description.d: A free text field to provide a description of the application object to end users. The maximum allowed size is 1024 characters.

DisplayName.d: DisplayName of the app

GroupMembershipClaims.d: A bitmask that configures the groups claim issued in a user or OAuth 2.0 access token that the application expects.

Homepage.d: The URL to the application's homepage.

IdentifierUris.d: User-defined URI(s) that uniquely identify a Web application within its Azure AD tenant, or within a verified custom domain.

IsFallbackPublicClient.d: Specifies the fallback application type as public client, such as an installed application running on a mobile device. The default value is false, which means the fallback application type is confidential client such as web app. There are certain scenarios where Microsoft Entra ID cannot determine the client application type (for example, ROPC flow where it is configured without specifying a redirect URI). In those cases, Microsoft Entra ID will interpret the application type based on the value of this property.

KeyCredentials.d: The collection of key credentials associated with the application. Not nullable. Supports $filter (eq, not, ge, le).

KnownClientApplications.d: Client applications that are tied to this resource application.

LogoutURL.d: The logout url for this application.

OnPremisesPublishing.d: Represents the set of properties required for configuring Application Proxy for this application. Configuring these properties allows you to publish your on-premises application for secure remote access.

OptionalClaims.d: Application developers can configure optional claims in their Microsoft Entra applications to specify the claims that are sent to their application by the Microsoft security token service. For more information, see How to: Provide optional claims to your app.

Owners.d: UPN or ObjectID values of the app's owners.

PasswordCredentials.d: The collection of password credentials associated with the application. Not nullable.

Permissions.d: API permissions for the Azure Active Directory Application.

PublicClient.d: Specifies whether this application is a public client (such as an installed application running on a mobile device). Default is false.

PublicClientRedirectUris.d: List of public clients redirect URIs.

ReplyURLs.d: Specifies the URLs that user tokens are sent to for sign in, or the redirect URIs that OAuth 2.0 authorization codes and access tokens are sent to.

SignInAudience.d: Specifies the Microsoft accounts that are supported for the current application. The possible values are: AzureADMyOrg (default), AzureADMultipleOrgs, AzureADandPersonalMicrosoftAccount, and PersonalMicrosoftAccount

Spa.d: List of single page application settings.

TokenLifetimePolicy.d: The Token Lifetime Policy assigned to the application with its DisplayName.

AADAttributeSet.d: Represents a group of related custom security attribute definitions.

Description.d: Identifier for the attribute set that is unique within a tenant. Can be up to 32 characters long and include Unicode characters. Cannot contain spaces or special characters. Cannot be changed later. Case insensitive

Id.d: Identifier for the attribute set that is unique within a tenant. Can be up to 32 characters long and include Unicode characters. Cannot contain spaces or special characters. Cannot be changed later. Case insensitive

MaxAttributesPerSet.d: Maximum number of custom security attributes that can be defined in this attribute set. Default value is null. If not specified, the administrator can add up to the maximum of 500 active attributes per tenant. Can be changed later.

AADAuthenticationContextClassReference.d: Represents a Microsoft Entra authentication context class reference. Authentication context class references are custom values that define a Conditional Access authentication requirement.

Description.d: A short explanation of the policies that are enforced by authenticationContextClassReference. This value should be used to provide secondary text to describe the authentication context class reference when building user-facing admin experiences. For example, a selection UX.

DisplayName.d: A friendly name that identifies the authenticationContextClassReference object when building user-facing admin experiences. For example, a selection UX

Id.d: Identifier used to reference the authentication context class. The id is used to trigger step-up authentication for the referenced authentication requirements and is the value that will be issued in the acrs claim of an access token. This value in the claim is used to verify that the required authentication context has been satisfied. The allowed values are c1 through c25.

IsAvailable.d: Indicates whether the authenticationContextClassReference has been published by the security admin and is ready for use by apps. When it's set to false, it shouldn't be shown in admin UX experiences because the value isn't currently available for selection.

AADAuthenticationFlowPolicy.d: Represents the policy configuration of self-service sign-up experience at a tenant level that lets external users request to sign up for approval. It contains information, such as the identifier, display name, and description, and indicates whether self-service sign-up is enabled for the policy.

Description.d: Description of the Authentication Flow Policy.

DisplayName.d: Display name of the Authentication Flow Policy.

Id.d: Unique identifier of the Authentication Flow Policy.

SelfServiceSignUpEnabled.d: Indicates whether self-service sign-up flow is enabled or disabled. The default value is false. This property isn't a key. Required.

AADAuthenticationMethodPolicy.d: This resource configures an Azure AD Authentication Method Policy.

ReconfirmationInDays.d: Days before the user will be asked to reconfirm their method.

RegistrationEnforcement.d: Enforce registration at sign-in time. This property can be used to remind users to set up targeted authentication methods.

ReportSuspiciousActivitySettings.d: Allows users to report suspicious activities if they receive an authentication request that they did not initiate.

SystemCredentialPreferences.d: Prompt users with their most-preferred credential for multifactor authentication.

AADAuthenticationMethodPolicyAuthenticat.d: This resource configures an Azure AD Authentication Method Policy Authenticator.

ExcludeTargets.d: Displayname of the groups of users that are excluded from a policy.

FeatureSettings.d: A collection of Microsoft Authenticator settings such as number matching and location context, and whether they are enabled for all users or specific users only.

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

IncludeTargets.d: Displayname of the groups of users that are included from a policy.

IsSoftwareOathEnabled.d: true if users can use the OTP code generated by the Microsoft Authenticator app, false otherwise.

State.d: The state of the policy. Possible values are: enabled, disabled.

AADAuthenticationMethodPolicyEmail.d: This resource configures an Azure AD Authentication Method Policy Email.

AllowExternalIdToUseEmailOtp.d: Determines whether email OTP is usable by external users for authentication. Possible values are: default, enabled, disabled, unknownFutureValue. Tenants in the default state who did not use public preview will automatically have email OTP enabled beginning in October 2021.

ExcludeTargets.d: Displayname of the groups of users that are excluded from a policy.

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

IncludeTargets.d: Displayname of the groups of users that are included from a policy.

State.d: The state of the policy. Possible values are: enabled, disabled.

AADAuthenticationMethodPolicyExternal.d: This resource configures an Azure AD Authentication Method Policy External.

AppId.d: The appId for the app registration in Microsoft Entra ID representing the integration with the external provider.

DisplayName.d: The displayName of the authentication policy configuration. Read-only.

ExcludeTargets.d: Displayname of the groups of users that are excluded from a policy.

IncludeTargets.d: Displayname of the groups of users that are included from a policy.

OpenIdConnectSetting.d: Open ID Connection settings used by this external authentication method.

State.d: The state of the policy. Possible values are: enabled, disabled.

AADAuthenticationMethodPolicyFido2.d: This resource configures an Azure AD Authentication Method Policy Fido2.

ExcludeTargets.d: Displayname of the groups of users that are excluded from a policy.

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

IncludeTargets.d: Displayname of the groups of users that are included from a policy.

IsAttestationEnforced.d: Determines whether attestation must be enforced for FIDO2 security key registration.

IsSelfServiceRegistrationAllowed.d: Determines if users can register new FIDO2 security keys.

KeyRestrictions.d: Controls whether key restrictions are enforced on FIDO2 security keys, either allowing or disallowing certain key types as defined by Authenticator Attestation GUID (AAGUID), an identifier that indicates the type (e.g. make and model) of the authenticator.

PasskeyProfiles.d: Passkey profiles configuration for FIDO2 authentication.

State.d: The state of the policy. Possible values are: enabled, disabled.

AADAuthenticationMethodPolicyHardware.d: This resource configures an Azure AD Authentication Method Policy Hardware.

ExcludeTargets.d: Displayname of the groups of users that are excluded from a policy.

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

IncludeTargets.d: Displayname of the groups of users that are included from a policy.

State.d: The state of the policy. Possible values are: enabled, disabled.

AADAuthenticationMethodPolicyQRCodeImage.d: This resource configures an Azure AD Authentication Method Policy QR Code Image.

ExcludeTargets.d: Displayname of the groups of users that are excluded from a policy.

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

IncludeTargets.d: Displayname of the groups of users that are included from a policy.

StandardQRCodeLifetimeInDays.d: Lifetime in days of the qr code.

State.d: The state of the policy. Possible values are: enabled, disabled.

AADAuthenticationMethodPolicySms.d: This resource configures an Azure AD Authentication Method Policy Sms.

ExcludeTargets.d: Displayname of the groups of users that are excluded from a policy.

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

IncludeTargets.d: Displayname of the groups of users that are included from a policy.

State.d: The state of the policy. Possible values are: enabled, disabled.

AADAuthenticationMethodPolicySoftware.d: This resource configures an Azure AD Authentication Method Policy Software.

ExcludeTargets.d: Displayname of the groups of users that are excluded from a policy.

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

IncludeTargets.d: Displayname of the groups of users that are included from a policy.

State.d: The state of the policy. Possible values are: enabled, disabled.

AADAuthenticationMethodPolicyTemporary.d: This resource configures an Azure AD Authentication Method Policy Temporary.

DefaultLength.d: Default length in characters of a Temporary Access Pass object. Must be between 8 and 48 characters.

DefaultLifetimeInMinutes.d: Default lifetime in minutes for a Temporary Access Pass. Value can be any integer between the minimumLifetimeInMinutes and maximumLifetimeInMinutes.

ExcludeTargets.d: Displayname of the groups of users that are excluded from a policy.

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

IncludeTargets.d: Displayname of the groups of users that are included from a policy.

IsUsableOnce.d: If true, all the passes in the tenant will be restricted to one-time use. If false, passes in the tenant can be created to be either one-time use or reusable.

MaximumLifetimeInMinutes.d: Maximum lifetime in minutes for any Temporary Access Pass created in the tenant. Value can be between 10 and 43200 minutes (equivalent to 30 days).

MinimumLifetimeInMinutes.d: Minimum lifetime in minutes for any Temporary Access Pass created in the tenant. Value can be between 10 and 43200 minutes (equivalent to 30 days).

State.d: The state of the policy. Possible values are: enabled, disabled.

AADAuthenticationMethodPolicyVoice.d: This resource configures an Azure AD Authentication Method Policy Voice.

ExcludeTargets.d: Displayname of the groups of users that are excluded from a policy.

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

IncludeTargets.d: Displayname of the groups of users that are included from a policy.

IsOfficePhoneAllowed.d: true if users can register office phones, otherwise, false.

State.d: The state of the policy. Possible values are: enabled, disabled.

AADAuthenticationMethodPolicyX509.d: This resource configures an Azure AD Authentication Method Policy X509.

AuthenticationModeConfiguration.d: Defines strong authentication configurations. This configuration includes the default authentication mode and the different rules for strong authentication bindings.

CertificateUserBindings.d: Defines fields in the X.509 certificate that map to attributes of the Azure AD user object in order to bind the certificate to the user. The priority of the object determines the order in which the binding is carried out. The first binding that matches will be used and the rest ignored.

ExcludeTargets.d: Displayname of the groups of users that are excluded from a policy.

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

IncludeTargets.d: Displayname of the groups of users that are included from a policy.

State.d: The state of the policy. Possible values are: enabled, disabled.

AADAuthenticationRequirement.d: This resource configures Azure AD Authentication MFA Requirements for a user.

PerUserMfaState.d: The state of the MFA enablement for the user. Possible values are: enabled, disabled.

UserPrincipalName.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

AADAuthenticationStrengthPolicy.d: This resource configures an Azure AD Authentication Strength Policy.

AllowedCombinations.d: The authentication method combinations allowed by this authentication strength policy.

Description.d: A description of the policy.

DisplayName.d: The name of the policy.

Id.d: The unique identifier of the policy.

AADAuthorizationPolicy.d: This resource configures the Azure Active Directory Authorization Policy.

AllowEmailVerifiedUsersToJoinOrganizatio.d: Boolean Indicates whether a user can join the tenant by email validation.

AllowInvitesFrom.d: Indicates who can invite external users to the organization. Possible values are: None, AdminsAndGuestInviters, AdminsGuestInvitersAndAllMembers, Everyone. Everyone is the default setting for all cloud environments except US Government.

AllowUserConsentForRiskyApps.d: Indicates whether user consent for risky apps is allowed.

AllowedToSignUpEmailBasedSubscriptions.d: Boolean Indicates whether users can sign up for email based subscriptions.

AllowedToUseSSPR.d: Boolean Indicates whether the Self-Serve Password Reset feature can be used by users on the tenant.

BlockMsolPowershell.d: Boolean To disable the use of MSOL PowerShell, set this property to true. This will also disable user-based access to the legacy service endpoint used by MSOL PowerShell. This does not affect Azure AD Connect or Microsoft Graph.

DefaultUserRoleAllowedToCreateApps.d: Boolean Indicates whether the default user role can create applications.

DefaultUserRoleAllowedToCreateSecurityGr.d: Boolean Indicates whether the default user role can create security groups.

DefaultUserRoleAllowedToCreateTenants.d: Indicates whether the default user role can create tenants. This setting corresponds to the Restrict non-admin users from creating tenants setting in the User settings menu in the Azure portal. When this setting is false, users assigned the Tenant Creator role can still create tenants.

DefaultUserRoleAllowedToReadBitlockerKey.d: Indicates whether the registered owners of a device can read their own BitLocker recovery keys with default user role.

DefaultUserRoleAllowedToReadOtherUsers.d: Boolean Indicates whether the default user role can read other users.

Description.d: Description of this policy.

DisplayName.d: Display name for this policy.

GuestUserRole.d: The role that should be granted to guest users. Refer to List unifiedRoleDefinitions to find the list of available role templates. Only supported roles today are User, Guest User, and Restricted Guest User (2af84b1e-32c8-42b7-82bc-daa82404023b).

PermissionGrantPolicyIdsAssignedToDefaul.d: String collection Indicates if user consent to apps is allowed, and if it is, which permission to grant consent and which app consent policy (permissionGrantPolicy) govern the permission for users to grant consent. Value should be in the format managePermissionGrantsForSelf.{id}, where {id} is the id of a built-in or custom app consent policy. An empty list indicates user consent to apps is disabled.

AADB2BManagementPolicy.d: This resource configures an Azure AD B2B Management Policy.

Definition.d: A string collection containing a JSON string that defines the rules and settings for a policy. The syntax for the definition differs for each derived policy type. Required.

AADB2CAuthenticationMethodsPolicy.d: B2C allows tenant admins to choose a mechanism for letting end users register via local accounts.

IsEmailPasswordAuthenticationEnabled.d: The tenant admin can configure local accounts using email if the email and password authentication method is enabled.

IsPhoneOneTimePasswordAuthenticationEnab.d: The tenant admin can configure local accounts using phone number if the phone number and one-time password authentication method is enabled.

IsUserNameAuthenticationEnabled.d: The tenant admin can configure local accounts using username if the username and password authentication method is enabled.

AADCertificateBasedApplicationConfigurat.d: Manages certificate-based application configurations that define trusted certificate authorities for application authentication.

Description.d: Description for the configuration.

DisplayName.d: Display name for the configuration.

Id.d: The unique identifier for the configuration.

AADClaimsMappingPolicy.d: This resource configures an Azure AD Claims Mapping Policy.

Definition.d: A string collection containing a JSON string that defines the rules and settings for a policy. The syntax for the definition differs for each derived policy type. Required.

Description.d: Description for this policy. Required.

DisplayName.d: Display name for this policy. Required.

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

IsOrganizationDefault.d: If set to true, activates this policy. There can be many policies for the same policy type, but only one can be activated as the organization default. Optional, default value is false.

AADConditionalAccessPolicy.d: This resource configures an Azure Active Directory Conditional Access Policy.

ApplicationEnforcedRestrictionsIsEnabled.d: Specifies, whether Application Enforced Restrictions are enabled in the Policy.

ApplicationsFilter.d: Rule syntax is similar to that used for membership rules for groups in Microsoft Entra ID.

ApplicationsFilterMode.d: Mode to use for the filter. Possible values are include or exclude.

AuthenticationStrength.d: Name of the associated authentication strength policy.

BuiltInControls.d: List of built-in Grant Controls to be applied by the Policy.

ClientAppTypes.d: Client App types in scope of the Policy.

CloudAppSecurityIsEnabled.d: Specifies, whether Cloud App Security is enforced by the Policy.

CloudAppSecurityType.d: Specifies, what Cloud App Security control is enforced by the Policy.

ContinuousAccessEvaluationMode.d: Specifies continuous access evaluation settings. The possible values are: disabled, strictEnforcement, strictLocation

CustomAuthenticationFactors.d: Custom Controls assigned to the grant property of this policy.

DeviceFilterMode.d: Client Device Filter mode of the Policy.

DeviceFilterRule.d: Client Device Filter rule of the Policy.

DisplayName.d: DisplayName of the AAD CA Policy

ExcludeApplications.d: Cloud Apps out of scope of the Policy.

ExcludeExternalTenantsMembers.d: Represents the Excluded collection of tenant ids in the scope of Conditional Access for guests and external users policy targeting.

ExcludeExternalTenantsMembershipKind.d: Represents the Excluded Tenants membership kind. The possible values are: all, enumerated, unknownFutureValue. enumerated references an object of conditionalAccessEnumeratedExternalTenants derived type.

ExcludeGroups.d: Groups out of scope of the Policy.

ExcludeGuestOrExternalUserTypes.d: Represents the Excluded internal guests or external user types. This is a multi-valued property. Supported values are: b2bCollaborationGuest, b2bCollaborationMember, b2bDirectConnectUser, internalGuest, OtherExternalUser, serviceProvider and unknownFutureValue.

ExcludeLocations.d: AAD Named Locations out of scope of the Policy.

ExcludePlatforms.d: Client Device Platforms out of scope of the Policy.

ExcludeRoles.d: AAD Admin Roles out of scope of the Policy.

ExcludeServicePrincipals.d: Service Principals out of scope of the Policy. 'Attribute Definition Reader' role is needed.

ExcludeUsers.d: Users out of scope of the Policy.

GrantControlOperator.d: Operator to be used for Grant Controls.

Id.d: Specifies the GUID for the Policy.

IncludeApplications.d: Cloud Apps in scope of the Policy.

IncludeExternalTenantsMembers.d: Represents the Included collection of tenant ids in the scope of Conditional Access for guests and external users policy targeting.

IncludeExternalTenantsMembershipKind.d: Represents the Included Tenants membership kind. The possible values are: all, enumerated, unknownFutureValue. enumerated references an object of conditionalAccessEnumeratedExternalTenants derived type.

IncludeGroups.d: Groups in scope of the Policy.

IncludeGuestOrExternalUserTypes.d: Represents the Included internal guests or external user types. This is a multi-valued property. Supported values are: b2bCollaborationGuest, b2bCollaborationMember, b2bDirectConnectUser, internalGuest, OtherExternalUser, serviceProvider and unknownFutureValue.

IncludeLocations.d: AAD Named Locations in scope of the Policy.

IncludePlatforms.d: Client Device Platforms in scope of the Policy.

IncludeRoles.d: AAD Admin Roles in scope of the Policy.

IncludeServicePrincipals.d: Service Principals in scope of the Policy. 'Attribute Definition Reader' role is needed.

IncludeUserActions.d: User Actions in scope of the Policy.

IncludeUsers.d: Users in scope of the Policy.

PersistentBrowserIsEnabled.d: Specifies, whether Browser Persistence is controlled by the Policy.

PersistentBrowserMode.d: Specifies, what Browser Persistence control is enforced by the Policy.

ProtocolFlows.d: Specifies the protocol flows to block.

SecureSignInSessionIsEnabled.d: Specifies if token protection for sign-in sessions is to be enforced by the policy.

ServicePrincipalFilterMode.d: Mode to use for the Service Principal filter. Possible values are include or exclude. 'Attribute Definition Reader' role is needed.

ServicePrincipalFilterRule.d: Rule syntax for the Service Principal filter. 'Attribute Definition Reader' role is needed.

ServicePrincipalRiskLevels.d: Service principal risk levels included in the policy.

SignInFrequencyInterval.d: Sign in frequency interval. Possible values are: 'timeBased', 'everyTime' and 'unknownFutureValue'.

SignInFrequencyIsEnabled.d: Specifies, whether sign-in frequency is enforced by the Policy.

SignInFrequencyType.d: Sign in frequency unit (days/hours) to be interpreted by the policy.

SignInFrequencyValue.d: Sign in frequency time in the given unit to be enforced by the policy.

SignInRiskLevels.d: AAD Identity Protection Sign-in Risk Levels in scope of the Policy.

State.d: Specifies the State of the Policy.

TermsOfUse.d: Display name of the terms of use to assign.

TransferMethods.d: Names of the associated authentication flow transfer methods. Possible values are '', 'deviceCodeFlow', 'authenticationTransfer', or 'deviceCodeFlow,authenticationTransfer'.

UserRiskLevels.d: AAD Identity Protection User Risk Levels in scope of the Policy.

AADConnectorGroupApplicationProxy.d: This resource configures an Azure AD Connector Group Application Proxy.

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

Name.d: The name associated with the connectorGroup.

Region.d: The region the connectorGroup is assigned to and will optimize traffic for. This region can only be set if no connectors or applications are assigned to the connectorGroup. The possible values are: nam (for North America), eur (for Europe), aus (for Australia), asia (for Asia), ind (for India), and unknownFutureValue.

AADCrossTenantAccessPolicy.d: This resource configures an Azure AD Cross Tenant Policy (XTAP).

AllowedCloudEndpoints.d: Used to specify which Microsoft clouds an organization would like to collaborate with. By default, this value is empty.

DisplayName.d: The name of the policy.

AADCrossTenantAccessPolicyConfigurationD.d: This resource configures an Azure AD Authentication Method Policy Configuration Default.

B2BCollaborationInbound.d: Defines your partner-specific configuration for users from other organizations accessing your resources via Azure AD B2B collaboration.

B2BCollaborationOutbound.d: Defines your partner-specific configuration for users in your organization going outbound to access resources in another organization via Azure AD B2B collaboration.

B2BDirectConnectInbound.d: Defines your partner-specific configuration for users from other organizations accessing your resources via Azure AD B2B direct connect.

B2BDirectConnectOutbound.d: Defines your partner-specific configuration for users in your organization going outbound to access resources in another organization via Azure AD B2B direct connect.

InboundTrust.d: Determines the partner-specific configuration for trusting other Conditional Access claims from external Azure AD organizations.

InvitationRedemptionIdentityProviderConf.d: Defines the priority order based on which an identity provider is selected during invitation redemption for a guest user.

TenantRestrictions.d: Defines the default tenant restrictions configuration for users in your organization who access an external organization on your network or devices.

AADCrossTenantAccessPolicyConfigurationP.d: This resource configures an Azure AD Authentication Method Policy Configuration Partner.

AutomaticUserConsentSettings.d: Determines the partner-specific configuration for accepting trust claims from other tenant invitations.

B2BCollaborationInbound.d: Defines your partner-specific configuration for users from other organizations accessing your resources via Azure AD B2B collaboration.

B2BCollaborationOutbound.d: Defines your partner-specific configuration for users in your organization going outbound to access resources in another organization via Azure AD B2B collaboration.

B2BDirectConnectInbound.d: Defines your partner-specific configuration for users from other organizations accessing your resources via Azure AD B2B direct connect.

B2BDirectConnectOutbound.d: Defines your partner-specific configuration for users in your organization going outbound to access resources in another organization via Azure AD B2B direct connect.

InboundTrust.d: Determines the partner-specific configuration for trusting other Conditional Access claims from external Azure AD organizations.

PartnerTenantId.d: The tenant identifier for the partner Azure Active Directory (Azure AD) organization.

AADCrossTenantIdentitySyncPolicyPartner.d: Create a cross-tenant user synchronization policy for a partner-specific configuration.

CrossTenantAccessPolicyConfigurationPart.d: Id of the associated partner tenant ID.

DisplayName.d: Display name for the cross-tenant user synchronization policy. Use the name of the partner Microsoft Entra tenant to easily identify the policy.

IsSyncAllowed.d: Defines whether user objects should be synchronized from the partner tenant. False causes any current user synchronization from the source tenant to the target tenant to stop. This property has no impact on existing users who have already been synchronized.

AADCustomAuthenticationExtension.d: Custom authentication extensions define interactions with external systems during a user authentication session.

AADCustomSecurityAttributeDefinition.d: Configures custom security attribute definitions in Entra Id.

AllowedValues.d: The allowed values of the attribute definition.

AttributeSet.d: Name of the attribute set. Case sensitive.

Description.d: Description of the custom security attribute. Can be up to 128 characters long and include Unicode characters. Can't contain spaces or special characters. Can be changed later.

Id.d: Unique identifier of the Attribute Definition.

IsCollection.d: Indicates whether multiple values can be assigned to the custom security attribute. Can't be changed later. If type is set to Boolean, isCollection can't be set to true.

IsSearchable.d: Indicates whether custom security attribute values are indexed for searching on objects that are assigned attribute values. Can't be changed later.

Name.d: Name of the custom security attribute. Must be unique within an attribute set. Can be up to 32 characters long and include Unicode characters. Can't contain spaces or special characters. Can't be changed later. Case sensitive.

Status.d: Specifies whether the custom security attribute is active or deactivated. Acceptable values are Available and Deprecated. Can be changed later.

Type.d: Data type for the custom security attribute values. Supported types are: Boolean, Integer, and String. Can't be changed later.

UsePreDefinedValuesOnly.d: Indicates whether only predefined values can be assigned to the custom security attribute. If set to false, free-form values are allowed. Can later be changed from true to false, but can't be changed from false to true. If type is set to Boolean, usePreDefinedValuesOnly can't be set to true.

AADDeviceRegistrationPolicy.d: Represents the policy scope that controls quota restrictions, additional authentication, and authorization policies to register device identities to your organization.

AzureADAllowedToJoin.d: Scope that a device registration policy applies to.

AzureADAllowedToJoinGroups.d: List of groups that this policy applies to.

AzureADAllowedToJoinUsers.d: List of users that this policy applies to.

AzureADJoinIsAdminConfigurable.d: Determines whether or not administrators can configure Azure AD Join.

AzureAdJoinLocalAdminsRegisteringGroups.d: List of groups that this policy applies to.

AzureAdJoinLocalAdminsRegisteringMode.d: Scope that a device registration policy applies to for local admins.

AzureAdJoinLocalAdminsRegisteringUsers.d: List of users that this policy applies to.

LocalAdminPasswordIsEnabled.d: Specifies whether this policy scope is configurable by the admin. The default value is false. An admin can set it to true to enable Local Admin Password Solution (LAPS) within their organzation.

LocalAdminsEnableGlobalAdmins.d: Indicates whether global administrators are local administrators on all Microsoft Entra-joined devices. This setting only applies to future registrations. Default is true.

MultiFactorAuthConfiguration.d: Specifies the authentication policy for a user to complete registration using Microsoft Entra join or Microsoft Entra registered within your organization.

UserDeviceQuota.d: Specifies the maximum number of devices that a user can have within your organization before blocking new device registrations. The default value is set to 50. If this property isn't specified during the policy update operation, it's automatically reset to 0 to indicate that users aren't allowed to join any devices.

AADDomain.d: Configures custom domain names in Entra Id.

AuthenticationType.d: Indicates the configured authentication type for the domain. The value is either Managed or Federated. Managed indicates a cloud managed domain where Microsoft Entra ID performs user authentication. Federated indicates authentication is federated with an identity provider such as the tenant's on-premises Active Directory via Active Directory Federation Services.

AvailabilityStatus.d: This property is always null except when the verify action is used. When the verify action is used, a domain entity is returned in the response. The availabilityStatus property of the domain entity in the response is either AvailableImmediately or EmailVerifiedDomainTakeoverScheduled.

Id.d: Custom domain name.

IsAdminManaged.d: The value of the property is false if the DNS record management of the domain is delegated to Microsoft 365. Otherwise, the value is true. Not nullable

IsDefault.d: True if this is the default domain that is used for user creation. There's only one default domain per company. Not nullable.

IsRoot.d: True if the domain is a verified root domain. Otherwise, false if the domain is a subdomain or unverified. Not nullable.

IsVerified.d: True if the domain completed domain ownership verification. Not nullable.

PasswordNotificationWindowInDays.d: Specifies the number of days before a user receives notification that their password expires. If the property isn't set, a default value of 14 days is used.

PasswordValidityPeriodInDays.d: Specifies the length of time that a password is valid before it must be changed. If the property isn't set, a default value of 90 days is used.

SupportedServices.d: The capabilities assigned to the domain. Can include 0, 1 or more of following values: Email, Sharepoint, EmailInternalRelayOnly, OfficeCommunicationsOnline, SharePointDefaultDomain, FullRedelegation, SharePointPublic, OrgIdAuthentication, Yammer, Intune. The values that you can add or remove using the API include: Email, OfficeCommunicationsOnline, Yammer. Not nullable.

AADDomainFederation.d: Manages Azure Active Directory Domain Federation configurations including SAML/WS-Fed settings, signing certificates, and authentication protocols for federated domains.

ActiveSignInUri.d: URI that active clients are directed to when signing in to Microsoft Entra services.

DisplayName.d: The display name of the federation configuration.

DomainId.d: The domain ID for which the federation configuration is being managed.

FederatedIdpMfaBehavior.d: Determines whether Microsoft Entra ID accepts the MFA performed by the federated IdP. Supported values are acceptIfMfaDoneByFederatedIdp, enforceMfaByFederatedIdp, rejectMfaByFederatedIdp.

Id.d: The unique identifier of the federation configuration.

IsSignedAuthenticationRequestRequired.d: Specifies whether the federation requires signed authentication requests.

IssuerUri.d: Issuer URI of the federation server.

MetadataExchangeUri.d: URI of the metadata exchange endpoint used for authentication.

NextSigningCertificate.d: Next signing certificate that can be used to sign tokens passed to the Microsoft identity platform. The certificate is formatted as a Base64 encoded string of the public portion of the federated IdP's token signing certificate.

PassiveSignInUri.d: URI that web-based clients are directed to when signing in to Microsoft Entra services.

PasswordResetUri.d: URI that clients are redirected to for resetting their password.

PreferredAuthenticationProtocol.d: Preferred authentication protocol. Supported values are wsFed and saml.

PromptLoginBehavior.d: Prompt login behavior of the federated IdP.

SignOutUri.d: URI to which clients are redirected when signing out of Microsoft Entra services.

SigningCertificate.d: Current certificate used to sign tokens passed to the Microsoft identity platform. The certificate is formatted as a Base64 encoded string of the public portion of the federated IdP's token signing certificate.

AADEntitlementManagementAccessPackage.d: This resource configures an Azure AD Entitlement Management Access Package.

AccessPackageResourceRoleScopes.d: The resources and roles included in the access package.

AccessPackagesIncompatibleWith.d: The access packages that are incompatible with this package.

CatalogId.d: Identifier of the access package catalog referencing this access package.

Description.d: The description of the access package.

DisplayName.d: The display name of the access package.

Id.d: The Id of the access package.

IncompatibleAccessPackages.d: The access packages whose assigned users are ineligible to be assigned this access package.

IncompatibleGroups.d: The groups whose members are ineligible to be assigned this access package.

IsHidden.d: Whether the access package is hidden from the requestor.

IsRoleScopesVisible.d: Indicates whether role scopes are visible.

AADEntitlementManagementAccessPackageAss.d: This resource configures an Azure AD Entitlement Management Access Package Assignment Policy.

AccessReviewSettings.d: Who must review, and how often, the assignments to the access package from this policy. This property is null if reviews are not required.

CanExtend.d: Indicates whether a user can extend the access package assignment duration after approval.

CustomExtensionHandlers.d: The collection of stages when to execute one or more custom access package workflow extensions.

Description.d: The description of the policy.

DisplayName.d: The display name of the policy.

DurationInDays.d: The number of days in which assignments from this policy last until they are expired.

ExpirationDateTime.d: The expiration date for assignments created in this policy. The Timestamp type represents date and time information using ISO 8601 format and is always in UTC time. For example, midnight UTC on Jan 1, 2014 is 2014-01-01T00:00:00Z

Id.d: Id of the access package assignment policy.

Questions.d: Questions that are posed to the requestor.

RequestApprovalSettings.d: Who must approve requests for access package in this policy.

RequestorSettings.d: Who can request this access package from this policy.

AADEntitlementManagementAccessPackageCat.d: This resource configures an Azure AD Entitlement Management Access Package Catalog.

CatalogStatus.d: Has the value Published if the access packages are available for management.

Description.d: The description of the access package catalog.

DisplayName.d: The display name of the access package catalog.

Id.d: The id of the access package catalog.

IsExternallyVisible.d: Whether the access packages in this catalog can be requested by users outside of the tenant.

AADEntitlementManagementAccessPackageCat.d: This resource configures an Azure AD Entitlement Management Access Package Catalog Resource.

AddedBy.d: The name of the user or application that first added this resource. Read-only.

AddedOn.d: The Timestamp type represents date and time information using ISO 8601 format and is always in UTC time. For example, midnight UTC on Jan 1, 2014 is 2014-01-01T00:00:00Z. Read-only.

Attributes.d: Contains information about the attributes to be collected from the requestor and sent to the resource application.

CatalogId.d: The unique ID of the access package catalog.

Description.d: A description for the resource.

DisplayName.d: The display name of the resource, such as the application name, group name or site name.

Id.d: Id of the access package catalog resource.

IsPendingOnboarding.d: True if the resource is not yet available for assignment. Read-only.

OriginId.d: The unique identifier of the resource in the origin system. In the case of an Azure AD group, this is the identifier of the group.

OriginSystem.d: The type of the resource in the origin system.

Url.d: A unique resource locator for the resource, such as the URL for signing a user into an application.

AADEntitlementManagementConnectedOrganiz.d: This resource configures an Azure AD Entitlement Management Connected Organization.

Description.d: The description of the connected organization.

DisplayName.d: The display name of the connected organization.

ExternalSponsors.d: Collection of objectID of external sponsors. the sponsor can be a user or a group.

Id.d: The Id of the Connected organization object.

IdentitySources.d: The identity sources in this connected organization.

InternalSponsors.d: Collection of objectID of internal sponsors. the sponsor can be a user or a group.

State.d: The state of a connected organization defines whether assignment policies with requestor scope type AllConfiguredConnectedOrganizationSubjects are applicable or not.

AADEntitlementManagementRoleAssignment.d: This resource configures an Azure AD Entitlement Management Role Assignments.

AppScopeId.d: Identifier of the app specific scope when the assignment scope is app specific. The scope of an assignment determines the set of resources for which the principal has been granted access. App scopes are scopes that are defined and understood by a resource application only.

DirectoryScopeId.d: Identifier of the directory object representing the scope of the assignment. The scope of an assignment determines the set of resources for which the principal has been granted access. Directory scopes are shared scopes stored in the directory that are understood by multiple applications, unlike app scopes that are defined and understood by a resource application only.

Id.d: Unique Id of the role assignment.

Principal.d: Identifier of the principal to which the assignment is granted.

RoleDefinition.d: Identifier of the unifiedRoleDefinition the assignment is for.

AADEntitlementManagementSettings.d: Manages Entra Id Entitlement Management settings.

DaysUntilExternalUserDeletedAfterBlocked.d: If externalUserLifecycleAction is blockSignInAndDelete, the duration, typically many days, after an external user is blocked from sign in before their account is deleted.

ExternalUserLifecycleAction.d: Automatic action that the service should take when an external user's last access package assignment is removed. The possible values are: none, blockSignIn, blockSignInAndDelete, unknownFutureValue.

AADExternalIdentityPolicy.d: Represents the tenant-wide policy that controls whether external users can leave the guest Microsoft Entra tenant via self-service controls.

AllowExternalIdentitiesToLeave.d: Defines whether external users can leave the guest tenant. If set to false, self-service controls are disabled, and the admin of the guest tenant must manually remove the external user from the guest tenant. When the external user leaves the tenant, their data in the guest tenant is first soft-deleted then permanently deleted in 30 days.

AADFeatureRolloutPolicy.d: This resource configures an Azure AD Policy Feature Rollout Policy.

AppliesTo.d: Indicates the DisplayName of the groups the policy is assigned to.

Description.d: A description for this feature rollout policy.

DisplayName.d: The display name for this feature rollout policy.

Feature.d: Possible values are: passthroughAuthentication, seamlessSso, passwordHashSync, emailAsAlternateId, unknownFutureValue, certificateBasedAuthentication. You must use the Prefer: include-unknown-enum-members request header to get the following value or values in this evolvable enum: certificateBasedAuthentication. For more information about the prerequisites for the enabled features, see Prerequisites for enabled features.

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

IsAppliedToOrganization.d: Indicates whether this feature rollout policy should be applied to the entire organization.

IsEnabled.d: Indicates whether the feature rollout is enabled.

DisplayName.d: The display name of the SAML/WS-Fed based identity provider. Inherited from identityProviderBase.

Id.d: Unique fientifier

IssuerUri.d: Issuer URI of the federation server. Inherited from samlOrWsFedProvider.

MetadataExchangeUri.d: URI of the metadata exchange endpoint used for authentication from rich client applications. Inherited from samlOrWsFedProvider.

PassiveSignInUri.d: URI that web-based clients are directed to when signing in to Microsoft Entra services. Inherited from samlOrWsFedProvider.

PreferredAuthenticationProtocol.d: Preferred authentication protocol. The possible values are: wsFed, saml. Inherited from samlOrWsFedProvider.

SigningCertificate.d: Current certificate used to sign tokens passed to the Microsoft identity platform. The certificate is formatted as a Base64 encoded string of the public portion of the federated IdP's token signing certificate and must be compatible with the X509Certificate2 class.

AADFilteringPolicy.d: Configures filtering policies in Entra Id.

Description.d: Description for the policy.

Id.d: Unique identifier of the policy.

Name.d: Name of the policy.

AADFilteringPolicyRule.d: Configures filtering rules in Entra Id.

Destinations.d: List of associated destinations with the rule.

Id.d: Unique Id for the rule.

Name.d: Name of the rule.

AADFilteringProfile.d: Configures filtering profiles in Entra Id.

Description.d: Description of the profile.

Id.d: Unique identifier for the profile.

Name.d: Profile name.

Policies.d: List of filtering policy names associated with the profile.

Priority.d: Priority level for the profile.

State.d: State of the profile.

AADGroup.d: This resource configures an Azure Active Directory group.

AssignedLicenses.d: List of Licenses assigned to the group.

AssignedToRole.d: DisplayName values for the roles that the group is assigned to.

Description.d: Specifies a description for the group.

DisplayName.d: DisplayName of the Azure Active Directory Group

GroupAsMembers.d: Displayname values for the groups member of the group.

GroupLifecyclePolicySelectedEnabled.d: Specifies if the current group is part of a selected Group Lifecycle Policy configuration. Only applicable for Microsoft 365 Groups.

GroupTypes.d: Specifies the type of the group. To create a security group, leave empty. To create a dynamic membership security group, specify DynamicMembership. To create a Microsoft 365 (aka Unified) group, specify Unified. To create a Microsoft 365 dynamic membership group, specify both Unified and DynamicMembership.

Id.d: Specifies an ID for the group.

IsAssignableToRole.d: Specifies whether this group can be assigned a role. Only available when creating a group and can't be modified after group is created.

MailEnabled.d: Specifies whether this group is mail enabled. Currently, you cannot create mail enabled groups in Microsoft Graph, instead the EXODistributionGroup resource can be used.

MailNickname.d: Specifies a mail nickname for the group.

MemberOf.d: DisplayName values for the groups that this group is a member of.

Members.d: User Service Principal values for the group's members.

MembershipRule.d: Specifies the membership rule for a dynamic group.

MembershipRuleProcessingState.d: Specifies the rule processing state. The acceptable values for this parameter are: On. Process the group rule or Paused. Stop processing the group rule.

Owners.d: User Service Principal values for the group's owners.

SecurityEnabled.d: Specifies whether the group is security enabled. For security groups, this value must be $True.

Visibility.d: This parameter determines the visibility of the group's content and members list.

AADGroupEligibilitySchedule.d: This resource configures an Azure AD Group Eligibility Schedule.

AccessId.d: The identifier of the membership or ownership eligibility to the group that is governed by PIM. Required. The possible values are: owner, member. Supports $filter (eq).

GroupDisplayName.d: Displayname of the group representing the scope of the membership or ownership eligibility through PIM for groups.

GroupId.d: The identifier of the group representing the scope of the membership or ownership eligibility through PIM for groups. Required. Supports $filter (eq).

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

MemberType.d: Indicates whether the assignment is derived from a group assignment. It can further imply whether the caller can manage the schedule. Required. The possible values are: direct, group, unknownFutureValue. Supports $filter (eq).

Principal.d: Displayname if the Principal is group, otherwise UserPrincipalName for user.

ScheduleInfo.d: Represents the period of the access assignment or eligibility. The scheduleInfo can represent a single occurrence or multiple recurring instances. Required.

AADGroupEligibilityScheduleSettings.d: This resource configures an Azure AD Group Eligibility Schedule Settings.

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

AADGroupLifecyclePolicy.d: This resource configures an Azure Active Directory Group Lifecycle Policy (e.g. Expiration).

AlternateNotificationEmails.d: Notification emails for groups that have no owners will be sent to these email addresses.

GroupLifetimeInDays.d: The number of days a group can exist before it needs to be renewed.

ManagedGroupTypes.d: This parameter allows the admin to select which office 365 groups the policy will apply to. 'None' will create the policy in a disabled state. 'All' will apply the policy to every Office 365 group in the tenant. 'Selected' will allow the admin to choose specific Office 365 groups that the policy will apply to.

AADGroupsNamingPolicy.d: This resource configures the Azure Active Directory groups naming policy.

CustomBlockedWordsList.d: Comma delimited list of words that should be blocked from being included in groups' names.

PrefixSuffixNamingRequirement.d: Prefixes and suffixes to add to the group name.

AADGroupsSettings.d: This resource configures the Azure Active Directory groups settings.

AllowGuestsToAccessGroups.d: Boolean indicating whether or not a guest user can have access to Office 365 groups content. This setting does not require an Azure Active Directory Premium P1 license.

AllowGuestsToBeGroupOwner.d: Boolean indicating whether or not a guest user can be an owner of groups.

AllowToAddGuests.d: A boolean indicating whether or not is allowed to add guests to this directory.

EnableGroupCreation.d: The flag indicating whether Office 365 group creation is allowed in the directory by non-admin users. This setting does not require an Azure Active Directory Premium P1 license.

EnableMIPLabels.d: Boolean indicating whether or not sensitivity labels can be assigned to M365-groups.

GroupCreationAllowedGroupName.d: Name of the security group for which the members are allowed to create Office 365 groups even when EnableGroupCreation == false.

GuestUsageGuidelinesUrl.d: The url of a link to the guest usage guidelines.

NewUnifiedGroupWritebackDefault.d: Boolean, a tenant-wide setting that assigns the default value to the writebackConfiguration/isEnabled property of new groups, if the property isn't specified during group creation. This setting is applicable when group writeback is configured in Microsoft Entra Connect.

UsageGuidelinesUrl.d: A link to the Group Usage Guidelines.

AADHomeRealmDiscoveryPolicy.d: This resource configures an Azure AD Home Realm Discovery Policy.

Definition.d: A string collection containing a complex object array that defines the rules and settings for a policy. The syntax for the definition differs for each derived policy type. Required.

Description.d: Description for this policy. Required.

DisplayName.d: Display name for this policy. Required.

IsOrganizationDefault.d: If set to true, activates this policy. There can be many policies for the same policy type, but only one can be activated as the organization default. Optional, default value is false.

AADIdentityAPIConnector.d: This resource configures an Azure AD Identity API Connector.

Certificates.d: List of certificates to be used in the API connector

DisplayName.d: The name of the API connector.

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

TargetUrl.d: The URL of the API endpoint to call.

AADIdentityB2XUserFlow.d: This resource configures an Azure AD Identity B2 X User Flow.

ApiConnectorConfiguration.d: Configuration for enabling an API connector for use as part of the self-service sign-up user flow. You can only obtain the value of this object using Get userFlowApiConnectorConfiguration.

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

IdentityProviders.d: The identity providers included in the user flow.

UserAttributeAssignments.d: The user attribute assignments included in the user flow.

AADIdentityGovernanceLifecycleWorkflow.d: Use this resource to manage Lifecycle workflows.

Description.d: Description of the Workflow

DisplayName.d: Specifies the Display Name of the Workflow

IsEnabled.d: Indicates if the Workflow is enabled

IsSchedulingEnabled.d: Indicates if scheduling is enabled for the Workflow

AADIdentityGovernanceLifecycleWorkflowCu.d: Configures custom extensions for Lifecycle workflows in Entra id.

Description.d: Description of the extension.

DisplayName.d: Display name of the custom extension.

Id.d: Unique Id of the extension.

AADIdentityGovernanceProgram.d: This resource configures an Azure AD Identity Governance Program.

Description.d: A description for this identity governance program.

DisplayName.d: The display name for this identity governance program.

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

AADIdentityProtectionPolicySettings.d: Use this resource to monitor the identity protection policy settings in AAD

IsUserRiskClearedOnPasswordReset.d: If true, user risk is cleared on password reset.

AADLifecycleWorkflowSettings.d: Update the properties of a lifecycleManagementSettings object.

SenderDomain.d: Specifies the domain that should be used when sending email notifications. This domain must be verified in order to be used. We recommend that you use a domain that has the appropriate DNS records to facilitate email validation, like SPF, DKIM, DMARC, and MX, because this then complies with the RFC compliance for sending and receiving email. For details, see Learn more about Exchange Online Email Routing.

UseCompanyBranding.d: Specifies if the organization's banner logo should be included in email notifications. The banner logo will replace the Microsoft logo at the top of the email notification. If true the banner logo will be taken from the tenant's branding settings. This value can only be set to true if the organizationalBranding bannerLogo property is set.

WorkflowScheduleIntervalInHours.d: The interval in hours at which all workflows running in the tenant should be scheduled for execution. This interval has a minimum value of 1 and a maximum value of 24. The default value is 3 hours.

AADMultiTenantOrganizationIdentitySyncPo.d: Defines an optional cross-tenant access policy template with user synchronization settings for multitenant organization tenants.

TemplateApplicationLevel.d: Specifies whether the template will be applied to user synchronization settings of certain tenants. The possible values are: none, newPartners, existingPartners, unknownFutureValue. You can also specify multiple values like newPartners,existingPartners (default). none indicates the template is not applied to any new or existing partner tenants. newPartners indicates the template is applied to new partner tenants. existingPartners indicates the template is applied to existing partner tenants, those who already had partner-specific user synchronization settings in place.

UserSyncInbound.d: Determines whether users can be synchronized from the partner tenant. false causes any current user synchronization from the source tenant to the target tenant to stop. This property has no impact on existing users who have already been synchronized.

AADNamedLocationPolicy.d: This resource configures the Azure AD Named Location Policies in Azure Active Directory.

CountriesAndRegions.d: Specifies the countries and regions for the Named Location in Azure Active Directory

CountryLookupMethod.d: Determines what method is used to decide which country the user is located in. Possible values are clientIpAddress(default) and authenticatorAppGps.

DisplayName.d: Specifies the Display Name of a Named Location in Azure Active Directory

Id.d: Specifies the ID of a Named Location in Azure Active Directory.

IncludeUnknownCountriesAndRegions.d: Specifies the includeUnknownCountriesAndRegions value for the Named Location in Azure Active Directory

IpRanges.d: Specifies the IP ranges of the Named Location in Azure Active Directory

IsTrusted.d: Specifies the isTrusted value for the Named Location (IP ranges only) in Azure Active Directory

OdataType.d: Specifies the Odata Type of a Named Location object in Azure Active Directory

AADNetworkAccessForwardingPolicy.d: Use this resource to monitor the forwarding policy rules associated with the forwarding policies.

Name.d: Name of the forwarding policy

PolicyRules.d: List of rules associated to this forwarding policy.

AADNetworkAccessForwardingProfile.d: This resource configures an Azure AD Network Access Forwarding Profile.

Id.d: Id of the profile. Unique Identifier

Name.d: Profile Name. Required.

Policies.d: Traffic forwarding policies associated with this profile.

State.d: status of the profile

AADNetworkAccessSettingConditionalAccess.d: Configures the adaptive access settings in Entra Id.

AADNetworkAccessSettingCrossTenantAccess.d: Configures the universal tenant restrictions in Entra Id.

AADOnPremisesPublishingProfilesSettings.d: Configures the settings for the on-premises publishing profiles in Entra Id.

IsEnabled.d: Enables of disables private net work connectors in Entra Id.

AADOrganizationCertificateBasedAuthConfi.d: This resource configures an Azure AD Organization Certificate Based Auth Configuration.

CertificateAuthorities.d: Collection of certificate authorities which creates a trusted certificate chain.

AADPIMGroupSetting.d: This resource configures an PIM Group.

ActivateApprover.d: Approver User UPN and/or Group Displayname

ActiveAlertNotificationAdditionalRecipie.d: Send notifications when members are assigned as active to this group: Group assignment alert, additional recipient (UPN)

ActiveAlertNotificationDefaultRecipient.d: Send notifications when members are assigned as active to this group: Group assignment alert, default recipient (True/False)

ActiveAlertNotificationOnlyCritical.d: Send notifications when members are assigned as active to this group: Group assignment alert, only critical Email (True/False)

ActiveApproveNotificationAdditionalRecip.d: Send notifications when members are assigned as active to this group: Request to approve a group assignment renewal/extension, additional recipient (UPN)

ActiveApproveNotificationDefaultRecipien.d: Send notifications when members are assigned as active to this group: Request to approve a group assignment renewal/extension, default recipient (True/False)

ActiveApproveNotificationOnlyCritical.d: Send notifications when members are assigned as active to this group: Request to approve a group assignment renewal/extension, only critical Email (True/False)

ActiveAssigneeNotificationAdditionalReci.d: Send notifications when members are assigned as active to this group: Notification to the assigned user (assignee), additional recipient (UPN)

ActiveAssigneeNotificationDefaultRecipie.d: Send notifications when members are assigned as active to this group: Notification to the assigned user (assignee), default recipient (True/False)

ActiveAssigneeNotificationOnlyCritical.d: Send notifications when members are assigned as active to this group: Notification to the assigned user (assignee), only critical Email (True/False)

AssignmentReqMFA.d: Require Azure Multi-Factor Authentication on active assignment (True/False)

AuthenticationContextName.d: Descriptive name of associated authorization context

DisplayName.d: RuleDefinition DisplayName

EligibilityAssignmentReqMFA.d: Require Azure Multi-Factor Authentication on eligible assignment (True/False)

EligibleAlertNotificationAdditionalRecip.d: Send notifications when members are assigned as eligible to this group: Group assignment alert, additional recipient (UPN)

EligibleAlertNotificationDefaultRecipien.d: Send notifications when members are assigned as eligible to this group: Group assignment alert, default recipient (True/False)

EligibleAlertNotificationOnlyCritical.d: Send notifications when members are assigned as eligible to this group: Group assignment alert, only critical Email (True/False)

EligibleApproveNotificationAdditionalRec.d: Send notifications when members are assigned as eligible to this group: Request to approve a group assignment renewal/extension, additional recipient (UPN)

EligibleApproveNotificationDefaultRecipi.d: Send notifications when members are assigned as eligible to this group: Request to approve a group assignment renewal/extension, default recipient (True/False)

EligibleApproveNotificationOnlyCritical.d: Send notifications when members are assigned as eligible to this group: Request to approve a group assignment renewal/extension, only critical Email (True/False)

EligibleAssigneeNotificationAdditionalRe.d: Send notifications when members are assigned as eligible to this group: Notification to the assigned user (assignee), additional recipient (UPN)

EligibleAssigneeNotificationDefaultRecip.d: Send notifications when members are assigned as eligible to this group: Notification to the assigned user (assignee), default recipient (True/False)

EligibleAssigneeNotificationOnlyCritical.d: Send notifications when members are assigned as eligible to this group: Notification to the assigned user (assignee), only critical Email (True/False)

EligibleAssignmentAlertNotificationAddit.d: Send notifications when eligible members activate this group: Group assignment alert, additional recipient (UPN)

EligibleAssignmentAlertNotificationDefau.d: Send notifications when eligible members activate this group: Group assignment alert, default recipient (True/False)

EligibleAssignmentAlertNotificationOnlyC.d: Send notifications when eligible members activate this group: Group assignment alert, only critical Email (True/False)

EligibleAssignmentAssigneeNotificationAd.d: Send notifications when eligible members activate this group: Notification to activated user (requestor), additional recipient (UPN)

EligibleAssignmentAssigneeNotificationDe.d: Send notifications when eligible members activate this group: Notification to activated user (requestor), default recipient (True/False)

EligibleAssignmentAssigneeNotificationOn.d: Send notifications when eligible members activate this group: Notification to activated user (requestor), only critical Email (True/False)

Id.d: Specifies the Group Policy Id.

RoleDefinitionId.d: The identifier of the membership or ownership eligibility to the group that is governed by PIM. Required. The possible values are: owner, member. Supports $filter (eq).

AADPasswordRuleSettings.d: This resource configures the Azure Active Directory Password Rule Settings.

BannedPasswordCheckOnPremisesMode.d: How should we enforce password policy check in on-premises system.

BannedPasswordList.d: A list of banned words in passwords.

EnableBannedPasswordCheck.d: Boolean indicating if the banned password check for tenant specific banned password list is turned on or not.

EnableBannedPasswordCheckOnPremises.d: Boolean indicating if the banned password check is turned on or not for on-premises system.

LockoutDurationInSeconds.d: The duration in seconds of the initial lockout period.

LockoutThreshold.d: The number of failed login attempts before the first lockout period begins.

AADPermissionGrantPolicy.d: This resource configures an Entra ID Permission Grant Policy.

AADRoleAssignmentScheduleRequest.d: This resource configures an Azure Active Directory Privilege Identity Management assignment.

Action.d: This parameter is deprecated and will be removed in a future release. Represents the type of operation on the role assignment request.The possible values are: adminAssign, adminUpdate, adminRemove, selfActivate, selfDeactivate, adminExtend, adminRenew, selfExtend, selfRenew, unknownFutureValue.

AppScopeId.d: Identifier of the app-specific scope when the role assignment is scoped to an app. The scope of a role assignment determines the set of resources for which the principal is eligible to access. App scopes are scopes that are defined and understood by this application only. Use / for tenant-wide app scopes. Use directoryScopeId to limit the scope to particular directory objects, for example, administrative units. Either directoryScopeId or appScopeId is required.

DirectoryScopeId.d: Identifier of the directory object representing the scope of the role assignment. The scope of an role assignment determines the set of resources for which the principal has been granted access. Directory scopes are shared scopes stored in the directory that are understood by multiple applications. Use / for tenant-wide scope. Use appScopeId to limit the scope to an application only. Either directoryScopeId or appScopeId is required.

Id.d: Identifier for the Role Assignment Schedule Request.

IsValidationOnly.d: This parameter is deprecated and will be removed in a future release. Determines whether the call is a validation or an actual call. Only set this property if you want to check whether an activation is subject to additional rules like MFA before actually submitting the request.

Justification.d: A message provided by users and administrators when create they create the unifiedRoileAssignmentScheduleRequest object. Optional when action is adminRemove. Whether this property is required or optional is also dependent on the settings for the Azure AD role.

Principal.d: User Principal Name of the assignment request.

PrincipalType.d: Represented the type of principal to assign the request to. Accepted values are: Group and User.

RoleDefinition.d: Role associated with the assignment request.

ScheduleInfo.d: The period of the role assignment. Optional when action is adminRemove. The period of assignment is dependent on the settings of the Azure AD role.

TicketInfo.d: This parameter is deprecated and will be removed in a future release. Ticket details linked to the role assignment request including details of the ticket number and ticket system.
