ExceptIfRecipientDomainIs.d: The ExceptIfRecipientDomainIs parameter specifies an exception that looks for recipients with email addresses in the specified domains. You can specify multiple domains separated by commas.

ExceptIfSentTo.d: The ExceptIfSentTo parameter specifies an exception that looks for recipients in messages. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the recipient.

ExceptIfSentToMemberOf.d: The ExceptIfSentToMemberOf parameter specifies an exception that looks for messages sent to members of groups. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the group.

Identity.d: The Identity parameter specifies the rule that you want to modify. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the rule.

EXOAcceptedDomain.d: This resource configures the Accepted Email Domains in Exchange Online.

DomainType.d: The type of AcceptedDomain. Currently the EXOAcceptedDomain DSC Resource accepts a value of 'Authoritative' and 'InternalRelay'.

Identity.d: Specify the Fully Qualified Domain Name for the AcceptedDomain.

MatchSubDomains.d: The MatchSubDomains parameter must be false on Authoritative domains. The default value is false.

OutboundOnly.d: OutboundOnly can only be enabled if the DomainType parameter is set to Authoritative or InternalRelay. The default value is false.

EXOActiveSyncDeviceAccessRule.d: This resource configures Active Sync Device Access Rules in Exchange Online.

AccessLevel.d: The AccessLevel parameter specifies whether the devices are allowed, blocked or quarantined.

Characteristic.d: The Characteristic parameter specifies the device characteristic or category that's used by the rule.

Identity.d: The Identity parameter specifies the identity of the device access rule.

QueryString.d: The QueryString parameter specifies the device identifier that's used by the rule. This parameter uses a text value that's used with Characteristic parameter value to define the device.

EXOActiveSyncMailboxPolicy.d: This resource manages Mobile Device mailbox policy for mailboxes accessed by mobile devices.

AllowApplePushNotifications.d: Specifies whether push notifications are allowed for Apple mobile devices.

AllowBluetooth.d: Specifies whether the Bluetooth capabilities of the mobile phone are allowed.

AllowBrowser.d: Specifies whether Microsoft Pocket Internet Explorer is allowed on the mobile phone.

AllowCamera.d: Specifies whether the mobile phone's camera is allowed.

AllowConsumerEmail.d: Specifies whether the mobile phone user can configure a personal email account on the device.

AllowDesktopSync.d: Specifies whether the mobile phone can synchronize with a desktop computer through a cable.

AllowExternalDeviceManagement.d: Specifies whether an external device management program is allowed to manage the device.

AllowGooglePushNotifications.d: The AllowGooglePushNotifications parameter controls whether the user can receive push notifications from Google for Outlook on the web for devices. Valid input for this parameter is $true or $false. The default value is $true.

AllowHTMLEmail.d: Specifies whether HTML email is enabled on the device.

AllowInternetSharing.d: Specifies whether the mobile phone can be used as a modem to connect a computer to the Internet.

AllowIrDA.d: Specifies whether infrared connections are allowed to the mobile phone.

AllowMicrosoftPushNotifications.d: The AllowMicrosoftPushNotifications parameter specifies whether push notifications are enabled on the mobile device. Valid input for this parameter is $true or $false. The default value is $true.

AllowMobileOTAUpdate.d: Specifies whether certain updates are seen by devices that implemented support for this restricting functionality.

AllowNonProvisionableDevices.d: Enables all devices to synchronize with the computer running Exchange, regardless of whether the device can enforce all the specific settings established in the Mobile Device mailbox policy.

AllowPOPIMAPEmail.d: Specifies whether the user can configure a POP3 or IMAP4 email account on the device.

AllowRemoteDesktop.d: Specifies whether the mobile phone can initiate a remote desktop connection.

AllowSMIMEEncryptionAlgorithmNegotiation.d: Specifies whether the messaging application on the device can negotiate the encryption algorithm in case a recipient's certificate doesn't support the specified encryption algorithm.

AllowSMIMESoftCerts.d: Specifies whether S/MIME software certificates are allowed.

AllowSimplePassword.d: Specifies whether a simple device password is allowed.

AllowStorageCard.d: Specifies whether the device can access information stored on a storage card.

AllowTextMessaging.d: Specifies whether text messaging is allowed from the device.

AllowUnsignedApplications.d: Specifies whether unsigned applications can be installed on the device.

AllowUnsignedInstallationPackages.d: Specifies whether unsigned installation packages can be run on the device.

AllowWiFi.d: Specifies whether wireless Internet access is allowed on the device.

AlphanumericPasswordRequired.d: Specifies whether the device password must be alphanumeric.

ApprovedApplicationList.d: Specifies a list of approved applications for the device.

AttachmentsEnabled.d: Specifies whether the user can download attachments.

DeviceEncryptionEnabled.d: Enables device encryption on the mobile phone.

DevicePolicyRefreshInterval.d: Specifies how often the policy is sent from the server to the mobile phone

Identity.d: Specifies the Mobile Device mailbox policy.

IrmEnabled.d: Specifies whether Information Rights Management (IRM) is enabled for the mailbox policy.

IsDefault.d: Specifies whether this policy is the default Mobile Device mailbox policy.

IsDefaultPolicy.d: Specifies whether this policy is the default Mobile Device mailbox policy.

MaxAttachmentSize.d: Specifies the maximum size of attachments that can be downloaded to the mobile phone.

MaxCalendarAgeFilter.d: Specifies the maximum range of calendar days that can be synchronized to the device.

MaxEmailAgeFilter.d: Specifies the maximum number of days of email items to synchronize to the device.

MaxEmailBodyTruncationSize.d: Specifies the maximum size at which email messages are truncated when synchronized to the device.

MaxEmailHTMLBodyTruncationSize.d: Specifies the maximum size at which HTML-formatted email messages are synchronized to the device.

MaxInactivityTimeLock.d: Specifies the length of time that the device can be inactive before the password is required to reactivate the device.

MaxPasswordFailedAttempts.d: Specifies the number of attempts a user can make to enter the correct password for the device.

MinPasswordComplexCharacters.d: Specifies the minimum number of complex characters required in a device password.

MinPasswordLength.d: Specifies the minimum number of characters in the device password.

Name.d: Specifies the name of the policy.

PasswordEnabled.d: Specifies that the user set a password for the device.

PasswordExpiration.d: Specifies the length of time, in days, that a password can be used.

PasswordHistory.d: Specifies the number of previously used passwords to store.

PasswordRecoveryEnabled.d: Specifies whether you can store the recovery password for the device on an Exchange server.

RequireDeviceEncryption.d: Specifies whether encryption is required on the device.

RequireEncryptedSMIMEMessages.d: Specifies whether you must encrypt S/MIME messages.

RequireEncryptionSMIMEAlgorithm.d: Specifies what required algorithm must be used when encrypting a message.

RequireManualSyncWhenRoaming.d: Specifies whether the device must synchronize manually while roaming.

RequireSignedSMIMEAlgorithm.d: Specifies what required algorithm must be used when signing a message.

RequireSignedSMIMEMessages.d: Specifies whether the device must send signed S/MIME messages.

RequireStorageCardEncryption.d: Specifies whether encryption of a storage card is required.

UNCAccessEnabled.d: Specifies whether access to Microsoft Windows file shares is enabled.

UnapprovedInROMApplicationList.d: Specifies a list of applications that can't be run in ROM.

WSSAccessEnabled.d: Specifies whether access to Microsoft Windows SharePoint Services is enabled.

EXOAddressBookPolicy.d: This resource configures Address Book Policies in Exchange Online.

AddressLists.d: The AddressLists parameter specifies the address lists that will be used by mailbox users who are assigned this address book policy. This parameter accepts multiple values.

GlobalAddressList.d: The GlobalAddressList parameter specifies the identity of the global address list (GAL) that will be used by mailbox users who are assigned this address book policy. You can specify only one GAL for each address book policy.

Name.d: The Name parameter specifies the name that you want this address book policy to be called.

OfflineAddressBook.d: The OfflineAddressBook parameter specifies the identity of the offline address book (OAB) that will be used by mailbox users who are assigned this address book policy. You can specify only one OAB for each address book policy.

RoomList.d: The RoomList parameter specifies the name of the room address list.

EXOAddressList.d: This resource configures Exchange Online address lists.

ConditionalCompany.d: The ConditionalCompany parameter specifies a precanned filter that's based on the value of the recipient's Company property.

ConditionalCustomAttribute1.d: The ConditionalCustomAttribute1 parameter specifies a precanned filter that's based on the value of the recipient's CustomAttribute1 property.

ConditionalCustomAttribute10.d: The ConditionalCustomAttribute10 parameter specifies a precanned filter that's based on the value of the recipient's CustomAttribute10 property.

ConditionalCustomAttribute11.d: The ConditionalCustomAttribute11 parameter specifies a precanned filter that's based on the value of the recipient's CustomAttribute11 property.

ConditionalCustomAttribute12.d: The ConditionalCustomAttribute12 parameter specifies a precanned filter that's based on the value of the recipient's CustomAttribute12 property.

ConditionalCustomAttribute13.d: The ConditionalCustomAttribute13 parameter specifies a precanned filter that's based on the value of the recipient's CustomAttribute13 property.

ConditionalCustomAttribute14.d: The ConditionalCustomAttribute14 parameter specifies a precanned filter that's based on the value of the recipient's CustomAttribute14 property.

ConditionalCustomAttribute15.d: The ConditionalCustomAttribute15 parameter specifies a precanned filter that's based on the value of the recipient's CustomAttribute15 property.

ConditionalCustomAttribute2.d: The ConditionalCustomAttribute2 parameter specifies a precanned filter that's based on the value of the recipient's CustomAttribute2 property.

ConditionalCustomAttribute3.d: The ConditionalCustomAttribute3 parameter specifies a precanned filter that's based on the value of the recipient's CustomAttribute3 property.

ConditionalCustomAttribute4.d: The ConditionalCustomAttribute4 parameter specifies a precanned filter that's based on the value of the recipient's CustomAttribute4 property.

ConditionalCustomAttribute5.d: The ConditionalCustomAttribute5 parameter specifies a precanned filter that's based on the value of the recipient's CustomAttribute5 property.

ConditionalCustomAttribute6.d: The ConditionalCustomAttribute6 parameter specifies a precanned filter that's based on the value of the recipient's CustomAttribute6 property.

ConditionalCustomAttribute7.d: The ConditionalCustomAttribute7 parameter specifies a precanned filter that's based on the value of the recipient's CustomAttribute7 property.

ConditionalCustomAttribute8.d: The ConditionalCustomAttribute8 parameter specifies a precanned filter that's based on the value of the recipient's CustomAttribute8 property.

ConditionalCustomAttribute9.d: The ConditionalCustomAttribute9 parameter specifies a precanned filter that's based on the value of the recipient's CustomAttribute9 property.

ConditionalDepartment.d: The ConditionalDepartment parameter specifies a precanned filter that's based on the value of the recipient's Department property.

ConditionalStateOrProvince.d: The ConditionalStateOrProvince parameter specifies a precanned filter that's based on the value of the recipient's StateOrProvince property.

DisplayName.d: The DisplayName parameter specifies the display name of the address list.

IncludedRecipients.d: The IncludedRecipients parameter specifies a precanned filter that's based on the recipient type.

Name.d: The Name parameter specifies a unique name for the address list.

RecipientFilter.d: The RecipientFilter parameter specifies a custom OPath filter that's based on the value of any available recipient property.

EXOAntiPhishPolicy.d: This resource configures an Anti-Phish Policy in Exchange Online.

AdminDisplayName.d: The AdminDisplayName parameter specifies a description for the policy.

AuthenticationFailAction.d: The AuthenticationFailAction parameter specifies the action to take when the message fails composite authentication.

DmarcQuarantineAction.d: The DmarcQuarantineAction parameter specifies the action to take when a message fails DMARC checks and the sender's DMARC policy is p=quarantine

DmarcRejectAction.d: The DmarcRejectAction parameter specifies the action to take when a message fails DMARC checks and the sender's DMARC policy is p=reject.

EnableFirstContactSafetyTips.d: The EnableFirstContactSafetyTips parameter specifies whether to enable or disable the safety tip that's shown when recipients first receive an email from a sender or do not often receive email from a sender.

EnableMailboxIntelligence.d: The EnableMailboxIntelligence parameter specifies whether to enable or disable mailbox intelligence (the first contact graph) in domain and user impersonation protection.

EnableMailboxIntelligenceProtection.d: The EnableMailboxIntelligenceProtection specifies whether to enable or disable enhanced impersonation results based on each user's individual sender map. This intelligence allows Microsoft 365 to customize user impersonation detection and better handle false positives.

EnableOrganizationDomainsProtection.d: The EnableOrganizationDomainsProtection parameter specifies whether to enable domain impersonation protection for all registered domains in the Office 365 organization.

EnableSimilarDomainsSafetyTips.d: The EnableSimilarDomainsSafetyTips parameter specifies whether to enable safety tips that are shown to recipients in messages for domain impersonation detections.

EnableSimilarUsersSafetyTips.d: The EnableSimilarUsersSafetyTips parameter specifies whether to enable safety tips that are shown to recipients in messages for user impersonation detections.

EnableSpoofIntelligence.d: The EnableSpoofIntelligence parameter specifies whether to enable or disable antispoofing protection for the policy.

EnableTargetedDomainsProtection.d: The EnableTargetedDomainsProtection parameter specifies whether to enable domain impersonation protection for a list of specified domains.

EnableTargetedUserProtection.d: The EnableTargetedUserProtection parameter specifies whether to enable user impersonation protection for the users specified by the TargetedUsersToProtect parameter

EnableUnauthenticatedSender.d: The EnableUnauthenticatedSender parameter enables or disables unauthenticated sender identification in Outlook.

EnableUnusualCharactersSafetyTips.d: The EnableUnusualCharactersSafetyTips parameter specifies whether to enable safety tips that are shown to recipients in messages for unusual characters in domain and user impersonation detections.

EnableViaTag.d: This setting is part of spoof protection. The EnableViaTag parameter enables or disables adding the via tag to the From address in Outlook.

Enabled.d: Specify if this policy should be enabled. Default is $true.

ExcludedDomains.d: The ExcludedDomains parameter specifies trusted domains that are excluded from scanning by antiphishing protection. You can specify multiple domains separated by commas.

ExcludedSenders.d: The ExcludedSenders parameter specifies a list of trusted sender email addresses that are excluded from scanning by antiphishing protection. You can specify multiple email addresses separated by commas.

HonorDmarcPolicy.d: The HonorDmarcPolicy enables or disables using the sender's DMARC policy to determine what to do to messages that fail DMARC checks.

Identity.d: The Identity parameter specifies the name of the antiphishing policy that you want to modify.

ImpersonationProtectionState.d: The ImpersonationProtectionState parameter specifies the configuration of impersonation protection.

MailboxIntelligenceProtectionAction.d: The MailboxIntelligenceProtectionAction parameter specifies what to do with messages that fail mailbox intelligence protection.

MailboxIntelligenceProtectionActionRecip.d: The MailboxIntelligenceProtectionActionRecipients parameter specifies the recipients to add to detected messages when the MailboxIntelligenceProtectionAction parameter is set to the value Redirect or BccMessage.

MailboxIntelligenceQuarantineTag.d: The MailboxIntelligenceQuarantineTag specifies the quarantine policy that's used on messages that are quarantined by mailbox intelligence.

MakeDefault.d: Make this the default antiphishing policy

PhishThresholdLevel.d: The PhishThresholdLevel parameter specifies the tolerance level that's used by machine learning in the handling of phishing messages.

SpoofQuarantineTag.d: The SpoofQuarantineTag specifies the quarantine policy that's used on messages that are quarantined by spoof intelligence.

TargetedDomainActionRecipients.d: The TargetedDomainActionRecipients parameter specifies the recipients to add to detected domain impersonation messages when the TargetedDomainProtectionAction parameter is set to the value Redirect or BccMessage. A valid value for this parameter is an email address. You can specify multiple email addresses separated by commas.

TargetedDomainProtectionAction.d: The TargetedDomainProtectionAction parameter specifies the action to take on detected domain impersonation messages.

TargetedDomainQuarantineTag.d: The TargetedDomainQuarantineTag specifies the quarantine policy that's used on messages that are quarantined by domain impersonation protection.

TargetedDomainsToProtect.d: The TargetedDomainsToProtect parameter specifies the domains that are included in domain impersonation protection when the EnableTargetedDomainsProtection parameter is set to $true.

TargetedUserActionRecipients.d: The TargetedUserActionRecipients parameter specifies the replacement or additional recipients for detected user impersonation messages when the TargetedUserProtectionAction parameter is set to the value Redirect or BccMessage. A valid value for this parameter is an email address. You can specify multiple email addresses separated by commas.

TargetedUserProtectionAction.d: The TargetedUserProtectionAction parameter specifies the action to take on detected user impersonation messages for the users specified by the TargetedUsersToProtect parameter.

TargetedUserQuarantineTag.d: The TargetedUserQuarantineTag specifies the quarantine policy that's used on messages that are quarantined by user impersonation protection.

TargetedUsersToProtect.d: The TargetedUsersToProtect parameter specifies the users that are included in user impersonation protection when the EnableTargetedUserProtection parameter is set to $true.

EXOAntiPhishRule.d: This resource configures an Anti-Phish Rule in Exchange Online.

AntiPhishPolicy.d: The AntiPhishPolicy parameter specifies the name of the antiphishing policy that's associated with the antiphishing rule.

Comments.d: The Comments parameter specifies informative comments for the rule, such as what the rule is used for or how it has changed over time. The length of the comment can't exceed 1024 characters.

Enabled.d: Specify if this rule should be enabled. Default is $true.

ExceptIfRecipientDomainIs.d: The ExceptIfRecipientDomainIs parameter specifies an exception that looks for recipients with email address in the specified domains. You can specify multiple domains separated by commas.

ExceptIfSentTo.d: The ExceptIfSentTo parameter specifies an exception that looks for recipients in messages. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the recipient.

ExceptIfSentToMemberOf.d: The ExceptIfSentToMemberOf parameter specifies an exception that looks for messages sent to members of groups. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the group.

Identity.d: The Identity parameter specifies the name of the antiphishing rule that you want to modify.

Priority.d: The Priority parameter specifies a priority value for the rule that determines the order of rule processing. A lower integer value indicates a higher priority, the value 0 is the highest priority, and rules can't have the same priority value.

RecipientDomainIs.d: The RecipientDomainIs parameter specifies a condition that looks for recipients with email address in the specified domains. You can specify multiple domains separated by commas.

SentTo.d: The SentTo parameter specifies a condition that looks for recipients in messages. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the recipient.

SentToMemberOf.d: The SentToMemberOf parameter looks for messages sent to members of groups. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the group.

EXOApplicationAccessPolicy.d: This resource configures Applications Access Policies in Exchange Online.

AccessRight.d: The AccessRight parameter specifies the permission that you want to assign in the application access policy.

AppID.d: The AppID parameter specifies the GUID of the apps to include in the policy.

Description.d: The Description parameter specifies a description for the policy.

Identity.d: The Identity parameter specifies the application access policy that you want to modify.

PolicyScopeGroupId.d: The PolicyScopeGroupID parameter specifies the recipient to define in the policy. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the recipient.

EXOArcConfig.d: This resource manages the list of trusted Authenticated Received Chain (ARC) sealers that are configured in the organization.

ArcTrustedSealers.d: The domain names of the ARC sealers.

EXOAtpPolicyForO365.d: This resource configures the Advanced Threat Protection (ATP) policy in Office 365. Tenant must be subscribed to ATP.

AllowSafeDocsOpen.d: The AllowSafeDocsOpen parameter specifies whether users can click through and bypass the Protected View container even when Safe Documents identifies a file as malicious.

EnableATPForSPOTeamsODB.d: The EnableATPForSPOTeamsODB parameter specifies whether ATP is enabled for SharePoint Online, OneDrive for Business and Microsoft Teams. Default is $false.

EnableSafeDocs.d: The EnableSafeDocs parameter specifies whether to enable the Safe Documents feature in the organization. Default is $false.

Identity.d: The Identity parameter specifies the ATP policy that you want to modify. There's only one policy named Default.

IsSingleInstance.d: Specifies the resource is a single instance, the value must be 'Yes'

EXOAtpProtectionPolicyRule.d: Manage ATP Protection policy rules that are associated with Microsoft Defender for Office 365 protections in preset security policies.

Comments.d: Informative comments for the rule, such as what the rule is used for or how it has changed over time. The length of the comment can't exceed 1024 characters.

Enabled.d: Specifies whether the rule is enabled

ExceptIfRecipientDomainIs.d: Specifies an exception that looks for recipients with email addresses in the specified domains.

ExceptIfSentTo.d: Specifies an exception that looks for recipients in messages. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the recipient

ExceptIfSentToMemberOf.d: Specifies an exception that looks for messages sent to members of groups. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the group.

Identity.d: Identifier for the rule

Name.d: Unique name for the rule. The maximum length is 64 characters.

Priority.d: Specifies a priority value for the rule that determines the order of rule processing. A lower integer value indicates a higher priority, the value 0 is the highest priority, and rules can't have the same priority value.

RecipientDomainIs.d: Specifies a condition that looks for recipients with email addresses in the specified domains.

SafeAttachmentPolicy.d: Specifies the existing Safe Attachments policy that's associated with the preset security policy.

SafeLinksPolicy.d: Specifies the existing Safe Links policy that's associated with the preset security policy.

SentTo.d: Specifies a condition that looks for recipients in messages. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the recipient.

SentToMemberOf.d: Specifies a condition that looks for messages sent to members of distribution groups, dynamic distribution groups, or mail-enabled security groups.

EXOAuthenticationPolicy.d: This resource configures Authentication Policies in Exchange Online.

AllowBasicAuthActiveSync.d: The AllowBasicAuthActiveSync switch specifies whether to allow Basic authentication with Exchange Active Sync.

AllowBasicAuthAutodiscover.d: The AllowBasicAuthAutodiscover switch specifies whether to allow Basic authentication with Autodiscover.

AllowBasicAuthImap.d: The AllowBasicAuthImap switch specifies whether to allow Basic authentication with IMAP.

AllowBasicAuthMapi.d: The AllowBasicAuthMapi switch specifies whether to allow Basic authentication with MAPI.

AllowBasicAuthOfflineAddressBook.d: The AllowBasicAuthOfflineAddressBook switch specifies whether to allow Basic authentication with Offline Address Books.

AllowBasicAuthOutlookService.d: The AllowBasicAuthOutlookService switch specifies whether to allow Basic authentication with the Outlook service.

AllowBasicAuthPop.d: The AllowBasicAuthPop switch specifies whether to allow Basic authentication with POP.

AllowBasicAuthPowershell.d: The AllowBasicAuthPowerShell switch specifies whether to allow Basic authentication with PowerShell.

AllowBasicAuthReportingWebServices.d: The AllowBasicAuthReporting Web Services switch specifies whether to allow Basic authentication with reporting web services.

AllowBasicAuthRpc.d: The AllowBasicAuthRpc switch specifies whether to allow Basic authentication with RPC.

AllowBasicAuthSmtp.d: The AllowBasicAuthSmtp switch specifies whether to allow Basic authentication with SMTP.

AllowBasicAuthWebServices.d: The AllowBasicAuthWebServices switch specifies whether to allow Basic authentication with Exchange Web Services (EWS).

Identity.d: The Identity parameter specifies the authentication policy you want to view or modify.

EXOAuthenticationPolicyAssignment.d: This resource assigns Exchange Online Authentication Policies to users.

UserName.d: Name of the user assigned to the authentication policy.

EXOAvailabilityAddressSpace.d: Create a new AvailabilityAddressSpace in your cloud-based organization.

AccessMethod.d: The AccessMethod parameter specifies how the free/busy data is accessed. Valid values are:PerUserFB, OrgWideFB, OrgWideFBToken, OrgWideFBBasic,InternalProxy

Credentials.d: The Credentials parameter specifies the username and password that's used to access the Availability services in the target forest.

ForestName.d: The ForestName parameter specifies the SMTP domain name of the target forest for users whose free/busy data must be retrieved. If your users are distributed among multiple SMTP domains in the target forest, run the Add-AvailabilityAddressSpace command once for each SMTP domain.

Identity.d: The Identity parameter specifies the AvailabilityAddressSpace you want to modify.

TargetAutodiscoverEpr.d: The TargetAutodiscoverEpr parameter specifies the Autodiscover URL of Exchange Web Services for the external organization. Exchange uses Autodiscover to automatically detect the correct server endpoint for external requests.

TargetServiceEpr.d: The TargetServiceEpr parameter specifies the Exchange Online Calendar Service URL of the external Microsoft 365 organization that you're trying to read free/busy information from.

TargetTenantId.d: The TargetTenantID parameter specifies the tenant ID of the external Microsoft 365 organization that you're trying to read free/busy information from.

EXOAvailabilityConfig.d: This resource configures the Availability Config in Exchange Online.

AllowedTenantIds.d: The AllowedTenantIds parameter specifies the tenant ID values of Microsoft 365 organization that you want to share free/busy information with (for example, d6b0a40e-029b-43f2-9852-f3724f68ead9). You can specify multiple values separated by commas. A maximum of 25 values are allowed.

OrgWideAccount.d: Specify the OrgWideAccount for the AvailabilityConfig.

EXOCASMailboxPlan.d: This resource configures Client Access services (CAS) mailbox plans in cloud-based organizations.

ActiveSyncEnabled.d: The ActiveSyncEnabled parameter enables or disables access to the mailbox by using Exchange Active Sync. Default is $true.

DisplayName.d: The display name of the CAS Mailbox Plan.

Identity.d: The Identity parameter specifies the CAS Mailbox Plan that you want to modify.

ImapEnabled.d: The ImapEnabled parameter enables or disables access to the mailbox by using IMAP4 clients. The default value is $true for all CAS mailbox plans except ExchangeOnlineDeskless which is $false by default.

OwaMailboxPolicy.d: The OwaMailboxPolicy parameter specifies the Outlook on the web (formerly known as Outlook Web App) mailbox policy for the mailbox plan. The default value is OwaMailboxPolicy-Default. You can use the Get-OwaMailboxPolicy cmdlet to view the available Outlook on the web mailbox policies.

PopEnabled.d: The PopEnabled parameter enables or disables access to the mailbox by using POP3 clients. Default is $true.

EXOCASMailboxSettings.d: This resource configures CAS mailbox settings.

ActiveSyncAllowedDeviceIDs.d: TheActiveSyncAllowedDeviceIDs parameter specifies one or more Exchange ActiveSync device IDs that are allowed to synchronize with the mailbox.

ActiveSyncBlockedDeviceIDs.d: The ActiveSyncBlockedDeviceIDs parameter specifies one or more Exchange ActiveSync device IDs that aren't allowed to synchronize with the mailbox.

ActiveSyncDebugLogging.d: The ActiveSyncDebugLogging parameter enables or disables Exchange ActiveSync debug logging for the mailbox.

ActiveSyncEnabled.d: The ActiveSyncEnabled parameter enables or disables access to the mailbox using Exchange ActiveSync.

ActiveSyncMailboxPolicy.d: The ActiveSyncMailboxPolicy parameter specifies the Exchange ActiveSync mailbox policy for the mailbox.

ActiveSyncSuppressReadReceipt.d: The ActiveSyncSuppressReadReceipt parameter controls the behavior of read receipts for Exchange ActiveSync clients that access the mailbox.

EwsAllowEntourage.d: The EwsAllowEntourage parameter enables or disables access to the mailbox by Microsoft Entourage clients that use Exchange Web Services.

EwsAllowList.d: The EwsAllowList parameter specifies the Exchange Web Services applications (user agent strings) that are allowed to access the mailbox.

EwsAllowMacOutlook.d: The EwsAllowMacOutlook parameter enables or disables access to the mailbox by Outlook for Mac clients that use Exchange Web Services.

EwsAllowOutlook.d: The EwsAllowOutlook parameter enables or disables access to the mailbox by Outlook clients that use Exchange Web Services.

EwsApplicationAccessPolicy.d: The EwsApplicationAccessPolicy parameter controls access to the mailbox using Exchange Web Services applications.

EwsBlockList.d: The EwsBlockList parameter specifies the Exchange Web Services applications (user agent strings) that aren't allowed to access the mailbox using Exchange Web Services.

EwsEnabled.d: The EwsEnabled parameter enables or disables access to the mailbox using Exchange Web Services clients.

Identity.d: The Identity parameter specifies the mailbox that you want to configure.

ImapEnabled.d: The ImapEnabled parameter enables or disables access to the mailbox using IMAP4 clients.

ImapForceICalForCalendarRetrievalOption.d: The ImapForceICalForCalendarRetrievalOption parameter specifies how meeting requests are presented to IMAP4 clients that access the mailbox.

ImapMessagesRetrievalMimeFormat.d: The ImapMessagesRetrievalMimeFormat parameter specifies the message format for IMAP4 clients that access the mailbox.

ImapSuppressReadReceipt.d: The ImapSuppressReadReceipt parameter controls the behavior of read receipts for IMAP4 clients that access the mailbox.

ImapUseProtocolDefaults.d: The ImapUseProtocolDefaults parameter specifies whether to use the IMAP4 protocol defaults for the mailbox.

MAPIEnabled.d: The MAPIEnabled parameter enables or disables access to the mailbox using MAPI clients (for example, Outlook).

MacOutlookEnabled.d: The MacOutlookEnabled parameter enables or disables access to the mailbox using Outlook for Mac clients that use Microsoft Sync technology.

OWAEnabled.d: The OWAEnabled parameter enables or disables access to the mailbox using Outlook on the web (formerly known as Outlook Web App or OWA).

OWAforDevicesEnabled.d: The OWAforDevicesEnabled parameter enables or disables access to the mailbox using the older Outlook Web App (OWA) app on iOS and Android devices.

OneWinNativeOutlookEnabled.d: The OneWinNativeOutlookEnabled parameter enables or disables access to the mailbox using the new Outlook for Windows.

OutlookMobileEnabled.d: The OutlookMobileEnabled parameter enables or disables access to the mailbox using Outlook for iOS and Android.

OwaMailboxPolicy.d: The OwaMailboxPolicy parameter specifies the Outlook on the web mailbox policy for the mailbox.

PopEnabled.d: The PopEnabled parameter enables or disables access to the mailbox using POP3 clients.

PopForceICalForCalendarRetrievalOption.d: The PopForceICalForCalendarRetrievalOption parameter specifies how meeting requests are presented to POP3 clients that access the mailbox.

PopMessagesRetrievalMimeFormat.d: The PopMessagesRetrievalMimeFormat parameter specifies the message format for POP3 clients that access the mailbox.

PopSuppressReadReceipt.d: The PopSuppressReadReceipt parameter controls the behavior of read receipts for POP3 clients that access the mailbox.

PopUseProtocolDefaults.d: The PopUseProtocolDefaults parameter specifies whether to use the POP3 protocol defaults for the mailbox.

PublicFolderClientAccess.d: The PublicFolderClientAccess parameter enables or disables access to public folders in Microsoft Outlook.

ShowGalAsDefaultView.d: The ShowGalAsDefaultView parameter specifies whether the global address list (GAL) is the default recipient picker for messages.

SmtpClientAuthenticationDisabled.d: The SmtpClientAuthenticationDisabled parameter specifies whether to disable authenticated SMTP (SMTP AUTH) for the mailbox.

UniversalOutlookEnabled.d: The UniversalOutlookEnabled parameter enables or disables access to the mailbox using Windows 10 Mail and Calendar.

EXOCalendarProcessing.d: This resource configures the Calendar Processing settings Exchange Online.

AddAdditionalResponse.d: The AddAdditionalResponse parameter specifies whether additional information (the value of the AdditionalResponse parameter) is added to meeting request responses

AddNewRequestsTentatively.d: The AddNewRequestsTentatively parameter specifies whether new meeting requests are added to the calendar as tentative

AddOrganizerToSubject.d: The AddOrganizerToSubject parameter specifies whether the meeting organizer's name is used as the subject of the meeting request.

AdditionalResponse.d: The AdditionalResponse parameter specifies the additional information to be included in responses to meeting requests when the value of the AddAdditionalResponse parameter is $true. If the value contains spaces, enclose the value in quotation marks.

AllBookInPolicy.d: The AllBookInPolicy parameter specifies whether to automatically approve in-policy requests from all users to the resource mailbox.

AllRequestInPolicy.d: The AllRequestInPolicy parameter specifies whether to allow all users to submit in-policy requests to the resource mailbox.

AllRequestOutOfPolicy.d: The AllRequestOutOfPolicy parameter specifies whether to allow all users to submit out-of-policy requests to the resource mailbox.

AllowConflicts.d: The AllowConflicts parameter specifies whether to allow conflicting meeting requests.

AllowRecurringMeetings.d: The AllowRecurringMeetings parameter specifies whether to allow recurring meetings in meeting requests.

AutomateProcessing.d: The AutomateProcessing parameter enables or disables calendar processing on the mailbox.

BookInPolicy.d: The BookInPolicy parameter specifies users or groups who are allowed to submit in-policy meeting requests to the resource mailbox that are automatically approved. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the user or group.

BookingType.d: The BookingType parameter specifies how reservations work on the resource mailbox.

BookingWindowInDays.d: The BookingWindowInDays parameter specifies the maximum number of days in advance that the resource can be reserved. A valid value is an integer from 0 through 1080. The default value is 180 days. The value 0 means today.

ConflictPercentageAllowed.d: The ConflictPercentageAllowed parameter specifies the maximum percentage of meeting conflicts for new recurring meeting requests. A valid value is an integer from 0 through 100. The default value is 0.

DeleteAttachments.d: The DeleteAttachments parameter specifies whether to remove attachments from all incoming messages.

DeleteComments.d: The DeleteComments parameter specifies whether to remove or keep any text in the message body of incoming meeting requests.

DeleteNonCalendarItems.d: The DeleteNonCalendarItems parameter specifies whether to remove or keep all non-calendar-related messages that are received by the resource mailbox.

DeleteSubject.d: The DeleteSubject parameter specifies whether to remove or keep the subject of incoming meeting requests.

EnableResponseDetails.d: The EnableResponseDetails parameter specifies whether to include the reasons for accepting or declining a meeting in the response email message.

EnforceCapacity.d: The EnforceCapacity parameter specifies whether to restrict the number of attendees to the capacity of the workspace. For example, if capacity is set to 10, then only 10 people can book the workspace.

EnforceSchedulingHorizon.d: The EnforceSchedulingHorizon parameter controls the behavior of recurring meetings that extend beyond the date specified by the BookingWindowInDays parameter.

ForwardRequestsToDelegates.d: The ForwardRequestsToDelegates parameter specifies whether to forward incoming meeting requests to the delegates that are configured for the resource mailbox.

Identity.d: The Identity parameter specifies the resource mailbox that you want to view. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the mailbox.

MaximumConflictInstances.d: The MaximumConflictInstances parameter specifies the maximum number of conflicts for new recurring meeting requests when the AllowRecurringMeetings parameter is set to $true. A valid value is an integer from 0 through INT32 (2147483647). The default value is 0.

MaximumDurationInMinutes.d: The MaximumDurationInMinutes parameter specifies the maximum duration in minutes for meeting requests. A valid value is an integer from 0 through INT32 (2147483647). The default value is 1440 (24 hours).

MinimumDurationInMinutes.d: The MinimumDurationInMinutes parameter specifies the minimum duration in minutes for meeting requests in workspace mailboxes. A valid value is an integer from 0 through INT32 (2147483647). The default value is 0, which means there is no minimum duration.

OrganizerInfo.d: The OrganizerInfo parameter specifies whether the resource mailbox sends organizer information when a meeting request is declined because of conflicts.

ProcessExternalMeetingMessages.d: The ProcessExternalMeetingMessages parameter specifies whether to process meeting requests that originate outside the Exchange organization.

RemoveCanceledMeetings.d: The RemoveCanceledMeetings parameter specifies whether to automatically delete meetings that were cancelled by the organizer from the resource mailbox's calendar.

RemoveForwardedMeetingNotifications.d: The RemoveForwardedMeetingNotifications parameter specifies whether forwarded meeting notifications are moved to the Deleted Items folder after they're processed by the Calendar Attendant.

RemoveOldMeetingMessages.d: The RemoveOldMeetingMessages parameter specifies whether the Calendar Attendant removes old and redundant updates and responses.

RemovePrivateProperty.d: The RemovePrivateProperty parameter specifies whether to clear the private flag for incoming meetings that were sent by the organizer in the original requests.

RequestInPolicy.d: The RemovePrivateProperty parameter specifies whether to clear the private flag for incoming meetings that were sent by the organizer in the original requests.

RequestOutOfPolicy.d: The RequestOutOfPolicy parameter specifies users who are allowed to submit out-of-policy requests that require approval by a resource mailbox delegate. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the user.

ResourceDelegates.d: The ResourceDelegates parameter specifies users can approve or reject requests that are sent to the resource mailbox. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the user.

ScheduleOnlyDuringWorkHours.d: The ScheduleOnlyDuringWorkHours parameter specifies whether to allow meetings to be scheduled outside of the working hours that are defined for the resource mailbox.

TentativePendingApproval.d: The TentativePendingApproval parameter specifies whether to mark pending requests as tentative on the calendar.

EXODataAtRestEncryptionPolicy.d: Microsoft 365 data-at-rest encryption policy for multi-workload usage.

AzureKeyIDs.d: The AzureKeyIDs parameter specifies the URL of the encryption key in the Azure Key Vault that's used for encryption.

Description.d: The Description parameter specifies an optional description for the policy.

Enabled.d: The Enabled parameter specifies whether the policy is enabled or disabled.

Identity.d: The Identity parameter specifies the data-at-rest encryption policy that you want to modify.

Name.d: The Name parameter specifies a unique name for the Microsoft 365 data-at-rest encryption policy.

EXODataAtRestEncryptionPolicyAssignment.d: Use the Set-M365DataAtRestEncryptionPolicyAssignment cmdlet to assign a Microsoft 365 data-at-rest encryption policy at the tenant level.

DataEncryptionPolicy.d: The DataEncryptionPolicy parameter specifies the Microsoft 365 data-at-rest encryption policy.

EXODataClassification.d: Create a new data classification policy in your cloud-based organization.

Description.d: The Description parameter specifies a description for the data classification rule. You use the Description parameter with the Locale and Name parameters to specify descriptions for the data classification rule in different languages.

Fingerprints.d: The Fingerprints parameter specifies the byte-encoded document files that are used as fingerprints by the data classification rule.

Identity.d: The Identity parameter specifies the data classification rule that you want to modify.

IsDefault.d: IsDefault is used with the Locale parameter to specify the default language for the data classification rule.

Locale.d: The Locale parameter adds or removes languages that are associated with the data classification rule.

Name.d: The Name parameter specifies a name for the data classification rule. The value must be less than 256 characters.

EXODataEncryptionPolicy.d: Create a new Data Encryption policy in your cloud-based organization.

AzureKeyIDs.d: The AzureKeyIDs parameter specifies the URI values of the Azure Key Vault keys to associate with the data encryption policy.

Description.d: The Description parameter specifies an optional description for the data encryption policy

Enabled.d: The Enabled parameter enables or disable the data encryption policy.

Identity.d: The Identity parameter specifies the data encryption policy that you want to modify.

Name.d: The Name parameter specifies the unique name for the data encryption policy.

PermanentDataPurgeContact.d: The PermanentDataPurgeContact parameter specifies a contact for the purge of all data that's encrypted by the data encryption policy.

PermanentDataPurgeReason.d: The PermanentDataPurgeReason parameter specifies a descriptive reason for the purge of all data that's encrypted by the data encryption policy

EXODistributionGroup.d: This resource configures Exchange Online distribution groups and mail-enabled security groups.

AcceptMessagesOnlyFrom.d: The AcceptMessagesOnlyFrom parameter specifies who is allowed to send messages to this recipient. Messages from other senders are rejected.

AcceptMessagesOnlyFromDLMembers.d: The AcceptMessagesOnlyFromDLMembers parameter specifies who is allowed to send messages to this recipient. Messages from other senders are rejected.

AcceptMessagesOnlyFromSendersOrMembers.d: The AcceptMessagesOnlyFromSendersOrMembers parameter specifies who is allowed to send messages to this recipient. Messages from other senders are rejected.

Alias.d: Exchange alias (also known as the mail nickname) for the recipient

BccBlocked.d: Is Bcc blocked for the distribution group.

BypassModerationFromSendersOrMembers.d: The BypassModerationFromSendersOrMembers parameter specifies who is allowed to send messages to this moderated recipient without approval from a moderator. Valid values for this parameter are individual senders and groups in your organization. Specifying a group means all members of the group are allowed to send messages to this recipient without approval from a moderator.

BypassNestedModerationEnabled.d: The ByPassNestedModerationEnabled parameter specifies how to handle message approval when a moderated group contains other moderated groups as members.

CustomAttribute1.d: This parameter specifies a value for the CustomAttribute1 property on the recipient. You can use this property to store custom information about the recipient, and to identify the recipient in filters. The maximum length is 1024 characters. If the value contains spaces, enclose the value in quotation marks.

Description.d: Description of the distribution group.

DisplayName.d: The DisplayName parameter specifies the display name of the group. The display name is visible in the Exchange admin center and in address lists. The maximum length is 256 characters.

EmailAddresses.d: The EmailAddresses parameter specifies all email addresses (proxy addresses) for the recipient, including the primary SMTP address. In on-premises Exchange organizations, the primary SMTP address and other proxy addresses are typically set by email address policies. However, you can use this parameter to configure other proxy addresses for the recipient.

GrantSendOnBehalfTo.d: The GrantSendOnBehalfTo parameter specifies who can send on behalf of this group. Although messages send on behalf of the group clearly show the sender in the From field (<Sender> on behalf of <Group>), replies to these messages are delivered to the group, not the sender.

HiddenFromAddressListsEnabled.d: The HiddenFromAddressListsEnabled parameter specifies whether this recipient is visible in address lists.

HiddenGroupMembershipEnabled.d: The HiddenGroupMembershipEnabled switch specifies whether to hide the members of the distribution group from members of the group and users who aren't members of the group.

Identity.d: The Identity parameter specifies the distribution group or mail-enabled security group that you want to modify. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the group.

ManagedBy.d: The ManagedBy parameter specifies an owner for the group. A group must have at least one owner.

MemberDepartRestriction.d: The MemberDepartRestriction parameter specifies the restrictions that you put on requests to leave the group. Valid values are: Open & Closed

MemberJoinRestriction.d: The MemberJoinRestriction parameter specifies the restrictions that you put on requests to join the group. Valid values are: Open, Closed & ApprovalRequired

Members.d: The Members parameter specifies the recipients (mail-enabled objects) that are members of the group. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the recipient.

ModeratedBy.d: The ModeratedBy parameter specifies one or more moderators for this group. A moderator approves messages sent to the group before the messages are delivered. A moderator must be a mailbox, mail user, or mail contact in your organization. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the moderator.

ModerationEnabled.d: The ModerationEnabled parameter specifies whether moderation is enabled for this recipient.

Name.d: The Name parameter specifies a unique name for the address list.

Notes.d: The Notes parameters specifies additional information about the object.

OrganizationalUnit.d: The OrganizationalUnit parameter specifies the location in Active Directory where the group is created.

PrimarySmtpAddress.d: The PrimarySmtpAddress parameter specifies the primary return email address that's used for the recipient.

RequireSenderAuthenticationEnabled.d: The RequireSenderAuthenticationEnabled parameter specifies whether to accept messages only from authenticated (internal) senders.

RoomList.d: The RoomList switch specifies that all members of this distribution group are room mailboxes. You don't need to specify a value with this switch.

SendModerationNotifications.d: The SendModerationNotifications parameter specifies when moderation notification messages are sent. Valid values are: Always, Internal, Never.

SendOofMessageToOriginatorEnabled.d: The SendOofMessageToOriginatorEnabled parameter specifies how to handle out of office (OOF) messages for members of the group.

Type.d: The Type parameter specifies the type of group that you want to create. Valid values are: Distribution, Security

EXODkimSigningConfig.d: This resource configures the DomainKeys Identified Mail (DKIM) signing policy settings for domains in a cloud-based organization.

AdminDisplayName.d: The AdminDisplayName parameter specifies a description for the policy.

BodyCanonicalization.d: The BodyCanonicalization parameter specifies the canonicalization algorithm that's used to create and verify the message body part of the DKIM signature. This value effectively controls the sensitivity of DKIM to changes to the message body in transit. Valid values are 'Simple' or 'Relaxed'. 'Relaxed' is the default.

Enabled.d: The Enabled parameter specifies whether the DKIM Signing Configuration is enabled or disabled. Default is $true.

HeaderCanonicalization.d: The HeaderCanonicalization parameter specifies the canonicalization algorithm that's used to create and verify the message header part of the DKIM signature. This value effectively controls the sensitivity of DKIM to changes to the message headers in transit. Valid values are 'Simple' or 'Relaxed'. 'Relaxed' is the default.

Identity.d: The Identity parameter specifies the DKIM signing policy that you want to modify. This should be the FQDN.

KeySize.d: The KeySize parameter specifies the size in bits of the public key that's used in the DKIM signing policy. Valid values are 1024 and 2048

EXODnssecForVerifiedDomain.d: This resource configures the DnsSec status for a verified domain.

DnssecFeatureStatus.d: Status of the DnsSec for the given Domain Name.

DomainName.d: DomainName parameter specifies the accepted domain in the Exchange Online organization where you want to view information about DNSSEC

EXODynamicDistributionGroup.d: This resource configures an EXO Dynamic Distribution Group

AcceptMessagesOnlyFrom.d: The AcceptMessagesOnlyFrom parameter specifies who is allowed to send messages to this recipient.

AcceptMessagesOnlyFromDLMembers.d: The AcceptMessagesOnlyFromDLMembers parameter specifies who is allowed to send messages to this recipient. Messages from other senders are rejected.

Alias.d: The Alias parameter specifies the Exchange alias (also known as the mail nickname) for the recipient. This value identifies the recipient as a mail-enabled object, and shouldn't be confused with multiple email addresses for the same recipient (also known as proxy addresses). A recipient can have only one Alias value. The maximum length is 64 characters.

BypassModerationFromSendersOrMembers.d: The BypassModerationFromSendersOrMembers parameter specifies who is allowed to send messages to this moderated recipient without approval from a moderator. Valid values for this parameter are individual senders and groups in your organization. Specifying a group means all members of the group are allowed to send messages to this recipient without approval from a moderator.

ConditionalCustomAttribute1.d: The ConditionalCustomAttribute1 parameter specifies a precanned filter that's based on the value of the recipient's CustomAttribute1 property. You can specify multiple values separated by commas. When you use multiple values for this parameter, the OR Boolean operator is applied.

ConditionalCustomAttribute10.d: The ConditionalCustomAttribute10 parameter specifies a precanned filter that's based on the value of the recipient's CustomAttribute10 property. DisplayName.d: The DisplayName parameter specifies the display name of the dynamic distribution group. The display name is visible in the Exchange admin center and in address lists. The maximum length is 256 characters.

EmailAddresses.d: The EmailAddresses parameter specifies all email addresses (proxy addresses) for the recipient, including the primary SMTP address.

ExtensionCustomAttribute1.d: This parameter specifies a value for the ExtensionCustomAttribute1 property on the recipient. You can use this property to store custom information about the recipient, and to identify the recipient in filters. You can specify up to 1300 values separated by commas.

ExtensionCustomAttribute2.d: This parameter specifies a value for the ExtensionCustomAttribute2 property on the recipient. You can use this property to store custom information about the recipient, and to identify the recipient in filters. You can specify up to 1300 values separated by commas.

ExtensionCustomAttribute3.d: This parameter specifies a value for the ExtensionCustomAttribute3 property on the recipient. You can use this property to store custom information about the recipient, and to identify the recipient in filters. You can specify up to 1300 values separated by commas.

ExtensionCustomAttribute4.d: This parameter specifies a value for the ExtensionCustomAttribute4 property on the recipient. You can use this property to store custom information about the recipient, and to identify the recipient in filters. You can specify up to 1300 values separated by commas.

ExtensionCustomAttribute5.d: This parameter specifies a value for the ExtensionCustomAttribute5 property on the recipient. You can use this property to store custom information about the recipient, and to identify the recipient in filters. You can specify up to 1300 values separated by commas.

GrantSendOnBehalfTo.d: The GrantSendOnBehalfTo parameter specifies who can send on behalf of this dynamic distribution group. Although messages send on behalf of the group clearly show the sender in the From field (<Sender> on behalf of <Group>), replies to these messages are delivered to the group, not the sender.

HiddenFromAddressListsEnabled.d: The HiddenFromAddressListsEnabled parameter specifies whether this recipient is visible in address lists. Valid values are: $true: The recipient isn't visible in address lists. $false: The recipient is visible in address lists. This value is the default.

Identity.d: The Identity parameter specifies the dynamic distribution group that you want to modify. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the dynamic distribution group.

IncludedRecipients.d: The IncludedRecipients parameter specifies a precanned filter that's based on the recipient type. Valid values are: AllRecipients: This value can be used only by itself. MailboxUsers. MailContacts. MailGroups. MailUsers. Resources: This value indicates room or equipment mailboxes. You can specify multiple values separated by commas. When you use multiple values, the OR Boolean operator is applied.

MailTip.d: The MailTip parameter specifies the custom MailTip text for this recipient. The MailTip is shown to senders when they start drafting an email message to this recipient. If the value contains spaces, enclose the value in quotation marks (").

MailTipTranslations.d: The MailTipTranslations parameter specifies additional languages for the custom MailTip text that's defined by the MailTip parameter. HTML tags are automatically added to the MailTip translation, additional HTML tags aren't supported, and the length of the MailTip translation can't exceed 175 displayed characters.

ManagedBy.d: The ManagedBy parameter specifies an owner for the group. A dynamic group can only have one owner.

ModeratedBy.d: The ModeratedBy parameter specifies one or more moderators for this recipient. A moderator approves messages sent to the recipient before the messages are delivered. A moderator must be a mailbox, mail user, or mail contact in your organization. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the moderator.

ModerationEnabled.d: The ModerationEnabled parameter specifies whether moderation is enabled for this recipient.

Name.d: The Name parameter specifies the unique name of the dynamic distribution group. The maximum length is 64 characters.

Notes.d: The Notes parameters specifies additional information about the object.

PhoneticDisplayName.d: The PhoneticDisplayName parameter specifies an alternate spelling of the user's name that's used for text to speech in Unified Messaging (UM) environments. Typically, you use this parameter when the pronunciation and spelling of the user's name don't match. The maximum length is 256 characters.

PrimarySmtpAddress.d: The PrimarySmtpAddress parameter specifies the primary return email address that's used for the recipient.

RecipientContainer.d: The RecipientContainer parameter specifies a filter that's based on the recipient's location in Active Directory. Valid input for this parameter is an organizational unit (OU) or domain that's returned by the Get-OrganizationalUnit cmdlet. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the OU or domain.

RecipientFilter.d: The RecipientFilter parameter specifies a custom OPATH filter that's based on the value of any available recipient property. You can use any available Windows PowerShell operator, and wildcards and partial matches are supported. The search criteria uses the syntax "Property -ComparisonOperator 'Value'".

RejectMessagesFrom.d: The RejectMessagesFrom parameter specifies who isn't allowed to send messages to this recipient. Messages from these senders are rejected.

RejectMessagesFromDLMembers.d: The RejectMessagesFromDLMembers parameter specifies who isn't allowed to send messages to this recipient. Messages from these senders are rejected.

RejectMessagesFromSendersOrMembers.d: The RejectMessagesFromSendersOrMembers parameter specifies who isn't allowed to send messages to this recipient. Messages from these senders are rejected.

ReportToManagerEnabled.d: The ReportToManagerEnabled parameter specifies whether delivery status notifications (also known as DSNs, non-delivery reports, NDRs, or bounce messages) are sent to the owners of the group (defined by the ManagedBy property).

ReportToOriginatorEnabled.d: The ReportToOriginatorEnabled parameter specifies whether delivery status notifications (also known as DSNs, non-delivery reports, NDRs, or bounce messages) are sent to senders who send messages to this group.

RequireSenderAuthenticationEnabled.d: The RequireSenderAuthenticationEnabled parameter specifies whether to accept messages only from authenticated (internal) senders.

SendModerationNotifications.d: The SendModerationNotifications parameter specifies when moderation notification messages are sent. Valid values are: Always: Notify all senders when their messages aren't approved. This value is the default. Internal: Notify senders in the organization when their messages aren't approved. Never: Don't notify anyone when a message isn't approved.

SendOofMessageToOriginatorEnabled.d: The SendOofMessageToOriginatorEnabled parameter specifies how to handle out of office (OOF) messages for members of the group.

SimpleDisplayName.d: The SimpleDisplayName parameter is used to display an alternative description of the object when only a limited set of characters is permitted.

WindowsEmailAddress.d: The WindowsEmailAddress parameter specifies the Windows email address for this recipient. This is a common Active Directory attribute that's present in all environments, including environments without Exchange.

EXOEOPProtectionPolicyRule.d: This resource configures EOP Protection Policy Rules.

Comments.d: The Comments parameter specifies informative comments for the rule, such as what the rule is used for or how it has changed over time. The length of the comment can't exceed 1024 characters.

ExceptIfRecipientDomainIs.d: The ExceptIfRecipientDomainIs parameter specifies an exception that looks for recipients with email addresses in the specified domains. You can specify multiple domains separated by commas.

ExceptIfSentTo.d: The ExceptIfSentTo parameter specifies an exception that looks for recipients in messages. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the recipient.

ExceptIfSentToMemberOf.d: The ExceptIfSentToMemberOf parameter specifies an exception that looks for messages sent to members of groups. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the group.

Identity.d: The Identity parameter specifies the rule that you want to view. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the rule.

Name.d: The Name parameter specifies a unique name for the rule. The maximum length is 64 characters.

Priority.d: The Priority parameter specifies a priority value for the rule that determines the order of rule processing. A lower integer value indicates a higher priority, the value 0 is the highest priority, and rules can't have the same priority value.

RecipientDomainIs.d: The RecipientDomainIs parameter specifies a condition that looks for recipients with email addresses in the specified domains. You can specify multiple domains separated by commas.

SentTo.d: The SentTo parameter specifies a condition that looks for recipients in messages. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the recipient.

SentToMemberOf.d: The SentToMemberOf parameter specifies a condition that looks for messages sent to members of distribution groups, dynamic distribution groups, or mail-enabled security groups. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the group.

State.d: This parameter define if the rule is enabled or disabled

EXOEmailAddressPolicy.d: This resource configures Email address policies in Exchange Online.

EnabledEmailAddressTemplates.d: The EnabledEmailAddressTemplates parameter specifies the rules in the email address policy that are used to generate email addresses for recipients.

EnabledPrimarySMTPAddressTemplate.d: The EnabledPrimarySMTPAddressTemplate parameter specifies the specifies the rule in the email address policy that's used to generate the primary SMTP email addresses for recipients. You can use this parameter instead of the EnabledEmailAddressTemplates if the policy only applies the primary email address and no additional proxy addresses.

ManagedByFilter.d: The ManagedByFilter parameter specifies the email address policies to apply to Office 365 groups based on the properties of the users who create the Office 365 groups.

Name.d: The Name parameter specifies the unique name of the email address policy. The maximum length is 64 characters.

Priority.d: The Priority parameter specifies the order that the email address policies are evaluated. By default, every time that you add a new email address policy, the policy is assigned a priority of N+1, where N is the number of email address policies that you've created.

EXOEmailTenantSettings.d: This resource allows users to manage email tenant settings.

EnablePriorityAccountProtection.d: Specifies whether priority account protection is enabled.

Identity.d: Identity which indicates the organization name.

IsValid.d: Specifies whether the migration configuration is valid.

Name.d: Specifies the name of the object.

ObjectState.d: Specifies the state of the object.

AllowList.d: The AllowList parameter specifies exceptions to external sender identification in supported versions of Outlook. Messages received from the specified senders or senders in the specified domains don't receive native External sender identification. The allow list uses the 5322.From address (also known as the From address or P2 sender). Valid values are an individual domain (contoso.com), a domain and all subdomains (*.contoso.com) or email addresses (admin@contoso.com).

Enabled.d: The Enabled parameter enables or disables external sender identification in supported versions of Outlook. Valid values are:$true: External sender identification in Outlook is enabled. An External icon is added in the area of the subject line of messages from external senders. To exempt specific senders or sender domains from this identification, use the AllowList parameter.$false: External sender identification in Outlook is disabled.

Identity.d: OrganisationIDParameter, not needed for cmdlet Functionality, use 'ExternalinOutlook' to use the same configuration for multiple Tenants

EXOFocusedInbox.d: Manage the Focused Inbox configuration for mailboxes in your organization.

FocusedInboxOn.d: The FocusedInboxOn parameter enables or disables Focused Inbox for the mailbox.

Identity.d: The Identity parameter specifies the mailbox that you want to modify.

EXOGlobalAddressList.d: This resource configures Global Address Lists in Exchange Online.

ConditionalCompany.d: The ConditionalCompany parameter specifies a precanned filter that's based on the value of the recipient's Company property.

ConditionalCustomAttribute1.d: The ConditionalCustomAttribute1 parameter specifies a precanned filter that's based on the value of the recipient's CustomAttribute1 property.

ConditionalCustomAttribute10.d: The ConditionalCustomAttribute10 parameter specifies a precanned filter that's based on the value of the recipient's CustomAttribute10 property.

ConditionalCustomAttribute11.d: The ConditionalCustomAttribute11 parameter specifies a precanned filter that's based on the value of the recipient's CustomAttribute11 property.

ConditionalCustomAttribute12.d: The ConditionalCustomAttribute12 parameter specifies a precanned filter that's based on the value of the recipient's CustomAttribute12 property.

ConditionalCustomAttribute13.d: The ConditionalCustomAttribute13 parameter specifies a precanned filter that's based on the value of the recipient's CustomAttribute13 property.

ConditionalCustomAttribute14.d: The ConditionalCustomAttribute14 parameter specifies a precanned filter that's based on the value of the recipient's CustomAttribute14 property.

ConditionalCustomAttribute15.d: The ConditionalCustomAttribute15 parameter specifies a precanned filter that's based on the value of the recipient's CustomAttribute15 property.

ConditionalCustomAttribute2.d: The ConditionalCustomAttribute2 parameter specifies a precanned filter that's based on the value of the recipient's CustomAttribute2 property.

ConditionalCustomAttribute3.d: The ConditionalCustomAttribute3 parameter specifies a precanned filter that's based on the value of the recipient's CustomAttribute3 property.

ConditionalCustomAttribute4.d: The ConditionalCustomAttribute4 parameter specifies a precanned filter that's based on the value of the recipient's CustomAttribute4 property.

ConditionalCustomAttribute5.d: The ConditionalCustomAttribute5 parameter specifies a precanned filter that's based on the value of the recipient's CustomAttribute5 property.

ConditionalCustomAttribute6.d: The ConditionalCustomAttribute6 parameter specifies a precanned filter that's based on the value of the recipient's CustomAttribute6 property.

ConditionalCustomAttribute7.d: The ConditionalCustomAttribute7 parameter specifies a precanned filter that's based on the value of the recipient's CustomAttribute7 property.

ConditionalCustomAttribute8.d: The ConditionalCustomAttribute8 parameter specifies a precanned filter that's based on the value of the recipient's CustomAttribute8 property.

ConditionalCustomAttribute9.d: The ConditionalCustomAttribute9 parameter specifies a precanned filter that's based on the value of the recipient's CustomAttribute9 property.

ConditionalDepartment.d: The ConditionalDepartment parameter specifies a precanned filter that's based on the value of the recipient's Department property.

ConditionalStateOrProvince.d: The ConditionalStateOrProvince parameter specifies a precanned filter that's based on the value of the recipient's StateOrProvince property.

IncludedRecipients.d: The IncludedRecipients parameter specifies a precanned filter that's based on the recipient type.

Name.d: The Name parameter specifies the unique name of the GAL. The maximum length is 64 characters.

RecipientFilter.d: The RecipientFilter parameter specifies an OPath filter that's based on the value of any available recipient property.

EXOGroupSettings.d: This resource configures mail related attributes on Microsoft 365 groups such as the custom attributes and language. To configure the Microsoft Graph related attributes on a Microsoft 365 group, or create a Microsoft 365 group, the AADGroup resource can be used.

AcceptMessagesOnlyFromSendersOrMembers.d: The AcceptMessagesOnlyFromSendersOrMembers parameter specifies who is allowed to send messages to this recipient. Messages from other senders are rejected.

AlwaysSubscribeMembersToCalendarEvents.d: The AlwaysSubscribeMembersToCalendarEvents switch controls the default subscription settings of new members that are added to the Microsoft 365 Group. Changing this setting doesn't affect existing group members.

AuditLogAgeLimit.d: The AlwaysSubscribeMembersToCalendarEvents switch controls the default subscription settings of new members that are added to the Microsoft 365 Group. Changing this setting doesn't affect existing group members.

AutoSubscribeNewMembers.d: The AutoSubscribeNewMembers switch specifies whether to automatically subscribe new members that are added to the Microsoft 365 Group to conversations and calendar events. Only users that are added to the group after you enable this setting are automatically subscribed to the group.

CalendarMemberReadOnly.d: The CalendarMemberReadOnly parameter specifies whether to set read-only Calendar permissions to the Microsoft 365 Group for members of the group.

Classification.d: The CalendarMemberReadOnly switch specifies whether to set read-only Calendar permissions to the Microsoft 365 Group for members of the group.

ConnectorsEnabled.d: The CalendarMemberReadOnly switch specifies whether to set read-only Calendar permissions to the Microsoft 365 Group for members of the group.

CustomAttribute1.d: This parameter specifies a value for the CustomAttribute1 property on the recipient. You can use this property to store custom information about the recipient, and to identify the recipient in filters. The maximum length is 1024 characters.

CustomAttribute10.d: This parameter specifies a value for the CustomAttribute10 property on the recipient. You can use this property to store custom information about the recipient, and to identify the recipient in filters. The maximum length is 1024 characters.

CustomAttribute11.d: This parameter specifies a value for the CustomAttribute11 property on the recipient. You can use this property to store custom information about the recipient, and to identify the recipient in filters. The maximum length is 1024 characters.

CustomAttribute12.d: This parameter specifies a value for the CustomAttribute12 property on the recipient. You can use this property to store custom information about the recipient, and to identify the recipient in filters. The maximum length is 1024 characters.

CustomAttribute13.d: This parameter specifies a value for the CustomAttribute13 property on the recipient. You can use this property to store custom information about the recipient, and to identify the recipient in filters. The maximum length is 1024 characters.

CustomAttribute14.d: This parameter specifies a value for the CustomAttribute14 property on the recipient. You can use this property to store custom information about the recipient, and to identify the recipient in filters. The maximum length is 1024 characters.

CustomAttribute15.d: This parameter specifies a value for the CustomAttribute15 property on the recipient. You can use this property to store custom information about the recipient, and to identify the recipient in filters. The maximum length is 1024 characters.

CustomAttribute2.d: This parameter specifies a value for the CustomAttribute2 property on the recipient. You can use this property to store custom information about the recipient, and to identify the recipient in filters. The maximum length is 1024 characters.

CustomAttribute3.d: This parameter specifies a value for the CustomAttribute3 property on the recipient. You can use this property to store custom information about the recipient, and to identify the recipient in filters. The maximum length is 1024 characters.

CustomAttribute4.d: This parameter specifies a value for the CustomAttribute4 property on the recipient. You can use this property to store custom information about the recipient, and to identify the recipient in filters. The maximum length is 1024 characters.

CustomAttribute5.d: This parameter specifies a value for the CustomAttribute5 property on the recipient. You can use this property to store custom information about the recipient, and to identify the recipient in filters. The maximum length is 1024 characters.

CustomAttribute6.d: This parameter specifies a value for the CustomAttribute6 property on the recipient. You can use this property to store custom information about the recipient, and to identify the recipient in filters. The maximum length is 1024 characters.

CustomAttribute7.d: This parameter specifies a value for the CustomAttribute7 property on the recipient. You can use this property to store custom information about the recipient, and to identify the recipient in filters. The maximum length is 1024 characters.

CustomAttribute8.d: This parameter specifies a value for the CustomAttribute8 property on the recipient. You can use this property to store custom information about the recipient, and to identify the recipient in filters. The maximum length is 1024 characters.

CustomAttribute9.d: This parameter specifies a value for the CustomAttribute9 property on the recipient. You can use this property to store custom information about the recipient, and to identify the recipient in filters. The maximum length is 1024 characters.

DataEncryptionPolicy.d: The DataEncryptionPolicy parameter specifies the data encryption policy that's applied to the Microsoft 365 Group.

DisplayName.d: The DisplayName parameter specifies the name of the Microsoft 365 Group. The display name is visible in the Exchange admin center, address lists, and Outlook. The maximum length is 64 characters.

EmailAddresses.d: The EmailAddresses parameter specifies all the email addresses (proxy addresses) for the recipient, including the primary SMTP address.

ExtensionCustomAttribute1.d: This parameter specifies a value for the ExtensionCustomAttribute1 property on the recipient. You can use this property to store custom information about the recipient, and to identify the recipient in filters. You can specify up to 1300 values separated by commas.

ExtensionCustomAttribute2.d: This parameter specifies a value for the ExtensionCustomAttribute2 property on the recipient. You can use this property to store custom information about the recipient, and to identify the recipient in filters. You can specify up to 1300 values separated by commas.

ExtensionCustomAttribute3.d: This parameter specifies a value for the ExtensionCustomAttribute3 property on the recipient. You can use this property to store custom information about the recipient, and to identify the recipient in filters. You can specify up to 1300 values separated by commas.

ExtensionCustomAttribute4.d: This parameter specifies a value for the ExtensionCustomAttribute4 property on the recipient. You can use this property to store custom information about the recipient, and to identify the recipient in filters. You can specify up to 1300 values separated by commas.

ExtensionCustomAttribute5.d: This parameter specifies a value for the ExtensionCustomAttribute5 property on the recipient. You can use this property to store custom information about the recipient, and to identify the recipient in filters. You can specify up to 1300 values separated by commas.

GrantSendOnBehalfTo.d: The GrantSendOnBehalfTo parameter specifies who can send on behalf of this Microsoft 365 Group.

HiddenFromAddressListsEnabled.d: The GrantSendOnBehalfTo parameter specifies who can send on behalf of this Microsoft 365 Group.

HiddenFromExchangeClientsEnabled.d: The HiddenFromExchangeClientsEnabled switch specifies whether the Microsoft 365 Group is hidden from Outlook clients connected to Microsoft 365.

Id.d: The unique Id of the group

InformationBarrierMode.d: The InformationBarrierMode parameter specifies the information barrier mode for the Microsoft 365 Group.

IsMemberAllowedToEditContent.d: This parameter specifies whether or not members are allow to edit content.

Language.d: The Language parameter specifies language preference for the Microsoft 365 Group.

MailTip.d: The MailTip parameter specifies the custom MailTip text for this recipient. The MailTip is shown to senders when they start drafting an email message to this recipient.

MailTipTranslations.d: The MailTipTranslations parameter specifies additional languages for the custom MailTip text that's defined by the MailTip parameter.

MailboxRegion.d: The MailboxRegion parameter specifies the preferred data location (PDL) for the Microsoft 365 Group in multi-geo environments.

MaxReceiveSize.d: The MaxReceiveSize parameter specifies the maximum size of an email message that can be sent to this group. Messages that exceed the maximum size are rejected by the group.

MaxSendSize.d: The MaxSendSize parameter specifies the maximum size of an email message that can be sent by this group.

ModeratedBy.d: The ModeratedBy parameter specifies one or more moderators for this recipient. A moderator approves messages sent to the recipient before the messages are delivered. A moderator must be a mailbox, mail user, or mail contact in your organization. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the moderator.

ModerationEnabled.d: The ModerationEnabled parameter specifies whether moderation is enabled for this recipient.

Notes.d: The Notes parameter specifies the description of the Microsoft 365 Group. If the value contains spaces, enclose the value in quotation marks.
