ExceptIfADComparisonOperator.d: The ExceptIfADComparisonOperator parameter specifies the comparison operator for the ExceptIfADComparisonAttribute parameter.

ExceptIfAnyOfCcHeader.d: The ExceptIfAnyOfCcHeader parameter specifies an exception that looks for recipients in the Cc field of messages.

ExceptIfAnyOfCcHeaderMemberOf.d: The ExceptIfAnyOfCcHeaderMemberOf parameter specifies an exception that looks for group members in the Cc field of messages. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the group.

ExceptIfAnyOfRecipientAddressContainsWor.d: The ExceptIfAnyOfRecipientAddressContainsWords parameter specifies an exception that looks for words in recipient email addresses.

ExceptIfAnyOfRecipientAddressMatchesPatt.d: The ExceptIfAnyOfRecipientAddressMatchesPatterns parameter specifies an exception that looks for text patterns in recipient email addresses by using regular expressions.

ExceptIfAnyOfToCcHeader.d: The ExceptIfAnyOfToCcHeader parameter specifies an exception that looks for recipients in the To or Cc fields of messages.

ExceptIfAnyOfToCcHeaderMemberOf.d: The ExceptIfAnyOfToCcHeaderMemberOf parameter specifies an exception that looks for group members in the To and Cc fields of messages.

ExceptIfAnyOfToHeader.d: The ExceptIfAnyOfToHeader parameter specifies an exception that looks for recipients in the To field of messages.

ExceptIfAnyOfToHeaderMemberOf.d: The ExceptIfAnyOfToHeaderMemberOf parameter specifies an exception that looks for group members in the To field of messages.

ExceptIfAttachmentContainsWords.d: The ExceptIfAttachmentContainsWords parameter specifies an exception that looks for words in message attachments.

ExceptIfAttachmentExtensionMatchesWords.d: The ExceptIfAttachmentExtensionMatchesWords parameter specifies an exception that looks for words in the file name extensions of message attachments.

ExceptIfAttachmentHasExecutableContent.d: The ExceptIfAttachmentHasExecutableContent parameter specifies an exception that looks for executable content in message attachments.

ExceptIfAttachmentIsPasswordProtected.d: The ExceptIfAttachmentIsPasswordProtected parameter specifies an exception that looks for password protected files in messages (because the contents of the file can't be inspected).

ExceptIfAttachmentIsUnsupported.d: The ExceptIfAttachmentIsUnsupported parameter specifies an exception that looks for unsupported file types in messages.

ExceptIfAttachmentMatchesPatterns.d: The ExceptIfAttachmentMatchesPatterns parameter specifies an exception that looks for text patterns in the content of message attachments by using regular expressions.

ExceptIfAttachmentNameMatchesPatterns.d: The ExceptIfAttachmentNameMatchesPatterns parameter specifies an exception that looks for text patterns in the file name of message attachments by using regular expressions.

ExceptIfAttachmentProcessingLimitExceede.d: The ExceptIfAttachmentProcessingLimitExceeded parameter specifies an exception that looks for messages where attachment scanning didn't complete.

ExceptIfAttachmentPropertyContainsWords.d: The ExceptIfAttachmentPropertyContainsWords parameter specifies an exception that looks for words in the properties of attached Office documents.

ExceptIfAttachmentSizeOver.d: The ExceptIfAttachmentSizeOver parameter specifies an exception that looks for messages where any attachment is greater than the specified size.

ExceptIfBetweenMemberOf1.d: The ExceptIfBetweenMemberOf1 parameter specifies an exception that looks for messages that are sent between group members.

ExceptIfBetweenMemberOf2.d: The ExceptIfBetweenMemberOf2 parameter specifies an exception that looks for messages that are sent between group members.

ExceptIfContentCharacterSetContainsWords.d: The ExceptIfContentCharacterSetContainsWords parameter specifies an exception that looks for character set names in messages.

ExceptIfFrom.d: The ExceptIfFrom parameter specifies an exception that looks for messages from specific senders.

ExceptIfFromAddressContainsWords.d: The ExceptIfFromAddressContainsWords parameter specifies an exception that looks for words in the sender's email address.

ExceptIfFromAddressMatchesPatterns.d: The ExceptIfFromAddressMatchesPatterns parameter specifies an exception that looks for text patterns in the sender's email address by using regular expressions.

ExceptIfFromMemberOf.d: The ExceptIfFromMemberOf parameter specifies an exception that looks for messages sent by group members.

ExceptIfFromScope.d: The ExceptIfFromScope parameter specifies an exception that looks for the location of message senders.

ExceptIfHasClassification.d: The ExceptIfHasClassification parameter specifies an exception that looks for messages with the specified message classification.

ExceptIfHasNoClassification.d: The ExceptIfHasNoClassification parameter specifies an exception that looks for messages with or without any message classifications.

ExceptIfHeaderContainsMessageHeader.d: The ExceptIfHeaderContainsMessageHeader parameter specifies the name of header field in the message header when searching for the words specified by the ExceptIfHeaderContainsWords parameter.

ExceptIfHeaderContainsWords.d: The ExceptIfHeaderContainsWords parameter specifies an exception that looks for words in a header field.

ExceptIfHeaderMatchesMessageHeader.d: The ExceptIfHeaderMatchesMessageHeader parameter specifies the name of header field in the message header when searching for the text patterns specified by the ExceptIfHeaderMatchesPatterns parameter.

ExceptIfHeaderMatchesPatterns.d: The ExceptIfHeaderMatchesPatterns parameter specifies an exception that looks for text patterns in a header field by using regular expressions.

ExceptIfManagerAddresses.d: The ExceptIfManagerAddresses parameter specifies the users (managers) for the ExceptIfManagerForEvaluatedUser parameter.

ExceptIfManagerForEvaluatedUser.d: The ExceptIfManagerForEvaluatedUser parameter specifies an exception that looks for users in the Manager attribute of senders or recipients.

ExceptIfMessageSizeOver.d: The ExceptIfMessageSizeOver parameter specifies an exception that looks for messages larger than the specified size.

ExceptIfMessageTypeMatches.d: The ExceptIfMessageTypeMatches parameter specifies an exception that looks for messages of the specified type.

ExceptIfRecipientADAttributeContainsWord.d: The ExceptIfRecipientADAttributeContainsWords parameter specifies an exception that looks for words in the Active Directory attributes of recipients.

ExceptIfRecipientADAttributeMatchesPatte.d: The ExceptIfRecipientADAttributeMatchesPatterns parameter specifies an exception that looks for text patterns in the Active Directory attributes of recipients by using regular expressions.

ExceptIfRecipientAddressContainsWords.d: The ExceptIfRecipientAddressContainsWords parameter specifies an exception that looks for words in recipient email addresses.

ExceptIfRecipientAddressMatchesPatterns.d: The ExceptIfRecipientAddressMatchesPatterns parameter specifies an exception that looks for text patterns in recipient email addresses by using regular expressions.

ExceptIfRecipientDomainIs.d: The ExceptIfRecipientDomainIs parameter specifies an exception that looks for recipients with email address in the specified domains.

ExceptIfRecipientInSenderList.d: This parameter is reserved for internal Microsoft use.

ExceptIfSCLOver.d: The ExceptIfSCLOver parameter specifies an exception that looks for the SCL value of messages

ExceptIfSenderADAttributeContainsWords.d: The ExceptIfSenderADAttributeContainsWords parameter specifies an exception that looks for words in Active Directory attributes of message senders.

ExceptIfSenderADAttributeMatchesPatterns.d: The ExceptIfSenderADAttributeMatchesPatterns parameter specifies an exception that looks for text patterns in Active Directory attributes of message senders by using regular expressions.

ExceptIfSenderDomainIs.d: The ExceptIfSenderDomainIs parameter specifies an exception that looks for senders with email address in the specified domains.

ExceptIfSenderInRecipientList.d: This parameter is reserved for internal Microsoft use.

ExceptIfSenderIpRanges.d: The ExceptIfSenderIpRanges parameter specifies an exception that looks for senders whose IP addresses matches the specified value, or fall within the specified ranges.

ExceptIfSenderManagementRelationship.d: The ExceptIfSenderManagementRelationship parameter specifies an exception that looks for the relationship between the sender and recipients in messages.

ExceptIfSentTo.d: The ExceptIfSentTo parameter specifies an exception that looks for recipients in messages. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the recipient.

ExceptIfSentToMemberOf.d: The ExceptIfSentToMemberOf parameter specifies an exception that looks for messages sent to members of groups. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the group.

ExceptIfSentToScope.d: The ExceptIfSentToScope parameter specifies an exception that looks for the location of a recipient.

ExceptIfSubjectContainsWords.d: The ExceptIfSubjectContainsWords parameter specifies an exception that looks for words in the Subject field of messages.

ExceptIfSubjectMatchesPatterns.d: The ExceptIfSubjectMatchesPatterns parameter specifies an exception that looks for text patterns in the Subject field of messages by using regular expressions.

ExceptIfSubjectOrBodyContainsWords.d: The ExceptIfSubjectOrBodyContainsWords parameter specifies an exception that looks for words in the Subject field or body of messages.

ExceptIfSubjectOrBodyMatchesPatterns.d: The ExceptIfSubjectOrBodyMatchesPatterns parameter specifies an exception that looks for text patterns in the Subject field or body of messages.

ExceptIfWithImportance.d: The ExceptIfWithImportance parameter specifies an exception that looks for messages with the specified importance level.

ExpiryDate.d: The ExpiryDate parameter specifies when this rule will stop processing messages. The rule won't take any action on messages after the specified date/time.

From.d: The From parameter specifies a condition that looks for messages from specific senders. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the sender.

FromAddressContainsWords.d: The FromAddressContainsWords parameter specifies a condition that looks for words in the sender's email address.

FromAddressMatchesPatterns.d: The FromAddressMatchesPatterns parameter specifies a condition that looks for text patterns in the sender's email address by using regular expressions.

FromMemberOf.d: The FromMemberOf parameter specifies a condition that looks for messages sent by group members.

FromScope.d: The FromScope parameter specifies a condition that looks for the location of message senders.

GenerateIncidentReport.d: The GenerateIncidentReport parameter specifies where to send the incident report that's defined by the IncidentReportContent parameter.

GenerateNotification.d: The GenerateNotification parameter specifies an action that sends a notification message to recipients.

HasClassification.d: The HasClassification parameter specifies a condition that looks for messages with the specified message classification.

HasNoClassification.d: The HasNoClassification parameter specifies a condition that looks for messages with or without any message classifications.

HeaderContainsMessageHeader.d: The HeaderContainsMessageHeader parameter specifies the name of header field in the message header when searching for the words specified by the HeaderContainsWords parameter.

HeaderContainsWords.d: The HeaderContainsWords parameter specifies a condition that looks for words in a header field.

HeaderMatchesMessageHeader.d: The HeaderMatchesMessageHeader parameter specifies the name of header field in the message header when searching for the text patterns specified by the HeaderMatchesPatterns parameter.

HeaderMatchesPatterns.d: The HeaderMatchesPatterns parameter specifies a condition that looks for text patterns in a header field by using regular expressions.

IncidentReportContent.d: The IncidentReportContent parameter specifies the message properties that are included in the incident report that's generated when a message violates a DLP policy.

ManagerAddresses.d: The ManagerAddresses parameter specifies the users (managers) for the ExceptIfManagerForEvaluatedUser parameter.

ManagerForEvaluatedUser.d: The ManagerForEvaluatedUser parameter specifies a condition that looks for users in the Manager attribute of senders or recipients.

MessageSizeOver.d: The MessageSizeOver parameter specifies a condition that looks for messages larger than the specified size. The size includes the message and all attachments.

MessageTypeMatches.d: The MessageTypeMatches parameter specifies a condition that looks for messages of the specified type.

Mode.d: The Mode parameter specifies how the rule operates.

ModerateMessageByManager.d: The ModerateMessageByManager parameter specifies an action that forwards messages for approval to the user that's specified in the sender's Manager attribute.

ModerateMessageByUser.d: The ModerateMessageByUser parameter specifies an action that forwards messages for approval to the specified users.

Name.d: The Name parameter specifies the display name of the transport rule to be created. The maximum length is 64 characters.

PrependSubject.d: The PrependSubject parameter specifies an action that adds text to add to the beginning of the Subject field of messages.

Priority.d: The Priority parameter specifies a priority value for the rule that determines the order of rule processing.

Quarantine.d: The Quarantine parameter specifies an action that quarantines messages.

RecipientADAttributeContainsWords.d: The RecipientADAttributeContainsWords parameter specifies a condition that looks for words in the Active Directory attributes of recipients.

RecipientADAttributeMatchesPatterns.d: The RecipientADAttributeMatchesPatterns parameter specifies a condition that looks for text patterns in the Active Directory attributes of recipients by using regular expressions.

RecipientAddressContainsWords.d: The RecipientAddressContainsWords parameter specifies a condition that looks for words in recipient email addresses.

RecipientAddressMatchesPatterns.d: The RecipientAddressMatchesPatterns parameter specifies a condition that looks for text patterns in recipient email addresses by using regular expressions.

RecipientAddressType.d: The RecipientAddressType parameter specifies how conditions and exceptions check recipient email addresses.

RecipientDomainIs.d: The RecipientDomainIs parameter specifies a condition that looks for recipients with email address in the specified domains.

RecipientInSenderList.d: This parameter is reserved for internal Microsoft use.

RedirectMessageTo.d: The RedirectMessageTo parameter specifies a rule action that redirects messages to the specified recipients.

RejectMessageEnhancedStatusCode.d: The RejectMessageEnhancedStatusCode parameter specifies the enhanced status code that's used when the rule rejects messages.

RejectMessageReasonText.d: The RejectMessageReasonText parameter specifies the explanation text that's used when the rule rejects messages.

RemoveHeader.d: The RemoveHeader parameter specifies an action that removes a header field from the message header.

RemoveOMEv2.d: The RemoveOMEv2 parameter specifies an action that removes Office 365 Message Encryption from messages and their attachments.

RemoveRMSAttachmentEncryption.d: This parameter specifies an action or part of an action for the rule.

RouteMessageOutboundConnector.d: The RouteMessageOutboundConnector parameter specifies an action that routes messages through the specified Outbound connector in Office 365.

RouteMessageOutboundRequireTls.d: The RouteMessageOutboundRequireTls parameter specifies an action that uses Transport Layer Security (TLS) encryption to deliver messages outside your organization.

RuleErrorAction.d: The RuleErrorAction parameter specifies what to do if rule processing can't be completed on messages.

RuleSubType.d: The RuleSubType parameter specifies the rule type.

SCLOver.d: The SCLOver parameter specifies a condition that looks for the SCL value of messages

SenderADAttributeContainsWords.d: The SenderADAttributeContainsWords parameter specifies a condition that looks for words in Active Directory attributes of message senders.

SenderADAttributeMatchesPatterns.d: The SenderADAttributeMatchesPatterns parameter specifies a condition that looks for text patterns in Active Directory attributes of message senders by using regular expressions.

SenderAddressLocation.d: The SenderAddressLocation parameter specifies where to look for sender addresses in conditions and exceptions that examine sender email addresses.

SenderDomainIs.d: The SenderDomainIs parameter specifies a condition that looks for senders with email address in the specified domains.

SenderInRecipientList.d: This parameter is reserved for internal Microsoft use.

SenderIpRanges.d: The SenderIpRanges parameter specifies a condition that looks for senders whose IP addresses matches the specified value, or fall within the specified ranges.

SenderManagementRelationship.d: The SenderManagementRelationship parameter specifies a condition that looks for the relationship between the sender and recipients in messages.

SentTo.d: The SentTo parameter specifies a condition that looks for recipients in messages.

SentToMemberOf.d: The SentToMemberOf parameter specifies a condition that looks for messages sent to members of distribution groups, dynamic distribution groups, or mail-enabled security groups.

SentToScope.d: The SentToScope parameter specifies a condition that looks for the location of recipients.

SetAuditSeverity.d: The SetAuditSeverity parameter specifies an action that sets the severity level of the incident report and the corresponding entry that's written to the message tracking log when messages violate DLP policies.

SetHeaderName.d: The SetHeaderName parameter specifies an action that adds or modifies a header field in the message header.

SetHeaderValue.d: The SetHeaderValue parameter specifies an action that adds or modifies a header field in the message header.

SetSCL.d: The SetSCL parameter specifies an action that adds or modifies the SCL value of messages.

StopRuleProcessing.d: The StopRuleProcessing parameter specifies an action that stops processing more rules.

SubjectContainsWords.d: The SubjectContainsWords parameter specifies a condition that looks for words in the Subject field of messages.

SubjectMatchesPatterns.d: The SubjectMatchesPatterns parameter specifies a condition that looks for text patterns in the Subject field of messages by using regular expressions.

SubjectOrBodyContainsWords.d: The SubjectOrBodyContainsWords parameter specifies a condition that looks for words in the Subject field or body of messages.

SubjectOrBodyMatchesPatterns.d: The SubjectOrBodyMatchesPatterns parameter specifies a condition that looks for text patterns in the Subject field or body of messages.

WithImportance.d: The WithImportance parameter specifies a condition that looks for messages with the specified importance level.

IntuneAccountProtectionLocalAdministrato.d: This resource configures an Intune Account Protection Local Administrator Password Solution Policy.

AdEncryptedPasswordHistorySize.d: Configures how many previous encrypted passwords will be remembered in Active Directory. Minimum - 0, Maximum - 12

AdPasswordEncryptionEnabled.d: Configures whether the password is encrypted before being stored in Active Directory.

AdPasswordEncryptionPrincipal.d: Configures the name or SID of a user or group that can decrypt the password stored in Active Directory.

AdministratorAccountName.d: Configures the name of the managed local administrator account.

Assignments.d: Assignments of the account protection local administrator password solution policy.

AutomaticAccountManagementEnableAccount.d: Automatic Account Management Enable Account - Depends on AutomaticAccountManagementEnabled (false: The target account will be disabled, true: The target account will be enabled)

AutomaticAccountManagementEnabled.d: Automatic Account Management Enabled (false: The target account will not be automatically managed, true: The target account will be automatically managed)

AutomaticAccountManagementNameOrPrefix.d: Automatic Account Management Name Or Prefix - Depends on AutomaticAccountManagementEnabled

AutomaticAccountManagementRandomizeName.d: Automatic Account Management Randomize Name - Depends on AutomaticAccountManagementEnabled (false: The name of the target account will not use a random numeric suffix., true: The name of the target account will use a random numeric suffix.)

AutomaticAccountManagementTarget.d: Automatic Account Management Target - Depends on AutomaticAccountManagementEnabled (0: Manage the built-in administrator account, 1: Manage a new custom administrator account)

BackupDirectory.d: Configures which directory the local admin account password is backed up to. 0 - Disabled, 1 - Azure AD, 2 - AD

Description.d: Description of the account protection local administrator password solution policy.

DisplayName.d: Display name of the account protection local administrator password solution policy.

Identity.d: Identity of the account protection local administrator password solution policy.

PassphraseLength.d: Passphrase Length (Minimum 3, Maximum 10 characters) - Depends on PasswordComplexity

PasswordAgeDays.d: Configures the maximum password age of the managed local administrator account for Active Directory. Minimum - 1, Maximum - 365

PasswordComplexity.d: Configures the password complexity of the managed local administrator account. (1: Large letters, 2: Large letters + small letters, 3: Large letters + small letters + numbers, 4: Large letters + small letters + numbers + special characters, 5: Large letters + small letters + numbers + special characters (improved readability), 6: Passphrase (long words), 7: Passphrase (short words), 8: Passphrase (short words with unique prefixes))

PasswordExpirationProtectionEnabled.d: Configures additional enforcement of maximum password age for the managed local administrator account.

PasswordLength.d: Configures the length of the password of the managed local administrator account. Minimum - 8, Maximum - 64

PostAuthenticationActions.d: Specifies the actions to take upon expiration of the configured grace period. (1: Reset password, 3: Reset the password and logoff the managed account, 5: Reset the password and reboot, 11: Reset the password, logoff the managed account, and terminate any remaining processes.)

PostAuthenticationResetDelay.d: Specifies the amount of time (in hours) to wait after an authentication before executing the specified post-authentication actions. Minimum - 0, Maximum - 24

RoleScopeTagIds.d: List of Scope Tags for this policy.

passwordagedays_aad.d: Configures the maximum password age of the managed local administrator account for Azure AD. Minimum - 7, Maximum - 365

IntuneAlertRuleWindows365.d: This resource configures an Intune Alert Rule Windows365.

AlertRuleTemplate.d: The rule template of the alert event. The possible values are: cloudPcProvisionScenario, cloudPcImageUploadScenario, cloudPcOnPremiseNetworkConnectionCheckScenario, cloudPcInGracePeriodScenario, cloudPcFrontlineInsufficientLicensesScenario, cloudPcInaccessibleScenario, cloudPcFrontlineConcurrencyScenario, cloudPcUserSettingsPersistenceScenario, cloudPcDeprovisionFailedScenario.

Conditions.d: The conditions that determine when to send alerts. For example, you can configure a condition to send an alert when provisioning fails for six or more Cloud PCs.

Enabled.d: The status of the rule that indicates whether the rule is enabled or disabled. If true, the rule is enabled otherwise, the rule is disabled.

NotificationChannels.d: The notification channels of the rule selected by the user.

Severity.d: The severity of the rule. The possible values are: informational, warning, critical.

DisplayName.d: Display name of the Intune policy.

Id.d: Id of the Intune policy.

appSupportsOemConfig.d: Whether or not this AppConfig is an OEMConfig policy. This property is read-only.

connectedAppsEnabled.d: Setting to specify whether to allow ConnectedApps experience for this app.

packageId.d: Android Enterprise app configuration package id.

payloadJson.d: Android Enterprise app configuration JSON payload.

permissionActions.d: List of Android app permissions and corresponding permission actions.

profileApplicability.d: Android Enterprise profile applicability (AndroidWorkProfile, DeviceOwner, or default (applies to both)). Possible values are: default, androidWorkProfile, androidDeviceOwner.

targetedMobileApps.d: the associated app. Inherited from managedDeviceMobileAppConfiguration

enableFileHashComputation.d: Enable file hash computation (false: Disabled, true: Enabled)

enabled.d: Enable / disable cloud delivered protection (false: Disabled, true: Enabled)

hideStatusMenuIcon.d: Show / hide status menu icon (false: Disabled, true: Enabled)

lowPriorityScheduledScan.d: Low priority scheduled scan (false: Disabled, true: Enabled)

maximumOnDemandScanThreads.d: Degree of parallelism for on-demand scans

offlineDefinitionUpdate.d: Enable offline security intelligence updates (enabled: enabled, disabled: disabled)

offlineDefinitionUpdateFallbackToCloud.d: Fallback to Microsoft cloud updates (false: Disabled, true: Enabled)

offlineDefinitionUpdateUrl.d: URL for a security intelligence updates mirror server

offlineDefinitionUpdateVerifySig.d: offline security intelligence updates signature verification (enabled: enabled, disabled: disabled)

passiveMode.d: Enable passive mode (deprecated) (false: Disabled, true: Enabled)

randomizeScanStartTime.d: Randomize scheduled scan start time. Must be between 0 and 23 hours.

runScanWhenIdle.d: Run scheduled scan when idle (false: Disabled, true: Enabled)

scanAfterDefinitionUpdate.d: Run a scan after definitions are updated (false: Disabled, true: Enabled)

scanArchives.d: Scanning inside archive files (false: Disabled, true: Enabled)

threatTypeSettingsMergePolicy.d: Threat type settings merge (0: merge, 1: admin_only)

DisableAccountProtectionUI.d: Disable Account Protection UI (0: (Disable) The users can see the display of the Account protection area in Windows Defender Security Center., 1: (Enable) The users cannot see the display of the Account protection area in Windows Defender Security Center.)

DisableAppBrowserUI.d: Disable App Browser UI (0: (Disable) The users can see the display of the app and browser protection area in Windows Defender Security Center., 1: (Enable) The users cannot see the display of the app and browser protection area in Windows Defender Security Center.)

DisableTpmFirmwareUpdateWarning.d: Disable Tpm Firmware Update Warning (0: (Disable or Not configured) A warning will be displayed if the firmware of the security processor (TPM) should be updated for TPMs that have a vulnerability., 1: (Enabled) No warning will be displayed if the firmware of the security processor (TPM) should be updated.)

DisableVirusUI.d: Disable Virus UI (0: (Disable) The users can see the display of the virus and threat protection area in Windows Defender Security Center., 1: (Enable) The users cannot see the display of the virus and threat protection area in Windows Defender Security Center.)

HideRansomwareDataRecovery.d: Hide Ransomware Data Recovery (0: (Disable or not configured) The Ransomware data recovery area will be visible., 1: (Enable) The Ransomware data recovery area is hidden.)

HideWindowsSecurityNotificationAreaContr.d: Hide Windows Security Notification Area Control (0: , 1: Enabled)

AllowBehaviorMonitoring.d: Allow Behavior Monitoring (0: Not allowed. Turns off behavior monitoring., 1: Allowed. Turns on real-time behavior monitoring.)

AllowCloudProtection.d: Allow Cloud Protection (0: Not allowed. Turns off the Microsoft Active Protection Service., 1: Allowed. Turns on the Microsoft Active Protection Service.)

AllowEmailScanning.d: Allow Email Scanning (0: Not allowed. Turns off email scanning., 1: Allowed. Turns on email scanning.)

AllowFullScanOnMappedNetworkDrives.d: Allow Full Scan On Mapped Network Drives (0: Not allowed. Disables scanning on mapped network drives., 1: Allowed. Scans mapped network drives.)

AllowFullScanRemovableDriveScanning.d: Allow Full Scan Removable Drive Scanning (0: Not allowed. Turns off scanning on removable drives., 1: Allowed. Scans removable drives.)

AllowOnAccessProtection.d: Allow On Access Protection (0: Not allowed., 1: Allowed.)

AllowScanningNetworkFiles.d: Allow Scanning Network Files (0: Not allowed. Turns off scanning of network files., 1: Allowed. Scans network files.)

AllowScriptScanning.d: Allow Script Scanning (0: Not allowed., 1: Allowed.)

DisablePrivacyMode.d: Allow users to view the full History results (0: No, 1: Yes)

DisableRestorePoint.d: Create a system restore point before computers are cleaned. (0: No, 1: Yes)

EnableLowCPUPriority.d: Enable Low CPU Priority (0: Disabled, 1: Enabled)

They will show in history along with other threats., 2: Audit mode. Windows Defender will detect potentially unwanted applications, but take no action. You can review information about the applications Windows Defender would have taken action against by searching for events created by Windows Defender in the Event Viewer.)

IntuneAntivirusPolicyWindows10SettingCat.d: This resource configures an Intune Endpoint Protection Antivirus policy for a Windows 10 Device.

AllowArchiveScanning.d: Allows or disallows scanning of archives. (0: disable feature. 1: enable feature)

AllowBehaviorMonitoring.d: Allows or disallows Windows Defender Behavior Monitoring functionality. (0: disable feature. 1: enable feature)

AllowCloudProtection.d: To best protect your PC, Windows Defender will send information to Microsoft about any problems it finds. Microsoft will analyze that information, learn more about problems affecting you and other customers, and offer improved solutions. (0: disable feature. 1: enable feature)

AllowDatagramProcessingOnWinServer.d: Allows or disallows Network Protection to enable datagram processing on Windows Server. (0: disable feature. 1: enable feature)

AllowEmailScanning.d: Allows or disallows scanning of email. (0: disable feature. 1: enable feature)

AllowFullScanOnMappedNetworkDrives.d: Allows or disallows a full scan of mapped network drives. (0: disable feature. 1: enable feature)

AllowFullScanRemovableDriveScanning.d: Allows or disallows a full scan of removable drives. During a quick scan, removable drives may still be scanned. (0: disable feature. 1: enable feature)

AllowIOAVProtection.d: Allows or disallows Windows Defender IOAVP Protection functionality. (0: disable feature. 1: enable feature)

AllowNetworkProtectionDownLevel.d: Allows or disallows Network Protection to be configured into block or audit mode on windows downlevel of RS3. (0: disable feature. 1: enable feature)

AllowOnAccessProtection.d: Allows or disallows Windows Defender On Access Protection functionality. (0: disable feature. 1: enable feature)

AllowRealtimeMonitoring.d: Allows or disallows Windows Defender real-time Monitoring functionality. (0: disable feature. 1: enable feature)

AllowScanningNetworkFiles.d: Allows or disallows a scanning of network files. (0: disable feature. 1: enable feature)

AllowScriptScanning.d: Allows or disallows Windows Defender Script Scanning functionality. (0: disable feature. 1: enable feature)

AllowUserUIAccess.d: Allows or disallows user access to the Windows Defender UI. I disallowed, all Windows Defender notifications will also be suppressed. (0: Prevents users from accessing UI. 1: Lets users access UI)

ArchiveMaxDepth.d: Specify the maximum folder depth to extract from archive files for scanning.

ArchiveMaxSize.d: Specify the maximum size, in KB, of archive files to be extracted and scanned.

Assignments.d: Represents the assignment to the Intune policy.

AvgCPULoadFactor.d: Represents the average CPU load factor for the Windows Defender scan (in percent).

CheckForSignaturesBeforeRunningScan.d: This policy setting allows you to manage whether a check for new virus and spyware definitions will occur before running a scan. (0: disable feature. 1: enable feature)

CloudBlockLevel.d: This policy setting determines how aggressive Microsoft Defender Antivirus will be in blocking and scanning suspicious files. Value type is integer.(0: Default windows defender blocking level, 2: High blocking level, 4:High+ blocking level, 6:Zero tolerance blocking level)

CloudExtendedTimeout.d: This feature allows Microsoft Defender Antivirus to block a suspicious file for up to 60 seconds, and scan it in the cloud to make sure it's safe. Value type is integer, range is 0 - 50.

CompanyName.d: The company name that is displayed to the users. CompanyName is required for both EnableCustomizedToasts and EnableInAppCustomization.

DaysToRetainCleanedMalware.d: Time period (in days) that quarantine items will be stored on the system.

Description.d: Description of the endpoint protection policy for Windows 10.

DisableAccountProtectionUI.d: Use this policy setting to specify if to display the Account protection area in Windows Defender Security Center. (0: disable feature. 1: enable feature)

DisableAppBrowserUI.d: Use this policy setting if you want to disable the display of the app and browser protection area in Windows Defender Security Center. (0: disable feature. 1: enable feature)

DisableCatchupFullScan.d: This policy setting allows you to configure catch-up scans for scheduled full scans. (1: disabled, 0: enabled)

DisableCatchupQuickScan.d: This policy setting allows you to configure catch-up scans for scheduled quick scans. (1: disabled, 0: enabled)

DisableClearTpmButton.d: Disable the Clear TPM button in Windows Security. (0: disable feature. 1: enable feature)

DisableCoreServiceECSIntegration.d: Disable Core Service ECS Integration. (0: disabled, 1: enabled)

DisableCoreServiceTelemetry.d: Disable Core Service Telemetry. (1: disabled, 0: enabled)

DisableDeviceSecurityUI.d: Use this policy setting if you want to disable the display of the Device security area in the Windows Defender Security Center. (0: disable feature. 1: enable feature)

DisableDnsOverTcpParsing.d: Disables or enables DNS over TCP Parsing for Network Protection. (0: enable feature. 1: disable feature)

DisableEnhancedNotifications.d: Use this policy setting if you want to disable the display of Windows Defender Security Center notifications. (0: disable feature. 1: enable feature)

DisableFamilyUI.d: Use this policy setting if you want to disable the display of the family options area in Windows Defender Security Center. (0: disable feature. 1: enable feature)

DisableHealthUI.d: Use this policy setting if you want to disable the display of the device performance and health area in Windows Defender Security Center. (0: disable feature. 1: enable feature)

DisableHttpParsing.d: Disables or enables HTTP Parsing for Network Protection. (0: enable feature. 1: disable feature)

DisableLocalAdminMerge.d: This policy setting controls whether or not complex list settings configured by a local administrator are merged with managed settings. (0: enable local admin merge, 1: disable local admin merge)

DisableNetworkUI.d: Use this policy setting if you want to disable the display of the firewall and network protection area in Windows Defender Security Center. (0: disable feature. 1: enable feature)

DisableSshParsing.d: Disable Ssh Parsing (1: SSH parsing is disabled, 0: SSH parsing is enabled)

DisableTlsParsing.d: This setting disables TLS Parsing for Network Protection. (0: enabled, 1: disabled)

DisableTpmFirmwareUpdateWarning.d: Hide the recommendation to update TPM Firmware when a vulnerable firmware is detected. (0: disable feature. 1: enable feature)

DisableVirusUI.d: Use this policy setting if you want to disable the display of the virus and threat protection area in Windows Defender Security Center. (0: disable feature. 1: enable feature)

DisplayName.d: Display name of the endpoint protection policy for Windows 10.

Email.d: The email address that is displayed to users. The default mail application is used to initiate email actions.

EnableCustomizedToasts.d: Enable this policy to display your company name and contact options in the notifications. (0: disable feature. 1: enable feature)

EnableInAppCustomization.d: Enable this policy to have your company name and contact options displayed in a contact card fly out in Windows Defender Security Center. (0: disable feature. 1: enable feature)

EnableLowCPUPriority.d: This policy setting allows you to enable or disable low CPU priority for scheduled scans. (0: disable feature. 1: enable feature)

EnableNetworkProtection.d: This policy allows you to turn on network protection (block/audit) or off. (0: disabled, 1: block mode, 2: audit mode)

EngineUpdatesChannel.d: Enable this policy to specify when devices receive Microsoft Defender engine updates during the monthly gradual rollout. (0: Not configured, 2: Beta Channel, 3: Current Channel (Preview), 4: Current Channel (Staged), 5: Current Channel (Broad), 6: Critical)

ExcludedExtensions.d: Allows an administrator to specify a list of file type extensions to ignore during a scan.

ExcludedPaths.d: Allows an administrator to specify a list of directory paths to ignore during a scan.

ExcludedProcesses.d: Allows an administrator to specify a list of files opened by processes to ignore during a scan.

HideRansomwareDataRecovery.d: Use this policy setting to hide the Ransomware data recovery area in Windows Defender Security Center. (0: disable feature. 1: enable feature)

HideWindowsSecurityNotificationAreaContr.d: This policy setting hides the Windows Security notification area control. (0: disable feature. 1: enable feature)

HighSeverityThreats.d: Allows an administrator to specify severe threats corresponding action ID to take.

Identity.d: Identity of the endpoint protection policy for Windows 10.

LowSeverityThreats.d: Allows an administrator to specify low severity threats corresponding action ID to take.

MeteredConnectionUpdates.d: Allow managed devices to update through metered connections. (0: disabled, 1: enabled)

ModerateSeverityThreats.d: Allows an administrator to specify moderate severity threats corresponding action ID to take.

PUAProtection.d: Specifies the level of detection for potentially unwanted applications (PUAs). (0: disabled, 1: block mode, 2: audit mode)

Phone.d: The phone number or Skype ID that is displayed to users. Skype is used to initiate the call.

PlatformUpdatesChannel.d: Enable this policy to specify when devices receive Microsoft Defender platform updates during the monthly gradual rollout. (0: Not configured, 2: Beta Channel, 3: Current Channel (Preview), 4: Current Channel (Staged), 5: Current Channel (Broad), 6: Critical)

RandomizeScheduleTaskTimes.d: Specifies if the start time of the scan is randomized. (0: no randomization, 1: randomized)

RealTimeScanDirection.d: Controls which sets of files should be monitored. (0: Monitor all files (bi-directional), 1: Monitor incoming files, 2: Monitor outgoing files)

RoleScopeTagIds.d: List of Scope Tags for this Entity instance.

ScanParameter.d: Selects whether to perform a quick scan or full scan. (1: Quick scan, 2: Full scan)

ScheduleQuickScanTime.d: Selects the time of day that the Windows Defender quick scan should run.

ScheduleScanDay.d: Selects the day that the Windows Defender scan should run. (0: Every day, 1: Sunday, 2: Monday, 3: Tuesday, 4: Wednesday, 5: Thursday, 6: Friday, 7: Saturday, 8: No scheduled scan)

ScheduleScanTime.d: Selects the time of day that the Windows Defender scan should run. Must be between 0 and 1380 minutes.

SecurityIntelligenceUpdatesChannel.d: Enable this policy to specify when devices receive Microsoft Defender security intelligence updates during the daily gradual rollout. (0: Not configured, 4: Current Channel (Staged), 5: Current Channel (Broad))

SevereThreats.d: Allows an administrator to specify high severity threats corresponding action ID to take.

SignatureUpdateFallbackOrder.d: This policy setting allows you to define the order in which different definition update sources should be contacted.

SignatureUpdateFileSharesSources.d: This policy setting allows you to configure UNC file share sources for downloading definition updates.

SignatureUpdateInterval.d: Specifies the interval (in hours) that will be used to check for signatures, so instead of using the ScheduleDay and ScheduleTime the check for new signatures will be set according to the interval. Must be between 0 and 24 hours.

SubmitSamplesConsent.d: Checks for the user consent level in Windows Defender to send data. (0: Always prompt, 1: Send safe samples automatically, 2: Never send, 3: Send all samples automatically)

TemplateId.d: Template Id of the policy. 0: Windows Security Experience, 1: Defender Update controls, 2: Microsoft Defender Antivirus exclusions, 3: Microsoft Defender Antivirus

URL.d: The help portal URL that is displayed to users. The default browser is used to initiate this action.

IntuneAppAndBrowserIsolationPolicyWindow.d: This resource configures an Intune App And Browser Isolation Policy for Windows10.

AllowCameraMicrophoneRedirection.d: Allow camera and microphone access (0: Microsoft Defender Application Guard cannot access the device's camera and microphone. When the policy is not configured, it is the same as disabled (0)., 1: Turns on the functionality to allow Microsoft Defender Application Guard to access the device's camera and microphone.)

AllowPersistence.d: Allow data persistence (0: Application Guard discards user-downloaded files and other items (such as, cookies, Favorites, and so on) during machine restart or user log-off., 1: Application Guard saves user-downloaded files and other items (such as, cookies, Favorites, and so on) for use in future Application Guard sessions.)

AllowVirtualGPU.d: Allow hardware-accelerated rendering (0: Cannot access the vGPU and uses the CPU to support rendering graphics. When the policy is not configured, it is the same as disabled (0)., 1: Turns on the functionality to access the vGPU offloading graphics rendering from the CPU. This can create a faster experience when working with graphics intense websites or watching video within the container.)

AllowWindowsDefenderApplicationGuard.d: Turn on Microsoft Defender Application Guard (0: Disable Microsoft Defender Application Guard, 1: Enable Microsoft Defender Application Guard for Microsoft Edge ONLY, 2: Enable Microsoft Defender Application Guard for isolated Windows environments ONLY, 3: Enable Microsoft Defender Application Guard for Microsoft Edge AND isolated Windows environments)

AuditApplicationGuard.d: Audit Application Guard (0: Audit event logs aren't collected for Application Guard., 1: Application Guard inherits its auditing policies from system and starts to audit security events for Application Guard container.)

ClipboardFileType.d: Clipboard content options (1: Allow text copying., 2: Allow image copying., 3: Allow text and image copying.)

ClipboardSettings.d: Clipboard behavior settings (0: Completely turns Off the clipboard functionality for the Application Guard., 1: Turns On clipboard operation from an isolated session to the host., 2: Turns On clipboard operation from the host to an isolated session., 3: Turns On clipboard operation in both the directions.)

EnterpriseProxyServersAreAuthoritative.d: Enterprise Proxy Servers Are Authoritative (1: Enable, 0: Disable)

AllowedAndroidDeviceModels.d: List of allowed Android device models.

AllowedDataStorageLocations.d: Data storage locations where a user may store managed data.

AllowedInboundDataTransferSources.d: Sources from which data is allowed to be transferred. Possible values are: allApps, managedApps, none.

AllowedOutboundClipboardSharingException.d: Maximum length of outbound clipboard sharing exceptions.

AllowedOutboundClipboardSharingLevel.d: The level to which the clipboard may be shared between apps on the managed device. Possible values are: allApps, managedAppsWithPasteIn, managedApps, blocked.

AllowedOutboundDataTransferDestinations.d: Destinations to which data is allowed to be transferred. Possible values are: allApps, managedApps, none.

Alloweddataingestionlocations.d: Sources from which data is allowed to be transferred.

AppActionIfAndroidDeviceManufacturerNotA.d: Defines a managed app behavior, either block or wipe, if the specified device manufacturer is not allowed.

AppActionIfAndroidDeviceModelNotAllowed.d: Defines a managed app behavior, either block or wipe, if the specified device model is not allowed.

AppActionIfAndroidSafetyNetAppsVerificat.d: Defines a managed app behavior, either warn or block, if the specified Android App Verification requirement fails.

AppActionIfAndroidSafetyNetDeviceAttesta.d: Defines a managed app behavior, either warn or block, if the specified Android SafetyNet Attestation requirement fails.

AppActionIfDeviceComplianceRequired.d: Defines a managed app behavior, either block or wipe, when the device is either rooted or jailbroken, if DeviceComplianceRequired is set to true.

AppActionIfDeviceLockNotSet.d: Defines a managed app behavior, either warn, block, or wipe, if the screen lock is required on an Android device but is not set.

AppActionIfMaximumPinRetriesExceeded.d: Defines a managed app behavior, either block or wipe, based on the maximum number of incorrect pin retry attempts.

AppGroupType.d: The apps controlled by this protection policy, overrides any values in Apps unless this value is 'selectedPublicApps'.

ApprovedKeyboards.d: If Keyboard Restriction is enabled, only keyboards in this approved list will be allowed. A key should be Android package id for a keyboard and value should be a friendly name.

Apps.d: List of IDs representing the Android apps controlled by this protection policy.

Assignments.d: Assignments of the Android Protection Policy.

BiometricAuthenticationBlocked.d: Indicates whether biometric authentication is blocked.

BlockAfterCompanyPortalUpdateDeferralInD.d: Number of days to block access after a company portal update deferral.

CustomBrowserPackageId.d: The application ID for a single browser. Web content (http/s) from policy managed applications will open in the specified browser.

CustomDialerAppPackageId.d: Package ID of the custom dialer app.

IntuneAppProtectionPolicyWindows10.d: This resource configures an Intune App Protection Policy for Windows10.

AllowedInboundDataTransferSources.d: Indicates the sources from which data is allowed to be transferred. Some possible values are allApps or none. Possible values are: allApps, none.

AllowedOutboundClipboardSharingLevel.d: Indicates the level to which the clipboard may be shared across org & non-org resources. Some possible values are anyDestinationAnySource or none. Possible values are: anyDestinationAnySource, none, orgDestinationAnySource, orgDestinationOrgSource, unknownFutureValue.

AllowedOutboundDataTransferDestinations.d: Indicates the destinations to which data is allowed to be transferred. Some possible values are allApps or none. Possible values are: allApps, none.

This happens when the user is deleted or disabled in AAD. Some possible values are block or wipe. If this property is not set, no action will be taken. Possible values are: block, wipe, warn, blockWhenSettingIsSupported.

MaximumWarningOsVersion.d: Versions bigger than the specified version will result in warning message on the managed app from accessing company data. For example: '8.1.0' or '13.1.1'.

MaximumWipeOsVersion.d: Versions bigger than the specified version will wipe the managed app and the associated company data. For example: '8.1.0' or '13.1.1'.

MinimumWarningAppVersion.d: Versions less than the specified version will result in warning message on the managed app from accessing company data. For example: '8.1.0' or '13.1.1'.

MinimumWipeAppVersion.d: Versions less than the specified version will wipe the managed app and the associated company data. For example: '8.1.0' or '13.1.1'.

MinimumWipeOsVersion.d: Versions less than the specified version will wipe the managed app and the associated company data. For example: '8.1.0' or '13.1.1'.

MinimumWipeSdkVersion.d: Versions less than the specified version will wipe the managed app and the associated company data. For example: '8.1.0' or '13.1.1'.

PeriodOfflineBeforeAccessCheck.d: The period after which access is checked when the device is not connected to the internet. For example, PT5M indicates that the interval is 5 minutes in duration. A timespan value of PT0S indicates that access will be blocked immediately when the device is not connected to the internet.

PeriodOfflineBeforeWipeIsEnforced.d: The amount of time an app is allowed to remain disconnected from the internet before all managed data it is wiped. For example, P5D indicates that the interval is 5 days in duration. A timespan value of PT0S indicates that managed data will never be wiped when the device is not connected to the internet.

PrintBlocked.d: When TRUE, indicates that printing is blocked from managed apps. When FALSE, indicates that printing is allowed from managed apps. Default value is FALSE.

BlockDataIngestionIntoOrganizationDocume.d: Indicates whether a user can bring data into org documents.

CustomBrowserProtocol.d: A custom browser protocol to open weblink on iOS.

DeviceComplianceRequired.d: Indicates whether device compliance is required.

DisableProtectionOfManagedOutboundOpenIn.d: Disable protection of data transferred to other apps through IOS 'OpenIn' option. This setting is only allowed to be True when AllowedOutboundDataTransferDestinations is set to ManagedApps.

ExemptedAppProtocols.d: Apps in this list will be exempt from the policy and will be able to receive data from managed apps.

ManagedBrowser.d: Indicates in which managed browser(s) that internet links should be opened. When this property is configured, ManagedBrowserToOpenLinksRequired should be true. Possible values are: notConfigured, microsoftEdge.

MaximumAllowedDeviceThreatLevel.d: Maximum allowed device threat level, as reported by the MTD app Inherited from managedAppProtection.

MaximumPinRetries.d: Maximum number of incorrect pin retry attempts before the managed app is either blocked or wiped.

MaximumWipeOsVersion.d: Versions bigger than the specified version will block the managed app from accessing company data. Inherited from managedAppProtection.

MinimumWipeOSVersion.d: Versions less than or equal to the specified version will wipe the managed app and the associated company data.

An app can only be actively using a single Mobile Threat Defense partner. When NULL, Microsoft Defender will be given preference. Otherwise setting the value to defenderOverThirdPartyPartner or thirdPartyPartnerOverDefender will make explicit which partner to prioritize.

MobileThreatDefenseRemediationAction.d: Determines what action to take if the mobile threat defense threat threshold isn't met. Warn isn't a supported value for this property Inherited from managedAppProtection.

PeriodBeforePinReset.d: TimePeriod before the all-level pin must be reset if PinRequired is set to True. Must be an ISO8601 timespan format.

PeriodOfflineBeforeAccessCheck.d: The period after which access is checked when the device is not connected to the internet. Must be an ISO8601 timespan format.

PeriodOnlineBeforeAccessCheck.d: The period after which access is checked when the device is connected to the internet. Must be an ISO8601 timespan format.

PinCharacterSet.d: Character set which may be used for an app-level pin if PinRequired is set to True. Possible values are: numeric, alphanumericAndSymbol.

PinRequired.d: Indicates whether an app-level pin is required.

ProtectInboundDataFromUnknownSources.d: Protect incoming data from unknown source. This setting is only allowed to be True when AllowedInboundDataTransferSources is set to AllApps.

IntuneApplicationControlPolicyWindows10.d: This resource configures a Intune Endpoint Protection Application Control policy for an Windows 10 Device.

Assignments.d: Assignments of the Intune Policy.

Description.d: Description of the endpoint protection application control policy for Windows 10.

DisplayName.d: Display name of the endpoint protection application control policy for Windows 10.

RoleScopeTagIds.d: List of Scope Tags for this Entity instance.

SmartScreenBlockOverrideForFiles.d: Indicates whether or not SmartScreen will not present an option for the user to disregard the warning and run the app.

SmartScreenEnableInshell.d: Enforce the use of SmartScreen for all users.

IntuneAttackSurfaceReductionRulesPolicyW.d: This resource configures an Intune Endpoint Protection ASR Rules policy for a Windows 10 Device for ConfigManager.

Assignments.d: Assignments of the endpoint protection attack surface protection rules policy for Windows 10.

AttackSurfaceReductionOnlyExclusions.d: Exclude files and paths from attack surface reduction rules

BlockAbuseOfExploitedVulnerableSignedDri.d: This rule prevents an application from writing a vulnerable signed driver to disk.

BlockAdobeReaderFromCreatingChildProcess.d: This rule prevents attacks by blocking Adobe Reader from creating processes.

BlockAllOfficeApplicationsFromCreatingCh.d: This rule blocks Office apps from creating child processes. Office apps include Word, Excel, PowerPoint, OneNote, and Access.

BlockCredentialStealingFromWindowsLocalS.d: This rule helps prevent credential stealing by locking down Local Security Authority Subsystem Service (LSASS).

BlockExecutableContentFromEmailClientAnd.d: This rule blocks the following file types from launching from email opened within the Microsoft Outlook application, or Outlook.com and other popular webmail providers.

BlockExecutableFilesRunningUnlessTheyMee.d: This rule blocks executable files that don't meet a prevalence, age, or trusted list criteria, such as .exe, .dll, or .scr, from launching.

BlockExecutionOfPotentiallyObfuscatedScr.d: This rule detects suspicious properties within an obfuscated script.

BlockJavaScriptOrVBScriptFromLaunchingDo.d: This rule prevents scripts from launching potentially malicious downloaded content.

BlockOfficeApplicationsFromCreatingExecu.d: This rule prevents Office apps, including Word, Excel, and PowerPoint, from creating potentially malicious executable content, by blocking malicious code from being written to disk.

BlockOfficeApplicationsFromInjectingCode.d: This rule blocks code injection attempts from Office apps into other processes.

BlockOfficeCommunicationAppFromCreatingC.d: This rule prevents Outlook from creating child processes, while still allowing legitimate Outlook functions.

BlockPersistenceThroughWMIEventSubscript.d: This rule prevents malware from abusing WMI to attain persistence on a device.

BlockProcessCreationsFromPSExecAndWMICom.d: This rule blocks processes created through PsExec and WMI from running.

BlockUntrustedUnsignedProcessesThatRunFr.d: With this rule, admins can prevent unsigned or untrusted executable files from running from USB removable drives, including SD cards.

BlockWin32APICallsFromOfficeMacros.d: This rule prevents VBA macros from calling Win32 APIs.

ControlledFolderAccessAllowedApplication.d: List of apps that have access to protected folders.

ControlledFolderAccessProtectedFolders.d: List of additional folders that need to be protected

Description.d: Description of the endpoint protection attack surface protection rules policy for Windows 10.

DisplayName.d: Display name of the endpoint protection attack surface protection rules policy for Windows 10.

EnableControlledFolderAccess.d: This rule enable Controlled folder access which protects your data by checking apps against a list of known, trusted apps.values 0:disable, 1:enable, 2:audit, 3: Block disk modification only, 4: Audit disk modification only

Identity.d: Identity of the endpoint protection attack surface protection rules policy for Windows 10.

RoleScopeTagIds.d: List of Scope Tags for this Entity instance.

UseAdvancedProtectionAgainstRansomware.d: This rule provides an extra layer of protection against ransomware.

IntuneAzureNetworkConnectionWindows365.d: This resource configures an Intune Azure Network Connection for Windows365.

AdDomainName.d: The fully qualified domain name (FQDN) of the Active Directory domain you want to join. Optional.

AdDomainPassword.d: The password associated with adDomainUsername. Cannot be exported and must be manually added before deploying the network connection.

AdDomainUsername.d: The username of an Active Directory account (user or service account) that has permissions to create computer objects in Active Directory. Required format: admincontoso.com. Optional.

ConnectionType.d: Specifies the method by which a provisioned Cloud PC is joined to Microsoft Entra. The azureADJoin option indicates the absence of an on-premises Active Directory (AD) in the current tenant that results in the Cloud PC device only joining to Microsoft Entra. The hybridAzureADJoin option indicates the presence of an on-premises AD in the current tenant and that the Cloud PC joins both the on-premises AD and Microsoft Entra. The selected option also determines the types of users who can be assigned and can sign into a Cloud PC. The azureADJoin option allows both cloud-only and hybrid users to be assigned and sign in, whereas hybridAzureADJoin is restricted to hybrid users only. The default value is hybridAzureADJoin. The possible values are: hybridAzureADJoin, azureADJoin.

DisplayName.d: The display name for the Azure network connection.

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

OrganizationalUnit.d: The organizational unit (OU) in which the computer account is created. If left null, the OU configured as the default (a well-known computer object container) in your Active Directory domain (OU) is used. Optional. Only applicable for the connection type 'hybridAzureADJoin'.

ResourceGroupId.d: The ID of the target resource group. Required format: /subscriptions/{subscription-id}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroupName}.

RoleScopeTagIds.d: List of Scope Tags for this Entity instance.

SubnetId.d: The ID of the target subnet. Required format: /subscriptions/{subscription-id}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroupName}/providers/Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks/{virtualNetworkId}/subnets/{subnetName}.

SubscriptionName.d: The name of the target Azure subscription.

VirtualNetworkId.d: The ID of the target virtual network. Required format: /subscriptions/{subscription-id}/{resourceGroups/resourceGroupName}/providers/Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks/{virtualNetworkName}.

IntuneCloudProvisioningPolicyWindows365.d: This resource configures an Intune Cloud Provisioning Policy for Windows365.

Assignments.d: Represents the assignment to the Intune policy.

Autopatch.d: Indicates the Windows Autopatch settings for Cloud PCs using this provisioning policy. The settings take effect when the tenant enrolls in Autopatch and the managedType of the microsoftManagedDesktop property is set as starterManaged.

AutopilotConfiguration.d: The specific settings for Windows Autopilot that enable Windows 365 customers to experience it on Cloud PC. Supports $select.

CloudPcNamingTemplate.d: The template used to name Cloud PCs provisioned using this policy. The template can contain custom text and replacement tokens, including %USERNAME:x% and %RAND:x%, which represent the user's name and a randomly generated number, respectively. For example, CPC-%USERNAME:4%-%RAND:5% means that the name of the Cloud PC starts with CPC-, followed by a four-character username, a - character, and then five random characters. The total length of the text generated by the template can't exceed 15 characters.

Description.d: The provisioning policy description.

DisplayName.d: The display name for the provisioning policy.

DomainJoinConfigurations.d: Specifies a list ordered by priority on how Cloud PCs join Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD). Supports $select.

EnableSingleSignOn.d: True if single sign-on can access the provisioned Cloud PC. False indicates that the provisioned Cloud PC doesn't support this feature. The default value is false. Windows 365 users can use single sign-on to authenticate to Microsoft Entra ID with passwordless options (for example, FIDO keys) to access their Cloud PC. Optional.

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

ImageDisplayName.d: The display name of the operating system image that is used for provisioning. For example, Windows 11 Preview + Microsoft 365 Apps 23H2 23H2.

ImageId.d: The unique identifier that represents an operating system image that is used for provisioning new Cloud PCs. The format for a gallery type image is: {publisherNameofferNameskuName}. Supported values for each of the parameters are:publisher: Microsoftwindowsdesktop offer: windows-ent-cpc sku: 21h1-ent-cpc-m365, 21h1-ent-cpc-os, 20h2-ent-cpc-m365, 20h2-ent-cpc-os, 20h1-ent-cpc-m365, 20h1-ent-cpc-os, 19h2-ent-cpc-m365, and 19h2-ent-cpc-os

ImageType.d: The type of operating system image (custom or gallery) that is used for provisioning on Cloud PCs. Possible values are: gallery, custom. The default value is gallery.

LocalAdminEnabled.d: When true, the local admin is enabled for Cloud PCs false indicates that the local admin isn't enabled for Cloud PCs. The default value is false.

ProvisioningType.d: Specifies the type of licenses to be used when provisioning Cloud PCs using this policy. The possible values are dedicated, shared, sharedByUser, sharedByEntraGroup. The shared member is deprecated and will stop returning on April 30, 2027 going forward, use the sharedByUser member. For example, a dedicated service plan can be assigned to only one user and provision only one Cloud PC. The shared and sharedByUser plans require customers to purchase a shared service plan. Each shared license purchased can enable up to three Cloud PCs, with only one user signed in at a time. The sharedByEntraGroup plan also requires the purchase of a shared service plan. Each shared license under this plan can enable one Cloud PC, which is shared for the group according to the assignments of this policy. By default, the license type is dedicated if the provisioningType isn't specified when you create the cloudPcProvisioningPolicy. You can't change this property after the cloudPcProvisioningPolicy is created.

RoleScopeTagIds.d: The Role Scope Tag Ids

UserExperienceType.d: Specifies the type of cloud object the end user can access. Possible values are: cloudPc, cloudApp. cloudPc indicates that the end user can access the entire desktop. cloudApp indicates that the end user can only access apps published under this provisioning policy. The type can't be changed once the provisioning policy is created. If not specified during creation, the default value is cloudPc. When cloudApp is selected, the provisioningType must be sharedByEntraGroup. Cannot be changed after creation.

WindowsSetting.d: Indicates a specific Windows setting to configure during the creation of Cloud PCs for this provisioning policy. Supports $select.

WindowsSettings.d: Specific Windows settings to configure during the creation of Cloud PCs for this provisioning policy. Supports $select. The windowsSettings property is deprecated and will stop returning data on January 31, 2024. Going forward, use the windowsSetting property.

IntuneDeviceConfigurationDeliveryOptimiz.d: This resource configures an Intune Device Configuration Delivery Optimization Policy for Windows10.

Assignments.d: Represents the assignment to the Intune policy.

BackgroundDownloadFromHttpDelayInSeconds.d: Specifies number of seconds to delay an HTTP source in a background download that is allowed to use peer-to-peer. Valid values 0 to 4294967295

BandwidthMode.d: Specifies foreground and background bandwidth usage using percentages, absolutes, or hours.

CacheServerBackgroundDownloadFallbackToH.d: Specifies number of seconds to delay a fall back from cache servers to an HTTP source for a background download. Valid values 0 to 2592000.

CacheServerForegroundDownloadFallbackToH.d: Specifies number of seconds to delay a fall back from cache servers to an HTTP source for a foreground download. Valid values 0 to 2592000.

DeliveryOptimizationMode.d: Specifies the download method that delivery optimization can use to manage network bandwidth consumption for large content distribution scenarios. Possible values are: userDefined, httpOnly, httpWithPeeringNat, httpWithPeeringPrivateGroup, httpWithInternetPeering, simpleDownload, bypassMode.

Description.d: Admin provided description of the Device Configuration.

DisplayName.d: Admin provided name of the device configuration.

ForegroundDownloadFromHttpDelayInSeconds.d: Specifies number of seconds to delay an HTTP source in a foreground download that is allowed to use peer-to-peer (0-86400). Valid values 0 to 86400 Specifying 0 sets Delivery Optimization to manage this setting using the cloud service. Valid values 0 to 86400

GroupIdSource.d: Specifies to restrict peer selection to a specific source. The options set in this policy only apply to Delivery Optimization mode Group (2) download mode. If Group (2) isn't set as Download mode, this policy will be ignored. For option 3 - DHCP Option ID, the client will query DHCP Option ID 234 and use the returned GUID value as the Group ID.

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

MaximumCacheAgeInDays.d: Specifies the maximum time in days that each file is held in the Delivery Optimization cache after downloading successfully (0-3650). Valid values 0 to 3650

MaximumCacheSize.d: Specifies the maximum cache size that Delivery Optimization either as a percentage or in GB.

MinimumBatteryPercentageAllowedToUpload.d: Specifies the minimum battery percentage to allow the device to upload data (0-100). Valid values 0 to 100 The default value is 0. The value 0 (zero) means 'not limited' and the cloud service default value will be used. Valid values 0 to 100

MinimumDiskSizeAllowedToPeerInGigabytes.d: Specifies the minimum disk size in GB to use Peer Caching (1-100000). Valid values 1 to 100000 Recommended values: 64 GB to 256 GB. Valid values 1 to 100000

MinimumFileSizeToCacheInMegabytes.d: Specifies the minimum content file size in MB enabled to use Peer Caching (1-100000). Valid values 1 to 100000 Recommended values: 1 MB to 100,000 MB. Valid values 1 to 100000

MinimumRamAllowedToPeerInGigabytes.d: Specifies the minimum RAM size in GB to use Peer Caching (1-100000). Valid values 1 to 100000

ModifyCacheLocation.d: Specifies the drive that Delivery Optimization should use for its cache.

RestrictPeerSelectionBy.d: Specifies to restrict peer selection via selected option.

RoleScopeTagIds.d: List of Scope Tags for this Entity instance.

VpnPeerCaching.d: Specifies whether the device is allowed to participate in Peer Caching while connected via VPN to the domain network.

Assignments.d: Represents the assignment to the Intune policy.

DODelayCacheServerFallbackForeground.d: DO Delay Cache Server Fallback Foreground

DODelayForegroundDownloadFromHttp.d: DO Delay Foreground Download From Http

DODisallowCacheServerDownloadsOnVPN.d: DO Disallow Cache Server Downloads On VPN (0: Not Set, 1: Enabled)

DODownloadMode.d: DO Download Mode (0: HTTP only, no peering, 1: HTTP blended with peering behind the same NAT, 2: HTTP blended with peering across a private group, 3: HTTP blended with Internet peering, 99: HTTP only, no peering, no use of DO cloud service, 100: Bypass mode, deprecated in Windows 11)

DOGroupIdSource.d: DO Group Id Source (0: Not Set, 1: AD site, 2: Authenticated domain SID, 3: DHCP Option ID, 4: DNS Suffix, 5: Entra ID Tenant ID)

DOMinDiskSizeAllowedToPeer.d: DO Min Disk Size Allowed To Peer

DOMinFileSizeToCache.d: DO Min File Size To Cache

DOMinRAMAllowedToPeer.d: DO Min RAM Allowed To Peer

DORestrictPeerSelectionBy.d: DO Restrict Peer Selection By (0: None, 1: Subnet mask, 2: Local discovery (DNS-SD))

DisplayName.d: Admin provided name of the device configuration.

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

OrganizationalUnit.d: Organizational unit (OU) where the computer account will be created. If this parameter is NULL, the well known computer object container will be used as published in the domain.

RoleScopeTagIds.d: List of Scope Tags for this Entity instance.

IntuneDeviceConfigurationEmailProfilePol.d: This resource configures an Intune Device Configuration Email Profile Policy for Windows10.

Assignments.d: Represents the assignment to the Intune policy.

CustomDomainName.d: Custom domain name value used while generating an email profile before installing on the device.

Description.d: Admin provided description of the Device Configuration.

DisplayName.d: Admin provided name of the device configuration.

DurationOfEmailToSync.d: Duration of email to sync. Possible values are: userDefined, oneDay, threeDays, oneWeek, twoWeeks, oneMonth, unlimited.

EmailAddressSource.d: Email attribute that is picked from AAD and injected into this profile before installing on the device. Possible values are: userPrincipalName, primarySmtpAddress.

EmailSyncSchedule.d: Email sync schedule. Possible values are: userDefined, asMessagesArrive, manual, fifteenMinutes, thirtyMinutes, sixtyMinutes, basedOnMyUsage.

HostName.d: Exchange location that (URL) that the native mail app connects to.

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

RequireSsl.d: Indicates whether or not to use SSL.

RoleScopeTagIds.d: List of Scope Tags for this Entity instance.

SyncCalendar.d: Whether or not to sync the calendar.

SyncContacts.d: Whether or not to sync contacts.

SyncTasks.d: Whether or not to sync tasks.

UserDomainNameSource.d: UserDomainname attribute that is picked from AAD and injected into this profile before installing on the device. Possible values are: fullDomainName, netBiosDomainName.

UsernameAADSource.d: Name of the AAD field, that will be used to retrieve UserName for email profile. Possible values are: userPrincipalName, primarySmtpAddress, samAccountName.

UsernameSource.d: Username attribute that is picked from AAD and injected into this profile before installing on the device. Possible values are: userPrincipalName, primarySmtpAddress.

IntuneDeviceConfigurationEndpointProtect.d: This resource configures an Intune Device Configuration Endpoint Protection Policy for Windows10.

AppLockerApplicationControl.d: Enables the Admin to choose what types of app to allow on devices. Possible values are: notConfigured, enforceComponentsAndStoreApps, auditComponentsAndStoreApps, enforceComponentsStoreAppsAndSmartlocker, auditComponentsStoreAppsAndSmartlocker.

ApplicationGuardAllowCameraMicrophoneRed.d: Gets or sets whether applications inside Microsoft Defender Application Guard can access the devices camera and microphone.

ApplicationGuardAllowFileSaveOnHost.d: Allow users to download files from Edge in the application guard container and save them on the host file system

ApplicationGuardAllowPersistence.d: Allow persisting user generated data inside the App Guard Container (favorites, cookies, web passwords, etc.)

ApplicationGuardAllowPrintToLocalPrinter.d: Allow printing to Local Printers from Container

ApplicationGuardAllowPrintToNetworkPrint.d: Allow printing to Network Printers from Container

ApplicationGuardAllowPrintToPDF.d: Allow printing to PDF from Container

ApplicationGuardAllowPrintToXPS.d: Allow printing to XPS from Container

ApplicationGuardAllowVirtualGPU.d: Allow application guard to use virtual GPU

ApplicationGuardBlockClipboardSharing.d: Block clipboard to share data from Host to Container, or from Container to Host, or both ways, or neither ways. Possible values are: notConfigured, blockBoth, blockHostToContainer, blockContainerToHost, blockNone.

ApplicationGuardBlockFileTransfer.d: Block clipboard to transfer image file, text file or neither of them. Possible values are: notConfigured, blockImageAndTextFile, blockImageFile, blockNone, blockTextFile.

ApplicationGuardBlockNonEnterpriseConten.d: Block enterprise sites to load non-enterprise content, such as third party plug-ins

ApplicationGuardCertificateThumbprints.d: Allows certain device level Root Certificates to be shared with the Microsoft Defender Application Guard container.

ApplicationGuardEnabledOptions.d: Enable Windows Defender Application Guard for newer Windows builds. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabledForEdge, enabledForOffice, enabledForEdgeAndOffice.

ApplicationGuardForceAuditing.d: Force auditing will persist Windows logs and events to meet security/compliance criteria (sample events are user login-logoff, use of privilege rights, software installation, system changes, etc.)

Assignments.d: Represents the assignment to the Intune policy.

BitLockerAllowStandardUserEncryption.d: Allows the admin to allow standard users to enable encryption during Azure AD Join.

BitLockerDisableWarningForOtherDiskEncry.d: Allows the Admin to disable the warning prompt for other disk encryption on the user machines.

BitLockerEnableStorageCardEncryptionOnMo.d: Allows the admin to require encryption to be turned on using BitLocker. This policy is valid only for a mobile SKU.

BitLockerEncryptDevice.d: Allows the admin to require encryption to be turned on using BitLocker.

BitLockerRecoveryPasswordRotation.d: This setting initiates a client-driven recovery password rotation after an OS drive recovery (either by using bootmgr or WinRE). Possible values are: notConfigured, disabled, enabledForAzureAd, enabledForAzureAdAndHybrid.

DefenderAdditionalGuardedFolders.d: List of folder paths to be added to the list of protected folders

DefenderAdobeReaderLaunchChildProcess.d: Value indicating the behavior of Adobe Reader from creating child processes. Possible values are: userDefined, enable, auditMode, warn, notConfigured.

DefenderAdvancedRansomewareProtectionTyp.d: Value indicating use of advanced protection against ransomeware. Possible values are: userDefined, enable, auditMode, warn, notConfigured.

DefenderAllowBehaviorMonitoring.d: Allows or disallows Windows Defender Behavior Monitoring functionality.

DefenderAllowCloudProtection.d: To best protect your PC, Windows Defender will send information to Microsoft about any problems it finds. Microsoft will analyze that information, learn more about problems affecting you and other customers, and offer improved solutions.

DefenderAllowEndUserAccess.d: Allows or disallows user access to the Windows Defender UI. If disallowed, all Windows Defender notifications will also be suppressed.

DefenderAllowIntrusionPreventionSystem.d: Allows or disallows Windows Defender Intrusion Prevention functionality.

DefenderAllowOnAccessProtection.d: Allows or disallows Windows Defender On Access Protection functionality.

DefenderAllowRealTimeMonitoring.d: Allows or disallows Windows Defender Realtime Monitoring functionality.

DefenderAllowScanArchiveFiles.d: Allows or disallows scanning of archives.

DefenderAllowScanDownloads.d: Allows or disallows Windows Defender IOAVP Protection functionality.

DefenderAllowScanNetworkFiles.d: Allows or disallows a scanning of network files.

DefenderAllowScanRemovableDrivesDuringFu.d: Allows or disallows a full scan of removable drives. During a quick scan, removable drives may still be scanned.

DefenderAllowScanScriptsLoadedInInternet.d: Allows or disallows Windows Defender Script Scanning functionality.

DefenderAttackSurfaceReductionExcludedPa.d: List of exe files and folders to be excluded from attack surface reduction rules

DefenderBlockEndUserAccess.d: Allows or disallows user access to the Windows Defender UI. If disallowed, all Windows Defender notifications will also be suppressed.

DefenderBlockPersistenceThroughWmiType.d: Value indicating the behavior ofBlock persistence through WMI event subscription. Possible values are: userDefined, block, auditMode, warn, disable.

DefenderCheckForSignaturesBeforeRunningS.d: This policy setting allows you to manage whether a check for new virus and spyware definitions will occur before running a scan.

DefenderCloudBlockLevel.d: Added in Windows 10, version 1709. This policy setting determines how aggressive Windows Defender Antivirus will be in blocking and scanning suspicious files. Value type is integer. This feature requires the 'Join Microsoft MAPS' setting enabled in order to function. Possible values are: notConfigured, high, highPlus, zeroTolerance.

DefenderCloudExtendedTimeoutInSeconds.d: Added in Windows 10, version 1709. This feature allows Windows Defender Antivirus to block a suspicious file for up to 60 seconds, and scan it in the cloud to make sure it's safe. Value type is integer, range is 0 - 50. This feature depends on three other MAPS settings the must all be enabled- 'Configure the 'Block at First Sight' feature 'Join Microsoft MAPS' 'Send file samples when further analysis is required'. Valid values 0 to 50

DefenderDaysBeforeDeletingQuarantinedMal.d: Time period (in days) that quarantine items will be stored on the system. Valid values 0 to 90

DefenderDetectedMalwareActions.d: Allows an administrator to specify any valid threat severity levels and the corresponding default action ID to take.

DefenderDisableBehaviorMonitoring.d: Allows or disallows Windows Defender Behavior Monitoring functionality.

DefenderDisableCatchupFullScan.d: This policy setting allows you to configure catch-up scans for scheduled full scans. A catch-up scan is a scan that is initiated because a regularly scheduled scan was missed. Usually these scheduled scans are missed because the computer was turned off at the scheduled time.

DefenderDisableCatchupQuickScan.d: This policy setting allows you to configure catch-up scans for scheduled quick scans. A catch-up scan is a scan that is initiated because a regularly scheduled scan was missed. Usually these scheduled scans are missed because the computer was turned off at the scheduled time.

DefenderDisableCloudProtection.d: To best protect your PC, Windows Defender will send information to Microsoft about any problems it finds. Microsoft will analyze that information, learn more about problems affecting you and other customers, and offer improved solutions.

DefenderDisableIntrusionPreventionSystem.d: Allows or disallows Windows Defender Intrusion Prevention functionality.

DefenderDisableOnAccessProtection.d: Allows or disallows Windows Defender On Access Protection functionality.

DefenderDisableRealTimeMonitoring.d: Allows or disallows Windows Defender Realtime Monitoring functionality.

DefenderDisableScanArchiveFiles.d: Allows or disallows scanning of archives.

DefenderDisableScanDownloads.d: Allows or disallows Windows Defender IOAVP Protection functionality.

DefenderDisableScanNetworkFiles.d: Allows or disallows a scanning of network files.

DefenderDisableScanRemovableDrivesDuring.d: Allows or disallows a full scan of removable drives. During a quick scan, removable drives may still be scanned.

DefenderDisableScanScriptsLoadedInIntern.d: Allows or disallows Windows Defender Script Scanning functionality.
