DefenderEmailContentExecution.d: Value indicating if execution of executable content (exe, dll, ps, js, vbs, etc) should be dropped from email (webmail/mail-client). Possible values are: userDefined, enable, auditMode, warn, notConfigured.

DefenderEmailContentExecutionType.d: Value indicating if execution of executable content (exe, dll, ps, js, vbs, etc) should be dropped from email (webmail/mail-client). Possible values are: userDefined, block, auditMode, warn, disable.

DefenderEnableLowCpuPriority.d: This policy setting allows you to enable or disable low CPU priority for scheduled scans.

DefenderEnableScanIncomingMail.d: Allows or disallows scanning of email.

DefenderEnableScanMappedNetworkDrivesDur.d: Allows or disallows a full scan of mapped network drives.

DefenderExploitProtectionXml.d: Xml content containing information regarding exploit protection details.

DefenderExploitProtectionXmlFileName.d: Name of the file from which DefenderExploitProtectionXml was obtained.

DefenderFileExtensionsToExclude.d: File extensions to exclude from scans and real time protection.

DefenderFilesAndFoldersToExclude.d: Files and folder to exclude from scans and real time protection.

DefenderGuardMyFoldersType.d: Value indicating the behavior of protected folders. Possible values are: userDefined, enable, auditMode, blockDiskModification, auditDiskModification.

DefenderGuardedFoldersAllowedAppPaths.d: List of paths to exe that are allowed to access protected folders

DefenderNetworkProtectionType.d: Value indicating the behavior of NetworkProtection. Possible values are: userDefined, enable, auditMode, warn, notConfigured.

DefenderOfficeAppsExecutableContentCreat.d: Value indicating the behavior of Office applications/macros creating or launching executable content. Possible values are: userDefined, enable, auditMode, warn, notConfigured.
Value indicating the behavior of Office applications/macros creating or launching executable content. Possible values are: userDefined, block, auditMode, warn, disable.

DefenderOfficeAppsLaunchChildProcess.d: Value indicating the behavior of Office application launching child processes. Possible values are: userDefined, enable, auditMode, warn, notConfigured.

DefenderOfficeAppsLaunchChildProcessType.d: Value indicating the behavior of Office application launching child processes. Possible values are: userDefined, block, auditMode, warn, disable.

DefenderOfficeAppsOtherProcessInjection.d: Value indicating the behavior of Office applications injecting into other processes. Possible values are: userDefined, enable, auditMode, warn, notConfigured.

DefenderOfficeAppsOtherProcessInjectionT.d: Value indicating the behavior ofOffice applications injecting into other processes. Possible values are: userDefined, block, auditMode, warn, disable.

DefenderOfficeCommunicationAppsLaunchChi.d: Value indicating the behavior of Office communication applications, including Microsoft Outlook, from creating child processes. Possible values are: userDefined, enable, auditMode, warn, notConfigured.

DefenderOfficeMacroCodeAllowWin32Imports.d: Value indicating the behavior of Win32 imports from Macro code in Office. Possible values are: userDefined, enable, auditMode, warn, notConfigured.
Value indicating the behavior of Win32 imports from Macro code in Office. Possible values are: userDefined, block, auditMode, warn, disable.

DefenderPotentiallyUnwantedAppAction.d: Added in Windows 10, version 1607. Specifies the level of detection for potentially unwanted applications (PUAs). Windows Defender alerts you when potentially unwanted software is being downloaded or attempts to install itself on your computer. Possible values are: userDefined, enable, auditMode, warn, notConfigured.

DefenderPreventCredentialStealingType.d: Value indicating if credential stealing from the Windows local security authority subsystem is permitted. Possible values are: userDefined, enable, auditMode, warn, notConfigured.

DefenderProcessCreation.d: Value indicating response to process creations originating from PSExec and WMI commands. Possible values are: userDefined, enable, auditMode, warn, notConfigured.

DefenderProcessCreationType.d: Value indicating response to process creations originating from PSExec and WMI commands. Possible values are: userDefined, block, auditMode, warn, disable.

DefenderProcessesToExclude.d: Processes to exclude from scans and real time protection.

DefenderScanDirection.d: Controls which sets of files should be monitored. Possible values are: monitorAllFiles, monitorIncomingFilesOnly, monitorOutgoingFilesOnly.

DefenderScanMaxCpuPercentage.d: Represents the average CPU load factor for the Windows Defender scan (in percent). The default value is 50. Valid values 0 to 100

DefenderScanType.d: Selects whether to perform a quick scan or full scan. Possible values are: userDefined, disabled, quick, full.

DefenderScheduledQuickScanTime.d: Selects the time of day that the Windows Defender quick scan should run. For example, a value of 0=12:00AM, a value of 60=1:00AM, a value of 120=2:00, and so on, up to a value of 1380=11:00PM. The default value is 120

DefenderScheduledScanDay.d: Selects the day that the Windows Defender scan should run. Possible values are: userDefined, everyday, sunday, monday, tuesday, wednesday, thursday, friday, saturday, noScheduledScan.

DefenderScheduledScanTime.d: Selects the time of day that the Windows Defender scan should run.

DefenderScriptDownloadedPayloadExecution.d: Value indicating the behavior of js/vbs executing payload downloaded from Internet. Possible values are: userDefined, enable, auditMode, warn, notConfigured.
Value indicating the behavior of js/vbs executing payload downloaded from Internet. Possible values are: userDefined, block, auditMode, warn, disable.

DefenderScriptObfuscatedMacroCode.d: Value indicating the behavior of obfuscated js/vbs/ps/macro code. Possible values are: userDefined, enable, auditMode, warn, notConfigured.

DefenderScriptObfuscatedMacroCodeType.d: Value indicating the behavior of obfuscated js/vbs/ps/macro code. Possible values are: userDefined, block, auditMode, warn, disable.

DefenderSecurityCenterBlockExploitProtec.d: Indicates whether or not to block user from overriding Exploit Protection settings.

DefenderSecurityCenterDisableAccountUI.d: Used to disable the display of the account protection area.

DefenderSecurityCenterDisableAppBrowserU.d: Used to disable the display of the app and browser protection area.

DefenderSecurityCenterDisableClearTpmUI.d: Used to disable the display of the Clear TPM button.

DefenderSecurityCenterDisableFamilyUI.d: Used to disable the display of the family options area.

DefenderSecurityCenterDisableHardwareUI.d: Used to disable the display of the hardware protection area.

DefenderSecurityCenterDisableHealthUI.d: Used to disable the display of the device performance and health area.

DefenderSecurityCenterDisableNetworkUI.d: Used to disable the display of the firewall and network protection area.

DefenderSecurityCenterDisableNotificatio.d: Used to disable the display of the notification area control. The user needs to either sign out and sign in or reboot the computer for this setting to take effect.

DefenderSecurityCenterDisableRansomwareU.d: Used to disable the display of the ransomware protection area.

DefenderSecurityCenterDisableSecureBootU.d: Used to disable the display of the secure boot area under Device security.

DefenderSecurityCenterDisableTroubleshoo.d: Used to disable the display of the security process troubleshooting under Device security.

DefenderSecurityCenterDisableVirusUI.d: Used to disable the display of the virus and threat protection area.

DefenderSecurityCenterDisableVulnerableT.d: Used to disable the display of update TPM Firmware when a vulnerable firmware is detected.

DefenderSecurityCenterHelpEmail.d: The email address that is displayed to users.

DefenderSecurityCenterHelpPhone.d: The phone number or Skype ID that is displayed to users.

DefenderSecurityCenterHelpURL.d: The help portal URL this is displayed to users.

DefenderSecurityCenterITContactDisplay.d: Configure where to display IT contact information to end users. Possible values are: notConfigured, displayInAppAndInNotifications, displayOnlyInApp, displayOnlyInNotifications.

DefenderSecurityCenterNotificationsFromA.d: Notifications to show from the displayed areas of app. Possible values are: notConfigured, blockNoncriticalNotifications, blockAllNotifications.

DefenderSecurityCenterOrganizationDispla.d: The company name that is displayed to the users.

DefenderSignatureUpdateIntervalInHours.d: Specifies the interval (in hours) that will be used to check for signatures, so instead of using the ScheduleDay and ScheduleTime the check for new signatures will be set according to the interval. Valid values 0 to 24

DefenderSubmitSamplesConsentType.d: Checks for the user consent level in Windows Defender to send data. Possible values are: sendSafeSamplesAutomatically, alwaysPrompt, neverSend, sendAllSamplesAutomatically.

DefenderUntrustedExecutable.d: Value indicating response to executables that don't meet a prevalence, age, or trusted list criteria. Possible values are: userDefined, enable, auditMode, warn, notConfigured.

DefenderUntrustedExecutableType.d: Value indicating response to executables that don't meet a prevalence, age, or trusted list criteria. Possible values are: userDefined, block, auditMode, warn, disable.

DefenderUntrustedUSBProcess.d: Value indicating response to untrusted and unsigned processes that run from USB. Possible values are: userDefined, enable, auditMode, warn, notConfigured.

DefenderUntrustedUSBProcessType.d: Value indicating response to untrusted and unsigned processes that run from USB. Possible values are: userDefined, block, auditMode, warn, disable.

Description.d: Admin provided description of the Device Configuration.

DeviceGuardEnableSecureBootWithDMA.d: This property will be deprecated in May 2019 and will be replaced with property DeviceGuardSecureBootWithDMA. Specifies whether Platform Security Level is enabled at next reboot.

DeviceGuardLaunchSystemGuard.d: Allows the IT admin to configure the launch of System Guard. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

DeviceGuardLocalSystemAuthorityCredentia.d: Turn on Credential Guard when Platform Security Level with Secure Boot and Virtualization Based Security are both enabled. Possible values are: notConfigured, enableWithUEFILock, enableWithoutUEFILock, disable.

DeviceGuardSecureBootWithDMA.d: Specifies whether Platform Security Level is enabled at next reboot. Possible values are: notConfigured, withoutDMA, withDMA.

DisplayName.d: Admin provided name of the device configuration.

DmaGuardDeviceEnumerationPolicy.d: This policy is intended to provide additional security against external DMA capable devices. It allows for more control over the enumeration of external DMA capable devices incompatible with DMA Remapping/device memory isolation and sandboxing. This policy only takes effect when Kernel DMA Protection is supported and enabled by the system firmware. Kernel DMA Protection is a platform feature that cannot be controlled via policy or by end user. It has to be supported by the system at the time of manufacturing. To check if the system supports Kernel DMA Protection, please check the Kernel DMA Protection field in the Summary page of MSINFO32.exe. Possible values are: deviceDefault, blockAll, allowAll.

FirewallBlockStatefulFTP.d: Blocks stateful FTP connections to the device

FirewallCertificateRevocationListCheckMe.d: Specify how the certificate revocation list is to be enforced. Possible values are: deviceDefault, none, attempt, require.

FirewallIPSecExemptionsAllowDHCP.d: Configures IPSec exemptions to allow both IPv4 and IPv6 DHCP traffic

FirewallIPSecExemptionsAllowICMP.d: Configures IPSec exemptions to allow ICMP

FirewallIPSecExemptionsAllowNeighborDisc.d: Configures IPSec exemptions to allow neighbor discovery IPv6 ICMP type-codes

FirewallIPSecExemptionsAllowRouterDiscov.d: Configures IPSec exemptions to allow router discovery IPv6 ICMP type-codes

FirewallIPSecExemptionsNone.d: Configures IPSec exemptions to no exemptions

FirewallIdleTimeoutForSecurityAssociatio.d: Configures the idle timeout for security associations, in seconds, from 300 to 3600 inclusive. This is the period after which security associations will expire and be deleted. Valid values 300 to 3600

FirewallMergeKeyingModuleSettings.d: If an authentication set is not fully supported by a keying module, direct the module to ignore only unsupported authentication suites rather than the entire set

FirewallPacketQueueingMethod.d: Configures how packet queueing should be applied in the tunnel gateway scenario. Possible values are: deviceDefault, disabled, queueInbound, queueOutbound, queueBoth.

FirewallPreSharedKeyEncodingMethod.d: Select the preshared key encoding to be used. Possible values are: deviceDefault, none, utF8.

FirewallProfileDomain.d: Configures the firewall profile settings for domain networks

FirewallProfilePrivate.d: Configures the firewall profile settings for private networks

FirewallProfilePublic.d: Configures the firewall profile settings for public networks

FirewallRules.d: Configures the firewall rule settings. This collection can contain a maximum of 150 elements.

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

LanManagerAuthenticationLevel.d: This security setting determines which challenge/response authentication protocol is used for network logons. Possible values are: lmAndNltm, lmNtlmAndNtlmV2, lmAndNtlmOnly, lmAndNtlmV2, lmNtlmV2AndNotLm, lmNtlmV2AndNotLmOrNtm.

LanManagerWorkstationDisableInsecureGues.d: If enabled,the SMB client will allow insecure guest logons. If not configured, the SMB client will reject insecure guest logons.

LocalSecurityOptionsAdministratorAccount.d: Define a different account name to be associated with the security identifier (SID) for the account 'Administrator'.

LocalSecurityOptionsAdministratorElevati.d: Define the behavior of the elevation prompt for admins in Admin Approval Mode. Possible values are: notConfigured, elevateWithoutPrompting, promptForCredentialsOnTheSecureDesktop, promptForConsentOnTheSecureDesktop, promptForCredentials, promptForConsent, promptForConsentForNonWindowsBinaries.

LocalSecurityOptionsAllowAnonymousEnumer.d: This security setting determines whether to allows anonymous users to perform certain activities, such as enumerating the names of domain accounts and network shares.

LocalSecurityOptionsAllowPKU2UAuthentica.d: Block PKU2U authentication requests to this device to use online identities.

LocalSecurityOptionsAllowRemoteCallsToSe.d: Edit the default Security Descriptor Definition Language string to allow or deny users and groups to make remote calls to the SAM.

LocalSecurityOptionsAllowSystemToBeShutD.d: This security setting determines whether a computer can be shut down without having to log on to Windows.

LocalSecurityOptionsAllowUIAccessApplica.d: Allow UIAccess apps to prompt for elevation without using the secure desktop.
Allow UIAccess apps to prompt for elevation without using the secure desktop.Default is enabled

LocalSecurityOptionsAllowUndockWithoutHa.d: Prevent a portable computer from being undocked without having to log in.

LocalSecurityOptionsBlockMicrosoftAccoun.d: Prevent users from adding new Microsoft accounts to this computer.

LocalSecurityOptionsBlockRemoteLogonWith.d: Enable Local accounts that are not password protected to log on from locations other than the physical device.Default is enabled

LocalSecurityOptionsBlockRemoteOpticalDr.d: Enabling this settings allows only interactively logged on user to access CD-ROM media.

LocalSecurityOptionsBlockUsersInstalling.d: Restrict installing printer drivers as part of connecting to a shared printer to admins only.

LocalSecurityOptionsClearVirtualMemoryPa.d: This security setting determines whether the virtual memory pagefile is cleared when the system is shut down.

LocalSecurityOptionsClientDigitallySignC.d: This security setting determines whether packet signing is required by the SMB client component.

LocalSecurityOptionsClientSendUnencrypte.d: If this security setting is enabled, the Server Message Block (SMB) redirector is allowed to send plaintext passwords to non-Microsoft SMB servers that do not support password encryption during authentication.

LocalSecurityOptionsDetectApplicationIns.d: App installations requiring elevated privileges will prompt for admin credentials.Default is enabled

LocalSecurityOptionsDisableAdministrator.d: Determines whether the Local Administrator account is enabled or disabled.

LocalSecurityOptionsDisableClientDigital.d: This security setting determines whether the SMB client attempts to negotiate SMB packet signing.

LocalSecurityOptionsDisableGuestAccount.d: Determines if the Guest account is enabled or disabled.

LocalSecurityOptionsDisableServerDigital.d: This security setting determines whether packet signing is required by the SMB server component.
This security setting determines whether the SMB server will negotiate SMB packet signing with clients that request it.

LocalSecurityOptionsDoNotAllowAnonymousE.d: This security setting determines what additional permissions will be granted for anonymous connections to the computer.

LocalSecurityOptionsDoNotRequireCtrlAltD.d: Require CTRL+ALT+DEL to be pressed before a user can log on.

LocalSecurityOptionsDoNotStoreLANManager.d: This security setting determines if, at the next password change, the LAN Manager (LM) hash value for the new password is stored. Its not stored by default.

LocalSecurityOptionsFormatAndEjectOfRemo.d: Define who is allowed to format and eject removable NTFS media. Possible values are: notConfigured, administrators, administratorsAndPowerUsers, administratorsAndInteractiveUsers.

LocalSecurityOptionsGuestAccountName.d: Define a different account name to be associated with the security identifier (SID) for the account 'Guest'.

LocalSecurityOptionsHideLastSignedInUser.d: Do not display the username of the last person who signed in on this device.

LocalSecurityOptionsHideUsernameAtSignIn.d: Do not display the username of the person signing in to this device after credentials are entered and before the devices desktop is shown.

LocalSecurityOptionsInformationDisplayed.d: Configure the user information that is displayed when the session is locked. If not configured, user display name, domain and username are shown. Possible values are: notConfigured, administrators, administratorsAndPowerUsers, administratorsAndInteractiveUsers.

LocalSecurityOptionsInformationShownOnLo.d: Configure the user information that is displayed when the session is locked. If not configured, user display name, domain and username are shown. Possible values are: notConfigured, userDisplayNameDomainUser, userDisplayNameOnly, doNotDisplayUser.

LocalSecurityOptionsLogOnMessageText.d: Set message text for users attempting to log in.

LocalSecurityOptionsLogOnMessageTitle.d: Set message title for users attempting to log in.

LocalSecurityOptionsMachineInactivityLim.d: Define maximum minutes of inactivity on the interactive desktops login screen until the screen saver runs. Valid values 0 to 9999
Define maximum minutes of inactivity on the interactive desktops login screen until the screen saver runs. Valid values 0 to 9999

LocalSecurityOptionsMinimumSessionSecuri.d: This security setting allows a client to require the negotiation of 128-bit encryption and/or NTLMv2 session security. Possible values are: none, requireNtmlV2SessionSecurity, require128BitEncryption, ntlmV2And128BitEncryption.

This security setting allows a server to require the negotiation of 128-bit encryption and/or NTLMv2 session security. Possible values are: none, requireNtmlV2SessionSecurity, require128BitEncryption, ntlmV2And128BitEncryption.

LocalSecurityOptionsOnlyElevateSignedExe.d: Enforce PKI certification path validation for a given executable file before it is permitted to run.

LocalSecurityOptionsRestrictAnonymousAcc.d: By default, this security setting restricts anonymous access to shares and pipes to the settings for named pipes that can be accessed anonymously and Shares that can be accessed anonymously

LocalSecurityOptionsSmartCardRemovalBeha.d: This security setting determines what happens when the smart card for a logged-on user is removed from the smart card reader. Possible values are: noAction, lockWorkstation, forceLogoff, disconnectRemoteDesktopSession.

LocalSecurityOptionsStandardUserElevatio.d: Define the behavior of the elevation prompt for standard users. Possible values are: notConfigured, automaticallyDenyElevationRequests, promptForCredentialsOnTheSecureDesktop, promptForCredentials.

LocalSecurityOptionsSwitchToSecureDeskto.d: Enable all elevation requests to go to the interactive user's desktop rather than the secure desktop. Prompt behavior policy settings for admins and standard users are used.

LocalSecurityOptionsUseAdminApprovalMode.d: Defines whether the built-in admin account uses Admin Approval Mode or runs all apps with full admin privileges.Default is enabled
Define whether Admin Approval Mode and all UAC policy settings are enabled, default is enabled

LocalSecurityOptionsVirtualizeFileAndReg.d: Virtualize file and registry write failures to per user locations

RoleScopeTagIds.d: List of Scope Tags for this Entity instance.

SmartScreenBlockOverrideForFiles.d: Allows IT Admins to control whether users can can ignore SmartScreen warnings and run malicious files.

SmartScreenEnableInShell.d: Allows IT Admins to configure SmartScreen for Windows.

UserRightsAccessCredentialManagerAsTrust.d: This user right is used by Credential Manager during Backup/Restore. Users' saved credentials might be compromised if this privilege is given to other entities. Only states NotConfigured and Allowed are supported

UserRightsActAsPartOfTheOperatingSystem.d: This user right allows a process to impersonate any user without authentication. The process can therefore gain access to the same local resources as that user. Only states NotConfigured and Allowed are supported

UserRightsAllowAccessFromNetwork.d: This user right determines which users and groups are allowed to connect to the computer over the network. State Allowed is supported.

UserRightsBackupData.d: This user right determines which users can bypass file, directory, registry, and other persistent objects permissions when backing up files and directories. Only states NotConfigured and Allowed are supported

UserRightsBlockAccessFromNetwork.d: This user right determines which users and groups are block from connecting to the computer over the network. State Block is supported.

UserRightsChangeSystemTime.d: This user right determines which users and groups can change the time and date on the internal clock of the computer. Only states NotConfigured and Allowed are supported

UserRightsCreateGlobalObjects.d: This security setting determines whether users can create global objects that are available to all sessions. Users who can create global objects could affect processes that run under other users' sessions, which could lead to application failure or data corruption. Only states NotConfigured and Allowed are supported

UserRightsCreatePageFile.d: This user right determines which users and groups can call an internal API to create and change the size of a page file. Only states NotConfigured and Allowed are supported

UserRightsCreatePermanentSharedObjects.d: This user right determines which accounts can be used by processes to create a directory object using the object manager. Only states NotConfigured and Allowed are supported

UserRightsCreateSymbolicLinks.d: This user right determines if the user can create a symbolic link from the computer to which they are logged on. Only states NotConfigured and Allowed are supported

UserRightsCreateToken.d: This user right determines which users/groups can be used by processes to create a token that can then be used to get access to any local resources when the process uses an internal API to create an access token. Only states NotConfigured and Allowed are supported

UserRightsDebugPrograms.d: This user right determines which users can attach a debugger to any process or to the kernel. Only states NotConfigured and Allowed are supported

UserRightsDelegation.d: This user right determines which users can set the Trusted for Delegation setting on a user or computer object. Only states NotConfigured and Allowed are supported.

UserRightsDenyLocalLogOn.d: This user right determines which users cannot log on to the computer. States NotConfigured, Blocked are supported

UserRightsGenerateSecurityAudits.d: This user right determines which accounts can be used by a process to add entries to the security log. The security log is used to trace unauthorized system access. Only states NotConfigured and Allowed are supported.

UserRightsImpersonateClient.d: Assigning this user right to a user allows programs running on behalf of that user to impersonate a client. Requiring this user right for this kind of impersonation prevents an unauthorized user from convincing a client to connect to a service that they have created and then impersonating that client, which can elevate the unauthorized user's permissions to administrative or system levels. Only states NotConfigured and Allowed are supported.

UserRightsIncreaseSchedulingPriority.d: This user right determines which accounts can use a process with Write Property access to another process to increase the execution priority assigned to the other process. Only states NotConfigured and Allowed are supported.

UserRightsLoadUnloadDrivers.d: This user right determines which users can dynamically load and unload device drivers or other code in to kernel mode. Only states NotConfigured and Allowed are supported.

UserRightsLocalLogOn.d: This user right determines which users can log on to the computer. States NotConfigured, Allowed are supported

UserRightsLockMemory.d: This user right determines which accounts can use a process to keep data in physical memory, which prevents the system from paging the data to virtual memory on disk. Only states NotConfigured and Allowed are supported.

UserRightsManageAuditingAndSecurityLogs.d: This user right determines which users can specify object access auditing options for individual resources, such as files, Active Directory objects, and registry keys. Only states NotConfigured and Allowed are supported.

UserRightsManageVolumes.d: This user right determines which users and groups can run maintenance tasks on a volume, such as remote defragmentation. Only states NotConfigured and Allowed are supported.

UserRightsModifyFirmwareEnvironment.d: This user right determines who can modify firmware environment values. Only states NotConfigured and Allowed are supported.

UserRightsModifyObjectLabels.d: This user right determines which user accounts can modify the integrity label of objects, such as files, registry keys, or processes owned by other users. Only states NotConfigured and Allowed are supported.

UserRightsProfileSingleProcess.d: This user right determines which users can use performance monitoring tools to monitor the performance of system processes. Only states NotConfigured and Allowed are supported.

UserRightsRemoteDesktopServicesLogOn.d: This user right determines which users and groups are prohibited from logging on as a Remote Desktop Services client. Only states NotConfigured and Blocked are supported

UserRightsRemoteShutdown.d: This user right determines which users are allowed to shut down a computer from a remote location on the network. Misuse of this user right can result in a denial of service. Only states NotConfigured and Allowed are supported.

UserRightsRestoreData.d: This user right determines which users can bypass file, directory, registry, and other persistent objects permissions when restoring backed up files and directories, and determines which users can set any valid security principal as the owner of an object. Only states NotConfigured and Allowed are supported.

UserRightsTakeOwnership.d: This user right determines which users can take ownership of any securable object in the system, including Active Directory objects, files and folders, printers, registry keys, processes, and threads. Only states NotConfigured and Allowed are supported.

WindowsDefenderTamperProtection.d: Configure windows defender TamperProtection settings. Possible values are: notConfigured, enable, disable.

XboxServicesAccessoryManagementServiceSt.d: This setting determines whether the Accessory management service's start type is Automatic(2), Manual(3), Disabled(4). Default: Manual. Possible values are: manual, automatic, disabled.

XboxServicesEnableXboxGameSaveTask.d: This setting determines whether xbox game save is enabled (1) or disabled (0).

XboxServicesLiveAuthManagerServiceStartu.d: This setting determines whether Live Auth Manager service's start type is Automatic(2), Manual(3), Disabled(4). Default: Manual. Possible values are: manual, automatic, disabled.

XboxServicesLiveGameSaveServiceStartupMo.d: This setting determines whether Live Game save service's start type is Automatic(2), Manual(3), Disabled(4). Default: Manual. Possible values are: manual, automatic, disabled.

XboxServicesLiveNetworkingServiceStartup.d: This setting determines whether Networking service's start type is Automatic(2), Manual(3), Disabled(4). Default: Manual. Possible values are: manual, automatic, disabled.

IntuneDeviceConfigurationFirmwareInterfa.d: This resource configures an Intune Device Configuration Firmware Interface Policy for Windows10.

Assignments.d: Represents the assignment to the Intune policy.

Bluetooth.d: Defines whether a user is allowed to enable Bluetooth. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

BootFromBuiltInNetworkAdapters.d: Defines whether a user is allowed to boot from built-in network adapters. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

BootFromExternalMedia.d: Defines whether a user is allowed to boot from external media. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

Cameras.d: Defines whether built-in cameras are enabled. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

ChangeUefiSettingsPermission.d: Defines the permission level granted to users to change UEFI settings. Possible values are: notConfiguredOnly, none.

Description.d: Admin provided description of the Device Configuration.

DisplayName.d: Admin provided name of the device configuration.

FrontCamera.d: Defines whether a user is allowed to enable Front Camera. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

InfraredCamera.d: Defines whether a user is allowed to enable Infrared camera. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

Microphone.d: Defines whether a user is allowed to enable Microphone. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

MicrophonesAndSpeakers.d: Defines whether built-in microphones or speakers are enabled. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

NearFieldCommunication.d: Defines whether a user is allowed to enable Near Field Communication. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

Radios.d: Defines whether built-in radios e.g. WIFI, NFC, Bluetooth, are enabled. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

RearCamera.d: Defines whether a user is allowed to enable rear camera. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

RoleScopeTagIds.d: List of Scope Tags for this Entity instance.

SdCard.d: Defines whether a user is allowed to enable SD Card Port. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

SimultaneousMultiThreading.d: Defines whether a user is allowed to enable Simultaneous MultiThreading. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

UsbTypeAPort.d: Defines whether a user is allowed to enable USB Type A Port. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

VirtualizationOfCpuAndIO.d: Defines whether CPU and IO virtualization is enabled. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

WakeOnLAN.d: Defines whether a user is allowed to enable Wake on LAN. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

WakeOnPower.d: Defines whether a user is allowed to enable Wake On Power. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

WiFi.d: Defines whether a user is allowed to enable WiFi. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

WindowsPlatformBinaryTable.d: Defines whether a user is allowed to enable Windows Platform Binary Table. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

WirelessWideAreaNetwork.d: Defines whether a user is allowed to enable Wireless Wide Area Network. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

IntuneDeviceConfigurationHealthMonitorin.d: This resource configures an Intune Device Configuration Health Monitoring Configuration Policy for Windows10.

AllowDeviceHealthMonitoring.d: Enables device health monitoring on the device. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

Assignments.d: Represents the assignment to the Intune policy.

ConfigDeviceHealthMonitoringCustomScope.d: Specifies custom set of events collected from the device where health monitoring is enabled

ConfigDeviceHealthMonitoringScope.d: Specifies set of events collected from the device where health monitoring is enabled. Possible values are: undefined, healthMonitoring, bootPerformance, windowsUpdates, privilegeManagement.

Description.d: Admin provided description of the Device Configuration.

DisplayName.d: Admin provided name of the device configuration.

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

RoleScopeTagIds.d: List of Scope Tags for this Entity instance.

IntuneDeviceConfigurationIdentityProtect.d: This resource configures an Intune Device Configuration Identity Protection Policy for Windows10.

Assignments.d: Represents the assignment to the Intune policy.

Description.d: Admin provided description of the Device Configuration.

DisplayName.d: Admin provided name of the device configuration.

EnhancedAntiSpoofingForFacialFeaturesEna.d: Boolean value used to enable enhanced anti-spoofing for facial feature recognition on Windows Hello face authentication.

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

PinExpirationInDays.d: Integer value specifies the period (in days) that a PIN can be used before the system requires the user to change it. Valid values are 0 to 730 inclusive. Valid values 0 to 730

PinLowercaseCharactersUsage.d: This value configures the use of lowercase characters in the Windows Hello for Business PIN. Possible values are: blocked, required, allowed, notConfigured.

PinMaximumLength.d: Integer value that sets the maximum number of characters allowed for the work PIN. Valid values are 4 to 127 inclusive and greater than or equal to the value set for the minimum PIN. Valid values 4 to 127

PinMinimumLength.d: Integer value that sets the minimum number of characters required for the Windows Hello for Business PIN. Valid values are 4 to 127 inclusive and less than or equal to the value set for the maximum PIN. Valid values 4 to 127

PinPreviousBlockCount.d: Controls the ability to prevent users from using past PINs. This must be set between 0 and 50, inclusive, and the current PIN of the user is included in that count. If set to 0, previous PINs are not stored. PIN history is not preserved through a PIN reset. Valid values 0 to 50

PinRecoveryEnabled.d: Boolean value that enables a user to change their PIN by using the Windows Hello for Business PIN recovery service.

PinSpecialCharactersUsage.d: Controls the ability to use special characters in the Windows Hello for Business PIN. Possible values are: blocked, required, allowed, notConfigured.

PinUppercaseCharactersUsage.d: This value configures the use of uppercase characters in the Windows Hello for Business PIN. Possible values are: blocked, required, allowed, notConfigured.

RoleScopeTagIds.d: List of Scope Tags for this Entity instance.

SecurityDeviceRequired.d: Controls whether to require a Trusted Platform Module (TPM) for provisioning Windows Hello for Business. A TPM provides an additional security benefit in that data stored on it cannot be used on other devices. If set to False, all devices can provision Windows Hello for Business even if there is not a usable TPM.

UnlockWithBiometricsEnabled.d: Controls the use of biometric gestures, such as face and fingerprint, as an alternative to the Windows Hello for Business PIN. If set to False, biometric gestures are not allowed. Users must still configure a PIN as a backup in case of failures.

UseCertificatesForOnPremisesAuthEnabled.d: Boolean value that enables Windows Hello for Business to use certificates to authenticate on-premise resources.

UseSecurityKeyForSignin.d: Boolean value used to enable the Windows Hello security key as a logon credential.

WindowsHelloForBusinessBlocked.d: Boolean value that blocks Windows Hello for Business as a method for signing into Windows.

IntuneDeviceConfigurationImportedPfxCert.d: This resource configures an Intune Device Configuration Imported Pfx Certificate Policy for Windows10.

Assignments.d: Represents the assignment to the Intune policy.

CertificateValidityPeriodScale.d: Scale for the Certificate Validity Period. Possible values are: days, months, years.

CertificateValidityPeriodValue.d: Value for the Certificate Validity Period

Description.d: Admin provided description of the Device Configuration.

DisplayName.d: Admin provided name of the device configuration.

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

IntendedPurpose.d: Intended Purpose of the Certificate Profile - which could be Unassigned, SmimeEncryption, SmimeSigning etc. Possible values are: unassigned, smimeEncryption, smimeSigning, vpn, wifi.

KeyStorageProvider.d: Key Storage Provider (KSP). Possible values are: useTpmKspOtherwiseUseSoftwareKsp, useTpmKspOtherwiseFail, usePassportForWorkKspOtherwiseFail, useSoftwareKsp.

RenewalThresholdPercentage.d: Certificate renewal threshold percentage. Valid values 1 to 99

RoleScopeTagIds.d: List of Scope Tags for this Entity instance.

SubjectAlternativeNameType.d: Certificate Subject Alternative Name Type. Possible values are: none, emailAddress, userPrincipalName, customAzureADAttribute, domainNameService, universalResourceIdentifier.

SubjectNameFormat.d: Certificate Subject Name Format. Possible values are: commonName, commonNameIncludingEmail, commonNameAsEmail, custom, commonNameAsIMEI, commonNameAsSerialNumber, commonNameAsAadDeviceId, commonNameAsIntuneDeviceId, commonNameAsDurableDeviceId.

IntuneDeviceConfigurationKioskPolicyWind.d: This resource configures an Intune Device Configuration Kiosk Policy for Windows10.

Assignments.d: Represents the assignment to the Intune policy.

Description.d: Admin provided description of the Device Configuration.

DisplayName.d: Admin provided name of the device configuration.

EdgeKioskEnablePublicBrowsing.d: Enable public browsing kiosk mode for the Microsoft Edge browser. The Default is false.

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

KioskBrowserBlockedURLs.d: Specify URLs that the kiosk browsers should not navigate to

KioskBrowserBlockedUrlExceptions.d: Specify URLs that the kiosk browser is allowed to navigate to

KioskBrowserDefaultUrl.d: Specify the default URL the browser should navigate to on launch.

KioskBrowserEnableEndSessionButton.d: Enable the kiosk browser's end session button. By default, the end session button is disabled.

KioskBrowserEnableHomeButton.d: Enable the kiosk browser's home button. By default, the home button is disabled.

KioskBrowserEnableNavigationButtons.d: Enable the kiosk browser's navigation buttons(forward/back). By default, the navigation buttons are disabled.

KioskBrowserRestartOnIdleTimeInMinutes.d: Specify the number of minutes the session is idle until the kiosk browser restarts in a fresh state. Valid values are 1-1440. Valid values 1 to 1440

KioskProfiles.d: This policy setting allows to define a list of Kiosk profiles for a Kiosk configuration. This collection can contain a maximum of 3 elements.

RoleScopeTagIds.d: List of Scope Tags for this Entity instance.

WindowsKioskForceUpdateSchedule.d: force update schedule for Kiosk devices.

IntuneDeviceConfigurationNetworkBoundary.d: This resource configures an Intune Device Configuration Network Boundary Policy for Windows10.

Assignments.d: Represents the assignment to the Intune policy.

Description.d: Admin provided description of the Device Configuration.

DisplayName.d: Admin provided name of the device configuration.

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

RoleScopeTagIds.d: List of Scope Tags for this Entity instance.

IntuneDeviceConfigurationPkcsCertificate.d: This resource configures an Intune Device Configuration Pkcs Certificate Policy for Windows10.

Assignments.d: Represents the assignment to the Intune policy.

CertificateStore.d: Target store certificate. Possible values are: user, machine.

CertificateValidityPeriodScale.d: Scale for the Certificate Validity Period. Possible values are: days, months, years.

CertificateValidityPeriodValue.d: Value for the Certificate Validity Period

CustomSubjectAlternativeNames.d: Custom Subject Alternative Name Settings. This collection can contain a maximum of 500 elements.

Description.d: Admin provided description of the Device Configuration.

DisplayName.d: Admin provided name of the device configuration.

ExtendedKeyUsages.d: Extended Key Usage (EKU) settings. This collection can contain a maximum of 500 elements.

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

KeyStorageProvider.d: Key Storage Provider (KSP). Possible values are: useTpmKspOtherwiseUseSoftwareKsp, useTpmKspOtherwiseFail, usePassportForWorkKspOtherwiseFail, useSoftwareKsp.

RenewalThresholdPercentage.d: Certificate renewal threshold percentage. Valid values 1 to 99

RoleScopeTagIds.d: List of Scope Tags for this Entity instance.

SubjectAlternativeNameFormatString.d: Custom String that defines the AAD Attribute.

SubjectAlternativeNameType.d: Certificate Subject Alternative Name Type. Possible values are: none, emailAddress, userPrincipalName, customAzureADAttribute, domainNameService, universalResourceIdentifier.

SubjectNameFormat.d: Certificate Subject Name Format. Possible values are: commonName, commonNameIncludingEmail, commonNameAsEmail, custom, commonNameAsIMEI, commonNameAsSerialNumber, commonNameAsAadDeviceId, commonNameAsIntuneDeviceId, commonNameAsDurableDeviceId.

SubjectNameFormatString.d: Custom format to use with SubjectNameFormat = Custom. Example: {{CN=EmailAddress,E=EmailAddress}},OU=Enterprise Users,O=Contoso Corporation,L=Redmond,ST=WA,C=US

IntuneDeviceConfigurationPlatformScriptL.d: This resource configures an Intune Device Configuration Platform Script for Linux.

Assignments.d: Represents the assignment to the Intune policy.

CustomConfigExecutionFrequency.d: Execution frequency (15: Every 15 minutes, 30: Every 30 minutes, 60: Every 1 hour, 120: Every 2 hours, 180: Every 3 hours, 360: Every 6 hours, 720: Every 12 hours, 1440: Every 1 day, 10080: Every 1 week)

CustomConfigExecutionRetries.d: Execution retries (0: No retries, 1: 1 time, 2: 2 times, 3: 3 times)

Description.d: Policy description

DisplayName.d: Policy name

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

RoleScopeTagIds.d: List of Scope Tags for this Entity instance.

IntuneDeviceConfigurationPlatformScriptM.d: This resource configures an Intune Device Configuration Platform Script MacOS.

Assignments.d: Represents the assignment to the Intune policy.

BlockExecutionNotifications.d: Does not notify the user a script is being executed

Description.d: Optional description for the device management script.

DisplayName.d: Name of the device management script.

ExecutionFrequency.d: The interval for script to run. If not defined the script will run once

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

RetryCount.d: Number of times for the script to be retried if it fails

RoleScopeTagIds.d: List of Scope Tag IDs for this PowerShellScript instance.

RunAsAccount.d: Indicates the type of execution context. Possible values are: system, user.

IntuneDeviceConfigurationPlatformScriptW.d: This resource configures an Intune Device Configuration Platform Script Windows.

Assignments.d: Represents the assignment to the Intune policy.

Description.d: Optional description for the device management script.

DisplayName.d: Name of the device management script.

EnforceSignatureCheck.d: Indicate whether the script signature needs be checked.

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

RoleScopeTagIds.d: List of Scope Tag IDs for this PowerShellScript instance.

RunAs32Bit.d: A value indicating whether the PowerShell script should run as 32-bit

RunAsAccount.d: Indicates the type of execution context. Possible values are: system, user.

IntuneDeviceConfigurationPolicyAndroidDe.d: This resource configures an Intune Device Configuration Policy Android Device Owner.

AccountsBlockModification.d: Block modification of accounts. Only supported on Dedicated devices.

AppsAllowInstallFromUnknownSources.d: When allowed, users can enable the 'unknown sources' setting to install apps from sources other than the Google Play Store.

AppsAutoUpdatePolicy.d: Devices check for app updates daily. The default behavior is to let device users decide. They'll be able to set their preferences in the managed Google Play app.

AppsDefaultPermissionPolicy.d: Define the default permission policy for requests for runtime permissions.

AppsRecommendSkippingFirstUseHints.d: Enable a suggestion to apps that they skip their user tutorials and any introductory hints when they first start up, if applicable.

Assignments.d: Represents the assignment to the Intune policy.

AzureAdSharedDeviceDataClearApps.d: A list of managed apps that will have their data cleared during a global sign-out in AAD shared device mode. This collection can contain a maximum of 500 elements.

BluetoothBlockContactSharing.d: Block access to work contacts from another device such as a car system when an Android device is paired via Bluetooth.

CameraBlocked.d: Block all cameras on the device

CellularBlockWiFiTethering.d: Block tethering and access to portable hotspots.

CertificateCredentialConfigurationDisabl.d: Blocks users from making any changes to credentials associated with certificates associated with certificates assigned to them.

CrossProfilePoliciesAllowCopyPaste.d: Indicates whether or not text copied from one profile (personal or work) can be pasted in the other.

CrossProfilePoliciesAllowDataSharing.d: Indicates whether data from one profile (personal or work) can be shared with apps in the other profile.

CrossProfilePoliciesShowWorkContactsInPe.d: Indicates whether or not contacts stored in work profile are shown in personal profile contact searches/incoming calls.

DateTimeConfigurationBlocked.d: Block user from manually setting the date and time.

Description.d: The description of the policy.

DetailedHelpText.d: Represents the customized detailed help text provided to users when they attempt to modify managed settings on their device.

DeviceOwnerLockScreenMessage.d: Represents the customized lock screen message provided to users when they attempt to modify managed settings on their device.

DisplayName.d: The display name of the policy.

FactoryResetDeviceAdministratorEmails.d: Email addresses of device admins for factory reset protection. When a device is factory reset, it will require that one of these admins log in with their Google account to unlock the device. If none are specified, factory reset protection is not enabled.

GlobalProxy.d: Proxy is set up directly with host, port and excluded hosts.

GoogleAccountsBlocked.d: Blocking prevents users from adding their personal Google account to their device.

Id.d: The Id of the policy.

KioskCustomizationDeviceSettingsBlocked.d: Indicates whether a user can access the device's Settings app while in Kiosk Mode.

KioskCustomizationPowerButtonActionsBloc.d: Whether the power menu is shown when a user long presses the Power button of a device in Kiosk Mode.

KioskCustomizationStatusBar.d: Indicates whether system info and notifications are disabled in Kiosk Mode

KioskCustomizationSystemErrorWarnings.d: Indicates whether system error dialogs for crashed or unresponsive apps are shown in Kiosk Mode.

KioskCustomizationSystemNavigation.d: Indicates which navigation features are enabled in Kiosk Mode.

KioskModeAppOrderEnabled.d: Whether or not to enable app ordering in Kiosk Mode.

KioskModeAppPositions.d: The ordering of items on Kiosk Mode Managed Home Screen. This collection can contain a maximum of 500 elements.

KioskModeApps.d: A list of managed apps that will be shown when the device is in Kiosk Mode. This collection can contain a maximum of 500 elements.

KioskModeAppsInFolderOrderedByName.d: Whether or not to alphabetize applications within a folder in Kiosk Mode.

KioskModeBluetoothConfigurationEnabled.d: Enable end-users to configure and pair devices over Bluetooth.

KioskModeDebugMenuEasyAccessEnabled.d: Whether or not to allow a user to easy access to the debug menu in Kiosk Mode

KioskModeExitCode.d: The 4-6 digit PIN will be the code an IT administrator enters on a multi-app dedicated device to pause kiosk mode.

KioskModeFlashlightConfigurationEnabled.d: Whether or not to allow a user to use the flashlight in Kiosk Mode.

KioskModeFolderIcon.d: Folder icon configuration for managed home screen in Kiosk Mode.

KioskModeGridHeight.d: Number of rows for Managed Home Screen grid with app ordering enabled in Kiosk Mode. Valid values 1 to 9999999.

KioskModeGridWidth.d: Number of columns for Managed Home Screen grid with app ordering enabled in Kiosk Mode. Valid values 1 to 9999999.

KioskModeIconSize.d: Icon size configuration for managed home screen in Kiosk Mode.

KioskModeLockHomeScreen.d: Whether or not to lock home screen to the end user in Kiosk Mode.

KioskModeManagedFolders.d: A list of managed folders for a device in Kiosk Mode. This collection can contain a maximum of 500 elements.

KioskModeManagedHomeScreenAutoSignout.d: Whether or not to automatically sign-out of MHS and Shared device mode applications after inactive for Managed Home Screen.

KioskModeManagedHomeScreenInactiveSignOu.d: Number of seconds to give user notice before automatically signing them out for Managed Home Screen. Valid values 0 to 9999999.
Number of seconds device is inactive before automatically signing user out for Managed Home Screen. Valid values 0 to 9999999.

KioskModeManagedHomeScreenPinComplexity.d: Complexity of PIN for sign-in session for Managed Home Screen.

KioskModeManagedHomeScreenPinRequired.d: Whether or not require user to set a PIN for sign-in session for Managed Home Screen.

KioskModeManagedHomeScreenPinRequiredToR.d: Whether or not required user to enter session PIN if screensaver has appeared for Managed Home Screen.

KioskModeManagedHomeScreenSignInBackgrou.d: Custom URL background for sign-in screen for Managed Home Screen.

KioskModeManagedHomeScreenSignInBranding.d: Custom URL branding logo for sign-in screen and session pin page for Managed Home Screen.

KioskModeManagedHomeScreenSignInEnabled.d: Whether or not show sign-in screen for Managed Home Screen.

KioskModeManagedSettingsEntryDisabled.d: Whether or not to use single app kiosk mode or multi-app kiosk mode.

KioskModeMediaVolumeConfigurationEnabled.d: Whether or not to allow a user to change the media volume in Kiosk Mode.

KioskModeScreenOrientation.d: Screen orientation configuration for managed home screen in Kiosk Mode.

KioskModeScreenSaverConfigurationEnabled.d: Start screen saver when the device screen times out or locks.

KioskModeScreenSaverDetectMediaDisabled.d: Whether or not the device screen should show the screen saver if audio/video is playing in Kiosk Mode.

KioskModeScreenSaverDisplayTimeInSeconds.d: The number of seconds that the device will display the screen saver for in Kiosk Mode. Valid values 0 to 9999999

KioskModeScreenSaverImageUrl.d: URL for an image that will be the device's screen saver in Kiosk Mode.

KioskModeScreenSaverStartDelayInSeconds.d: The number of seconds the device needs to be inactive for before the screen saver is shown in Kiosk Mode. Valid values 1 to 9999999

KioskModeShowAppNotificationBadge.d: Whether or not to display application notification badges in Kiosk Mode.

KioskModeShowDeviceInfo.d: Whether or not to allow a user to access basic device information.

KioskModeUseManagedHomeScreenApp.d: Whether or not to use single app kiosk mode or multi-app kiosk mode.

KioskModeVirtualHomeButtonEnabled.d: Enable IT administrators to temporarily leave multi-app kiosk mode to make changes on the device.

KioskModeVirtualHomeButtonType.d: Enable a soft-key button that returns users to the Managed Home Screen. Choose between a persistent, floating button or a button activated by a swipe-up gesture.

KioskModeWallpaperUrl.d: Customize the appearance of the screen background for assigned groups.

KioskModeWiFiConfigurationEnabled.d: Enable end-users to connect to different Wi-Fi networks.

KioskModeWifiAllowedSsids.d: The restricted set of WIFI SSIDs available for the user to configure in Kiosk Mode. This collection can contain a maximum of 500 elements.

MicrophoneForceMute.d: Block unmuting the microphone and adjusting the microphone volume.

MicrosoftLauncherConfigurationEnabled.d: Indicates whether or not to you want configure Microsoft Launcher.

MicrosoftLauncherCustomWallpaperAllowUse.d: Indicates whether or not the user can modify the wallpaper to personalize their device.

MicrosoftLauncherCustomWallpaperEnabled.d: Indicates whether or not to configure the wallpaper on the targeted devices.

MicrosoftLauncherCustomWallpaperImageUrl.d: Indicates the URL for the image file to use as the wallpaper on the targeted devices.

MicrosoftLauncherDockPresenceAllowUserMo.d: Indicates whether or not the user can modify the device dock configuration on the device.

MicrosoftLauncherDockPresenceConfigurati.d: Indicates whether or not you want to configure the device dock.

MicrosoftLauncherFeedAllowUserModificati.d: Indicates whether or not the user can modify the launcher feed on the device.

MicrosoftLauncherFeedEnabled.d: Indicates whether or not the user can modify the launcher feed on the device.

MicrosoftLauncherSearchBarPlacementConfi.d: Indicates whether or not you want to configure the device dock.

NetworkEscapeHatchAllowed.d: Whether the network escape hatch is enabled. If a network connection can't be made at boot time, the escape hatch prompts the user to temporarily connect to a network in order to refresh the device policy. After applying policy, the temporary network will be forgotten and the device will continue booting. This prevents being unable to connect to a network if there is no suitable network in the last policy and the device boots into an app in lock task mode, or the user is otherwise unable to reach device settings.

NfcBlockOutgoingBeam.d: Block usage of NFC to beam data from apps.

PasswordBlockKeyguardFeatures.d: These features are accessible to users when the device is locked. Users will not be able to see or access disabled features.

PasswordExpirationDays.d: Number of days until device password must be changed. (1-365)

PasswordMinimumLength.d: Indicates the minimum length of the password required on the device. Valid values 4 to 16

PasswordMinimumLetterCharacters.d: Indicates the minimum number of letter characters required for device password. Valid values 1 to 16

PasswordMinimumLowerCaseCharacters.d: Indicates the minimum number of lower case characters required for device password. Valid values 1 to 16

PasswordMinimumNonLetterCharacters.d: Indicates the minimum number of non-letter characters required for device password. Valid values 1 to 16

PasswordMinimumNumericCharacters.d: Indicates the minimum number of numeric characters required for device password. Valid values 1 to 16

PasswordMinimumSymbolCharacters.d: Indicates the minimum number of symbol characters required for device password. Valid values 1 to 16

PasswordMinimumUpperCaseCharacters.d: Indicates the minimum number of upper case letter characters required for device password. Valid values 1 to 16

PasswordMinutesOfInactivityBeforeScreenT.d: Maximum time after which the device will lock. Can disable screen lock as well so that it never times out.

PasswordPreviousPasswordCountToBlock.d: Enter the number of unique passwords required before a user can reuse an old one. (1-24)

PasswordRequireUnlock.d: Indicates the timeout period after which a device must be unlocked using a form of strong authentication.

PasswordRequiredType.d: Set the password's complexity requirements. Additional password requirements will become available based on your selection.

PasswordSignInFailureCountBeforeFactoryR.d: Number of consecutive times an incorrect password can be entered before device is wiped of all data. (4-11)

PersonalProfileAppsAllowInstallFromUnkno.d: Indicates whether the user can install apps from unknown sources on the personal profile.

PersonalProfileCameraBlocked.d: Indicates whether to disable the use of the camera on the personal profile.

PersonalProfilePersonalApplications.d: Policy applied to applications in the personal profile. This collection can contain a maximum of 500 elements.

PersonalProfilePlayStoreMode.d: Used together with PersonalProfilePersonalApplications to control how apps in the personal profile are allowed or blocked

PersonalProfileScreenCaptureBlocked.d: Indicates whether to disable the capability to take screenshots on the personal profile.

PlayStoreMode.d: Users get access to all apps, except the ones you've required uninstall in Client Apps. If you choose 'Not configured' for this setting, users can only access the apps you've listed as available or required in Client Apps.

RoleScopeTagIds.d: List of Scope Tags for this Entity instance.

SecurityCommonCriteriaModeEnabled.d: Represents the security common criteria mode enabled provided to users when they attempt to modify managed settings on their device.

SecurityDeveloperSettingsEnabled.d: Indicates whether or not the user is allowed to access developer settings like developer options and safe boot on the device.

SecurityRequireVerifyApps.d: Enable Google Play Protect to scan apps before and after they're installed. If it detects a threat, it might warn the user to remove the app from the device. Required by default.

ShortHelpText.d: Represents the customized short help text provided to users when they attempt to modify managed settings on their device.

StatusBarBlocked.d: Block access to the status bar, including notifications and quick settings.

StayOnModes.d: The battery plugged in modes for which the device stays on. When using this setting, it is recommended to clear the Time to lock screen setting so that the device doesn't lock itself while it stays on.

StorageBlockUsbFileTransfer.d: Block transfer of files over USB.

SystemUpdateFreezePeriods.d: Indicates the annually repeating time periods during which system updates are postponed. This collection can contain a maximum of 500 elements.

SystemUpdateInstallType.d: When over-the-air updates are available for this device, they will be installed based on this policy.?

SystemUpdateWindowEndMinutesAfterMidnigh.d: End of the maintenance window in the device's time zone.?

SystemUpdateWindowStartMinutesAfterMidni.d: Beginning of the maintenance window in the device's time zone.?

SystemWindowsBlocked.d: Disable window notifications such as toasts, incoming calls, outgoing calls, system alerts, and system errors.?

UsersBlockAdd.d: Blocks users from adding and signing in to personal accounts while on the device.

VpnAlwaysOnLockdownMode.d: Enabling this forces all network traffic through the VPN tunnel. If a connection to the VPN can't be established, no network traffic will be allowed.

VpnAlwaysOnPackageIdentifier.d: Android app package name for app that will handle an always-on VPN connection.

WifiBlockEditConfigurations.d: Block user creation or editing of any Wi-Fi configurations.

WifiBlockEditPolicyDefinedConfigurations.d: Block changes to Wi-Fi configurations created by the device owner. Users can create their own Wi-Fi configurations.

WorkProfilePasswordExpirationDays.d: Indicates the number of days that a work profile password can be set before it expires and a new password will be required. Valid values 1 to 365

WorkProfilePasswordMinimumLength.d: Indicates the minimum length of the work profile password. Valid values 4 to 16

WorkProfilePasswordMinimumLetterCharacte.d: Indicates the minimum number of numeric characters required for the work profile password. Valid values 1 to 16

WorkProfilePasswordMinimumLowerCaseChara.d: Indicates the minimum number of non-letter characters required for the work profile password. Valid values 1 to 16

WorkProfilePasswordMinimumNonLetterChara.d: Indicates the minimum number of letter characters required for the work profile password. Valid values 1 to 16

WorkProfilePasswordMinimumNumericCharact.d: Indicates the minimum number of lower-case characters required for the work profile password. Valid values 1 to 16

WorkProfilePasswordMinimumSymbolCharacte.d: Indicates the minimum number of upper-case letter characters required for the work profile password. Valid values 1 to 16

WorkProfilePasswordMinimumUpperCaseChara.d: Indicates the minimum number of symbol characters required for the work profile password. Valid values 1 to 16

WorkProfilePasswordPreviousPasswordCount.d: Indicates the length of the work profile password history, where the user will not be able to enter a new password that is the same as any password in the history. Valid values 0 to 24

WorkProfilePasswordRequireUnlock.d: Indicates the timeout period after which a work profile must be unlocked using a form of strong authentication.

WorkProfilePasswordRequiredType.d: Indicates the minimum password quality required on the work profile password.

WorkProfilePasswordSignInFailureCountBef.d: Indicates the number of times a user can enter an incorrect work profile password before the device is wiped. Valid values 4 to 11

IntuneDeviceConfigurationPolicyAndroidOp.d: This resource configures an Intune device configuration profile for an Android open Source Project device.

Assignments.d: Represents the assignment to the Intune policy.

CameraBlocked.d: Prevents access to the device camera.

Description.d: Description of the Intune policy.

DisplayName.d: Display name of the Intune policy.

Id.d: Id of the Intune policy.

PasswordMinimumLength.d: Minimum number of characters required for the password.

PasswordMinutesOfInactivityBeforeScreenT.d: Maximum minutes of inactivity until screen locks.

PasswordSignInFailureCountBeforeFactoryR.d: Number of sign-in failures before wiping device.

RoleScopeTagIds.d: List of Scope Tags for this Entity instance.

IntuneDeviceConfigurationPolicyAndroidWo.d: This resource configures an Intune device configuration profile for an Android Workprofile device.

Assignments.d: Assignments of the Intune Policy.

Description.d: Description of the device general configuration policy for Android WorkProfile

DisplayName.d: Display name of the device general configuration policy for Android WorkProfile.

Id.d: The Id of the policy.

PasswordBlockFaceUnlock.d: Indicates whether or not to block face unlock.

PasswordBlockFingerprintUnlock.d: Indicates whether or not to block fingerprint unlock

PasswordBlockIrisUnlock.d: Indicates whether or not to block iris unlock.

PasswordExpirationDays.d: Number of days before the password expires

PasswordMinutesOfInactivityBeforeScreenT.d: Minutes of inactivity before the screen times out

PasswordPreviousPasswordBlockCount.d: Number of previous passwords to block

PasswordRequiredType.d: Type of password that is required

PasswordSignInFailureCountBeforeFactoryR.d: Number of sign in failures allowed before factory reset

RequiredPasswordComplexity.d: Indicates the required device password complexity on Android. One of: NONE, LOW, MEDIUM, HIGH.

RoleScopeTagIds.d: List of Scope Tags for this Entity instance.

SecurityRequireVerifyApps.d: Require the Android Verify apps feature is turned on

VpnEnableAlwaysOnLockdownMode.d: Enable lockdown mode for always-on VPN.

WorkProfileAllowAppInstallsFromUnknownSo.d: Indicates whether to allow installation of apps from unknown sources.

WorkProfileAllowWidgets.d: Allow widgets from work profile apps.

WorkProfileBlockAddingAccounts.d: Block users from adding/removing accounts in work profile

WorkProfileBlockCrossProfileCallerId.d: Block display work profile caller ID in personal profile

WorkProfileBlockCrossProfileContactsSear.d: Block work profile contacts availability in personal profile

WorkProfileBlockCrossProfileCopyPaste.d: Boolean that indicates if the setting disallow cross profile copy paste is enabled

WorkProfileBlockNotificationsWhileDevice.d: Indicates whether or not to block notifications while device locked

WorkProfileBlockPersonalAppInstallsFromU.d: Prevent app installations from unknown sources in the personal profile.

WorkProfileBlockScreenCapture.d: Block screen capture in work profile

WorkProfileBluetoothEnableContactSharing.d: Allow bluetooth devices to access enterprise contacts

WorkProfileDataSharingType.d: Type of data sharing that is allowed

WorkProfileDefaultAppPermissionPolicy.d: Type of password that is required

WorkProfilePasswordBlockFaceUnlock.d: Indicates whether or not to block face unlock in work profile.

WorkProfilePasswordBlockFingerprintUnloc.d: Indicates whether or not to block fingerprint unlock in work profile

WorkProfilePasswordBlockIrisUnlock.d: Indicates whether or not to block iris unlock in work profile.

WorkProfilePasswordBlockTrustAgents.d: Indicates whether or not to block Smart Lock and other trust agents for work profile

WorkProfilePasswordExpirationDays.d: Number of days before the work profile password expires

WorkProfilePasswordMinLetterCharacters.d: Minimum count of letter characters required in work profile password

WorkProfilePasswordMinLowerCaseCharacter.d: Minimum count of lower-case characters required in work profile password

WorkProfilePasswordMinNonLetterCharacter.d: Minimum count of non-letter characters required in work profile password

WorkProfilePasswordMinNumericCharacters.d: Minimum count of numeric characters required in work profile password

WorkProfilePasswordMinSymbolCharacters.d: Minimum count of symbols required in work profile password

WorkProfilePasswordMinUpperCaseCharacter.d: Minimum count of upper-case characters required in work profile password

WorkProfilePasswordMinimumLength.d: Minimum length of work profile password

WorkProfilePasswordMinutesOfInactivityBe.d: Minutes of inactivity before the screen times out

WorkProfilePasswordPreviousPasswordBlock.d: Number of previous work profile passwords to block

WorkProfilePasswordRequiredType.d: Type of work profile password that is required

WorkProfilePasswordSignInFailureCountBef.d: Number of sign in failures allowed before work profile is removed and all corporate data deleted

WorkProfileRequirePassword.d: Password is required or not for work profile

WorkProfileRequiredPasswordComplexity.d: Indicates the required device password complexity on Android. One of: NONE, LOW, MEDIUM, HIGH in work profile.

passwordBlockTrustAgents.d: Indicates whether or not to block Smart Lock and other trust agents.

IntuneDeviceConfigurationPolicyMacOS.d: This resource configures an Intune device configuration profile for an MacOS Device.

AddingGameCenterFriendsBlocked.d: Configures users from adding friends to Game Center. Available for devices running macOS versions 10.13 and later.

AirDropBlocked.d: Configures whether or not to allow AirDrop.

AppleWatchBlockAutoUnlock.d: Blocks users from unlocking their Mac with Apple Watch.

Assignments.d: Represents the assignment to the Intune policy.

CameraBlocked.d: Blocks users from taking photographs and videos.

ClassroomAppBlockRemoteScreenObservation.d: Blocks AirPlay, screen sharing to other devices, and a Classroom app feature used by teachers to view their students' screens. This setting isn't available if you've blocked screenshots.

ClassroomAppForceUnpromptedScreenObserva.d: Unprompted observation means that teachers can view screens without warning students first. This setting isn't available if you've blocked screenshots.

ClassroomForceAutomaticallyJoinClasses.d: Students can join a class without prompting the teacher.

ClassroomForceRequestPermissionToLeaveCl.d: Students enrolled in an unmanaged Classroom course must get teacher consent to leave the course.

ClassroomForceUnpromptedAppAndDeviceLock.d: Teachers can lock a student's device or app without the student's approval.

CompliantAppListType.d: Device compliance can be viewed in the Restricted Apps Compliance report.

CompliantAppsList.d: List of apps in the compliance (either allow list or block list, controlled by CompliantAppListType).

ContentCachingBlocked.d: Configures whether or not to allow content caching.

DefinitionLookupBlocked.d: Block look up, a feature that looks up the definition of a highlighted word.

Description.d: Description of the Intune policy.

DisplayName.d: Display name of the Intune policy.

EmailInDomainSuffixes.d: Emails that the user sends or receives which don't match the domains you specify here will be marked as untrusted.

EraseContentAndSettingsBlocked.d: Configures the reset option on supervised devices. Available for devices running macOS versions 12.0 and later.

GameCenterBlocked.d: Configured if the Game Center icon is removed from the Home screen. Available for devices running macOS versions 10.13 and later.

ICloudBlockActivityContinuation.d: Handoff lets users start work on one MacOS device, and continue it on another MacOS or iOS device. Available for macOS 10.15 and later.

ICloudBlockDocumentSync.d: Blocks iCloud from syncing documents and data.

ICloudBlockPhotoLibrary.d: Any photos not fully downloaded from iCloud Photo Library to device will be removed from local storage.

ICloudDesktopAndDocumentsBlocked.d: Configures if the synchronization of cloud desktop and documents is blocked. Available for devices running macOS 10.12.4 and later.

ICloudPrivateRelayBlocked.d: Configures if iCloud private relay is blocked or not. Available for devices running macOS 12 and later.

ITunesBlockFileSharing.d: Blocks files from being transferred using iTunes.

ITunesBlockMusicService.d: Configures whether or not to block files from being transferred using iTunes.

Id.d: Id of the Intune policy.

KeyboardBlockDictation.d: Block dictation, which is a feature that converts the user's voice to text.

KeychainBlockCloudSync.d: Disables syncing credentials stored in the Keychain to iCloud

MultiplayerGamingBlocked.d: Configures whether multiplayer gaming when using Game Center is blocked. Available for devices running macOS versions 10.13 and later.

PasswordBlockAirDropSharing.d: Configures whether or not to block sharing passwords with the AirDrop passwords feature.

PasswordBlockAutoFill.d: Configures whether or not to block the AutoFill Passwords feature.

PasswordBlockFingerprintUnlock.d: Requires user to set a non-biometric passcode or password to unlock the device.

PasswordBlockModification.d: Blocks user from changing the set passcode.

PasswordBlockProximityRequests.d: Configures whether or not to block requesting passwords from nearby devices.

PasswordBlockSimple.d: Block simple password sequences, such as 1234 or 1111.

PasswordExpirationDays.d: Number of days until device password must be changed. (1-65535)

PasswordMaximumAttemptCount.d: Configures the number of allowed failed attempts to enter the passcode at the device's lock screen. Valid values 2 to 11

PasswordMinimumCharacterSetCount.d: Minimum number (0-4) of non-alphanumeric characters, such as #, %, !, etc., required in the password. The default value is 0.

PasswordMinimumLength.d: Minimum number of digits or characters in password (4-16).

PasswordMinutesOfInactivityBeforeLock.d: Set to 0 to require a password immediately. There is no maximum number of minutes, and this number overrides the number currently set on the device.

PasswordMinutesOfInactivityBeforeScreenT.d: Set to 0 to use the device's minimum possible value. This number (0-60 minutes) overrides the number currently set on the device.

PasswordMinutesUntilFailedLoginReset.d: Configures the number of minutes before the login is reset after the maximum number of unsuccessful login attempts is reached.

PasswordPreviousPasswordBlockCount.d: Number of new passwords that must be used until an old one can be reused. (1-24)

PasswordRequired.d: Specify the type of password required.

PasswordRequiredType.d: Specify the type of password required.

PrivacyAccessControls.d: Configure an app's access to specific data, folders, and apps on a device. These settings apply to devices running macOS Mojave 10.14 and later.

RoleScopeTagIds.d: List of Scope Tags for this Entity instance.

SafariBlockAutofill.d: Blocks Safari from remembering what users enter in web forms.

ScreenCaptureBlocked.d: Configures whether or not to block the user from taking Screenshots.

SoftwareUpdateMajorOSDeferredInstallDela.d: Specify the number of days (1-90) to delay visibility of major OS software updates. Available for devices running macOS versions 11.3 and later. Valid values 0 to 90

SoftwareUpdateMinorOSDeferredInstallDela.d: Specify the number of days (1-90) to delay visibility of minor OS software updates. Available for devices running macOS versions 11.3 and later. Valid values 0 to 90

SoftwareUpdateNonOSDeferredInstallDelayI.d: Specify the number of days (1-90) to delay visibility of non-OS software updates. Available for devices running macOS versions 11.3 and later. Valid values 0 to 90

SoftwareUpdatesEnforcedDelayInDays.d: Delay the user's software update for this many days. The maximum is 90 days. (1-90)

SpotlightBlockInternetResults.d: Blocks Spotlight from returning any results from an Internet search

TouchIdTimeoutInHours.d: Configures the maximum hours after which the user must enter their password to unlock the device instead of using Touch ID. Available for devices running macOS 12 and later. Valid values 0 to 2147483647

UpdateDelayPolicy.d: Configures whether to delay OS and/or app updates for macOS.

WallpaperModificationBlocked.d: Configures whether the wallpaper can be changed. Available for devices running macOS versions 10.13 and later.

IntuneDeviceConfigurationPolicyWindows10.d: This resource configures an Intune Device Configuration Policy for Windows10.

AccountsBlockAddingNonMicrosoftAccountEm.d: Indicates whether or not to Block the user from adding email accounts to the device that are not associated with a Microsoft account.

ActivateAppsWithVoice.d: Specifies if Windows apps can be activated by voice. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

AntiTheftModeBlocked.d: Indicates whether or not to block the user from selecting an AntiTheft mode preference (Windows 10 Mobile only).

AppManagementMSIAllowUserControlOverInst.d: This policy setting permits users to change installation options that typically are available only to system administrators.

AppManagementMSIAlwaysInstallWithElevate.d: This policy setting directs Windows Installer to use elevated permissions when it installs any program on the system.

AppManagementPackageFamilyNamesToLaunchA.d: List of semi-colon delimited Package Family Names of Windows apps. Listed Windows apps are to be launched after logon.

AppsAllowTrustedAppsSideloading.d: Indicates whether apps from AppX packages signed with a trusted certificate can be side loaded. Possible values are: notConfigured, blocked, allowed.

AppsBlockWindowsStoreOriginatedApps.d: Indicates whether or not to disable the launch of all apps from Windows Store that came pre-installed or were downloaded.

Assignments.d: Represents the assignment to the Intune policy.

AuthenticationAllowSecondaryDevice.d: Allows secondary authentication devices to work with Windows.

AuthenticationPreferredAzureADTenantDoma.d: Specifies the preferred domain among available domains in the Azure AD tenant.

AuthenticationWebSignIn.d: Indicates whether or not Web Credential Provider will be enabled. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

BluetoothAllowedServices.d: Specify a list of allowed Bluetooth services and profiles in hex formatted strings.

BluetoothBlockAdvertising.d: Whether or not to Block the user from using bluetooth advertising.

BluetoothBlockDiscoverableMode.d: Whether or not to Block the user from using bluetooth discoverable mode.

BluetoothBlockPrePairing.d: Whether or not to block specific bundled Bluetooth peripherals to automatically pair with the host device.

BluetoothBlockPromptedProximalConnection.d: Whether or not to block the users from using Swift Pair and other proximity based scenarios.

BluetoothBlocked.d: Whether or not to Block the user from using bluetooth.

CameraBlocked.d: Whether or not to Block the user from accessing the camera of the device.

CellularBlockDataWhenRoaming.d: Whether or not to Block the user from using data over cellular while roaming.

CellularBlockVpn.d: Whether or not to Block the user from using VPN over cellular.

CellularBlockVpnWhenRoaming.d: Whether or not to Block the user from using VPN when roaming over cellular.

CellularData.d: Whether or not to allow the cellular data channel on the device. If not configured, the cellular data channel is allowed and the user can turn it off. Possible values are: blocked, required, allowed, notConfigured.

CertificatesBlockManualRootCertificateIn.d: Whether or not to Block the user from doing manual root certificate installation.

ConfigureTimeZone.d: Specifies the time zone to be applied to the device. This is the standard Windows name for the target time zone.

ConnectedDevicesServiceBlocked.d: Whether or not to block Connected Devices Service which enables discovery and connection to other devices, remote messaging, remote app sessions and other cross-device experiences.

CopyPasteBlocked.d: Whether or not to Block the user from using copy paste.

CortanaBlocked.d: Whether or not to Block the user from using Cortana.

CryptographyAllowFipsAlgorithmPolicy.d: Specify whether to allow or disallow the Federal Information Processing Standard (FIPS) policy.

DataProtectionBlockDirectMemoryAccess.d: This policy setting allows you to block direct memory access (DMA) for all hot pluggable PCI downstream ports until a user logs into Windows.

DefenderBlockEndUserAccess.d: Whether or not to block end user access to Defender.

DefenderBlockOnAccessProtection.d: Allows or disallows Windows Defender On Access Protection functionality.

DefenderCloudBlockLevel.d: Specifies the level of cloud-delivered protection. Possible values are: notConfigured, high, highPlus, zeroTolerance.

DefenderCloudExtendedTimeout.d: Timeout extension for file scanning by the cloud. Valid values 0 to 50
