DefenderCloudExtendedTimeoutInSeconds.d: Timeout extension for file scanning by the cloud. Valid values 0 to 50

DefenderDaysBeforeDeletingQuarantinedMal.d: Number of days before deleting quarantined malware. Valid values 0 to 90

DefenderDetectedMalwareActions.d: Gets or sets Defenders actions to take on detected Malware per threat level.

DefenderDisableCatchupFullScan.d: When blocked, catch-up scans for scheduled full scans will be turned off.

DefenderDisableCatchupQuickScan.d: When blocked, catch-up scans for scheduled quick scans will be turned off.

DefenderFileExtensionsToExclude.d: File extensions to exclude from scans and real time protection.

DefenderFilesAndFoldersToExclude.d: Files and folder to exclude from scans and real time protection.

DefenderMonitorFileActivity.d: Value for monitoring file activity. Possible values are: userDefined, disable, monitorAllFiles, monitorIncomingFilesOnly, monitorOutgoingFilesOnly.

DefenderPotentiallyUnwantedAppAction.d: Gets or sets Defenders action to take on Potentially Unwanted Application (PUA), which includes software with behaviors of ad-injection, software bundling, persistent solicitation for payment or subscription, etc. Defender alerts user when PUA is being downloaded or attempts to install itself. Added in Windows 10 for desktop. Possible values are: deviceDefault, block, audit.

DefenderPotentiallyUnwantedAppActionSett.d: Gets or sets Defenders action to take on Potentially Unwanted Application (PUA), which includes software with behaviors of ad-injection, software bundling, persistent solicitation for payment or subscription, etc. Defender alerts user when PUA is being downloaded or attempts to install itself. Added in Windows 10 for desktop. Possible values are: userDefined, enable, auditMode, warn, notConfigured.

DefenderProcessesToExclude.d: Processes to exclude from scans and real time protection.

DefenderPromptForSampleSubmission.d: The configuration for how to prompt user for sample submission. Possible values are: userDefined, alwaysPrompt, promptBeforeSendingPersonalData, neverSendData, sendAllDataWithoutPrompting.

DefenderRequireBehaviorMonitoring.d: Indicates whether or not to require behavior monitoring.

DefenderRequireCloudProtection.d: Indicates whether or not to require cloud protection.

DefenderRequireNetworkInspectionSystem.d: Indicates whether or not to require network inspection system.

DefenderRequireRealTimeMonitoring.d: Indicates whether or not to require real time monitoring.

DefenderScanArchiveFiles.d: Indicates whether or not to scan archive files.

DefenderScanDownloads.d: Indicates whether or not to scan downloads.

DefenderScanIncomingMail.d: Indicates whether or not to scan incoming mail messages.

DefenderScanMappedNetworkDrivesDuringFul.d: Indicates whether or not to scan mapped network drives during full scan.

DefenderScanMaxCpu.d: Max CPU usage percentage during scan. Valid values 0 to 100

DefenderScanNetworkFiles.d: Indicates whether or not to scan files opened from a network folder.

DefenderScanRemovableDrivesDuringFullSca.d: Indicates whether or not to scan removable drives during full scan.

DefenderScanScriptsLoadedInInternetExplo.d: Indicates whether or not to scan scripts loaded in Internet Explorer browser.

DefenderScanType.d: The defender system scan type. Possible values are: userDefined, disabled, quick, full.

DefenderScheduleScanEnableLowCpuPriority.d: When enabled, low CPU priority will be used during scheduled scans.

DefenderScheduledQuickScanTime.d: The time to perform a daily quick scan.

DefenderScheduledScanTime.d: The defender time for the system scan.

DefenderSignatureUpdateIntervalInHours.d: The signature update interval in hours. Specify 0 not to check. Valid values 0 to 24

DefenderSubmitSamplesConsentType.d: Checks for the user consent level in Windows Defender to send data. Possible values are: sendSafeSamplesAutomatically, alwaysPrompt, neverSend, sendAllSamplesAutomatically.

DefenderSystemScanSchedule.d: Defender day of the week for the system scan. Possible values are: userDefined, everyday, sunday, monday, tuesday, wednesday, thursday, friday, saturday, noScheduledScan.

Description.d: Admin provided description of the Device Configuration.

DeveloperUnlockSetting.d: Indicates whether or not to allow developer unlock. Possible values are: notConfigured, blocked, allowed.

DeviceManagementBlockFactoryResetOnMobil.d: Indicates whether or not to Block the user from resetting their phone.

DeviceManagementBlockManualUnenroll.d: Indicates whether or not to Block the user from doing manual un-enrollment from device management.

DiagnosticsDataSubmissionMode.d: Gets or sets a value allowing the device to send diagnostic and usage telemetry data, such as Watson. Possible values are: userDefined, none, basic, enhanced, full.

DisplayAppListWithGdiDPIScalingTurnedOff.d: List of legacy applications that have GDI DPI Scaling turned off.

DisplayAppListWithGdiDPIScalingTurnedOn.d: List of legacy applications that have GDI DPI Scaling turned on.

DisplayName.d: Admin provided name of the device configuration.

EdgeAllowStartPagesModification.d: Allow users to change Start pages on Edge. Use the EdgeHomepageUrls to specify the Start pages that the user would see by default when they open Edge.

EdgeBlockAccessToAboutFlags.d: Indicates whether or not to prevent access to about flags on Edge browser.

EdgeBlockAddressBarDropdown.d: Block the address bar dropdown functionality in Microsoft Edge. Disable this settings to minimize network connections from Microsoft Edge to Microsoft services.

EdgeBlockAutofill.d: Indicates whether or not to block auto fill.

EdgeBlockCompatibilityList.d: Block Microsoft compatibility list in Microsoft Edge. This list from Microsoft helps Edge properly display sites with known compatibility issues.

EdgeBlockDeveloperTools.d: Indicates whether or not to block developer tools in the Edge browser.

EdgeBlockEditFavorites.d: Indicates whether or not to Block the user from making changes to Favorites.

EdgeBlockExtensions.d: Indicates whether or not to block extensions in the Edge browser.

EdgeBlockFullScreenMode.d: Allow or prevent Edge from entering the full screen mode.

EdgeBlockInPrivateBrowsing.d: Indicates whether or not to block InPrivate browsing on corporate networks, in the Edge browser.

EdgeBlockJavaScript.d: Indicates whether or not to Block the user from using JavaScript.

EdgeBlockLiveTileDataCollection.d: Block the collection of information by Microsoft for live tile creation when users pin a site to Start from Microsoft Edge.

EdgeBlockPasswordManager.d: Indicates whether or not to Block password manager.

EdgeBlockPopups.d: Indicates whether or not to block popups.

EdgeBlockPrelaunch.d: Decide whether Microsoft Edge is prelaunched at Windows startup.

EdgeBlockPrinting.d: Configure Edge to allow or block printing.

EdgeBlockSavingHistory.d: Configure Edge to allow browsing history to be saved or to never save browsing history.

EdgeBlockSearchEngineCustomization.d: Indicates whether or not to block the user from adding new search engine or changing the default search engine.

EdgeBlockSearchSuggestions.d: Indicates whether or not to block the user from using the search suggestions in the address bar.

EdgeBlockSendingDoNotTrackHeader.d: Indicates whether or not to Block the user from sending the do not track header.

EdgeBlockSendingIntranetTrafficToInterne.d: Indicates whether or not to switch the intranet traffic from Edge to Internet Explorer. Note: the name of this property is misleading the property is obsolete, use EdgeSendIntranetTrafficToInternetExplorer instead.

EdgeBlockSideloadingExtensions.d: Indicates whether the user can sideload extensions.

EdgeBlockTabPreloading.d: Configure whether Edge preloads the new tab page at Windows startup.

EdgeBlockWebContentOnNewTabPage.d: Configure to load a blank page in Edge instead of the default New tab page and prevent users from changing it.

EdgeBlocked.d: Indicates whether or not to Block the user from using the Edge browser.

EdgeClearBrowsingDataOnExit.d: Clear browsing data on exiting Microsoft Edge.

EdgeCookiePolicy.d: Indicates which cookies to block in the Edge browser. Possible values are: userDefined, allow, blockThirdParty, blockAll.

EdgeDisableFirstRunPage.d: Block the Microsoft web page that opens on the first use of Microsoft Edge. This policy allows enterprises, like those enrolled in zero emissions configurations, to block this page.

EdgeEnterpriseModeSiteListLocation.d: Indicates the enterprise mode site list location. Could be a local file, local network or http location.

EdgeFavoritesBarVisibility.d: Get or set a value that specifies whether to set the favorites bar to always be visible or hidden on any page. Possible values are: notConfigured, hide, show.

EdgeFavoritesListLocation.d: The location of the favorites list to provision. Could be a local file, local network or http location.

EdgeFirstRunUrl.d: The first run URL for when Edge browser is opened for the first time.

EdgeHomeButtonConfiguration.d: Causes the Home button to either hide, load the default Start page, load a New tab page, or a custom URL

EdgeHomepageUrls.d: The list of URLs for homepages shodwn on MDM-enrolled devices on Edge browser.

EdgeKioskModeRestriction.d: Controls how the Microsoft Edge settings are restricted based on the configure kiosk mode. Possible values are: notConfigured, digitalSignage, normalMode, publicBrowsingSingleApp, publicBrowsingMultiApp.

EdgeKioskResetAfterIdleTimeInMinutes.d: Specifies the time in minutes from the last user activity before Microsoft Edge kiosk resets. Valid values are 0-1440. The default is 5. 0 indicates no reset. Valid values 0 to 1440

EdgeNewTabPageURL.d: Specify the page opened when new tabs are created.

EdgeOpensWith.d: Specify what kind of pages are open at start. Possible values are: notConfigured, startPage, newTabPage, previousPages, specificPages.

EdgePreventCertificateErrorOverride.d: Allow or prevent users from overriding certificate errors.

EdgeRequireSmartScreen.d: Indicates whether or not to Require the user to use the smart screen filter.

EdgeRequiredExtensionPackageFamilyNames.d: Specify the list of package family names of browser extensions that are required and cannot be turned off by the user.

EdgeSearchEngine.d: Allows IT admins to set a default search engine for MDM-Controlled devices. Users can override this and change their default search engine provided the AllowSearchEngineCustomization policy is not set.

EdgeSendIntranetTrafficToInternetExplore.d: Indicates whether or not to switch the intranet traffic from Edge to Internet Explorer.

EdgeShowMessageWhenOpeningInternetExplor.d: Controls the message displayed by Edge before switching to Internet Explorer. Possible values are: notConfigured, disabled, enabled, keepGoing.

EdgeSyncFavoritesWithInternetExplorer.d: Enable favorites sync between Internet Explorer and Microsoft Edge. Additions, deletions, modifications and order changes to favorites are shared between browsers.

EdgeTelemetryForMicrosoft365Analytics.d: Specifies what type of telemetry data (none, intranet, internet, both) is sent to Microsoft 365 Analytics. Possible values are: notConfigured, intranet, internet, intranetAndInternet.

EnableAutomaticRedeployment.d: Allow users with administrative rights to delete all user data and settings using CTRL + Win + R at the device lock screen so that the device can be automatically re-configured and re-enrolled into management.

EnergySaverOnBatteryThresholdPercentage.d: This setting allows you to specify battery charge level at which Energy Saver is turned on. While on battery, Energy Saver is automatically turned on at (and below) the specified battery charge level. Valid input range (0-100). Valid values 0 to 100

EnergySaverPluggedInThresholdPercentage.d: This setting allows you to specify battery charge level at which Energy Saver is turned on. While plugged in, Energy Saver is automatically turned on at (and below) the specified battery charge level. Valid input range (0-100). Valid values 0 to 100

EnterpriseCloudPrintDiscoveryMaxLimit.d: Maximum number of printers that should be queried from a discovery endpoint. This is a mobile only setting. Valid values 1 to 65535

EnterpriseCloudPrintMopriaDiscoveryResou.d: OAuth resource URI for printer discovery service as configured in Azure portal.

EnterpriseCloudPrintOAuthAuthority.d: Authentication endpoint for acquiring OAuth tokens.

EnterpriseCloudPrintOAuthClientIdentifie.d: GUID of a client application authorized to retrieve OAuth tokens from the OAuth Authority.

EnterpriseCloudPrintResourceIdentifier.d: OAuth resource URI for print service as configured in the Azure portal.

ExperienceBlockDeviceDiscovery.d: Indicates whether or not to enable device discovery UX.

ExperienceBlockErrorDialogWhenNoSIM.d: Indicates whether or not to allow the error dialog from displaying if no SIM card is detected.

ExperienceBlockTaskSwitcher.d: Indicates whether or not to enable task switching on the device.

ExperienceDoNotSyncBrowserSettings.d: Allow or prevent the syncing of Microsoft Edge Browser settings. Option for IT admins to prevent syncing across devices, but allow user override. Possible values are: notConfigured, blockedWithUserOverride, blocked.

FindMyFiles.d: Controls if the user can configure search to Find My Files mode, which searches files in secondary hard drives and also outside of the user profile. Find My Files does not allow users to search files or locations to which they do not have access. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

GameDvrBlocked.d: Indicates whether or not to block DVR and broadcasting.

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

InkWorkspaceAccess.d: Controls the user access to the ink workspace, from the desktop and from above the lock screen. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

InkWorkspaceAccessState.d: Controls the user access to the ink workspace, from the desktop and from above the lock screen. Possible values are: notConfigured, blocked, allowed.

InkWorkspaceBlockSuggestedApps.d: Specify whether to show recommended app suggestions in the ink workspace.

InternetSharingBlocked.d: Indicates whether or not to Block the user from using internet sharing.

LocationServicesBlocked.d: Indicates whether or not to Block the user from location services.

LockScreenActivateAppsWithVoice.d: This policy setting specifies whether Windows apps can be activated by voice while the system is locked. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

LockScreenAllowTimeoutConfiguration.d: Specify whether to show a user-configurable setting to control the screen timeout while on the lock screen of Windows 10 Mobile devices. If this policy is set to Allow, the value set by lockScreenTimeoutInSeconds is ignored.

LockScreenBlockActionCenterNotifications.d: Indicates whether or not to block action center notifications over lock screen.

LockScreenBlockCortana.d: Indicates whether or not the user can interact with Cortana using speech while the system is locked.

LockScreenBlockToastNotifications.d: Indicates whether to allow toast notifications above the device lock screen.

LockScreenTimeoutInSeconds.d: Set the duration (in seconds) from the screen locking to the screen turning off for Windows 10 Mobile devices. Supported values are 11-1800. Valid values 11 to 1800

LogonBlockFastUserSwitching.d: Disables the ability to quickly switch between users that are logged on simultaneously without logging off.

MessagingBlockMMS.d: Indicates whether or not to block the MMS send/receive functionality on the device.

MessagingBlockRichCommunicationServices.d: Indicates whether or not to block the RCS send/receive functionality on the device.

MessagingBlockSync.d: Indicates whether or not to block text message back up and restore and Messaging Everywhere.

MicrosoftAccountBlockSettingsSync.d: Indicates whether or not to Block Microsoft account settings sync.

MicrosoftAccountBlocked.d: Indicates whether or not to Block a Microsoft account.

MicrosoftAccountSignInAssistantSettings.d: Controls the Microsoft Account Sign-In Assistant (wlidsvc) NT service. Possible values are: notConfigured, disabled.

NetworkProxyApplySettingsDeviceWide.d: If set, proxy settings will be applied to all processes and accounts in the device. Otherwise, it will be applied to the user account that's enrolled into MDM.

NetworkProxyAutomaticConfigurationUrl.d: Address to the proxy auto-config (PAC) script you want to use.

NetworkProxyDisableAutoDetect.d: Disable automatic detection of settings. If enabled, the system will try to find the path to a proxy auto-config (PAC) script.

NfcBlocked.d: Indicates whether or not to Block the user from using near field communication.

OneDriveDisableFileSync.d: Gets or sets a value allowing IT admins to prevent apps and features from working with files on OneDrive.

PasswordBlockSimple.d: Specify whether PINs or passwords such as '1111' or '1234' are allowed. For Windows 10 desktops, it also controls the use of picture passwords.

PasswordExpirationDays.d: The password expiration in days. Valid values 0 to 730

PasswordMinimumAgeInDays.d: This security setting determines the period of time (in days) that a password must be used before the user can change it. Valid values 0 to 998

PasswordMinimumCharacterSetCount.d: The number of character sets required in the password.

PasswordMinimumLength.d: The minimum password length. Valid values 4 to 16

PasswordMinutesOfInactivityBeforeScreenT.d: The minutes of inactivity before the screen times out.

PasswordPreviousPasswordBlockCount.d: The number of previous passwords to prevent reuse of. Valid values 0 to 50

PasswordRequireWhenResumeFromIdleState.d: Indicates whether or not to require a password upon resuming from an idle state.

PasswordRequired.d: Indicates whether or not to require the user to have a password.

PasswordRequiredType.d: The required password type. Possible values are: deviceDefault, alphanumeric, numeric.

PasswordSignInFailureCountBeforeFactoryR.d: The number of sign in failures before factory reset. Valid values 0 to 999

PersonalizationDesktopImageUrl.d: A http or https Url to a jpg, jpeg or png image that needs to be downloaded and used as the Desktop Image or a file Url to a local image on the file system that needs to used as the Desktop Image.

PersonalizationLockScreenImageUrl.d: A http or https Url to a jpg, jpeg or png image that needs to be downloaded and used as the Lock Screen Image or a file Url to a local image on the file system that needs to be used as the Lock Screen Image.

PowerButtonActionOnBattery.d: This setting specifies the action that Windows takes when a user presses the Power button while on battery. Possible values are: notConfigured, noAction, sleep, hibernate, shutdown.

PowerButtonActionPluggedIn.d: This setting specifies the action that Windows takes when a user presses the Power button while plugged in. Possible values are: notConfigured, noAction, sleep, hibernate, shutdown.

PowerHybridSleepOnBattery.d: This setting allows you to turn off hybrid sleep while on battery. If you set this setting to disable, a hiberfile is not generated when the system transitions to sleep (Stand By). If you set this setting to enable or do not configure this policy setting, users control this setting. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

PowerHybridSleepPluggedIn.d: This setting allows you to turn off hybrid sleep while plugged in. If you set this setting to disable, a hiberfile is not generated when the system transitions to sleep (Stand By). If you set this setting to enable or do not configure this policy setting, users control this setting. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

PowerLidCloseActionOnBattery.d: This setting specifies the action that Windows takes when a user closes the lid on a mobile PC while on battery. Possible values are: notConfigured, noAction, sleep, hibernate, shutdown.

PowerLidCloseActionPluggedIn.d: This setting specifies the action that Windows takes when a user closes the lid on a mobile PC while plugged in. Possible values are: notConfigured, noAction, sleep, hibernate, shutdown.

PowerSleepButtonActionOnBattery.d: This setting specifies the action that Windows takes when a user presses the Sleep button while on battery. Possible values are: notConfigured, noAction, sleep, hibernate, shutdown.

PowerSleepButtonActionPluggedIn.d: This setting specifies the action that Windows takes when a user presses the Sleep button while plugged in. Possible values are: notConfigured, noAction, sleep, hibernate, shutdown.

PrinterBlockAddition.d: Prevent user installation of additional printers from printers settings.

PrinterDefaultName.d: Name (network host name) of an installed printer.

PrinterNames.d: Automatically provision printers based on their names (network host names).

PrivacyAdvertisingId.d: Enables or disables the use of advertising ID. Added in Windows 10, version 1607. Possible values are: notConfigured, blocked, allowed.

PrivacyAutoAcceptPairingAndConsentPrompt.d: Indicates whether or not to allow the automatic acceptance of the pairing and privacy user consent dialog when launching apps.

PrivacyBlockActivityFeed.d: Blocks the usage of cloud based speech services for Cortana, Dictation, or Store applications.

PrivacyBlockInputPersonalization.d: Indicates whether or not to block the usage of cloud based speech services for Cortana, Dictation, or Store applications.

PrivacyBlockPublishUserActivities.d: Blocks the shared experiences/discovery of recently used resources in task switcher etc.

PrivacyDisableLaunchExperience.d: This policy prevents the privacy experience from launching during user logon for new and upgraded users.

ResetProtectionModeBlocked.d: Indicates whether or not to Block the user from reset protection mode.

RoleScopeTagIds.d: List of Scope Tags for this Entity instance.

SafeSearchFilter.d: Specifies what filter level of safe search is required. Possible values are: userDefined, strict, moderate.

ScreenCaptureBlocked.d: Indicates whether or not to Block the user from taking Screenshots.

SearchBlockDiacritics.d: Specifies if search can use diacritics.

SearchBlockWebResults.d: Indicates whether or not to block the web search.

SearchDisableAutoLanguageDetection.d: Specifies whether to use automatic language detection when indexing content and properties.

SearchDisableIndexerBackoff.d: Indicates whether or not to disable the search indexer backoff feature.

SearchDisableIndexingEncryptedItems.d: Indicates whether or not to block indexing of WIP-protected items to prevent them from appearing in search results for Cortana or Explorer.

SearchDisableIndexingRemovableDrive.d: Indicates whether or not to allow users to add locations on removable drives to libraries and to be indexed.

SearchDisableLocation.d: Specifies if search can use location information.

SearchDisableUseLocation.d: Specifies if search can use location information.

SearchEnableAutomaticIndexSizeManangemen.d: Specifies minimum amount of hard drive space on the same drive as the index location before indexing stops.

SearchEnableRemoteQueries.d: Indicates whether or not to block remote queries of this computers index.

SecurityBlockAzureADJoinedDevicesAutoEnc.d: Specify whether to allow automatic device encryption during OOBE when the device is Azure AD joined (desktop only).

SettingsBlockAccountsPage.d: Indicates whether or not to block access to Accounts in Settings app.

SettingsBlockAddProvisioningPackage.d: Indicates whether or not to block the user from installing provisioning packages.

SettingsBlockAppsPage.d: Indicates whether or not to block access to Apps in Settings app.

SettingsBlockChangeLanguage.d: Indicates whether or not to block the user from changing the language settings.

SettingsBlockChangePowerSleep.d: Indicates whether or not to block the user from changing power and sleep settings.

SettingsBlockChangeRegion.d: Indicates whether or not to block the user from changing the region settings.

SettingsBlockChangeSystemTime.d: Indicates whether or not to block the user from changing date and time settings.

SettingsBlockDevicesPage.d: Indicates whether or not to block access to Devices in Settings app.

SettingsBlockEaseOfAccessPage.d: Indicates whether or not to block access to Ease of Access in Settings app.

SettingsBlockEditDeviceName.d: Indicates whether or not to block the user from editing the device name.

SettingsBlockGamingPage.d: Indicates whether or not to block access to Gaming in Settings app.

SettingsBlockNetworkInternetPage.d: Indicates whether or not to block access to Network & Internet in Settings app.

SettingsBlockPersonalizationPage.d: Indicates whether or not to block access to Personalization in Settings app.

SettingsBlockPrivacyPage.d: Indicates whether or not to block access to Privacy in Settings app.

SettingsBlockRemoveProvisioningPackage.d: Indicates whether or not to block the runtime configuration agent from removing provisioning packages.

SettingsBlockSettingsApp.d: Indicates whether or not to block access to Settings app.

SettingsBlockSystemPage.d: Indicates whether or not to block access to System in Settings app.

SettingsBlockTimeLanguagePage.d: Indicates whether or not to block access to Time & Language in Settings app.

SettingsBlockUpdateSecurityPage.d: Indicates whether or not to block access to Update & Security in Settings app.

SharedUserAppDataAllowed.d: Indicates whether or not to block multiple users of the same app to share data.

SmartScreenAppInstallControl.d: Added in Windows 10, version 1703. Allows IT Admins to control whether users are allowed to install apps from places other than the Store. Possible values are: notConfigured, anywhere, storeOnly, recommendations, preferStore.

SmartScreenBlockPromptOverride.d: Indicates whether or not users can override SmartScreen Filter warnings about potentially malicious websites.

SmartScreenBlockPromptOverrideForFiles.d: Indicates whether or not users can override the SmartScreen Filter warnings about downloading unverified files

SmartScreenEnableAppInstallControl.d: This property will be deprecated in July 2019 and will be replaced by property SmartScreenAppInstallControl. Allows IT Admins to control whether users are allowed to install apps from places other than the Store.

StartBlockUnpinningAppsFromTaskbar.d: Indicates whether or not to block the user from unpinning apps from taskbar.

StartMenuAppListVisibility.d: Setting the value of this collapses the app list, removes the app list entirely, or disables the corresponding toggle in the Settings app. Possible values are: userDefined, collapse, remove, disableSettingsApp.

StartMenuHideChangeAccountSettings.d: Enabling this policy hides the change account setting from appearing in the user tile in the start menu.

StartMenuHideFrequentlyUsedApps.d: Enabling this policy hides the most used apps from appearing on the start menu and disables the corresponding toggle in the Settings app.

StartMenuHideHibernate.d: Enabling this policy hides hibernate from appearing in the power button in the start menu.

StartMenuHideLock.d: Enabling this policy hides lock from appearing in the user tile in the start menu.

StartMenuHidePowerButton.d: Enabling this policy hides the power button from appearing in the start menu.

StartMenuHideRecentJumpLists.d: Enabling this policy hides recent jump lists from appearing on the start menu/taskbar and disables the corresponding toggle in the Settings app.

StartMenuHideRecentlyAddedApps.d: Enabling this policy hides recently added apps from appearing on the start menu and disables the corresponding toggle in the Settings app.

StartMenuHideRestartOptions.d: Enabling this policy hides 'Restart/Update and Restart' from appearing in the power button in the start menu.

StartMenuHideShutDown.d: Enabling this policy hides shut down/update and shut down from appearing in the power button in the start menu.

StartMenuHideSignOut.d: Enabling this policy hides sign out from appearing in the user tile in the start menu.

StartMenuHideSleep.d: Enabling this policy hides sleep from appearing in the power button in the start menu.

StartMenuHideSwitchAccount.d: Enabling this policy hides switch account from appearing in the user tile in the start menu.

StartMenuHideUserTile.d: Enabling this policy hides the user tile from appearing in the start menu.

StartMenuLayoutEdgeAssetsXml.d: This policy setting allows you to import Edge assets to be used with startMenuLayoutXml policy. Start layout can contain secondary tile from Edge app which looks for Edge local asset file. Edge local asset would not exist and cause Edge secondary tile to appear empty in this case. This policy only gets applied when startMenuLayoutXml policy is modified. The value should be a UTF-8 Base64 encoded byte array.

StartMenuLayoutXml.d: Allows admins to override the default Start menu layout and prevents the user from changing it. The layout is modified by specifying an XML file based on a layout modification schema. XML needs to be in a UTF8 encoded byte array format.

StartMenuMode.d: Allows admins to decide how the Start menu is displayed. Possible values are: userDefined, fullScreen, nonFullScreen.

StartMenuPinnedFolderDocuments.d: Enforces the visibility (Show/Hide) of the Documents folder shortcut on the Start menu. Possible values are: notConfigured, hide, show.

StartMenuPinnedFolderDownloads.d: Enforces the visibility (Show/Hide) of the Downloads folder shortcut on the Start menu. Possible values are: notConfigured, hide, show.

StartMenuPinnedFolderFileExplorer.d: Enforces the visibility (Show/Hide) of the FileExplorer shortcut on the Start menu. Possible values are: notConfigured, hide, show.

StartMenuPinnedFolderHomeGroup.d: Enforces the visibility (Show/Hide) of the HomeGroup folder shortcut on the Start menu. Possible values are: notConfigured, hide, show.

StartMenuPinnedFolderMusic.d: Enforces the visibility (Show/Hide) of the Music folder shortcut on the Start menu. Possible values are: notConfigured, hide, show.

StartMenuPinnedFolderNetwork.d: Enforces the visibility (Show/Hide) of the Network folder shortcut on the Start menu. Possible values are: notConfigured, hide, show.

StartMenuPinnedFolderPersonalFolder.d: Enforces the visibility (Show/Hide) of the PersonalFolder shortcut on the Start menu. Possible values are: notConfigured, hide, show.

StartMenuPinnedFolderPictures.d: Enforces the visibility (Show/Hide) of the Pictures folder shortcut on the Start menu. Possible values are: notConfigured, hide, show.

StartMenuPinnedFolderSettings.d: Enforces the visibility (Show/Hide) of the Settings folder shortcut on the Start menu. Possible values are: notConfigured, hide, show.

StartMenuPinnedFolderVideos.d: Enforces the visibility (Show/Hide) of the Videos folder shortcut on the Start menu. Possible values are: notConfigured, hide, show.

StorageBlockRemovableStorage.d: Indicates whether or not to Block the user from using removable storage.

StorageRequireMobileDeviceEncryption.d: Indicating whether or not to require encryption on a mobile device.

StorageRestrictAppDataToSystemVolume.d: Indicates whether application data is restricted to the system drive.

StorageRestrictAppInstallToSystemVolume.d: Indicates whether the installation of applications is restricted to the system drive.

SystemTelemetryProxyServer.d: Gets or sets the fully qualified domain name (FQDN) or IP address of a proxy server to forward Connected User Experiences and Telemetry requests.

TaskManagerBlockEndTask.d: Specify whether non-administrators can use Task Manager to end tasks.

TenantLockdownRequireNetworkDuringOutOfB.d: Whether the device is required to connect to the network.

UninstallBuiltInApps.d: Indicates whether or not to uninstall a fixed list of built-in Windows apps.

UsbBlocked.d: Indicates whether or not to Block the user from USB connection.

VoiceRecordingBlocked.d: Indicates whether or not to Block the user from voice recording.

WebRtcBlockLocalhostIpAddress.d: Indicates whether or not user's localhost IP address is displayed while making phone calls using the WebRTC

WiFiBlockAutomaticConnectHotspots.d: Indicating whether or not to block automatically connecting to Wi-Fi hotspots. Has no impact if Wi-Fi is blocked.

WiFiBlockManualConfiguration.d: Indicates whether or not to Block the user from using Wi-Fi manual configuration.

WiFiBlocked.d: Indicates whether or not to Block the user from using Wi-Fi.

WiFiScanInterval.d: Specify how often devices scan for Wi-Fi networks. Supported values are 1-500, where 100 = default, and 500 = low frequency. Valid values 1 to 500

Windows10AppsForceUpdateSchedule.d: Windows 10 force update schedule for Apps.

WindowsSpotlightBlockConsumerSpecificFea.d: Allows IT admins to block experiences that are typically for consumers only, such as Start suggestions, Membership notifications, Post-OOBE app install and redirect tiles.

WindowsSpotlightBlockOnActionCenter.d: Block suggestions from Microsoft that show after each OS clean install, upgrade or in an on-going basis to introduce users to what is new or changed

WindowsSpotlightBlockTailoredExperiences.d: Block personalized content in Windows spotlight based on users device usage.

WindowsSpotlightBlockThirdPartyNotificat.d: Block third party content delivered via Windows Spotlight

WindowsSpotlightBlockWelcomeExperience.d: Block Windows Spotlight Windows welcome experience

WindowsSpotlightBlockWindowsTips.d: Allows IT admins to turn off the popup of Windows Tips.

WindowsSpotlightBlocked.d: Allows IT admins to turn off all Windows Spotlight features

WindowsSpotlightConfigureOnLockScreen.d: Specifies the type of Spotlight. Possible values are: notConfigured, disabled, enabled.

WindowsStoreBlockAutoUpdate.d: Indicates whether or not to block automatic update of apps from Windows Store.

WindowsStoreBlocked.d: Indicates whether or not to Block the user from using the Windows store.

WindowsStoreEnablePrivateStoreOnly.d: Indicates whether or not to enable Private Store Only.

WirelessDisplayBlockProjectionToThisDevi.d: Indicates whether or not to allow other devices from discovering this PC for projection.

WirelessDisplayBlockUserInputFromReceive.d: Indicates whether or not to allow user input from wireless display receiver.

WirelessDisplayRequirePinForPairing.d: Indicates whether or not to require a PIN for new devices to initiate pairing.

IntuneDeviceConfigurationPolicyiOS.d: This resource configures an Intune Device Configuration Profile for an iOS Device.

collectionId.d: The collection Id that is the target of the assignment.(ConfigMgr)

dataType.d: The type of the target assignment.

deviceAndAppManagementAssignmentFilterDi.d: The display name of the filter for the target assignment.

deviceAndAppManagementAssignmentFilterId.d: The Id of the filter for the target assignment.

deviceAndAppManagementAssignmentFilterTy.d: The type of filter of the target assignment i.e. Exclude or Include. Possible values are:none, include, exclude.

groupDisplayName.d: The group Display Name that is the target of the assignment.

groupId.d: The group Id that is the target of the assignment.

IntuneDeviceConfigurationSCEPCertificate.d: This resource configures an Intune Device Configuration Scep Certificate Policy for Windows10.

Assignments.d: Represents the assignment to the Intune policy.

CertificateStore.d: Target store certificate. Possible values are: user, machine.

CertificateValidityPeriodScale.d: Scale for the Certificate Validity Period. Possible values are: days, months, years.

CertificateValidityPeriodValue.d: Value for the Certificate Validity Period

CustomSubjectAlternativeNames.d: Custom Subject Alternative Name Settings. This collection can contain a maximum of 500 elements.

Description.d: Admin provided description of the Device Configuration.

DisplayName.d: Admin provided name of the device configuration.

ExtendedKeyUsages.d: Extended Key Usage (EKU) settings. This collection can contain a maximum of 500 elements.

HashAlgorithm.d: SCEP Hash Algorithm. Possible values are: sha1, sha2.

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

KeySize.d: SCEP Key Size. Possible values are: size1024, size2048, size4096.

KeyStorageProvider.d: Key Storage Provider (KSP). Possible values are: useTpmKspOtherwiseUseSoftwareKsp, useTpmKspOtherwiseFail, usePassportForWorkKspOtherwiseFail, useSoftwareKsp.

KeyUsage.d: SCEP Key Usage. Possible values are: keyEncipherment, digitalSignature.

RenewalThresholdPercentage.d: Certificate renewal threshold percentage. Valid values 1 to 99

RoleScopeTagIds.d: List of Scope Tags for this Entity instance.

SubjectAlternativeNameFormatString.d: Custom String that defines the AAD Attribute.

SubjectAlternativeNameType.d: Certificate Subject Alternative Name Type. Possible values are: none, emailAddress, userPrincipalName, customAzureADAttribute, domainNameService, universalResourceIdentifier.

SubjectNameFormat.d: Certificate Subject Name Format. Possible values are: commonName, commonNameIncludingEmail, commonNameAsEmail, custom, commonNameAsIMEI, commonNameAsSerialNumber, commonNameAsAadDeviceId, commonNameAsIntuneDeviceId, commonNameAsDurableDeviceId.

SubjectNameFormatString.d: Custom format to use with SubjectNameFormat = Custom. Example: CN={{UserName}},E={{EmailAddress}},OU=Enterprise Users,O=Contoso Corporation,L=Redmond,ST=WA,C=US

IntuneDeviceConfigurationSecureAssessmen.d: This resource configures an Intune Device Configuration Secure Assessment Policy for Windows10.

AllowPrinting.d: Indicates whether or not to allow the app from printing during the test.

AllowScreenCapture.d: Indicates whether or not to allow screen capture capability during a test.

AllowTextSuggestion.d: Indicates whether or not to allow text suggestions during the test.

AssessmentAppUserModelId.d: Specifies the application user model ID of the assessment app launched when a user signs in to a secure assessment with a local guest account. Important notice: this property must be set with localGuestAccountName in order to make the local guest account sign-in experience work properly for secure assessments.

Assignments.d: Represents the assignment to the Intune policy.

ConfigurationAccount.d: The account used to configure the Windows device for taking the test. The user can be a domain account (domain/user), an AAD account (usernametenant.com) or a local account (username).

ConfigurationAccountType.d: The account type used to by ConfigurationAccount. Possible values are: azureADAccount, domainAccount, localAccount, localGuestAccount.

Description.d: Admin provided description of the Device Configuration.

DisplayName.d: Admin provided name of the device configuration.

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

LaunchUri.d: Url link to an assessment that's automatically loaded when the secure assessment browser is launched. It has to be a valid Url (https://msdn.microsoft.com/).

LocalGuestAccountName.d: Specifies the display text for the local guest account shown on the sign-in screen. Typically is the name of an assessment. When the user clicks the local guest account on the sign-in screen, an assessment app is launched with a specified assessment URL. Secure assessments can only be configured with local guest account sign-in on devices running Windows 10, version 1903 or later. Important notice: this property must be set with assessmentAppUserModelID in order to make the local guest account sign-in experience work properly for secure assessments.

RoleScopeTagIds.d: List of Scope Tags for this Entity instance.

IntuneDeviceConfigurationSharedMultiDevi.d: This resource configures an Intune Device Configuration Shared Multi Device Policy for Windows10.

AccountManagerPolicy.d: Specifies how accounts are managed on a shared PC. Only applies when disableAccountManager is false.

AllowLocalStorage.d: Specifies whether local storage is allowed on a shared PC.

AllowedAccounts.d: Indicates which type of accounts are allowed to use on a shared PC. Possible values are: notConfigured, guest, domain.

Assignments.d: Represents the assignment to the Intune policy.

Description.d: Admin provided description of the Device Configuration.

DisableAccountManager.d: Disables the account manager for shared PC mode.

DisableEduPolicies.d: Specifies whether the default shared PC education environment policies should be disabled. For Windows 10 RS2 and later, this policy will be applied without setting Enabled to true.

DisablePowerPolicies.d: Specifies whether the default shared PC power policies should be disabled.

DisableSignInOnResume.d: Disables the requirement to sign in whenever the device wakes up from sleep mode.

DisplayName.d: Admin provided name of the device configuration.

Enabled.d: Enables shared PC mode and applies the shared pc policies.

FastFirstSignIn.d: Specifies whether to auto connect new non-admin Azure AD accounts to pre-configured candidate local accounts. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

IdleTimeBeforeSleepInSeconds.d: Specifies the time in seconds that a device must sit idle before the PC goes to sleep. Setting this value to 0 prevents the sleep timeout from occurring.

KioskAppDisplayName.d: Specifies the display text for the account shown on the sign-in screen which launches the app specified by SetKioskAppUserModelId. Only applies when KioskAppUserModelId is set.

KioskAppUserModelId.d: Specifies the application user model ID of the app to use with assigned access.

LocalStorage.d: Specifies whether local storage is allowed on a shared PC. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

MaintenanceStartTime.d: Specifies the daily start time of maintenance hour.

RoleScopeTagIds.d: List of Scope Tags for this Entity instance.

SetAccountManager.d: Disables the account manager for shared PC mode. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

SetEduPolicies.d: Specifies whether the default shared PC education environment policies should be enabled/disabled/not configured. For Windows 10 RS2 and later, this policy will be applied without setting Enabled to true. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

SetPowerPolicies.d: Specifies whether the default shared PC power policies should be enabled/disabled. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

SignInOnResume.d: Specifies the requirement to sign in whenever the device wakes up from sleep mode. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

IntuneDeviceConfigurationTrustedCertific.d: This resource configures an Intune Device Configuration Trusted Certificate Policy for Windows10.

Assignments.d: Represents the assignment to the Intune policy.

CertFileName.d: File name to display in UI.

Description.d: Admin provided description of the Device Configuration.

DestinationStore.d: Destination store location for the Trusted Root Certificate. Possible values are: computerCertStoreRoot, computerCertStoreIntermediate, userCertStoreIntermediate.

DisplayName.d: Admin provided name of the device configuration.

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

RoleScopeTagIds.d: List of Scope Tags for this Entity instance.

IntuneDeviceConfigurationVpnPolicyWindow.d: This resource configures an Intune Device Configuration Vpn Policy for Windows10.

Assignments.d: Represents the assignment to the Intune policy.

AssociatedApps.d: Associated Apps. This collection can contain a maximum of 10000 elements.

AuthenticationMethod.d: Authentication method. Possible values are: certificate, usernameAndPassword, customEapXml, derivedCredential.

ConnectionName.d: Connection name displayed to the user.

ConnectionType.d: Connection type. Possible values are: pulseSecure, f5EdgeClient, dellSonicWallMobileConnect, checkPointCapsuleVpn, automatic, ikEv2, l2tp, pptp, citrix, paloAltoGlobalProtect, ciscoAnyConnect, unknownFutureValue, microsoftTunnel.

CryptographySuite.d: Cryptography Suite security settings for IKEv2 VPN in Windows10 and above

CustomXml.d: Custom XML commands that configures the VPN connection. (UTF8 encoded byte array)

Description.d: Admin provided description of the Device Configuration.

DisplayName.d: Admin provided name of the device configuration.

DnsRules.d: DNS rules. This collection can contain a maximum of 1000 elements.

DnsSuffixes.d: Specify DNS suffixes to add to the DNS search list to properly route short names.

EapXml.d: Extensible Authentication Protocol (EAP) XML. (UTF8 encoded byte array)

EnableDnsRegistration.d: Enable IP address registration with internal DNS.

EnableSingleSignOnWithAlternateCertifica.d: Enable single sign-on (SSO) with alternate certificate.

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

MicrosoftTunnelSiteId.d: ID of the Microsoft Tunnel site associated with the VPN profile.

OnlyAssociatedAppsCanUseConnection.d: Only associated Apps can use connection (per-app VPN).

ProfileTarget.d: Profile target type. Possible values are: user, device, autoPilotDevice.

RoleScopeTagIds.d: List of Scope Tags for this Entity instance.

AuthenticationType.d: Specify whether to authenticate the user, the device, either, or to use guest authentication (none). Possible values are: none, user, machine, machineOrUser, guest, unknownFutureValue.

EapType.d: Extensible Authentication Protocol (EAP). Indicates the type of EAP protocol set on the Wi-Fi endpoint (router). Possible values are: eapTls, leap, eapSim, eapTtls, peap, eapFast, teap. Possible values are: eapTls, leap, eapSim, eapTtls, peap, eapFast, teap.

EapolStartPeriodInSeconds.d: Specify the number of seconds to wait before sending an EAPOL (Extensible Authentication Protocol over LAN) Start message. Valid range 1-3600.

ForceFIPSCompliance.d: When TRUE, forces FIPS compliance. When FALSE, does not enable FIPS compliance. Default value is FALSE.

MaximumEAPOLStartMessages.d: Specify the maximum number of EAPOL (Extensible Authentication Protocol over LAN) Start messages to be sent before returning failure. Valid range 1-100.

OuterIdentityPrivacyTemporaryValue.d: Specify the string to replace usernames for privacy when using EAP TTLS or PEAP.

IntuneDeviceControlPolicySetting.d: This resource configures an Intune Device Control Policy Setting.

Description.d: Description of the setting.

DisplayName.d: Display Name of the setting.

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

MatchType.d: The match type of the policy settings. Possible values: Any, All

PrinterPolicySettings.d: The Printer Device Control policy settings.

StoragePolicySettings.d: The Storage Device Control policy settings.

IntuneDeviceControlPolicyWindows10.d: This resource configures an Intune Device Control Policy for Windows10.

AllowAdvertising.d: Allow Advertising (0: Not allowed. When set to 0, the device will not send out advertisements. To verify, use any Bluetooth LE app and enable it to do advertising. Then, verify that the advertisement is not received by the peripheral., 1: Allowed. When set to 1, the device will send out advertisements. To verify, use any Bluetooth LE app and enable it to do advertising. Then, verify that the advertisement is received by the peripheral.)

AllowBluetooth.d: Allow Bluetooth (0: Disallow Bluetooth. If this is set to 0, the radio in the Bluetooth control panel will be grayed out and the user will not be able to turn Bluetooth on., 1: Reserved. If this is set to 1, the radio in the Bluetooth control panel will be functional and the user will be able to turn Bluetooth on., 2: Allow Bluetooth. If this is set to 2, the radio in the Bluetooth control panel will be functional and the user will be able to turn Bluetooth on.)

AllowDirectMemoryAccess.d: Allow Direct Memory Access (0: Not allowed., 1: Allowed.)

AllowDiscoverableMode.d: Allow Discoverable Mode (0: Not allowed. When set to 0, other devices will not be able to detect the device. To verify, open the Bluetooth control panel on the device. Then, go to another Bluetooth-enabled device, open the Bluetooth control panel, and verify that you cannot see the name of the device., 1: Allowed. When set to 1, other devices will be able to detect the device. To verify, open the Bluetooth control panel on the device. Then, go to another Bluetooth-enabled device, open the Bluetooth control panel and verify that you can discover it.)

AllowFullScanRemovableDriveScanning.d: Allow Full Scan Removable Drive Scanning (0: Not allowed. Turns off scanning on removable drives., 1: Allowed. Scans removable drives.)

AllowPromptedProximalConnections.d: Allow Prompted Proximal Connections (0: Disallow. Block users on these managed devices from using Swift Pair and other proximity based scenarios, 1: Allow. Allow users on these managed devices to use Swift Pair and other proximity based scenarios)

AllowStorageCard.d: Allow Storage Card (0: SD card use is not allowed and USB drives are disabled. This setting does not prevent programmatic access to the storage card., 1: Allow a storage card.)

AllowUSBConnection.d: Allow USB Connection (0: Not allowed., 1: Allowed.)

Assignments.d: Represents the assignment to the Intune policy.

DefaultEnforcement.d: Default Enforcement (1: Default Allow Enforcement, 2: Default Deny Enforcement)

Description.d: Policy description

DeviceControlEnabled.d: Device Control Enabled (0: Device Control is disabled, 1: Device Control is enabled)

DeviceEnumerationPolicy.d: Device Enumeration Policy (0: Block all (Most restrictive), 1: Only after log in/screen unlock, 2: Allow all (Least restrictive))

DeviceInstall_Allow_Deny_Layered.d: Apply layered order of evaluation for Allow and Prevent device installation policies across all device match criteria (0: Disabled, 1: Enabled)

DeviceInstall_Classes_Allow.d: Allow installation of devices using drivers that match these device setup classes (0: Disabled, 1: Enabled)

DeviceInstall_Classes_Deny.d: Prevent installation of devices using drivers that match these device setup classes (0: Disabled, 1: Enabled)

DeviceInstall_Classes_Deny_Retroactive.d: Also apply to matching devices that are already installed. (0: False, 1: True)

DeviceInstall_IDs_Allow.d: Allow installation of devices that match any of these device IDs (0: Disabled, 1: Enabled)

DeviceInstall_IDs_Deny.d: Prevent installation of devices that match any of these device IDs (0: Disabled, 1: Enabled)

DeviceInstall_IDs_Deny_Retroactive.d: Also apply to matching devices that are already installed. (0: False, 1: True)

DeviceInstall_Instance_IDs_Allow.d: Allow installation of devices that match any of these device instance IDs (0: Disabled, 1: Enabled)

DeviceInstall_Instance_IDs_Deny.d: Prevent installation of devices that match any of these device instance IDs (0: Disabled, 1: Enabled)

DeviceInstall_Instance_IDs_Deny_Retroact.d: Also apply to matching devices that are already installed. (Device) (0: False, 1: True)

DeviceInstall_Removable_Deny.d: Prevent installation of removable devices (0: Disabled, 1: Enabled)

DeviceInstall_Unspecified_Deny.d: Prevent installation of devices not described by other policy settings (0: Disabled, 1: Enabled)

DisplayName.d: Policy name

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

PolicyRule.d: The list of policy rules to apply.

RemovableDiskDenyWriteAccess.d: Removable Disk Deny Write Access (0: Disabled., 1: Enabled.)

RoleScopeTagIds.d: List of Scope Tags for this Entity instance.

WPDDevices_DenyRead_Access_1.d: WPD Devices: Deny read access (User) (0: Disabled, 1: Enabled)

WPDDevices_DenyRead_Access_2.d: WPD Devices: Deny read access (0: Disabled, 1: Enabled)

WPDDevices_DenyWrite_Access_1.d: WPD Devices: Deny write access (User) (0: Disabled, 1: Enabled)

WPDDevices_DenyWrite_Access_2.d: WPD Devices: Deny write access (0: Disabled, 1: Enabled)

IntuneMobileAppsManagedGooglePlayApp.d: This resource configures an Intune Mobile Apps Managed Google Play Store App for Android.

Assignments.d: Represents the assignment to the Intune policy.

DisplayName.d: The admin provided or imported title of the app.

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

PackageId.d: The package identifier. Required to sync from Google Play Store. Cannot be changed after creation.

RoleScopeTagIds.d: List of scope tag ids for this mobile app.

IntuneMobileAppsMicrosoft365SuiteMacOS.d: This resource configures an Intune Mobile Apps Microsoft365 Suite for macOS.

Assignments.d: Represents the assignment to the Intune policy.

Categories.d: The list of categories for this app.

Description.d: The description of the app.

DisplayName.d: The admin provided or imported title of the app.

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

IsFeatured.d: The value indicating whether the app is marked as featured by the admin.

LargeIcon.d: The large icon, to be displayed in the app details and used for upload of the icon.

RoleScopeTagIds.d: List of scope tag ids for this mobile app.

IntuneMobileAppsMicrosoftEdge.d: This resource configures an Intune Mobile Apps Microsoft Edge for the Windows and MacOS platform.

Assignments.d: Represents the assignment to the Intune policy.

Categories.d: The list of categories for this app.

Channel.d: The channel to install on target devices. Cannot be updated after creation. Instead, a new policy must be created. The possible values are dev, beta, and stable. By default, this property is set to dev. Possible values are: dev, beta, stable, unknownFutureValue.

Description.d: The description of the app.

DisplayLanguageLocale.d: The language locale to use when the Edge app displays text to the user. Only applicable for the 'windows' target platform.

DisplayName.d: The admin provided or imported title of the app.

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

IsFeatured.d: The value indicating whether the app is marked as featured by the admin.

LargeIcon.d: The large icon, to be displayed in the app details and used for upload of the icon.

RoleScopeTagIds.d: List of scope tag ids for this mobile app.

TargetPlatform.d: The target platform of the mobile app.

IntuneMobileAppsMicrosoftStoreAppWindows.d: This resource configures an Intune Mobile Apps Microsoft Store App for Windows10 (new experience).

Assignments.d: Represents the assignment to the Intune policy.

Categories.d: The list of categories for this app.

Description.d: The description of the app.

DisplayName.d: The admin provided or imported title of the app.

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

InstallExperience.d: The install experience settings associated with this application, which are used to ensure the desired install experiences on the target device are taken into account. This includes the account type (System or User) that actions should be run as on target devices. Cannot be changed after creation.

IsFeatured.d: The value indicating whether the app is marked as featured by the admin.

LargeIcon.d: The large icon, to be displayed in the app details and used for upload of the icon.

PackageIdentifier.d: The PackageIdentifier from the WinGet source repository REST API. This also maps to the Id when using the WinGet client command line application. Required at creation time, cannot be modified on existing objects.

RoleScopeTagIds.d: List of scope tag ids for this mobile app.

IntuneMobileAppsStoreApp.d: This resource configures an Intune Mobile Apps Store App for Android and iOS.

AppStoreUrl.d: The App Store URL. Cannot be changed after creation.

ApplicableDeviceType.d: The architecture for which this app can run on. Only applicable for the 'iOS' TargetPlatform.

Assignments.d: Represents the assignment to the Intune policy.

BundleId.d: The Identity Name. Only applicable for the 'iOS' TargetPlatform.

Categories.d: The list of categories for this app.

Description.d: The description of the app.

DisplayName.d: The admin provided or imported title of the app.

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

IsFeatured.d: The value indicating whether the app is marked as featured by the admin.

LargeIcon.d: The large icon, to be displayed in the app details and used for upload of the icon.

MinimumSupportedOperatingSystem.d: The value for the minimum applicable operating system.

RoleScopeTagIds.d: List of scope tag ids for this mobile app.

TargetPlatform.d: The target platform of the mobile app.

IntuneMobileAppsSystemAppAndroid.d: This resource configures an Intune Mobile Apps System App for Android.

AppIdentifier.d: The app identifier. Cannot be changed after creation.

Assignments.d: Represents the assignment to the Intune policy.

DisplayName.d: The admin provided or imported title of the app.

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

RoleScopeTagIds.d: List of scope tag ids for this mobile app.

IntuneMobileAppsWebLink.d: This resource configures an Intune Mobile Apps Web Link for the iOS, MacOS, Windows and general platform.

AppUrl.d: Indicates the web app URL. Example: 'https://www.contoso.com'. Cannot be updated after creation.

Assignments.d: Represents the assignment to the Intune policy.

Categories.d: The list of categories for this app.

Description.d: The description of the app.

DisplayName.d: The admin provided or imported title of the app.

FullScreenEnabled.d: Whether or not to open the web clip as a full-screen web app. Defaults to false. If TRUE, opens the web clip as a full-screen web app. If FALSE, the web clip opens inside of another app such as Safary or the app specified with targetApplicationBundleIdentifier. Only applicable for the 'iosiPadOSWebClip' and 'macOSWebClip' target type.

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

IgnoreManifestScope.d: Whether or not a full screen web clip can navigate to an external web site without showing the Safari UI. Defaults to false. If FALSE, the Safari UI appears when navigating away. If TRUE, the Safari UI will not be shown. Only applicable for the 'iosiPadOSWebClip' target type.

IsFeatured.d: The value indicating whether the app is marked as featured by the admin.

LargeIcon.d: The large icon, to be displayed in the app details and used for upload of the icon.

PreComposedIconEnabled.d: Whether or not the icon for the app is precomosed. Defaults to false. If TRUE, prevents SpringBoard from adding 'shine' to the icon. If FALSE, SpringBoard can add 'shine'. Only applicable for the 'iosiPadOSWebClip' and 'macOSWebClip' target type.

RoleScopeTagIds.d: List of scope tag ids for this mobile app.

TargetApplicationBundleIdentifier.d: Specifies the application bundle identifier which opens the URL. Available in iOS 14 and later. Only applicable for the 'iosiPadOSWebClip' target type.

TargetType.d: The target type of the web link.

UseManagedBrowser.d: Whether or not to use managed browser (on iOS it's Microsoft Edge). This property is only applicable 'webApp' and 'iosiPadOSWebClip' target type.

IntuneMobileAppsWin32AppWindows10.d: This resource configures an Intune Mobile Apps Win32 App for Windows10.

AllowAvailableUninstall.d: Indicates whether the uninstall is supported from the company portal for the Win32 app with an available assignment. When TRUE, indicates that uninstall is supported from the company portal for the Windows app (Win32) with an available assignment. When FALSE, indicates that uninstall is not supported for the Windows app (Win32) with an Available assignment.

AllowedArchitectures.d: Indicates the Windows architecture(s) this app should be installed on. The app will be treated as not applicable for devices with architectures not matching the selected value. The value 'none' cannot be combined with other values.

Assignments.d: Represents the assignment to the Intune policy.

Categories.d: The list of categories for this app.

Description.d: The description of the app.

DisplayName.d: The admin provided or imported title of the app.

DisplayVersion.d: Indicates the version displayed in the UX for this app. Used to set the version of the app. Example: 1.0.3.215.

FileName.d: The file name of the app. Required for creating the resource.

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

InstallCommandLine.d: Indicates the command line to install this app. Used to install the Win32 app. Example: msiexec /i 'Orca.Msi' /qn.

InstallExperience.d: Indicates the install experience for this app.

IsFeatured.d: The value indicating whether the app is marked as featured by the admin.

LargeIcon.d: The large icon, to be displayed in the app details and used for upload of the icon.

MinimumCpuSpeedInMHz.d: Indicates the value for the minimum CPU speed which is required to install this app.

MinimumFreeDiskSpaceInMB.d: Indicates the value for the minimum free disk space which is required to install this app.

MinimumMemoryInMB.d: Indicates the value for the minimum physical memory which is required to install this app.

MinimumNumberOfProcessors.d: Indicates the value for the minimum number of processors which is required to install this app.

MinimumSupportedWindowsRelease.d: Indicates the value for the minimum supported windows release. Format for Windows 10: <VersionNumber>, e.g. '1709' or '20H2'. For Windows 11: 'Windows11_<VersionNumber>', e.g. 'Windows11_21H2'

MsiInformation.d: Indicates the MSI details if this Win32 app is an MSI app.

ReturnCodes.d: Indicates the return codes for post installation behavior.

RoleScopeTagIds.d: List of scope tag ids for this mobile app.

Rules.d: Indicates the detection and requirement rules for this app.

SetupFilePath.d: Indicates the relative path of the setup file in the encrypted Win32LobApp package.

UninstallCommandLine.d: Indicates the command line to uninstall this app. Used to uninstall the app. Example: msiexec /x '{85F4CBCB-9BBC-4B50-A7D8-E1106771498D}' /qn.

IntuneMobileAppsWindowsOfficeSuiteApp.d: This resource configures an Intune mobile app.

Assignments.d: The list of assignments for this app.

AutoAcceptEula.d: Specifies if the EULA is accepted automatically on the end user's device.

Categories.d: The list of categories for this app.

Description.d: The description of the app. Inherited from mobileApp.

DisplayName.d: The admin provided or imported title of the app. Inherited from mobileApp.

ExcludedApps.d: The property that represents the apps excluded from the selected Office 365 Product ID.

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only. Inherited from mobileApp object.

InformationUrl.d: The InformationUrl of the app. Inherited from mobileApp.

InstallProgressDisplayLevel.d: Specifies the display level of the installation progress for Office apps. Use 'Full' to display the installation UI, or 'None' for a silent installation.

IsFeatured.d: The value indicating whether the app is marked as featured by the admin. Inherited from mobileApp.

LocalesToInstall.d: Specifies the locales to be installed when the Office 365 apps are deployed. Uses the standard RFC 5646 format (e.g., 'en-US', 'fr-FR').

Notes.d: Notes for the app. Inherited from mobileApp.

OfficeConfigurationXml.d: A base64-encoded XML configuration file that specifies Office ProPlus installation settings. Takes precedence over all other properties. When present, this XML file will be used to create the app.

OfficePlatformArchitecture.d: The architecture of the Office installation (e.g., 'X86', 'X64', or 'Arm64'). Cannot be changed after creation.

OfficeSuiteAppDefaultFileFormat.d: Specifies the default file format type for Office apps, such as 'OfficeOpenXMLFormat' or 'OfficeOpenDocumentFormat'.

PrivacyInformationUrl.d: The privacy statement Url. Inherited from mobileApp.

ProductIds.d: The Product IDs that represent the Office 365 Suite SKU, such as 'O365ProPlusRetail' or 'VisioProRetail'.

RoleScopeTagIds.d: List of Scope Tag IDs for mobile app.

ShouldUninstallOlderVersionsOfOffice.d: Indicates whether older versions of Office should be uninstalled when deploying the Office 365 app suite.

TargetVersion.d: The specific target version of the Office 365 app suite to be deployed.

UpdateChannel.d: Specifies the update channel for the Office 365 app suite, such as 'Current' or 'Deferred'.

UpdateVersion.d: The update version in which the target version is available for the Office 365 app suite.

UseSharedComputerActivation.d: Indicates whether shared computer activation is used for Office installations.

IntuneMobileThreatDefenseConnector.d: This resource configures a connection to Mobile Threat Defense partner.

AllowPartnerToCollectIOSPersonalApplicat.d: When TRUE, indicates the Mobile Threat Defense partner may collect metadata about personally installed applications from Intune for IOS devices. When FALSE, indicates the Mobile Threat Defense partner may not collect metadata about personally installed applications from Intune for IOS devices. Default value is FALSE.

AllowPartnerToCollectIosApplicationMetad.d: When TRUE, indicates the Mobile Threat Defense partner may collect metadata about installed applications from Intune for IOS devices. When FALSE, indicates the Mobile Threat Defense partner may not collect metadata about installed applications from Intune for IOS devices. Default value is FALSE.

AndroidDeviceBlockedOnMissingPartnerData.d: For Android, set whether Intune must receive data from the Mobile Threat Defense partner prior to marking a device compliant.

AndroidEnabled.d: For Android, set whether data from the Mobile Threat Defense partner should be used during compliance evaluations.

AndroidMobileApplicationManagementEnable.d: When TRUE, indicates that data from the Mobile Threat Defense partner can be used during Mobile Application Management (MAM) evaluations for Android devices. When FALSE, indicates that data from the Mobile Threat Defense partner should not be used during Mobile Application Management (MAM) evaluations for Android devices. Only one partner per platform may be enabled for Mobile Application Management (MAM) evaluation. Default value is FALSE.

DisplayName.d: The DisplayName of the Mobile Threat Defense Connector partner. NOTE: Hard coded for convenience, not returned by the Graph API.

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

IosDeviceBlockedOnMissingPartnerData.d: For IOS, set whether Intune must receive data from the Mobile Threat Defense partner prior to marking a device compliant.

IosEnabled.d: For IOS, get or set whether data from the Mobile Threat Defense partner should be used during compliance evaluations.

IosMobileApplicationManagementEnabled.d: When TRUE, indicates that data from the Mobile Threat Defense partner can be used during Mobile Application Management (MAM) evaluations for IOS devices. When FALSE, indicates that data from the Mobile Threat Defense partner should not be used during Mobile Application Management (MAM) evaluations for IOS devices. Only one partner per platform may be enabled for Mobile Application Management (MAM) evaluation. Default value is FALSE.

LastHeartbeatDateTime.d: DateTime of last Heartbeat received from the Mobile Threat Defense partner.

MicrosoftDefenderForEndpointAttachEnable.d: When TRUE, indicates that configuration profile management via Microsoft Defender for Endpoint is enabled. When FALSE, inidicates that configuration profile management via Microsoft Defender for Endpoint is disabled. Default value is FALSE.

PartnerUnresponsivenessThresholdInDays.d: Get or Set days the per tenant tolerance to unresponsiveness for this partner integration.

PartnerUnsupportedOSVersionBlocked.d: Get or set whether to block devices on the enabled platforms that do not meet the minimum version requirements of the Mobile Threat Defense partner.

WindowsDeviceBlockedOnMissingPartnerData.d: When TRUE, indicates that Intune must receive data from the Mobile Threat Defense partner prior to marking a device compliant for Windows. When FALSE, indicates that Intune may make a device compliant without receiving data from the Mobile Threat Defense partner for Windows. Default value is FALSE.

WindowsEnabled.d: When TRUE, indicates that data from the Mobile Threat Defense partner can be used during compliance evaluations for Windows. When FALSE, it indicates that data from the Mobile Threat Defense partner should not be used during compliance evaluations for Windows. Default value is FALSE.

IntunePolicySets.d: This resource configures an Intune Policy Sets.

Assignments.d: Represents the assignment to the Intune policy.

Description.d: Description of the PolicySet.

DisplayName.d: DisplayName of the PolicySet.

Items.d: Represents the assignment to the Intune policy.

IntuneRoleAssignment.d: This resource configures an Intune Role Assignment.

Description.d: Description of the Role Assignment.

DisplayName.d: The display or friendly name of the role Assignment.

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

Members.d: The list of ids of role member security groups. These are IDs from Azure Active Directory.

MembersDisplayNames.d: The list of Displaynames of role member security groups. These are Displaynamnes from Azure Active Directory.

ResourceScopes.d: List of ids of role scope member security groups. These are IDs from Azure Active Directory. Ignored if ScopeType is not 'ResourceScope'

ResourceScopesDisplayNames.d: List of DisplayName of role scope member security groups. These are Displayname from Azure Active Directory. Ignored if ScopeType is not 'ResourceScope'

ScopeType.d: Specifies the type of scope for a Role Assignment. Default type 'ResourceScope' allows assignment of ResourceScopes. Possible values are: resourceScope, allDevices, allLicensedUsers, allDevicesAndLicensedUsers.

IntuneRoleAssignmentWindows365.d: This resource configures an Intune Role Assignment Windows365.

AppScopeIds.d: Ids of the app specific scopes when the assignment scopes are app specific. The scopes of an assignment determine the set of resources for which the principal has access.

Description.d: Description of the role assignment.

DirectoryScopes.d: Display Names of the groups that represent the scopes of the assignment.

DisplayName.d: Name of the role assignment. Required.

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

Principals.d: Display name of the principals to which the assignment is granted.

RoleDefinition.d: Display name of the IntuneRoleDefinitionWindows365 the assignment is for.

IntuneRoleDefinition.d: This resource configures an Intune Role Definition.

Description.d: Description of the Role definition.

DisplayName.d: Display Name of the Role definition.

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

IsBuiltIn.d: Type of Role. Set to True if it is built-in, or set to False if it is a custom role definition.

NotAllowedResourceActions.d: List of not allowed resource actions

RoleScopeTagIds.d: Id of the Scope Tags to assign

IntuneRoleDefinitionWindows365.d: This resource configures an Intune Role Definition Windows365.

Description.d: The description for the unifiedRoleDefinition. Read-only when isBuiltIn is true.

DisplayName.d: The display name for the Windows 365 Role Definition.

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

IsBuiltIn.d: Flag indicating if the unifiedRoleDefinition is part of the default set included with the product or custom. Read-only. Supports $filter (eq).

RolePermissions.d: List of permissions included in the role. Read-only when isBuiltIn is true. Required.

IntuneRoleScopeTag.d: This resource configures an Intune Role Scope Tag.

Description.d: Description of the Role Scope Tag.

DisplayName.d: The display or friendly name of the Role Scope Tag.

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

AADGroupMembershipCacheValidityInDays.d: AAD Group Membership Cache Validity In Days

AllowAddProvisioningPackage.d: Allow Add Provisioning Package (0: Not allowed., 1: Allowed.)

AllowAllTrustedApps.d: Allow All Trusted Apps (0: Explicit deny., 1: Explicit allow unlock., 65535: Not configured.)

AllowAppStoreAutoUpdate.d: Allow apps from the Microsoft app store to auto update (0: Not allowed., 1: Allowed., 2: Not configured.)

AllowBluetooth.d: Allow Bluetooth (0: Disallow Bluetooth. If this is set to 0, the radio in the Bluetooth control panel will be grayed out and the user will not be able to turn Bluetooth on., 1: Reserved. If this is set to 1, the radio in the Bluetooth control panel will be functional and the user will be able to turn Bluetooth on., 2: Allow Bluetooth. If this is set to 2, the radio in the Bluetooth control panel will be functional and the user will be able to turn Bluetooth on.)

AllowCookies.d: Allow Cookies (0: Block all cookies from all sites, 1: Block only cookies from third party websites, 2: Allow all cookies from all sites)

AllowDeveloperUnlock.d: Allow Developer Unlock (0: Explicit deny., 1: Explicit allow unlock., 65535: Not configured.)

AllowDoNotTrack.d: Allow Do Not Track (0: Never send tracking information., 1: Send tracking information.)

AllowManualMDMUnenrollment.d: Allow Manual MDM Unenrollment (0: Not allowed., 1: Allowed.)

AllowManualWiFiConfiguration.d: Allow Manual Wi Fi Configuration (0: No Wi-Fi connection outside of MDM provisioned network is allowed., 1: Adding new network SSIDs beyond the already MDM provisioned ones is allowed.)

AllowMicrosoftAccountConnection.d: Allow Microsoft Account Connection (0: Not allowed., 1: Allowed.)

AllowPasswordManager.d: Allow Password Manager (0: Not allowed., 1: Allowed.)

AllowPopups.d: Allow Popups (0: Turn off Pop-up Blocker letting pop-up windows open., 1: Turn on Pop-up Blocker stopping pop-up windows from opening.)

AllowSearchSuggestionsinAddressBar.d: Allow Search Suggestionsin Address Bar (0: Prevented/Not allowed. Hide the search suggestions., 1: Allowed. Show the search suggestions.)

AllowSearchToUseLocation.d: Allow Search To Use Location (0: Not allowed., 1: Allowed.)

AllowSimpleDevicePassword.d: Allow Simple Device Password - Depends on DevicePasswordEnabled (0: Not allowed., 1: Allowed.)

AllowSmartScreen.d: Allow Smart Screen (0: Turned off. Do not protect users from potential threats and prevent users from turning it on., 1: Turned on. Protect users from potential threats and prevent users from turning it off.)

AllowStorageCard.d: Allow Storage Card (0: SD card use is not allowed and USB drives are disabled. This setting does not prevent programmatic access to the storage card., 1: Allow a storage card.)

AllowTelemetry.d: Allow Telemetry (0: Security. Information that is required to help keep Windows more secure, including data about the Connected User Experience and Telemetry component settings, the Malicious Software Removal Tool, and Windows Defender, 1: Basic. Basic device info, including: quality-related data, app compatibility, app usage data, and data from the Security level, 3: Full. All data necessary to identify and help to fix problems, plus data from the Security, Basic, and Enhanced levels.)

AllowUSBConnection.d: Allow USB Connection (0: Not allowed., 1: Allowed.)

AllowUpdateService.d: Allow Update Service (0: Not allowed., 1: Allowed.)

AlphanumericDevicePasswordRequired.d: Alphanumeric Device Password Required - Depends on DevicePasswordEnabled (0: Password or Alphanumeric PIN required., 1: Password or Numeric PIN required., 2: Password, Numeric PIN, or Alphanumeric PIN required.)

Assignments.d: Represents the assignment to the Intune policy.

AutofillAddressEnabled.d: Enable AutoFill for addresses (0: Disabled, 1: Enabled)

AutofillCreditCardEnabled.d: Enable AutoFill for payment instruments (0: Disabled, 1: Enabled)

BlockThirdPartyCookies.d: Block third party cookies (0: Disabled, 1: Enabled)

ConfigureDoNotTrack.d: Configure Do Not Track (0: Disabled, 1: Enabled)

DefaultPopupsSetting_DefaultPopupsSettin.d: Default pop-up window setting (Device) - Depends on MicrosoftEdge_ContentSettings_DefaultPopupsSetting (1: Allow all sites to show pop-ups, 2: Do not allow any site to show popups)

DeletionPolicy.d: Deletion Policy (0: Delete immediately upon device returning to a state with no currently active users, 1: Delete at storage capacity threshold, 2: Delete at both storage capacity threshold and profile inactivity threshold)

Description.d: Policy description

DevicePasswordExpiration.d: Device Password Expiration - Depends on DevicePasswordEnabled

DevicePasswordHistory.d: Device Password History - Depends on DevicePasswordEnabled

Digits.d: Digits - Depends on TenantId (0: Allows the use of digits in PIN., 1: Requires the use of at least one digits in PIN., 2: Does not allow the use of digits in PIN.)

DisplayName.d: Policy name

EnablePinRecovery.d: Enable Pin Recovery - Depends on TenantId (false: Disabled, true: Enabled)

EnableProfileManager.d: Enable Profile Manager (false: False, true: True)

EnterVideoACPowerDownTimeOut.d: When plugged in, turn display off after (seconds) - Depends on VideoPowerDownTimeOutAC_2

ExtensionInstallBlocklist.d: Control which extensions cannot be installed (0: Disabled, 1: Enabled)

ExtensionInstallBlocklistDesc.d: Extension IDs the user should be prevented from installing (or * for all) (Device) - Depends on ExtensionInstallBlocklist

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

LetAppsAccessAccountInfo.d: Let Apps Access Account Info (0: User in control., 1: Force allow., 2: Force deny.)

LetAppsAccessAccountInfo_ForceAllowThese.d: Let Apps Access Account Info Force Allow These Apps

LetAppsAccessBackgroundSpatialPerception.d: Let Apps Access Background Spatial Perception (0: User in control., 1: Force allow., 2: Force deny.)
Let Apps Access Background Spatial Perception Force Allow These Apps

LetAppsAccessCamera.d: Let Apps Access Camera (0: User in control., 1: Force allow., 2: Force deny.)

LetAppsAccessCamera_ForceAllowTheseApps.d: Let Apps Access Camera Force Allow These Apps

LetAppsAccessMicrophone.d: Let Apps Access Microphone (0: User in control., 1: Force allow., 2: Force deny.)

LetAppsAccessMicrophone_ForceAllowTheseA.d: Let Apps Access Microphone Force Allow These Apps

LowercaseLetters.d: Lowercase Letters - Depends on TenantId (0: Allows the use of lowercase letters in PIN., 1: Requires the use of at least one lowercase letters in PIN., 2: Does not allow the use of lowercase letters in PIN.)

ManagePreviewBuilds.d: Manage Preview Builds (0: Disable Preview builds, 1: Disable Preview builds once the next release is public, 2: Enable Preview builds, 3: Preview builds is left to user selection)

MaxDevicePasswordFailedAttempts.d: Max Device Password Failed Attempts - Depends on DevicePasswordEnabled

MaxInactivityTimeDeviceLock.d: Max Inactivity Time Device Lock - Depends on DevicePasswordEnabled

MaximumPINLength.d: Maximum PIN Length - Depends on TenantId

MicrosoftEdge_ContentSettings_DefaultPop.d: Default pop-up window setting (0: Disabled, 1: Enabled)
