MinDevicePasswordComplexCharacters.d: Min Device Password Complex Characters - Depends on DevicePasswordEnabled (1: Digits only, 2: Digits and lowercase letters are required, 3: Digits lowercase letters and uppercase letters are required. Not supported in desktop Microsoft accounts and domain accounts, 4: Digits lowercase letters uppercase letters and special characters are required. Not supported in desktop)

MinDevicePasswordLength.d: Min Device Password Length - Depends on DevicePasswordEnabled

MinimumPINLength.d: Minimum PIN Length - Depends on TenantId

PasswordManagerEnabled.d: Enable saving passwords to the password manager (0: Disabled, 1: Enabled)

PrimaryPasswordSetting_PrimaryPasswordSe.d: Configures a setting that asks users to enter their device password while using password autofill (Device) - Depends on MicrosoftEdge_PasswordManager_PrimaryPasswordSetting (0: Automatically, 1: With device password, 2: With custom primary password, 3: Autofill off)

RequireNetworkInOOBE.d: Require Network In OOBE (Device) (true: true, false: false)

RequireSecurityDevice.d: Require Security Device - Depends on TenantId (false: Disabled, true: Enabled)

RoleScopeTagIds.d: List of Scope Tags for this Entity instance.

SmartScreenEnabled.d: Configure Microsoft Defender SmartScreen (0: Disabled, 1: Enabled)

SpecialCharacters.d: Special Characters - Depends on TenantId (0: Allows the use of special characters in PIN., 1: Requires the use of at least one special characters in PIN., 2: Does not allow the use of special characters in PIN.)

IntuneSettingCatalogASRRulesPolicyWindow.d: This resource configures a Intune Endpoint Protection Attack Surface Reduction rules policy for a Windows 10 Device.

Assignments.d: Assignments of the endpoint protection.

AttackSurfaceReductionOnlyExclusions.d: Exclude files and paths from attack surface reduction rules

BlockAbuseOfExploitedVulnerableSignedDri.d: This rule prevents an application from writing a vulnerable signed driver to disk.

BlockAdobeReaderFromCreatingChildProcess.d: This rule prevents attacks by blocking Adobe Reader from creating processes.

BlockAllOfficeApplicationsFromCreatingCh.d: This rule blocks Office apps from creating child processes. Office apps include Word, Excel, PowerPoint, OneNote, and Access.
ASR Only Per Rule Exclusions (off: Off, block: Block, audit: Audit, warn: Warn)

BlockCredentialStealingFromWindowsLocalS.d: This rule helps prevent credential stealing by locking down Local Security Authority Subsystem Service (LSASS).

BlockExecutableContentFromEmailClientAnd.d: This rule blocks the following file types from launching from email opened within the Microsoft Outlook application, or Outlook.com and other popular webmail providers.

BlockExecutableFilesRunningUnlessTheyMee.d: This rule blocks executable files that don't meet a prevalence, age, or trusted list criteria, such as .exe, .dll, or .scr, from launching.

BlockExecutionOfPotentiallyObfuscatedScr.d: This rule detects suspicious properties within an obfuscated script.

BlockJavaScriptOrVBScriptFromLaunchingDo.d: This rule prevents scripts from launching potentially malicious downloaded content.

BlockOfficeApplicationsFromCreatingExecu.d: This rule prevents Office apps, including Word, Excel, and PowerPoint, from creating potentially malicious executable content, by blocking malicious code from being written to disk.

BlockOfficeApplicationsFromInjectingCode.d: This rule blocks code injection attempts from Office apps into other processes.

BlockOfficeCommunicationAppFromCreatingC.d: This rule prevents Outlook from creating child processes, while still allowing legitimate Outlook functions.

BlockPersistenceThroughWMIEventSubscript.d: This rule prevents malware from abusing WMI to attain persistence on a device.

BlockProcessCreationsFromPSExecAndWMICom.d: This rule blocks processes created through PsExec and WMI from running.

BlockRebootingMachineInSafeMode.d: This rule prevents the execution of commands to restart machines in Safe Mode.

BlockUntrustedUnsignedProcessesThatRunFr.d: With this rule, admins can prevent unsigned or untrusted executable files from running from USB removable drives, including SD cards.

BlockUseOfCopiedOrImpersonatedSystemTool.d: This rule blocks the use of executable files that are identified as copies of Windows system tools. These files are either duplicates or impostors of the original system tools.

BlockWebShellCreationForServers.d: This rule blocks webshell creation for servers.

BlockWin32APICallsFromOfficeMacros.d: This rule prevents VBA macros from calling Win32 APIs.

ControlledFolderAccessAllowedApplication.d: List of apps that have access to protected folders.

ControlledFolderAccessProtectedFolders.d: List of additional folders that need to be protected

Description.d: Description of the endpoint protection attack surface protection rules policy for Windows 10.

DisplayName.d: Display name of the endpoint protection attack surface protection rules policy for Windows 10.

EnableControlledFolderAccess.d: This rule enables Controlled folder access which protects your data by checking apps against a list of known, trusted apps. Possible values: 0=disable, 1=enable, 2=audit, 3=block disk modification only, 4=audit disk modification only

Identity.d: Identity of the endpoint protection attack surface protection rules policy for Windows 10.

RoleScopeTagIds.d: List of Scope Tags for this Entity instance.

UseAdvancedProtectionAgainstRansomware.d: This rule provides an extra layer of protection against ransomware.

CrossRegionDisasterRecoverySetting.d: Defines whether the user's Cloud PC enables cross-region disaster recovery and specifies the network for the disaster recovery.

DisplayName.d: The setting name displayed in the user interface.

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

LocalAdminEnabled.d: Indicates whether the local admin option is enabled. Default value is false. To enable the local admin option, change the setting to true. If the local admin option is enabled, the end user can be an admin of the Cloud PC device.

NotificationSetting.d: Defines the setting of the Cloud PC notification prompts for the Cloud PC user.

ResetEnabled.d: Indicates whether an end user is allowed to reset their Cloud PC. When true, the user is allowed to reset their Cloud PC. When false, end-user initiated reset isn't allowed. The default value is false.

RestorePointSetting.d: Defines how frequently a restore point is created (that is, a snapshot is taken) for users' provisioned Cloud PCs (default is 12 hours), and whether the user is allowed to restore their own Cloud PCs to a backup made at a specific point in time.

Assignments.d: Represents the assignment to the Intune policy.

Description.d: Description of the Intune policy.

DisplayName.d: Display name of the Intune policy.

Id.d: Id of the Intune policy.

RoleScopeTagIds.d: List of Scope Tags for this Entity instance.

associatedDomains.d: Associated Domains. These domains will be linked with the VPN configuration.

authenticationMethod.d: Authentication method for this VPN connection.

cloudName.d: Zscaler only. Zscaler cloud which the user is assigned to.

connectionName.d: Connection name displayed to the user.

connectionType.d: Connection type. Possible values are: ciscoAnyConnect, pulseSecure, f5EdgeClient, dellSonicWallMobileConnect, checkPointCapsuleVpn, customVpn, ciscoIPSec, citrix, ciscoAnyConnectV2, paloAltoGlobalProtect, zscalerPrivateAccess, f5Access2018, citrixSso, paloAltoGlobalProtectV2, ikEv2, alwaysOn, microsoftTunnel, netMotionMobility, microsoftProtect.

customData.d: Use this field to enable functionality not supported by Intune, but available in your VPN solution. Contact your VPN vendor to learn how to add these key/value pairs. This collection can contain a maximum of 25 elements

customKeyValueData.d: Use this field to enable functionality not supported by Intune, but available in your VPN solution. Contact your VPN vendor to learn how to add these key/value pairs. This collection can contain a maximum of 25 elements

disableOnDemandUserOverride.d: Toggle to prevent user from disabling automatic VPN in the Settings app Inherited from appleVpnConfiguration.

disconnectOnIdle.d: Whether to disconnect after on-demand connection idles Inherited from appleVpnConfiguration

disconnectOnIdleTimerInSeconds.d: The length of time in seconds to wait before disconnecting an on-demand connection. Valid values 0 to 65535 Inherited from appleVpnConfiguration.

enablePerApp.d: Setting this to true creates Per-App VPN payload which can later be associated with Apps that can trigger this VPN conneciton on the end user's iOS device. Inherited from appleVpnConfiguration.

enableSplitTunneling.d: Send all network traffic through VPN.

excludedDomains.d: Domains that are accessed through the public internet instead of through VPN, even when per-app VPN is activated.

identifier.d: Identifier provided by VPN vendor when connection type is set to Custom VPN. For example: Cisco AnyConnect uses an identifier of the form com.cisco.anyconnect.applevpn.plugin Inherited from appleVpnConfiguration.

loginGroupOrDomain.d: Login group or domain when connection type is set to Dell SonicWALL Mobile Connection. Inherited from appleVpnConfiguration.

onDemandRules.d: On-Demand Rules. This collection can contain a maximum of 500 elements.

optInToDeviceIdSharing.d: Opt-In to sharing the device's Id to third-party vpn clients for use during network access control validation.

providerType.d: Provider type for per-app VPN. Inherited from appleVpnConfiguration. Possible values are: notConfigured, appProxy, packetTunnel.

proxyServer.d: Represents the assignment to the Intune policy.

realm.d: Realm when connection type is set to Pulse Secure. Inherited from appleVpnConfiguration.

role.d: Role when connection type is set to Pulse Secure. Inherited from appleVpnConfiguration.

safariDomains.d: Safari domains when this VPN per App setting is enabled. In addition to the apps associated with this VPN, Safari domains specified here will also be able to trigger this VPN connection.

server.d: VPN Server on the network. Make sure end users can access this network location.

strictEnforcement.d: Zscaler only. Blocks network traffic until the user signs into Zscaler app. True means traffic is blocked.

userDomain.d: Zscaler only. Enter a static domain to pre-populate the login field with in the Zscaler app. If this is left empty, the user's Azure Active Directory domain will be used instead.

version.d: Version of the device configuration. Inherited from deviceConfiguration.

RoleScopeTagIds.d: List of Scope Tags for this Entity instance.

Timeout.d: Minutes allowed before showing installation error

IntuneWindowsBackupForOrganizationConfig.d: This resource configures an Intune Windows Backup For Organization Configuration.

State.d: Indicates the configuration state of the Windows Restore setting. Possible values are 'notConfigured', 'enabled', and 'disabled'. Default is: notConfigured. This is a tenant level default setting that is not targetable. This property's value is applied during Enrollment. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

IntuneWindowsHelloForBusinessGlobalPolic.d: This resource configures an Intune Windows Hello For Business Global Policy.

EnhancedBiometricsState.d: Controls the ability to use the anti-spoofing features for facial recognition on devices which support it. If set to disabled, anti-spoofing features are not allowed. If set to Not Configured, the user can choose whether they want to use anti-spoofing. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

EnhancedSignInSecurity.d: Setting to configure Enhanced sign-in security. Default is Not Configured

PinExpirationInDays.d: Controls the period of time (in days) that a PIN can be used before the system requires the user to change it. This must be set between 0 and 730, inclusive. If set to 0, the user's PIN will never expire

PinLowercaseCharactersUsage.d: Controls the ability to use lowercase letters in the Windows Hello for Business PIN. Allowed permits the use of lowercase letter(s), whereas Required ensures they are present. If set to Not Allowed, lowercase letters will not be permitted. Possible values are: allowed, required, disallowed.

PinMaximumLength.d: Controls the maximum number of characters allowed for the Windows Hello for Business PIN. This value must be between 4 and 127, inclusive. This value must be greater than or equal to the value set for the minimum PIN.

PinMinimumLength.d: Controls the minimum number of characters required for the Windows Hello for Business PIN. This value must be between 4 and 127, inclusive, and less than or equal to the value set for the maximum PIN.

PinPreviousBlockCount.d: Controls the ability to prevent users from using past PINs. This must be set between 0 and 50, inclusive, and the current PIN of the user is included in that count. If set to 0, previous PINs are not stored. PIN history is not preserved through a PIN reset.

PinSpecialCharactersUsage.d: Controls the ability to use special characters in the Windows Hello for Business PIN. Allowed permits the use of special character(s), whereas Required ensures they are present. If set to Not Allowed, special character(s) will not be permitted. Possible values are: allowed, required, disallowed.

PinUppercaseCharactersUsage.d: Controls the ability to use uppercase letters in the Windows Hello for Business PIN. Allowed permits the use of uppercase letter(s), whereas Required ensures they are present. If set to Not Allowed, uppercase letters will not be permitted. Possible values are: allowed, required, disallowed.

RemotePassportEnabled.d: Controls the use of Remote Windows Hello for Business. Remote Windows Hello for Business provides the ability for a portable, registered device to be usable as a companion for desktop authentication. The desktop must be Azure AD joined and the companion device must have a Windows Hello for Business PIN.

SecurityDeviceRequired.d: Controls whether to require a Trusted Platform Module (TPM) for provisioning Windows Hello for Business. A TPM provides an additional security benefit in that data stored on it cannot be used on other devices. If set to False, all devices can provision Windows Hello for Business even if there is not a usable TPM.

SecurityKeyForSignIn.d: Security key for Sign In provides the capacity for remotely turning ON/OFF Windows Hello Sercurity Keyl Not configured will honor configurations done on the clinet. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

State.d: Controls whether to allow the device to be configured for Windows Hello for Business. If set to disabled, the user cannot provision Windows Hello for Business except on Azure Active Directory joined mobile phones if otherwise required. If set to Not Configured, Intune will not override client defaults. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

UnlockWithBiometricsEnabled.d: Controls the use of biometric gestures, such as face and fingerprint, as an alternative to the Windows Hello for Business PIN. If set to False, biometric gestures are not allowed. Users must still configure a PIN as a backup in case of failures.

IntuneWindowsInformationProtectionPolicy.d: This resource configures an Intune Windows Information Protection Policy for Windows10 Mdm Enrolled.

Assignments.d: Represents the assignment to the Intune policy.

AzureRightsManagementServicesAllowed.d: Specifies whether to allow Azure RMS encryption for WIP

DataRecoveryCertificate.d: Specifies a recovery certificate that can be used for data recovery of encrypted files. This is the same as the data recovery agent(DRA) certificate for encrypting file system(EFS)

Description.d: The policy's description.

DisplayName.d: Policy display name.

EnforcementLevel.d: WIP enforcement level.See the Enum definition for supported values. Possible values are: noProtection, encryptAndAuditOnly, encryptAuditAndPrompt, encryptAuditAndBlock.

EnterpriseIPRanges.d: Sets the enterprise IP ranges that define the computers in the enterprise network. Data that comes from those computers will be considered part of the enterprise and protected. These locations will be considered a safe destination for enterprise data to be shared to

EnterpriseIPRangesAreAuthoritative.d: Boolean value that tells the client to accept the configured list and not to use heuristics to attempt to find other subnets. Default is false

EnterpriseInternalProxyServers.d: This is the comma-separated list of internal proxy servers. For example, '157.54.14.28, 157.54.11.118, 10.202.14.167, 157.53.14.163, 157.69.210.59'. These proxies have been configured by the admin to connect to specific resources on the Internet. They are considered to be enterprise network locations. The proxies are only leveraged in configuring the EnterpriseProxiedDomains policy to force traffic to the matched domains through these proxies

EnterpriseNetworkDomainNames.d: This is the list of domains that comprise the boundaries of the enterprise. Data from one of these domains that is sent to a device will be considered enterprise data and protected These locations will be considered a safe destination for enterprise data to be shared to

EnterpriseProtectedDomainNames.d: List of enterprise domains to be protected

EnterpriseProxiedDomains.d: Contains a list of Enterprise resource domains hosted in the cloud that need to be protected. Connections to these resources are considered enterprise data. If a proxy is paired with a cloud resource, traffic to the cloud resource will be routed through the enterprise network via the denoted proxy server (on Port 80). A proxy server used for this purpose must also be configured using the EnterpriseInternalProxyServers policy

EnterpriseProxyServers.d: This is a list of proxy servers. Any server not on this list is considered non-enterprise

EnterpriseProxyServersAreAuthoritative.d: Boolean value that tells the client to accept the configured list of proxies and not try to detect other work proxies. Default is false

ExemptApps.d: Exempt applications can also access enterprise data, but the data handled by those applications are not protected. This is because some critical enterprise applications may have compatibility problems with encrypted data.

IconsVisible.d: Determines whether overlays are added to icons for WIP protected files in Explorer and enterprise only app tiles in the Start menu. Starting in Windows 10, version 1703 this setting also configures the visibility of the WIP icon in the title bar of a WIP-protected app

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

IndexingEncryptedStoresOrItemsBlocked.d: This switch is for the Windows Search Indexer, to allow or disallow indexing of items

NeutralDomainResources.d: List of domain names that can used for work or personal resource

ProtectedApps.d: Protected applications can access enterprise data and the data handled by those applications are protected with encryption

ProtectionUnderLockConfigRequired.d: Specifies whether the protection under lock feature (also known as encrypt under pin) should be configured

RevokeOnUnenrollDisabled.d: This policy controls whether to revoke the WIP keys when a device unenrolls from the management service. If set to 1 (Don't revoke keys), the keys will not be revoked and the user will continue to have access to protected files after unenrollment. If the keys are not revoked, there will be no revoked file cleanup subsequently.

RightsManagementServicesTemplateId.d: TemplateID GUID to use for RMS encryption. The RMS template allows the IT admin to configure the details about who has access to RMS-protected file and how long they have access

RoleScopeTagIds.d: List of Scope Tags for this Entity instance.

SmbAutoEncryptedFileExtensions.d: Specifies a list of file extensions, so that files with these extensions are encrypted when copying from an SMB share within the corporate boundary

IntuneWindowsUpdateForBusinessDriverUpda.d: This resource configures an Intune Windows Update For Business Driver Update Profile for Windows 10.

ApprovalType.d: Driver update profile approval type. For example, manual or automatic approval. Possible values are: manual, automatic.

Assignments.d: Represents the assignment to the Intune policy.

DeploymentDeferralInDays.d: Deployment deferral settings in days, only applicable when ApprovalType is set to automatic approval.

Description.d: Description of the Intune policy.

DisplayName.d: Display name of the Intune policy.

Id.d: Id of the Intune policy.

RoleScopeTagIds.d: List of Scope Tag IDs for the Driver Update entity.

IntuneWindowsUpdateForBusinessFeatureUpd.d: This resource configures an Intune Windows Update For Business Feature Update Profile for Windows10.

Assignments.d: Represents the assignment to the Intune policy.

Description.d: The description of the profile which is specified by the user.

DisplayName.d: The display name of the profile.

FeatureUpdateVersion.d: The feature update version that will be deployed to the devices targeted by this profile. The version could be any supported version for example 1709, 1803 or 1809 and so on.

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

InstallFeatureUpdatesOptional.d: If true, the Windows 11 update will become optional

InstallLatestWindows10OnWindows11Ineligi.d: If true, the latest Microsoft Windows 10 update will be installed on devices ineligible for Microsoft Windows 11. Cannot be changed after creation of the policy.

RoleScopeTagIds.d: List of Scope Tags for this Entity instance.

RolloutSettings.d: The windows update rollout settings, including offer start date time, offer end date time, and days between each set of offers. For 'as soon as possible' installation, set this setting to $null or do not configure it.

IntuneWindowsUpdateForBusinessHotpatchPr.d: This resource configures an Intune Windows Update For Business Hotpatch Profile for Windows10.

Assignments.d: Represents the assignment to the Intune policy.

Description.d: Indicates the description for the device clean up rule.

DisplayName.d: Indicates the display name of the device cleanup rule.

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

RoleScopeTagIds.d: List of Scope Tags for this Entity instance.

IntuneWindowsUpdateForBusinessQualityUpd.d: This resource configures an Intune Windows Update For Business Quality Update Profile for Windows10.

Assignments.d: Represents the assignment to the Intune policy.

Description.d: The description of the profile.

DisplayName.d: The display name for the profile.

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

RoleScopeTagIds.d: List of Scope Tags for this Quality Update entity.

IntuneWindowsUpdateForBusinessRingUpdate.d: This resource configures an Intune Windows Update For Business Ring Update Profile for Windows 10.

AllowWindows11Upgrade.d: When TRUE, allows eligible Windows 10 devices to upgrade to Windows 11. When FALSE, implies the device stays on the existing operating system. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.

Assignments.d: Represents the assignment to the Intune policy.

AutoRestartNotificationDismissal.d: Specify the method by which the auto-restart required notification is dismissed. Possible values are: NotConfigured, Automatic, User. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported. Possible values are: notConfigured, automatic, user, unknownFutureValue.

AutomaticUpdateMode.d: The Automatic Update Mode. Possible values are: UserDefined, NotifyDownload, AutoInstallAtMaintenanceTime, AutoInstallAndRebootAtMaintenanceTime, AutoInstallAndRebootAtScheduledTime, AutoInstallAndRebootWithoutEndUserControl, WindowsDefault. UserDefined is the default value, no intent. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported. Possible values are: userDefined, notifyDownload, autoInstallAtMaintenanceTime, autoInstallAndRebootAtMaintenanceTime, autoInstallAndRebootAtScheduledTime, autoInstallAndRebootWithoutEndUserControl, windowsDefault.

BusinessReadyUpdatesOnly.d: Determines which branch devices will receive their updates from. Possible values are: UserDefined, All, BusinessReadyOnly, WindowsInsiderBuildFast, WindowsInsiderBuildSlow, WindowsInsiderBuildRelease. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported. Possible values are: userDefined, all, businessReadyOnly, windowsInsiderBuildFast, windowsInsiderBuildSlow, windowsInsiderBuildRelease.

DeadlineForFeatureUpdatesInDays.d: Number of days before feature updates are installed automatically with valid range from 0 to 30 days. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.

DeadlineForQualityUpdatesInDays.d: Number of days before quality updates are installed automatically with valid range from 0 to 30 days. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.

DeadlineGracePeriodInDays.d: Number of days after deadline until restarts occur automatically with valid range from 0 to 7 days. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.

DeliveryOptimizationMode.d: The Delivery Optimization Mode. Possible values are: UserDefined, HttpOnly, HttpWithPeeringNat, HttpWithPeeringPrivateGroup, HttpWithInternetPeering, SimpleDownload, BypassMode. UserDefined allows the user to set. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported. Possible values are: userDefined, httpOnly, httpWithPeeringNat, httpWithPeeringPrivateGroup, httpWithInternetPeering, simpleDownload, bypassMode.

Description.d: Admin provided description of the Device Configuration.

DisplayName.d: Admin provided name of the device configuration.

DriversExcluded.d: When TRUE, excludes Windows update Drivers. When FALSE, does not exclude Windows update Drivers. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.

EngagedRestartDeadlineInDays.d: Deadline in days before automatically scheduling and executing a pending restart outside of active hours, with valid range from 2 to 30 days. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.

EngagedRestartSnoozeScheduleInDays.d: Number of days a user can snooze Engaged Restart reminder notifications with valid range from 1 to 3 days. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.

EngagedRestartTransitionScheduleInDays.d: Number of days before transitioning from Auto Restarts scheduled outside of active hours to Engaged Restart, which requires the user to schedule, with valid range from 0 to 30 days. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.

FeatureUpdatesDeferralPeriodInDays.d: Defer Feature Updates by these many days with valid range from 0 to 30 days. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.

FeatureUpdatesPauseExpiryDateTime.d: The Feature Updates Pause Expiry datetime. This value is 35 days from the time admin paused or extended the pause for the ring. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.

FeatureUpdatesPauseStartDate.d: The Feature Updates Pause start date. This value is the time when the admin paused or extended the pause for the ring. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported. This property is read-only.

FeatureUpdatesPaused.d: When TRUE, assigned devices are paused from receiving feature updates for up to 35 days from the time you pause the ring. When FALSE, does not pause Feature Updates. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.s

FeatureUpdatesRollbackStartDateTime.d: The Feature Updates Rollback Start datetime.This value is the time when the admin rolled back the Feature update for the ring.Returned by default.Query parameters are not supported.

FeatureUpdatesRollbackWindowInDays.d: The number of days after a Feature Update for which a rollback is valid with valid range from 2 to 60 days. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.

Id.d: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only.

InstallationSchedule.d: The Installation Schedule. Possible values are: ActiveHoursStart, ActiveHoursEnd, ScheduledInstallDay, ScheduledInstallTime. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.

MicrosoftUpdateServiceAllowed.d: When TRUE, allows Microsoft Update Service. When FALSE, does not allow Microsoft Update Service. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.

PostponeRebootUntilAfterDeadline.d: When TRUE the device should wait until deadline for rebooting outside of active hours. When FALSE the device should not wait until deadline for rebooting outside of active hours. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.

PrereleaseFeatures.d: The Pre-Release Features. Possible values are: UserDefined, SettingsOnly, SettingsAndExperimentations, NotAllowed. UserDefined is the default value, no intent. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported. Possible values are: userDefined, settingsOnly, settingsAndExperimentations, notAllowed.

QualityUpdatesDeferralPeriodInDays.d: Defer Quality Updates by these many days with valid range from 0 to 30 days. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.

QualityUpdatesPauseExpiryDateTime.d: The Quality Updates Pause Expiry datetime. This value is 35 days from the time admin paused or extended the pause for the ring. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.

QualityUpdatesPauseStartDate.d: The Quality Updates Pause start date. This value is the time when the admin paused or extended the pause for the ring. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported. This property is read-only.

QualityUpdatesPaused.d: When TRUE, assigned devices are paused from receiving quality updates for up to 35 days from the time you pause the ring. When FALSE, does not pause Quality Updates. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.

QualityUpdatesRollbackStartDateTime.d: The Quality Updates Rollback Start datetime. This value is the time when the admin rolled back the Quality update for the ring. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.

RoleScopeTagIds.d: List of Scope Tags for this Entity instance.

ScheduleImminentRestartWarningInMinutes.d: Specify the period for auto-restart imminent warning notifications. Supported values: 15, 30 or 60 (minutes). Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.

ScheduleRestartWarningInHours.d: Specify the period for auto-restart warning reminder notifications. Supported values: 2, 4, 8, 12 or 24 (hours). Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.

SkipChecksBeforeRestart.d: When TRUE, skips all checks before restart: Battery level = 40%, User presence, Display Needed, Presentation mode, Full screen mode, phone call state, game mode etc. When FALSE, does not skip all checks before restart. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported.

UpdateNotificationLevel.d: Specifies what Windows Update notifications users see. Possible values are: NotConfigured, DefaultNotifications, RestartWarningsOnly, DisableAllNotifications. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported. Possible values are: notConfigured, defaultNotifications, restartWarningsOnly, disableAllNotifications, unknownFutureValue.

UpdateWeeks.d: Schedule the update installation on the weeks of the month. Possible values are: UserDefined, FirstWeek, SecondWeek, ThirdWeek, FourthWeek, EveryWeek. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported. Possible values are: userDefined, firstWeek, secondWeek, thirdWeek, fourthWeek, everyWeek, unknownFutureValue.

UserPauseAccess.d: Specifies whether to enable end user's access to pause software updates. Possible values are: NotConfigured, Enabled, Disabled. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

UserWindowsUpdateScanAccess.d: Specifies whether to disable user's access to scan Windows Update. Possible values are: NotConfigured, Enabled, Disabled. Returned by default. Query parameters are not supported. Possible values are: notConfigured, enabled, disabled.

M365DSCGraphAPIRuleEvaluation.d: This resource monitors Graph API endpoints against the defined rules.

AfterRuleCountQuery.d: Query to check how many instances exist, using PowerShell format

InstanceIdentifier.d: For logging purposes only. This represents the unique identifier of instances returned by the Graph API call.

InstancesProperty.d: Name of the parent property of the response, which contains the instances. Default is 'value'.

RuleName.d: Custom display name for the rule. This will show up in the logs on drift detection.

M365DSCRuleEvaluation.d: This resource configures the Azure AD Tenant Details.

AfterRuleCountQuery.d: Query to check how many instances exist, using PowerShell format

Filter.d: Specifies a filter for the current resource type to be evaluated. This reduces the overall set of instances the rule will be evaluated against.

ResourceTypeName.d: Name of the resource to monitor

RuleDefinition.d: Specify the rules to monitor the resource for.

RuleName.d: Custom display name for the rule. This will show up in the logs on drift detection.

O365AdminAuditLogConfig.d: This resource configures a Purview Admin Audit Log.

IsSingleInstance.d: Specifies the resource is a single instance, the value must be 'Yes'

UnifiedAuditLogIngestionEnabled.d: Determins if Unified Audit Log Ingestion is enabled

O365CopilotSettingsPeopleEnhancedPersona.d: Represents a setting that controls whether Microsoft 365 Copilot can use features that enhance its personalization capabilities.

AuthorizedAppIds.d: A collection of application IDs for registered Microsoft Entra apps that are allowed to manage the externalConnection and to index content in the externalConnection.

Description.d: The description of the external connector.

Id.d: The unique identifier of the external connector.

Name.d: The name of the external connector.

O365Group.d: This resource allows users to create Office 365 Users and assign them licenses.

Description.d: The group's description.

DisplayName.d: The display name for the group.

O365OrgCustomizationSetting.d: This resource configures the tenant settings so that the tenant runs in a fully configurable mode. Usually tenants are created in a mode, that limits options to customize the tenant. This setting is reflected by the Organization Config proptery `IsDehydrated: True`. Running this resource will set the property to false.

IsSingleInstance.d: Specifies the resource is a single instance, the value must be 'Yes'

O365OrgSettings.d: This resource configures the Org settings for a Microsoft 365 tenant.

AdminCenterReportDisplayConcealedNames.d: Controls whether or not the Admin Center reports will conceale user, group and site names.

AppsAndServicesIsAppAndServicesTrialEnab.d: Allow people in your organization to start trial subscriptions for apps and services that support trials. Admins manage licenses for these trials in the same way as other licenses in your organization. Only admins can upgrade these trials to paid subscriptions, so they won't affect your billing.

AppsAndServicesIsOfficeStoreEnabled.d: Allow people in your organization to access the Office Store using their work account. The Office Store provides access to apps that aren't curated or managed by Microsoft.

CortanaEnabled.d: Allow Cortana in windows 10 (version 1909 and earlier), and the Cortana app on iOS and Android, to access Microsoft-hosted data on behalf of people in your organization.

DynamicsCustomerVoiceIsInOrgFormsPhishin.d: Automatically block any internal surveys that request confidential information. Admins will be notified in the Message Center when a survey is blocked.

DynamicsCustomerVoiceIsRecordIdentityByD.d: Capture the first and last names of respondents in your organization that complete a survey. You can still change this for individual surveys.

DynamicsCustomerVoiceIsRestrictedSurveyA.d: Capture the first and last names of respondents in your organization that complete a survey. You can still change this for individual surveys.

FormsIsExternalSendFormEnabled.d: External Sharing - Send a link to the form and collect responses.

FormsIsExternalShareCollaborationEnabled.d: External Sharing - Share to collaborate on the form layout and structure.

FormsIsExternalShareResultEnabled.d: External Sharing - Share form result summary.

FormsIsExternalShareTemplateEnabled.d: External Sharing - Share the form as a template that can be duplicated.

FormsIsRecordIdentityByDefaultEnabled.d: Record names of people in your org.

InstallationOptionsAppsForMac.d: Defines the apps users can install on Mac devices.

InstallationOptionsAppsForWindows.d: Defines the apps users can install on Windows and mobile devices.

InstallationOptionsUpdateChannel.d: Defines how often you want your users to get feature updates for Microsoft 365 apps installed on devices running Windows

IsSingleInstance.d: Specifies the resource is a single instance, the value must be 'Yes'

M365WebEnableUsersToOpenFilesFrom3PStora.d: Let users open files stored in third-party storage services in Microsoft 365 on the Web.

PlannerAllowCalendarSharing.d: Allow Planner users to publish their plans and assigned tasks to Outlook or other calendars through iCalendar feeds.

ToDoIsExternalJoinEnabled.d: To Do - Allow external users to join.

ToDoIsExternalShareEnabled.d: To Do - Allow sharing with external users.

ToDoIsPushNotificationEnabled.d: To Do - Allow your users to receive push notifications.

VivaInsightsDigestEmail.d: Specifies whether or not to allow users to have access to use the Viva Insights digest email feature.

VivaInsightsOutlookAddInAndInlineSuggest.d: Specifies whether or not to allow users to have access to use the Viva Insights Outlook add-in and inline suggestions.

VivaInsightsScheduleSendSuggestions.d: Specifies whether or not to allow users to have access to use the Viva Insights schedule send suggestions feature.

VivaInsightsWebExperience.d: Specifies whether or not to allow users to have access to use the Viva Insights web experience.

O365SearchAndIntelligenceConfigurations.d: This resource configures the Search And Intelligence configuration settings.

IsSingleInstance.d: Specifies the resource is a single instance, the value must be 'Yes'

ItemInsightsDisabledForGroup.d: Specifies a single Azure AD Group for which Item Insights needs to be disabled.

ItemInsightsIsEnabledInOrganization.d: Specifies whether or not Item Insights should be available for the organization.

MeetingInsightsIsEnabledInOrganization.d: Specifies whether or not Meeting Insights should be available for the organization.

PersonInsightsDisabledForGroup.d: Specifies a single Azure AD Group for which Person Insights needs to be disabled.

PersonInsightsIsEnabledInOrganization.d: Specifies whether or not Person Insights should be available for the organization.

ODSettings.d: This resource allows admins to manage blocked file types, blocked domains and MAC Sync.

DisableAddToOneDrive.d: Allows configuration on Add shortcut to OneDrive feature in SharePoint document libraries. If set to $true, then this feature will be disabled on all sites in the tenant. If set to $false, it will be enabled on all sites in the tenant.

DisplayNamesOfFileViewers.d: Allows configuring whether display name of people who view the file are visible in the property pane of the site in OneDrive for business sites.

ExcludedFileExtensions.d: Exclude files from being synced to OneDrive

ODBAccessRequests.d: Lets administrators set policy on access requests and requests to share in OneDrive for Business

ODBMembersCanShare.d: Lets administrators set policy on re-sharing behavior in OneDrive for Business

OneDriveStorageQuota.d: The resource quota to apply to the OneDrive sites

OrphanedPersonalSitesRetentionPeriod.d: Number of days after a user's account is deleted that their OneDrive for Business content will be deleted.

TenantRestrictionEnabled.d: Enable/disable Safe domain List - if disabled overrides DomainGuids value

PPAdminDLPPolicy.d: Manages a policy's environment and default API group settings.

DisplayName.d: Creates the policy with the input display name

Environments.d: Comma separated string list used as input environments to either include or exclude, depending on the FilterType.

FilterType.d: Identifies which filter type the policy will have, none, include, or exclude.

PPDLPPolicyConnectorConfigurations.d: Configures connectors Data Loss Prevention policies in Power Platforms.

ConnectorActionConfigurations.d: Set of cnnector actions associated with the policy.

PPPowerAppPolicyUrlPatterns.d: Configures custom connector patterns for Data Loss Prevention policies in Power Platforms.

RuleSet.d: Set of custom connector pattern rules associated with the policy.

PPPowerAppsEnvironment.d: This resources configures the PowerApps Environment.

CurrencyName.d: The default currency for the database, use Get-AdminPowerAppCdsDatabaseCurrencies to get the supported values.

DisplayName.d: Display name for the PowerApps environment

LanguageName.d: The default languages for the database, use Get-AdminPowerAppCdsDatabaseLanguages to get the support values.

ProvisionDatabase.d: The switch to provision a Dataverse database when creating the environment. If set, LanguageName and CurrencyName are mandatory to pass as arguments.

PPTenantIsolationSettings.d: This resource configures the Tenant Isolation settings for a Power Platform Tenant.

Enabled.d: When set to true this will enable the tenant isolation settings.

Rules.d: The exact list of tenant rules to be configured.

RulesToExclude.d: A list of tenant rules that is now allowed to be added.

RulesToInclude.d: A list of tenant rules that has to be added.

PPTenantSettings.d: This resource configures a Power Platform Tenant.

DisableAdminDigest.d: Disables the weekly admin digest email for Managed Environments. Default value is false.

DisableBillingPolicyCreationByNonAdminUs.d: This is a legacy setting that is no longer used by the platform. Default value is false.

DisableBingVideoSearch.d: When this setting is true, users in the environment can see a message that indicates video search categories have been turned off by the administrator. Default value is false.

DisableCapacityAllocationByEnvironmentAd.d: Ability to disable capacity allocation by environment administrators. Default value is false.

DisableChampionsInvitationReachout.d: This setting isn't currently used by the platform but might be used in the future.

DisableCommunitySearch.d: When this setting is true, users in the environment can see a message that indicates community and blog search categories have been turned off by the administrator. Default value is false.

DisableConnectionSharingWithEveryone.d: Gets or sets a value indicating whether non-admin users in the tenant can share connections with everyone. Default value is false.

DisableCopilot.d: Disables cloud flows copilot in Power Automate. It doesn't control the ability to add AI-related connectors or actions in the flow designer. For example, the Skills connector or AI Builder creates text with a GPT action. Default value is false.

DisableCopilotFeedback.d: When using Copilot in Power Apps, allow users to submit feedback to Microsoft. Default value is false.

DisableCopilotFeedbackMetadata.d: When using Copilot in Power Apps, allow users to share their prompts, questions, and requests with Microsoft. Default value is true.

DisableCopilotWithBing.d: Disables the copilot-enhanced help feature within Power Automate to enhance answers on product documentation through Bing Search. Default value is false.

DisableCreateFromFigma.d: Allow people to create a canvas app based on a Figma file. Default value is false.

DisableCreateFromImage.d: Allow people to use AI to generate an app based on an image. Default value is false.

DisableDataLogging.d: Ability to disable data logging and remove all data logged for Power Automate Copilot AI feature customer data (inputs and outputs). Default value is false.

DisableDeveloperEnvironmentCreationByNon.d: Restrict all developer environments to be created by tenant admins, Power Platform admins, or Dynamics 365 service admins. Default is false.

DisableDocsSearch.d: When this setting is true, users in the environment can see a message that indicates Microsoft Learn and documentation search categories have been turned off by the administrator. Default value is false.

DisableEnvironmentCreationByNonAdminUser.d: Restrict all environments to be created by tenant admins, Power Platform admins, or Dynamics 365 service admins. Default value is false.

DisableNPSCommentsReachout.d: Ability to disable re-surveying users who left prior feedback via NPS prompts in Power Platform. Default value is false.

DisableNewsletterSendout.d: Ability to disable the newsletter sendout feature. Default value is false.

DisablePortalsCreationByNonAdminUsers.d: Restrict all portals to be created by tenant admins, Power Platform admins, or Dynamics 365 service admins. Default value is false.

DisablePreferredDataLocationForTeamsEnvi.d: Ignore the Teams group-preferred data location when provisioning a Teams environment. Default value is false.

DisableShareWithEveryone.d: Ability to turn off the Share with Everyone capability for nonadmin users in all Power Apps. Default value is true.

DisableSkillsMatchInvitationReachout.d: This setting isn't currently used by the platform but might be used in the future.

DisableSupportTicketsVisibleByAllUsers.d: Allows users, who already have access to the Help + Support page in Power Platform admin center, to see support requests created by other users in the tenant. Default value is True, which means this feature is turned off by default.

DisableSurveyFeedback.d: Ability to disable all NPS survey feedback prompts in Power Platform. Default value is false.

DisableTrialEnvironmentCreationByNonAdmi.d: Restrict all trial environments to be created by tenant admins, Power Platform admins, or Dynamics 365 service admins. Default value is false.

DisableUseOfUnassignedAIBuilderCredits.d: Ability to use unallocated AI Builder credits in environments without allocated credits. Default value is true.

EnableCanvasAppInsights.d: Allow users to collect telemetry data about their app in Azure Application Insights. Setting this to False blocks the transmission of this data.

EnableDefaultEnvironmentRouting.d: Enables the Default Environment routing feature that creates personal, developer environments for new makers. Default value is false.

EnableDesktopFlowDataPolicyManagement.d: When this setting is true, admins can view and manage desktop flow action groups in DLP policies in the Power Platform admin center. Default value is false.

EnableGuestsToMake.d: When set to true this will enable the ability for guests in your tenant to create Power Platform resources.

EnableModelDataSharing.d: Ability to allow Microsoft to read Power Automate Copilot AI feature customer data (inputs and outputs) and provide improved models. Default value is false.

EnableOpenAiBotPublishing.d: This setting isn't currently used by the platform but might be used in the future.

EnableTenantCapacityReportForEnvironment.d: Ability to allow tenant, Power Platform, or Dynamics 365 admins to grant permissions to an environment administrator to view the Capacity summary tab. Default value is false.

EnableTenantLicensingReportForEnvironmen.d: Ability to allow tenant, Power Platform, or Dynamics 365 admins to grant permissions to an environment administrator to view the tenant-scoped license reports. Default value is false.

PowerCatalogAudienceSetting.d: This setting is reserved for future use. No enforcement is driven by this setting at the current time.

ShareWithColleaguesUserLimit.d: Maximum value setting for the number of users in a security group used to share an app built using Power Apps on Microsoft Teams. Default value is 10000 but can be increased or decreased, as required.

StorageCapacityConsumptionWarningThresho.d: This setting isn't currently used by the platform but might be used in the future.

WalkMeOptOut.d: This is a legacy setting that is no longer used by the platform. Default value is false.

PlannerBucket.d: This resource is used to configure the Planner Buckets.

BucketId.d: Id of the Bucket, if known.

Name.d: The Name of the Planner Bucket.

PlanId.d: Id of the Plan to which the bucket is associated with.

PlannerPlan.d: This resource is used to configure the Planner Plans.

OwnerGroup.d: Name of Id of the Azure Active Directory Group who owns the plan

Title.d: The Title of the Planner Plan.

PlannerTask.d: This resource is used to configure the Planner Tasks.

AssignedUsers.d: List of users assigned to the tasks (ex: @('john.smith@contoso.com', 'bob.houle@contoso.com')).

Attachments.d: List of links to attachments assigned to the task.

Bucket.d: The Id of the bucket that contains the task.

Categories.d: List of categories assigned to the task.

Checklist.d: List checklist items associated with the task.

ConversationThreadId.d: Id of the group conversation thread associated with the comments section for this task.

DueDateTime.d: Date and Time for the task is due for completion.

PercentComplete.d: Percentage completed of the Task. Value can only be between 0 and 100.

PlanId.d: Id of the Planner Plan which contains the Task.

Priority.d: Priority of the Task. Value can only be between 1 and 10.

StartDateTime.d: Date and Time for the start of the Task.

TaskId.d: Id of the Task, if known.

Title.d: The Title of the Planner Task.

SCAuditConfigurationPolicy.d: This resource configures an Audit Configuration Policy in Purview.

SCAutoSensitivityLabelPolicy.d: This resource configures an Auto Sensitivity Label Policy in Purview.

AddExchangeLocation.d: This AddExchangeLocation parameter specifies new Exchange locations to be added to the policy without affecting the existing ones.

AddOneDriveLocation.d: The AddOneDriveLocation parameter specifies the OneDrive for Business sites to add to the list of included sites when you aren't using the value All for the OneDriveLocation parameter.

AddOneDriveLocationException.d: This parameter specifies the OneDrive for Business sites to exclude when you use the value All for the OneDriveLocation parameter.

AddSharePointLocation.d: The AddSharePointLocation parameter specifies the SharePoint Online sites to add to the list of included sites when you aren't using the value All for the SharePointLocation parameter.

AddSharePointLocationException.d: The AddSharePointLocation parameter specifies the SharePoint Online sites to add to the list of included sites when you aren't using the value All for the SharePointLocation parameter.

ApplySensitivityLabel.d: The ApplySensitivityLabel parameter specifies the label to use for the auto label policy.

Comment.d: The Comment parameter specifies an optional comment.

ExchangeLocation.d: The ExchangeSender parameter specifies which senders to include in the policy.

ExchangeSender.d: The ExchangeSender parameter specifies which senders to include in the policy.

ExchangeSenderException.d: The ExchangeSenderException parameter specifies which senders to exclude in the policy.

ExchangeSenderMemberOf.d: The ExchangeSenderMemberOf parameter specifies the distribution groups, mail-enabled security groups, or dynamic distribution groups to include in the auto-labeling policy.

ExchangeSenderMemberOfException.d: he ExchangeSenderMemberOf parameter specifies the distribution groups, mail-enabled security groups, or dynamic distribution groups to exclude from the auto-labeling policy.

Mode.d: The Mode parameter specifies the action and notification level of the auto-labeling policy.

Name.d: The Name parameter specifies the unique name for the sensitivity label. The maximum length is 64 characters. If the value contains spaces, enclose the value in quotation marks.

OneDriveLocation.d: The OneDriveLocation parameter specifies the OneDrive for Business sites to include. You identify the site by its URL value, or you can use the value.

OneDriveLocationException.d: The AddOneDriveLocationException parameter specifies the OneDrive for Business sites to add to the list of excluded sites when you use the value All for the OneDriveLocation parameter.

Priority.d: The Priority parameter specifies the priority of the policy. The highest priority policy will take action over lower priority policies if two policies are applicable for a file.

RemoveExchangeLocation.d: The RemoveExchangeLocation parameter removes locations on Exchange from the policy.

RemoveOneDriveLocation.d: The RemoveOneDriveLocation parameter specifies the OneDrive for Business sites to remove from the list of included sites when you aren't using the value All for the OneDriveLocation parameter.

RemoveOneDriveLocationException.d: This RemoveOneDriveLocationException parameter specifies the OneDrive for Business sites to remove from the list of excluded sites when you use the value All for the OneDriveLocation parameter.

RemoveSharePointLocation.d: The RemoveSharePointLocation parameter specifies the SharePoint Online sites to remove from the list of included sites when you aren't using the value All for the SharePointLocation parameter.

RemoveSharePointLocationException.d: The RemoveSharePointLocationException parameter specifies the SharePoint Online sites to remove from the list of excluded sites when you use the value All for the SharePointLocation parameter.

SharePointLocation.d: The SharePointLocation parameter specifies the SharePoint Online sites to include. You identify the site by its URL value, or you can use the value All to include all sites.

SharePointLocationException.d: This parameter specifies the SharePoint Online sites to exclude when you use the value All for the SharePointLocation parameter.

SCAutoSensitivityLabelRule.d: This resource configures an Auto Sensitivity Label Rule in Purview.

AccessScope.d: The AccessScope parameter specifies a condition for the auto-labeling policy rule that's based on the access scope of the content. The rule is applied to content that matches the specified access scope. Valid values are: InOrganization, NotInOrganization, None

AnyOfRecipientAddressContainsWords.d: The AnyOfRecipientAddressContainsWords parameter specifies a condition for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for words or phrases in recipient email addresses. You can specify multiple words or phrases separated by commas.

AnyOfRecipientAddressMatchesPatterns.d: The AnyOfRecipientAddressMatchesPatterns parameter specifies a condition for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for text patterns in recipient email addresses by using regular expressions.

Comment.d: The Comment parameter specifies an optional comment.

ContentContainsSensitiveInformation.d: The ContentContainsSensitiveInformation parameter specifies a condition for the rule that's based on a sensitive information type match in content. The rule is applied to content that contains the specified sensitive information type.

ContentExtensionMatchesWords.d: The ContentExtensionMatchesWords parameter specifies a condition for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for words in file name extensions. You can specify multiple words separated by commas.

Disabled.d: The Disabled parameter specifies whether the auto-labeling policy rule is enabled or disabled.

DocumentIsPasswordProtected.d: The DocumentIsPasswordProtected parameter specifies a condition for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for password protected files (because the contents of the file can't be inspected). Password detection only works for Office documents and .zip files.

DocumentIsUnsupported.d: The DocumentIsUnsupported parameter specifies a condition for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for files that can't be scanned.

ExceptIfAccessScope.d: The ExceptIfAccessScopeAccessScope parameter specifies an exception for the auto-labeling policy rule that's based on the access scope of the content. The rule isn't applied to content that matches the specified access scope. Valid values are: InOrganization, NotInOrganization, None

ExceptIfAnyOfRecipientAddressContainsWor.d: The ExceptIfAnyOfRecipientAddressContainsWords parameter specifies an exception for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for words or phrases in recipient email addresses. You can specify multiple words separated by commas.

ExceptIfAnyOfRecipientAddressMatchesPatt.d: The ExceptIfAnyOfRecipientAddressMatchesPatterns parameter specifies an exception for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for text patterns in recipient email addresses by using regular expressions.

ExceptIfContentContainsSensitiveInformat.d: The ExceptIfContentContainsSensitiveInformation parameter specifies an exception for the auto-labeling policy rule that's based on a sensitive information type match in content. The rule isn't applied to content that contains the specified sensitive information type.

ExceptIfContentExtensionMatchesWords.d: The ExceptIfContentExtensionMatchesWords parameter specifies an exception for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for words in file name extensions. You can specify multiple words separated by commas.

ExceptIfDocumentIsPasswordProtected.d: The ExceptIfDocumentIsPasswordProtected parameter specifies an exception for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for password protected files (because the contents of the file can't be inspected). Password detection only works for Office documents and .zip files.

ExceptIfDocumentIsUnsupported.d: The ExceptIfDocumentIsUnsupported parameter specifies an exception for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for files that can't be scanned.

ExceptIfFrom.d: The ExceptIfFrom parameter specifies an exception for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for messages from specific senders. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the sender.

ExceptIfFromAddressContainsWords.d: The ExceptIfFromAddressContainsWords parameter specifies an exception for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for words or phrases in the sender's email address. You can specify multiple words or phrases separated by commas.

ExceptIfFromAddressMatchesPatterns.d: The ExceptIfFromAddressMatchesPatterns parameter specifies an exception for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for text patterns in the sender's email address by using regular expressions.

ExceptIfFromMemberOf.d: The ExceptIfFromMemberOf parameter specifies an exception for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for messages sent by group members. You identify the group members by their email addresses. You can enter multiple values separated by commas.

ExceptIfHeaderMatchesPatterns.d: The HeaderMatchesPatterns parameter specifies an exception for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for text patterns in a header field by using regular expressions.

ExceptIfProcessingLimitExceeded.d: The ExceptIfProcessingLimitExceeded parameter specifies an exception for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for files where scanning couldn't complete.

ExceptIfRecipientDomainIs.d: The ExceptIfRecipientDomainIs parameter specifies an exception for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for recipients with email address in the specified domains. You can specify multiple domains separated by commas.

ExceptIfSenderDomainIs.d: The ExceptIfSenderDomainIs parameter specifies an exception for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for messages from senders with email address in the specified domains. You can specify multiple values separated by commas.

ExceptIfSenderIPRanges.d: The ExceptIfSenderIpRanges parameter specifies an exception for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for senders whose IP addresses matches the specified value, or fall within the specified ranges.

ExceptIfSentTo.d: The ExceptIfSentTo parameter specifies an exception for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for recipients in messages. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the recipient.

ExceptIfSentToMemberOf.d: The ExceptIfSentToMemberOf parameter specifies an exception for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for messages sent to members of distribution groups, dynamic distribution groups, or mail-enabled security groups. You identify the groups by email address. You can specify multiple values separated by commas.

ExceptIfSubjectMatchesPatterns.d: The ExceptIfSubjectMatchesPatterns parameter specifies an exception for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for text patterns in the Subject field of messages by using regular expressions.

FromAddressContainsWords.d: The FromAddressContainsWords parameter specifies a condition for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for words or phrases in the sender's email address. You can specify multiple words or phrases separated by commas.

FromAddressMatchesPatterns.d: The FromAddressMatchesPatterns parameter specifies a condition for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for text patterns in the sender's email address by using regular expressions.

HeaderMatchesPatterns.d: The HeaderMatchesPatterns parameter specifies a condition for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for text patterns in a header field by using regular expressions.

Name.d: Name of the Rule.

ProcessingLimitExceeded.d: The ProcessingLimitExceeded parameter specifies a condition for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for files where scanning couldn't complete. You can use this condition to create rules that work together to identify and process messages where the content couldn't be fully scanned.

RecipientDomainIs.d: The RecipientDomainIs parameter specifies a condition for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for recipients with email address in the specified domains. You can specify multiple domains separated by commas.

ReportSeverityLevel.d: The ReportSeverityLevel parameter specifies the severity level of the incident report for content detections based on the rule. Valid values are: None, Low, Medium, High

RuleErrorAction.d: The RuleErrorAction parameter specifies what to do if an error is encountered during the evaluation of the rule. Valid values are: Ignore, RetryThenBlock, *blank*

SenderDomainIs.d: The SenderDomainIs parameter specifies a condition for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for messages from senders with email address in the specified domains.

SenderIPRanges.d: The SenderIpRanges parameter specifies a condition for the auto-sensitivity policy rule that looks for senders whose IP addresses matches the specified value, or fall within the specified ranges.

SentTo.d: The SentTo parameter specifies a condition for the auto-sensitivity policy rule that looks for recipients in messages. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the recipient.

SentToMemberOf.d: The SentToMemberOf parameter specifies a condition for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for messages sent to members of distribution groups, dynamic distribution groups, or mail-enabled security groups. You identify the groups by email address.

SubjectMatchesPatterns.d: The SubjectMatchesPatterns parameter specifies a condition for the auto-labeling policy rule that looks for text patterns in the Subject field of messages by using regular expressions.

Workload.d: Workload the rule is associated with. Value can be: Exchange, SharePoint, OneDriveForBusiness, Applications, Azure, AWS and PowerBI

SCCaseHoldPolicy.d: This resource configures a Case Hold Policy in Purview.

Case.d: The Case parameter specifies the eDiscovery case that you want to associate with the case hold policy.

Comment.d: The Comment parameter specifies an optional comment.

Enabled.d: The Enabled parameter specifies whether the policy is enabled or disabled.

ExchangeLocation.d: The ExchangeLocation parameter specifies the mailboxes to include in the policy.

Name.d: The Name parameter specifies the unique name of the case hold policy.

PublicFolderLocation.d: The PublicFolderLocation parameter specifies that you want to include all public folders in the case hold policy. You use the value All for this parameter.

SharePointLocation.d: The SharePointLocation parameter specifies the SharePoint Online and OneDrive for Business sites to include. You identify a site by its URL value.

SCCaseHoldRule.d: This resource configures a Case Hold Rule in Purview.

Comment.d: The Comment parameter specifies an optional comment.

ContentMatchQuery.d: The ContentMatchQuery parameter specifies a content search filter. Use this parameter to create a query-based hold so only the content that matches the specified search query is placed on hold. This parameter uses a text search string or a query that's formatted by using the Keyword Query Language (KQL).

Disabled.d: The Disabled parameter specifies whether the case hold rule is enabled or disabled.

Name.d: The Name parameter specifies a unique name for the case hold rule.

Policy.d: The Policy parameter specifies the case hold policy that contains the rule. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the policy.

SCComplianceCase.d: This resource configures a Compliance Case in Purview.

Description.d: The description of the case.

Name.d: The Name parameter specifies the unique name of the compliance case.

Status.d: Status for the case. Can either be 'Active' or 'Closed'

SCComplianceSearch.d: This resource configures a Compliance Search in Purview.

AllowNotFoundExchangeLocationsEnabled.d: The AllowNotFoundExchangeLocationsEnabled parameter specifies whether to include mailboxes other than regular user mailboxes in the compliance search.

Case.d: Compliance Case (eDiscovery) that this Search is associated with

ContentMatchQuery.d: The ContentMatchQuery parameter specifies a content search filter. This parameter uses a text search string or a query that's formatted by using the Keyword Query Language (KQL).

Description.d: The Description parameter specifies an optional description for the compliance search. If the value contains spaces, enclose the value in quotation marks.

ExchangeLocation.d: The ExchangeLocation parameter specifies the mailboxes to include.

ExchangeLocationExclusion.d: This parameter specifies the mailboxes to exclude when you use the value All for the ExchangeLocation parameter.

HoldNames.d: The HoldNames parameter specifies that the content locations that have been placed on hold in the specified eDiscovery case will be searched. You use the value All for this parameter. You also need to specify the name of an eDiscovery case by using the Case parameter.

IncludeUserAppContent.d: The IncludeUserAppContent parameter specifies that you want to search the cloud-based storage location for users who don't have a regular Office 365 user account in your organization. These types of users include users without an Exchange Online license who use Office applications, Office 365 guest users, and on-premises users whose identity is synchronized with your Office 365 organization.

Language.d: The Language parameter specifies the language for the compliance search. Valid input for this parameter is a supported culture code value from the Microsoft .NET Framework CultureInfo class. For example, da-DK for Danish or ja-JP for Japanese.

Name.d: The Name parameter specifies the unique name of the complaiance tag.

PublicFolderLocation.d: The PublicFolderLocation parameter specifies that you want to include all public folders in the search. You use the value All for this parameter.

SharePointLocation.d: The SharePointLocation parameter specifies the SharePoint Online sites to include. You identify the site by its URL value, or you can use the value All to include all sites.

SharePointLocationExclusion.d: This parameter specifies the SharePoint Online sites to exclude when you use the value All for the SharePointLocation parameter. You identify the site by its URL value.

SCComplianceSearchAction.d: This resource configures a Compliance Search Action in Purview.

Action.d: The Action parameter specifies what type of action to define. Accepted values are Export, Retention and Purge.

ActionScope.d: The ActionScope parameter specifies the items to include when the action is Export.

EnableDedupe.d: The EnableDedupe parameter eliminates duplication of messages when you export content search results.

FileTypeExclusionsForUnindexedItems.d: The FileTypeExclusionsForUnindexedItems specifies the file types to exclude because they can't be indexed. You can specify multiple values separated by commas.

IncludeCredential.d: The IncludeCredential switch specifies whether to include the credential in the results.

IncludeSharePointDocumentVersions.d: The IncludeSharePointDocumentVersions parameter specifies whether to export previous versions of the document when you use the Export switch.

PurgeType.d: The PurgeType parameter specifies how to remove items when the action is Purge.

RetryOnError.d: The RetryOnError switch specifies whether to retry the action on any items that failed without re-running the entire action all over again.

SearchName.d: The SearchName parameter specifies the name of the existing content search to associate with the content search action. You can specify multiple content searches separated by commas.

SCComplianceTag.d: This resource configures a Compliance Tag in Purview.

Comment.d: The Comment parameter specifies an optional comment.

EventType.d: The EventType parameter specifies the retention rule that's associated with the label.

FilePlanProperty.d: The FilePlanProperty parameter specifies the file plan properties to include in the label.

IsRecordLabel.d: The IsRecordLabel parameter specifies whether the label is a record label.

Name.d: The Name parameter specifies the unique name of the complaiance tag.

Notes.d: The Notes parameter specifies an optional note. If you specify a value that contains spaces, enclose the value in quotation marks, for example: 'This is a user note'

RetentionAction.d: The RetentionAction parameter specifies the action for the label. Valid values are: Delete, Keep or KeepAndDelete.

RetentionDuration.d: The RetentionDuration parameter specifies the hold duration for the retention rule. Valid values are: An integer - The hold duration in days, Unlimited - The content is held indefinitely.

RetentionType.d: The RetentionType parameter specifies whether the retention duration is calculated from the content creation date, tagged date, or last modification date. Valid values are: CreationAgeInDays, EventAgeInDays,ModificationAgeInDays, or TaggedAgeInDays.

ReviewerEmail.d: The ReviewerEmail parameter specifies the email address of a reviewer for Delete and KeepAndDelete retention actions. You can specify multiple email addresses separated by commas.

SCDLPCompliancePolicy.d: This resource configures a DLP Compliance Policy in Purview.

Comment.d: The Comment parameter specifies an optional comment.

EndpointDlpLocation.d: The EndpointDLPLocation parameter specifies the user accounts to include in the DLP policy for Endpoint DLP when they are logged on to an onboarded device. You identify the account by name or email address. You can use the value All to include all user accounts.

EndpointDlpLocationException.d: The EndpointDlpLocationException parameter specifies the user accounts to exclude from Endpoint DLP when you use the value All for the EndpointDlpLocation parameter. You identify the account by name or email address.

ExceptIfOneDriveSharedBy.d: The ExceptIfOneDriveSharedBy parameter specifies the users to exclude from the DLP policy (the sites of the OneDrive user accounts are included in the policy). You identify the users by UPN.

ExceptIfOneDriveSharedByMemberOf.d: The ExceptIfOneDriveSharedByMemberOf parameter specifies the distribution groups or mail-enabled security groups to exclude from the DLP policy (the OneDrive sites of group members are excluded from the policy). You identify the groups by email address.

ExchangeLocation.d: The ExchangeLocation parameter specifies Exchange Online mailboxes to include in the DLP policy. You can only use the value All for this parameter to include all mailboxes.

Mode.d: The Mode parameter specifies the action and notification level of the DLP policy. Valid values are: Enable, TestWithNotifications, TestWithoutNotifications, Disable and PendingDeletion.

Name.d: The Name parameter specifies the unique name of the DLP policy. If the value contains spaces, enclose the value in quotation marks.

OnPremisesScannerDlpLocation.d: The OnPremisesScannerDlpLocation parameter specifies the on-premises file shares and SharePoint document libraries and folders to include in the DLP policy. You can use the value All to include all on-premises file shares and SharePoint document libraries and folders.

OnPremisesScannerDlpLocationException.d: The OnPremisesScannerDlpLocationException parameter specifies the on-premises file shares and SharePoint document libraries and folders to exclude from the DLP policy if you use the value All for the OnPremisesScannerDlpLocation parameter.

OneDriveLocation.d: The OneDriveLocation parameter specifies the OneDrive for Business sites to include. You identify the site by its URL value, or you can use the value All to include all sites.

OneDriveLocationException.d: This parameter specifies the OneDrive for Business sites to exclude when you use the value All for the OneDriveLocation parameter. You identify the site by its URL value.

OneDriveSharedBy.d: The OneDriveSharedBy parameter specifies the users to include in the DLP policy (the sites of the OneDrive user accounts are included in the policy). You identify the users by UPN.

OneDriveSharedByMemberOf.d: The OneDriveSharedByMemberOf parameter specifies the distribution groups or mail-enabled security groups to include in the DLP policy (the OneDrive sites of group members are included in the policy). You identify the groups by email address.

PowerBIDlpLocation.d: The PowerBIDlpLocation parameter specifies the Power BI workspace IDs to include in the DLP policy. Only workspaces hosted in Premium Gen2 capacities are permitted. You can use the value All to include all supported workspaces.

PowerBIDlpLocationException.d: The PowerBIDlpLocationException parameter specifies the Power BI workspace IDs to exclude from the DLP policy when you use the value All for the PowerBIDlpLocation parameter. Only workspaces hosted in Premium Gen2 capacities are permitted.

Priority.d: Priority for the Policy.

SharePointLocation.d: The SharePointLocation parameter specifies the SharePoint Online sites to include. You identify the site by its URL value, or you can use the value All to include all sites.

SharePointLocationException.d: This parameter specifies the SharePoint Online sites to exclude when you use the value All for the SharePointLocation parameter. You identify the site by its URL value.

ThirdPartyAppDlpLocation.d: The ThirdPartyAppDlpLocation parameter specifies the non-Microsoft cloud apps to include in the DLP policy. You can use the value All to include all connected apps.

ThirdPartyAppDlpLocationException.d: The ThirdPartyAppDlpLocationException parameter specifies the non-Microsoft cloud apps to exclude from the DLP policy when you use the value All for the ThirdPartyAppDlpLocation parameter.

SCDLPComplianceRule.d: This resource configures a DLP Compliance Rule in Purview.

AccessScope.d: The AccessScope parameter specifies a condition for the DLP rule that's based on the access scope of the content. The rule is applied to content that matches the specified access scope.

AdvancedRule.d: The AdvancedRule parameter uses complex rule syntax that supports multiple AND, OR, and NOT operators and nested groups

AnyOfRecipientAddressContainsWords.d: The AnyOfRecipientAddressContainsWords parameter specifies a condition for the DLP rule that looks for words or phrases in recipient email addresses.

AnyOfRecipientAddressMatchesPatterns.d: The AnyOfRecipientAddressMatchesPatterns parameter specifies a condition for the DLP rule that looks for text patterns in recipient email addresses by using regular expressions.

BlockAccess.d: The BlockAccess parameter specifies an action for the DLP rule that blocks access to the source item when the conditions of the rule are met. $true: Blocks further access to the source item that matched the rule. The owner, author, and site owner can still access the item. $false: Allows access to the source item that matched the rule. This is the default value.

BlockAccessScope.d: The BlockAccessScope parameter specifies the scope of the block access action.

Comment.d: The Comment parameter specifies an optional comment. If you specify a value that contains spaces, enclose the value in quotation marks.

ContentCharacterSetContainsWords.d: The ContentCharacterSetContainsWords parameter specifies a condition for the rule that looks for character set names in messages. You can specify multiple values separated by commas.

ContentContainsSensitiveInformation.d: The ContentContainsSensitiveInformation parameter specifies a condition for the rule that's based on a sensitive information type match in content. The rule is applied to content that contains the specified sensitive information type.

ContentExtensionMatchesWords.d: The ContentExtensionMatchesWords parameter specifies a condition for the DLP rule that looks for words in file name extensions. You can specify multiple words separated by commas.

ContentIsNotLabeled.d: The ContentIsNotLabeled parameter specifies if the content is labeled. A True or False condition.

ContentPropertyContainsWords.d: The ContentPropertyContainsWords parameter specifies a condition for the DLP rule that's based on a property match in content. The rule is applied to content that contains the specified property.

Disabled.d: The Disabled parameter specifies whether the DLP rule is disabled.

DocumentContainsWords.d: The DocumentContainsWords parameter specifies a condition for the DLP rule that looks for words in message attachments. Only supported attachment types are checked.

DocumentIsPasswordProtected.d: The DocumentIsPasswordProtected parameter specifies a condition for the DLP rule that looks for password protected files (because the contents of the file can't be inspected). Password detection only works for Office documents and .zip files.

DocumentIsUnsupported.d: The DocumentIsUnsupported parameter specifies a condition for the DLP rule that looks for files that can't be scanned.

DocumentNameMatchesPatterns.d: The DocumentNameMatchesPatterns parameter specifies a condition for the DLP rule that looks for text patterns in the name of message attachments by using regular expressions.

DocumentNameMatchesWords.d: The DocumentNameMatchesWords parameter specifies a condition for the DLP rule that looks for words or phrases in the name of message attachments.

ExceptIfAnyOfRecipientAddressContainsWor.d: he ExceptIfAnyOfRecipientAddressContainsWords parameter specifies an exception for the DLP rule that looks for words or phrases in recipient email addresses.

ExceptIfAnyOfRecipientAddressMatchesPatt.d: The ExceptIfAnyOfRecipientAddressMatchesPatterns parameter specifies an exception for the DLP rule that looks for text patterns in recipient email addresses by using regular expressions.

ExceptIfContentCharacterSetContainsWords.d: The ExceptIfContentCharacterSetContainsWords parameter specifies an exception for the rule that looks for character set names in messages.
