ExceptIfContentContainsSensitiveInformat.d: The ExceptIfContentContainsSensitiveInformation parameter specifies an exception for the rule that's based on a sensitive information type match in content. The rule isn't applied to content that contains the specified sensitive information type.

ExceptIfContentExtensionMatchesWords.d: The ExceptIfContentExtensionMatchesWords parameter specifies an exception for the DLP rule that looks for words in file name extensions. You can specify multiple words separated by commas.

ExceptIfContentPropertyContainsWords.d: The ExceptIfContentPropertyContainsWords parameter specifies an exception for the DLP rule that's based on a property match in content.

ExceptIfDocumentIsPasswordProtected.d: The ExceptIfDocumentIsPasswordProtected parameter specifies an exception for the DLP rule that looks for password protected files (because the contents of the file can't be inspected). Password detection only works for Office documents and .zip files.

ExceptIfDocumentIsUnsupported.d: The ExceptIfDocumentIsUnsupported parameter specifies an exception for the DLP rule that looks for files that can't be scanned.

ExceptIfDocumentNameMatchesPatterns.d: The ExceptIfDocumentNameMatchesPatterns parameter specifies an exception for the DLP rule that looks for text patterns in the name of message attachments by using regular expressions.

ExceptIfDocumentNameMatchesWords.d: The ExceptIfDocumentNameMatchesWords parameter specifies an exception for the DLP rule that looks for words or phrases in the name of message attachments.

ExceptIfFromAddressContainsWords.d: The ExceptIfFromAddressContainsWords parameter specifies an exception for the DLP rule that looks for words or phrases in the sender's email address.

ExceptIfFromAddressMatchesPatterns.d: The ExceptIfFromAddressMatchesPatterns parameter specifies an exception for the DLP rule that looks for text patterns in the sender's email address by using regular expressions.

ExceptIfFromScope.d: The ExceptIfFromScope parameter specifies whether messages from inside or outside the organisation are in scope for the DLP rule.

ExceptIfHasSenderOverride.d: The ExceptIfHasSenderOverride parameter specifies an exception for the rule that looks for messages where the sender chose to override a DLP policy.

ExceptIfMessageTypeMatches.d: The ExceptIfMessageTypeMatches parameter specifies an exception for the rule that looks for messages of the specified type.

ExceptIfProcessingLimitExceeded.d: The ExceptIfProcessingLimitExceeded parameter specifies an exception for the DLP rule that looks for files where scanning couldn't complete.

ExceptIfRecipientDomainIs.d: The ExceptIfRecipientDomainIs parameter specifies an exception for the DLP rule that looks for recipients with email addresses in the specified domains.

ExceptIfSenderDomainIs.d: The ExceptIfSenderDomainIs parameter specifies an exception for the DLP rule that looks for messages from senders with email address in the specified domains.

ExceptIfSenderIPRanges.d: The ExceptIfSenderIpRanges parameter specifies an exception for the DLP rule that looks for senders whose IP addresses matches the specified value, or fall within the specified ranges.

ExceptIfSentTo.d: The ExceptIfSentTo parameter specifies an exception for the DLP rule that looks for recipients in messages. You identify the recipients by email address.

ExceptIfSubjectContainsWords.d: The ExceptIfSubjectContainsWords parameter specifies an exception for the DLP rule that looks for words or phrases in the Subject field of messages.

ExceptIfSubjectMatchesPatterns.d: The ExceptIfSubjectMatchesPatterns parameter specifies an exception for the DLP rule that looks for text patterns in the Subject field of messages by using regular expressions.

ExceptIfSubjectOrBodyContainsWords.d: The ExceptIfSubjectOrBodyContainsWords parameter specifies an exception for the rule that looks for words in the Subject field or body of messages.

ExceptIfSubjectOrBodyMatchesPatterns.d: The ExceptIfSubjectOrBodyMatchesPatterns parameter specifies an exception for the rule that looks for text patterns in the Subject field or body of messages.

FromAddressContainsWords.d: The FromAddressContainsWords parameter specifies a condition for the DLP rule that looks for words or phrases in the sender's email address.

FromAddressMatchesPatterns.d: The FromAddressMatchesPatterns parameter specifies a condition for the DLP rule that looks for text patterns in the sender's email address by using regular expressions.

FromScope.d: The FromScope parameter specifies whether messages from inside or outside the organisation are in scope for the DLP rule.

GenerateAlert.d: The GenerateAlert parameter specifies an action for the DLP rule that notifies the specified users when the conditions of the rule are met.

GenerateIncidentReport.d: The GenerateIncidentReport parameter specifies an action for the DLP rule that sends an incident report to the specified users when the conditions of the rule are met.

HasSenderOverride.d: The SenderOverride parameter specifies a condition for the rule that looks for messages where the sender chose to override a DLP policy.

IncidentReportContent.d: The IncidentReportContent parameter specifies the content to include in the report when you use the GenerateIncidentReport parameter.

MessageTypeMatches.d: The MessageTypeMatches parameter specifies a condition for the DLP rule that looks for types of SMIME message patterns.

Name.d: Name of the Rule.

NotifyAllowOverride.d: The NotifyAllowOverride parameter specifies the notification override options when the conditions of the rule are met.

NotifyEmailCustomText.d: The NotifyEmailCustomText parameter specifies the custom text in the email notification message that's sent to recipients when the conditions of the rule are met.

NotifyPolicyTipCustomText.d: The NotifyPolicyTipCustomText parameter specifies the custom text in the Policy Tip notification message that's shown to recipients when the conditions of the rule are met. The maximum length is 256 characters. HTML tags and tokens (variables) aren't supported.

NotifyUser.d: The NotifyUser parameter specifies an action for the DLP rule that notifies the specified users when the conditions of the rule are met.

Policy.d: Name of the associated DLP Compliance Policy.

ProcessingLimitExceeded.d: The ProcessingLimitExceeded parameter specifies a condition for the DLP rule that looks for files where scanning couldn't complete.

Quarantine.d: The Quarantine parameter specifies an action that quarantines messages. Valid values are: $true: The message is delivered to the hosted quarantine. $false: The message is not quarantined.

RecipientDomainIs.d: The RecipientDomainIs parameter specifies a condition for the DLP rule that looks for recipients with email addresses in the specified domains.

RemoveRMSTemplate.d: The RemoveRMSTemplate parameter specifies an action for the DLP rule that removes Office 365 Message Encryption from messages and their attachments.

ReportSeverityLevel.d: The ReportSeverityLevel parameter specifies the severity level of the incident report for content detections based on the rule.

RuleErrorAction.d: The RuleErrorAction parameter specifies what to do if an error is encountered during the evaluation of the rule.

SentToMemberOf.d: The SentToMemberOf parameter specifies a condition for the DLP rule that looks for messages sent to members of distribution groups, dynamic distribution groups, or mail-enabled security groups.

SetHeader.d: The SetHeader The SetHeader parameter specifies an action for the DLP rule that adds or modifies a header field and value in the message header. You can specify multiple header name and value pairs separated by commas

StopPolicyProcessing.d: The StopPolicyProcessing parameter specifies an action that stops processing more DLP policy rules.

SubjectContainsWords.d: The SubjectContainsWords parameter specifies a condition for the DLP rule that looks for words or phrases in the Subject field of messages. You can specify multiple words or phrases separated by commas.

SubjectMatchesPatterns.d: The SubjectMatchesPatterns parameter specifies a condition for the DLP rule that looks for text patterns in the Subject field of messages by using regular expressions.

SubjectOrBodyContainsWords.d: The SubjectOrBodyContainsWords parameter specifies a condition for the rule that looks for words in the Subject field or body of messages.

SubjectOrBodyMatchesPatterns.d: The SubjectOrBodyMatchesPatterns parameter specifies a condition for the rule that looks for text patterns in the Subject field or body of messages.

SCDLPSensitiveInformationType.d: Manages sensitive information types in Purview.

Description.d: The Description parameter specifies a description for the sensitive information type rule.

FileData.d: The fingerprint file data for the Sensitive Information Type.

Identity.d: Unique identifier of the Sensitive Information Type.

Locale.d: The Locale parameter specifies the language that's associated with the sensitive information type rule.

Name.d: The Name parameter specifies a name for the sensitive information type rule. The value must be less than 256 characters.

SCDLPSensitiveInformationTypeRulePackage.d: Manages sensitive information type rule packages in Purview.

Identity.d: Unique identifier of the Sensitive Information Type Rule Package.

Name.d: The Name parameter specifies a name for the sensitive information type rule package. The value must be less than 256 characters.

XmlFileData.d: The XML file data for the Sensitive Information Type Rule Package.

SCDeviceConditionalAccessPolicy.d: This resource configures a Device Conditional Access Policy in Purview.

Comment.d: The Comment parameter specifies an optional comment.

Enabled.d: The Enabled parameter specifies whether the policy is enabled.

Name.d: The name of the Device Conditional Access Policy.

SCDeviceConditionalAccessRule.d: Manages Purview Device Conditional Access rules.

AccountName.d: The AccountName parameter specifies the account name.

AccountUserName.d: The AccountUserName parameter specifies the account user name.

AllowAppStore.d: The AllowAppStore parameter specifies whether to allow access to the app store on devices.

AllowAssistantWhileLocked.d: The AllowAssistantWhileLocked parameter specifies whether to allow the use of the voice assistant while devices are locked.

AllowConvenienceLogon.d: The AllowConvenienceLogon parameter specifies whether to allow convenience logons on devices.

AllowDiagnosticSubmission.d: The AllowDiagnosticSubmission parameter specifies whether to allow diagnostic submissions from devices.

AllowJailbroken.d: The AllowJailbroken parameter specifies whether to allow access to your organization by jailbroken or rooted devices.

AllowPassbookWhileLocked.d: The AllowPassbookWhileLocked parameter specifies whether to allow the use of Apple Passbook while devices are locked.

AllowScreenshot.d: The AllowScreenshot parameter specifies whether to allow screenshots on devices.

AllowSimplePassword.d: The AllowSimplePassword parameter specifies whether to allow simple or non-complex passwords on devices.

AllowVideoConferencing.d: The AllowVideoConferencing parameter specifies whether to allow video conferencing on devices.

AllowVoiceAssistant.d: The AllowVoiceAssistant parameter specifies whether to allow using the voice assistant on devices.

AllowVoiceDialing.d: The AllowVoiceDialing parameter specifies whether to allow voice-activated telephone dialing.

AllowiCloudBackup.d: The AllowiCloudBackup parameter specifies whether to allow Apple iCloud Backup from devices.

AllowiCloudDocSync.d: The AllowiCloudDocSync parameter specifies whether to allow Apple iCloud Documents & Data sync on devices.

AllowiCloudPhotoSync.d: The AllowiCloudPhotoSync parameter specifies whether to allow Apple iCloud Photos sync on devices.

AntiVirusSignatureStatus.d: The AntiVirusSignatureStatus parameter specifies the antivirus signature status.

AntiVirusStatus.d: The AntiVirusStatus parameter specifies the antivirus status.

AppsRating.d: The AppsRating parameter species the maximum or most restrictive rating of apps that are allowed on devices.

AutoUpdateStatus.d: The AutoUpdateStatus parameter specifies the update settings for devices.

BluetoothEnabled.d: The BluetoothEnabled parameter specifies whether to enable or disable Bluetooth on devices.

CameraEnabled.d: The BluetoothEnabled parameter specifies whether to enable or disable Bluetooth on devices.

EmailAddress.d: The EmailAddress parameter specifies the email address.

EnableRemovableStorage.d: The EnableRemovableStorage parameter specifies whether removable storage can be used by devices.

ExchangeActiveSyncHost.d: The ExchangeActiveSyncHost parameter specifies the Exchange ActiveSync host.

FirewallStatus.d: The FirewallStatus parameter specifies the acceptable firewall status values on devices.

ForceAppStorePassword.d: The ForceAppStorePassword parameter specifies whether to require a password to use the app store on devices.

ForceEncryptedBackup.d: The ForceEncryptedBackup parameter specifies whether to force encrypted backups for devices.

MaxPasswordAttemptsBeforeWipe.d: The MaxPasswordAttemptsBeforeWipe parameter specifies the number of incorrect password attempts that cause devices to be automatically wiped.

MaxPasswordGracePeriod.d: The MaxPasswordGracePeriod parameter specifies the length of time users are allowed to reset expired passwords on devices.

MoviesRating.d: The MoviesRating parameter species the maximum or most restrictive rating of movies that are allowed on devices. You specify the country/region rating system to use with the RegionRatings parameter.

Name.d: Name for the rule.

PasswordComplexity.d: The PasswordComplexity parameter specifies the password complexity.

PasswordExpirationDays.d: The PasswordExpirationDays parameter specifies the number of days that the same password can be used on devices before users are required to change their passwords.

PasswordHistoryCount.d: The PasswordHistoryCount parameter specifies the minimum number of unique new passwords that are required on devices before an old password can be reused.

PasswordMinComplexChars.d: The PasswordMinComplexChars parameter specifies the minimum number of complex characters that are required for device passwords. A complex character isn't a letter.

PasswordMinimumLength.d: The PasswordMinimumLength parameter specifies the minimum number of characters that are required for device passwords.

PasswordQuality.d: The PasswordQuality parameter specifies the minimum password quality rating that's required for device passwords. Password quality is a numeric scale that indicates the security and complexity of the password. A higher quality value indicates a more secure password.

PasswordRequired.d: The PasswordRequired parameter specifies whether a password is required to access devices.

PasswordTimeout.d: The PasswordTimeout parameter specifies the length of time that devices can be inactive before a password is required to reactivate them.

PhoneMemoryEncrypted.d: The PhoneMemoryEncrypted parameter specifies whether to encrypt the memory on devices.

RegionRatings.d: The RegionRatings parameter specifies the rating system (country/region) to use for movie and television ratings with the MoviesRating and TVShowsRating parameters.

RequireEmailProfile.d: The RequireEmailProfile parameter specifies whether an email profile is required on devices.

SmartScreenEnabled.d: The SmartScreenEnabled parameter specifies whether to requireWindows SmartScreen on devices.

SystemSecurityTLS.d: The SystemSecurityTLS parameter specifies whether TLS encryption is used on devices.

TVShowsRating.d: The TVShowsRating parameter species the maximum or most restrictive rating of television shows that are allowed on devices. You specify the country/region rating system to use with the RegionRatings parameter.

TargetGroups.d: The display names of the graoups targeted by the policy.

UserAccountControlStatus.d: The UserAccountControlStatus parameter specifies how User Account Control messages are presented on devices.

WLANEnabled.d: The WLANEnabled parameter specifies whether Wi-Fi is enabled devices.

WorkFoldersSyncUrl.d: The WorkFoldersSyncUrl parameter specifies the URL that's used to synchronize company data on devices.

SCInsiderRiskEntityList.d: Configures settings for Insider Risk in Purview.

Description.d: Description for the group or setting.

DisplayName.d: The display name of the group or setting.

ExcludedSensitiveInformationTypeGroups.d: List of excluded sensitive information types.

Name.d: The name of the group or setting.

SCInsiderRiskPolicy.d: Configures Insider Risk Policies in Purview.

AIAppRiskyPrompt.d: Policy indicators > Entering risky prompt in other AI apps

AdaptiveProtectionEnabled.d: Determines if Adaptive Protection is enabled for Purview.

AnalyticsNewInsightEnabled.d: Send a monthly email summarizing new analytics scan insights.

AnalyticsTurnedOffEnabled.d: Send an email when analytics is turned off for your organization.

Anonymization.d: For users who perform activities matching your insider risk policies, decide whether to show their actual names or use pseudonymized versions to mask their identities.

CCFinancialRegulatoryRiskyTextSent.d: Detect messages matching specific trainable classifiers.

CCInappropriateContentSent.d: Detect messages matching specific trainable classifiers.

CCInappropriateImagesSent.d: Detect messages matching specific trainable classifiers.

CCPromptShields.d: Policy indicators > Entering prompt attacks in AI apps

CCProtectedMaterialDetection.d: Policy indicators > Receiving AI app responses containing protected materials

CCSensitiveInformationType.d: Policy indicators > Sending messages that contain specific sesitive info types

CCSupervisionRuleMatch.d: Policy indicators > Detect messages matched by specific Communication Compliance policies

CompromisedSignInAlerts.d: Policy indicators > Potentially risky sign-in activity

CompromisedUserAlerts.d: Policy indicators > User account potentially compromised

ConnectedAIAppRiskyPrompt.d: Policy indicators > Entering risky prompt in enterprise AI apps

ConnectedAIAppSensitiveResponse.d: Policy indicators > Receiving sensitive response from enterprise AI apps

CopilotRiskyPrompt.d: Policy indicators > Entering risky prompt in Copilot

CopilotSensitiveResponse.d: Policy indicators > Receiving sensitive response from Copilot

DLPUserRiskSync.d: When turned on, admins with the correct permissions will be able to review user risk details from Insider Risk Management within other solutions such as Data Loss Prevention (DLP), Communication Compliance, and user entity pages in Microsoft Defender.

EmailSignatureExclusionSettingsEnabled.d: When turned on, if an email containing only a signature as attachment is sent to someone outside your org, your policies will attempt to ignore the activity when assigning risk scores, thereby helping reduce inessential alerts.

EnableTeam.d: Integrate Microsoft Teams capabilities with insider risk case management to enhance collaboration with stakeholders.

FabricExternalDataSharingSwitchEnabled.d: Policy indicators > Enabling external sharing of Microsoft Fabric data

FileVolCutoffLimits.d: Minimum number of daily events to boost score for unusual activity.

HighSeverityAlertsEnabled.d: Send a daily email when new high severity alerts are generated.

HighSeverityAlertsRoleGroups.d: Specifies the groups of high severity alerts to include. Possible values are: InsiderRiskManagement, InsiderRiskManagementAnalysts, and InsiderRiskManagementInvestigators.

HighSeverityDlpRuleMatch.d: Policy indicators > Generating alerts from selected DLP policies

HistoricTimeSpan.d: Determines how far back a policy should go to detect user activity and is triggered when a user performs the first activity matching a policy.

IRASettingsEnabled.d: When turned on, data is aggregated at tenant level and is shown as insights in Analytics reports.

InScopeTimeSpan.d: Determines how long policies will actively detect activity for users and is triggered when a user performs the first activity matching a policy.

InlineAlertPolicyCustomization.d: Enable inline alert customization for all alert reviewers.

InsiderRiskScenario.d: Name of the scenario supported by the policy.

LakehouseArtifactDeleted.d: Policy indicators > Deleting Microsoft Fabric lakehouses

LakehouseExternalDataShareCreated.d: Policy indicators > Sharing lakehouse data with people outside the organization

LakehouseFileOrBlobDeleted.d: Policy indicators > Deleted lakehouse files or tables

LakehouseSensitivityLabelDowngraded.d: Policy indicators > Downgrading sensitivity labels of lakehouses

LakehouseSensitivityLabelRemoved.d: Policy indicators > Removing sensitivity labels of lakehouses

MDATPTriageStatus.d: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint alert statuses.

Mcas3rdPartyAppDownload.d: Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps indicator.

Mcas3rdPartyAppFileDelete.d: Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps indicator.

Mcas3rdPartyAppFileSharing.d: Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps indicator.

McasActivityFromInfrequentCountry.d: Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps indicator.

McasImpossibleTravel.d: Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps indicator.

McasMultipleFailedLogins.d: Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps indicator.

McasMultipleStorageDeletion.d: Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps indicator.

McasMultipleVMCreation.d: Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps indicator.

McasMultipleVMDeletion.d: Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps indicator.

McasSuspiciousAdminActivities.d: Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps indicator.

McasSuspiciousCloudCreation.d: Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps indicator.

McasSuspiciousCloudTrailLoggingChange.d: Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps indicator.

McasTerminatedEmployeeActivity.d: Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps indicator.

Name.d: Name of the insider risk policy.

NetworkDownloadFile.d: Policy indicators > Files downloaded from the web

NetworkDownloadText.d: Policy indicators > Sensitive text downloaded from the web

NetworkUploadFile.d: Policy indicators > Files uploaded to the web

NetworkUploadText.d: Policy indicators > Sensitive text uploaded to the web

NotificationDetailsEnabled.d: Send a notification email when the first alert is generated for a new policy.

NotificationDetailsRoleGroups.d: Specifies the groups to notify when the first alert is generated. Possible values are: InsiderRiskManagement, InsiderRiskManagementAnalysts, and InsiderRiskManagementInvestigators.

OptInIRMDataExport.d: When turned on, admins with the correct permissions will be able to review user risk details from Insider Risk Management within other solutions such as Data Loss Prevention (DLP), Communication Compliance, and user entity pages in Microsoft Defender.

PoliciesHealthEnabled.d: Send a weekly email summarizing policies that have unresolved warnings.

PoliciesHealthRoleGroups.d: Specifies the groups to notify with weekly email. Possible values are: InsiderRiskManagement and InsiderRiskManagementAdmins.

RaiseAuditAlert.d: Insider risk management alert information is exportable to security information and event management (SIEM) services by using Office 365 Management Activity APIs. Turn this on to use these APIs to export insider risk alert details to other applications your organization might use to manage or aggregate insider risk data.

UserAnalyticsSettingsEnabled.d: When turned on, data is aggregated at user level and is shown as insights in user activity summary of Data Loss Prevention, Communication Compliance and Microsoft Defender along with Advanced Hunting tables. Data sharing needs to be turned on along with this.

SCLabelPolicy.d: This resource configures a Label Policy in Purview.

AddExchangeLocation.d: The AddExchangeLocation parameter specifies the mailboxes to add in the existing policy.

AddExchangeLocationException.d: The AddExchangeLocationException parameter specifies the mailboxes to add to exclusions when you use the value All for the ExchangeLocation parameter.

AddLabels.d: The AddLabels parameter specifies the sensitivity labels to add to the policy. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the label.

AddModernGroupLocation.d: The AddModernGroupLocation parameter specifies the Microsoft 365 Groups to add to include the policy.

AddModernGroupLocationException.d: The AddModernGroupLocationException parameter specifies the Microsoft 365 Groups to add to exclusions when you're using the value All for the ModernGroupLocation parameter.

AdvancedSettings.d: The AdvancedSettings parameter enables client-specific features and capabilities on the sensitivity label. The settings that you configure with this parameter only affect apps that are designed for the setting.

Comment.d: The Comment parameter specifies an optional comment.

ExchangeLocation.d: The ExchangeLocation parameter specifies the mailboxes to include in the policy.

ExchangeLocationException.d: The ExchangeLocationException parameter specifies the mailboxes to exclude when you use the value All for the ExchangeLocation parameter.

Labels.d: The Labels parameter specifies the sensitivity labels that are associated with the policy. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the label.

ModernGroupLocation.d: The ModernGroupLocation parameter specifies the Microsoft 365 Groups to include in the policy.

ModernGroupLocationException.d: The ModernGroupLocationException parameter specifies the Microsoft 365 Groups to exclude when you're using the value All for the ModernGroupLocation parameter.

Name.d: The Name parameter specifies the unique name for the sensitivity label. The maximum length is 64 characters. If the value contains spaces, enclose the value in quotation marks.

RemoveExchangeLocation.d: The RemoveExchangeLocation parameter specifies the mailboxes to remove from the policy.

RemoveExchangeLocationException.d: The RemoveExchangeLocationException parameter specifies the mailboxes to remove when you use the value All for the ExchangeLocation parameter.

RemoveLabels.d: The RemoveLabels parameter specifies the sensitivity labels that are removed from the policy. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the label.

RemoveModernGroupLocation.d: The RemoveModernGroupLocation parameter specifies the Microsoft 365 Groups to remove from the policy.

RemoveModernGroupLocationException.d: The RemoveModernGroupLocationException parameter specifies the Microsoft 365 Groups to remove from excluded values when you're using the value All for the ModernGroupLocation parameter.

SCPolicyConfig.d: Configures the Data Loss Prevention settings in Purview.

AdvancedClassificationEnabled.d: Default value is false. If set to false, then you cannot specify BandwidthLimitEnabled nor DailyBandwidthLimitInMb

CustomBusinessJustificationNotification.d: Default value is 0. If set to 0, you cannot specify the BusinessJustificationList parameter as part of your configuration.

EnableLabelCoauth.d: The EnableLabelCoauth parameter enables or disables co-authoring support in Office desktop apps for the entire organization. Default value is false.

EnableSpoAipMigration.d: The EnableSpoAipMigration parameter enables or disables built-in labeling for supported Office files in SharePoint and OneDrive.

SCProtectionAlert.d: This resource configures a Protection Alert in Purview.

AggregationType.d: Specifies how the alert policy triggers alerts for multiple occurrences of monitored activity

AlertBy.d: Specifies the scope for aggregated alert policies

AlertFor.d: This parameter is reserved for internal Microsoft use

Category.d: Specifies a category for the alert policy

Comment.d: Specifies an optional comment

Disabled.d: Enables or disables the alert policy

Filter.d: The Filter parameter uses OPATH syntax to filter the results by the specified properties and values

Name.d: Specifies the unique name for the alert policy

NotificationCulture.d: Specifies the language or locale that's used for notifications. For example, da-DK for Danish

NotifyUser.d: Specifies the SMTP address of the user who receives notification messages for the alert policy. You can specify multiple values separated by commas

NotifyUserOnFilterMatch.d: Specifies whether to trigger an alert for a single event when the alert policy is configured for aggregated activity

NotifyUserSuppressionExpiryDate.d: Specifies whether to temporarily suspend notifications for the alert policy. Until the specified date-time, no notifications are sent for detected activities.

NotifyUserThrottleThreshold.d: Specifies the maximum number of notifications for the alert policy within the time period specified by the NotifyUserThrottleWindow parameter. Once the maximum number of notifications has been reached in the time period, no more notifications are sent for the alert.

NotifyUserThrottleWindow.d: Specifies the time interval in minutes that's used by the NotifyUserThrottleThreshold parameter

Operation.d: Specifies the activities that are monitored by the alert policy

Severity.d: specifies the severity of the detection

ThreatType.d: Specifies the type of activities that are monitored by the alert policy

Threshold.d: Specifies the number of detections that trigger the alert policy within the time period specified by the TimeWindow parameter. A valid value is an integer that's greater than or equal to 3.

TimeWindow.d: Specifies the time interval in minutes for number of detections specified by the Threshold parameter. A valid value is an integer that's greater than 60 (one hour).

SCRecordReviewNotificationTemplateConfig.d: Configures Purview Records Management disposition settings.

CustomizedNotificationDataString.d: The CustomizedNotificationDataString parameter specifies the customized review notification text to use. If the value contains spaces, enclose the value in quotation marks.

CustomizedReminderDataString.d: The CustomizedReminderDataString parameter specifies the customized review reminder text to use. If the value contains spaces, enclose the value in quotation marks.

IsCustomizedNotificationTemplate.d: The IsCustomizedNotificationTemplate switch specifies whether to use a customized review notification instead of the system default notification.

IsCustomizedReminderTemplate.d: The IsCustomizedReminderTemplate switch specifies whether to use a customized review reminder instead of the system default reminder.

IsSingleInstance.d: Specifies the resource is a single instance, the value must be 'Yes'

SCRetentionCompliancePolicy.d: This resource configures a Retention Compliance Policy in Purview.

Comment.d: The Comment parameter specifies an optional comment.

DynamicScopeLocation.d: Location of the dynamic scope for this policy.

Enabled.d: Determines if the policy is enabled or not.

ExchangeLocation.d: The ExchangeLocation parameter specifies the mailboxes to include.

ExchangeLocationException.d: This parameter specifies the mailboxes to remove from the list of excluded mailboxes when you use the value All for the ExchangeLocation parameter

ModernGroupLocation.d: The ModernGroupLocation parameter specifies the Office 365 groups to include in the policy.

ModernGroupLocationException.d: The ModernGroupLocationException parameter specifies the Office 365 groups to exclude when you're using the value All for the ModernGroupLocation parameter.

Name.d: The Name parameter specifies the unique name of the retention policy.

OneDriveLocation.d: The OneDriveLocation parameter specifies the OneDrive for Business sites to include. You identify the site by its URL value, or you can use the value All to include all sites.

OneDriveLocationException.d: This parameter specifies the OneDrive for Business sites to exclude when you use the value All for the OneDriveLocation parameter. You identify the site by its URL value.

PublicFolderLocation.d: The PublicFolderLocation parameter specifies that you want to include all public folders in the retention policy. You use the value All for this parameter.

RestrictiveRetention.d: The RestrictiveRetention parameter specifies whether Preservation Lock is enabled for the policy.

SharePointLocation.d: The SharePointLocation parameter specifies the SharePoint Online sites to include. You identify the site by its URL value, or you can use the value All to include all sites.

SharePointLocationException.d: This parameter specifies the SharePoint Online sites to exclude when you use the value All for the SharePointLocation parameter. You identify the site by its URL value.

SkypeLocation.d: The SkypeLocation parameter specifies the Skype for Business Online users to include in the policy.

SkypeLocationException.d: This parameter is reserved for internal Microsoft use.

TeamsChannelLocation.d: The TeamsChannelLocation parameter specifies the Teams Channel to include in the policy.

TeamsChannelLocationException.d: This parameter specifies the SharePoint Online sites to exclude when you use the value All for the TeamsChannelLocation parameter. You identify the site by its URL value.

TeamsChatLocation.d: The TeamsChatLocation parameter specifies the Teams Chat to include in the policy.

TeamsChatLocationException.d: This parameter specifies the SharePoint Online sites to exclude when you use the value All for the TeamsChatLocation parameter. You identify the site by its URL value.

SCRetentionComplianceRule.d: This resource configures a Retention Compliance Rule in Purview.

Comment.d: The Comment parameter specifies an optional comment.

ContentMatchQuery.d: The ContentMatchQuery parameter specifies a content search filter.

ExcludedItemClasses.d: The ExcludedItemClasses parameter specifies the types of messages to exclude from the rule. You can use this parameter only to exclude items from a hold policy, which excludes the specified item class from being held. Using this parameter won't exclude items from deletion policies. Typically, you use this parameter to exclude voicemail messages, IM conversations, and other Skype for Business Online content from being held by a hold policy.

ExpirationDateOption.d: The ExpirationDateOption parameter specifies whether the expiration date is calculated from the content creation date or last modification date. Valid values are: CreationAgeInDays and ModificationAgeInDays.

Name.d: The Name parameter specifies the unique name of the retention rule.

Policy.d: The Policy parameter specifies the policy to contain the rule.

RetentionComplianceAction.d: The RetentionComplianceAction parameter specifies the retention action for the rule. Valid values are: Delete, Keep and KeepAndDelete.

RetentionDuration.d: The RetentionDuration parameter specifies the hold duration for the retention rule. Valid values are: An integer - The hold duration in days, Unlimited - The content is held indefinitely.

RetentionDurationDisplayHint.d: The RetentionDurationDisplayHint parameter specifies the units that are used to display the retention duration in the Security and Compliance Center. Valid values are: Days, Months or Years.

SCRetentionEventType.d: This resource configures a Retention Event Type in Purview.

Comment.d: The Comment parameter specifies an optional comment.

Name.d: The Name parameter specifies the unique name of the retention event type.

SCRoleGroup.d: This resource configures a Role Group in Purview.

Description.d: The Description parameter specifies the description that's displayed when the role group is viewed using the Get-RoleGroup cmdlet. Enclose the description in quotation marks

DisplayName.d: The DisplayName parameter specifies the friendly name of the role group. If the name contains spaces, enclose the name in quotation marks. This parameter has a maximum length of 256 characters.

Name.d: The Name parameter specifies the name of the role. The maximum length of the name is 64 characters.

Roles.d: The Roles parameter specifies the management roles to assign to the role group when it's created. If a role name contains spaces, enclose the name in quotation marks. If you want to assign more that one role, separate the role names with commas.

SCRoleGroupMember.d: This resource configures a Role Group Member in Purview.

Description.d: The Description parameter specifies the description that's displayed when the role group is viewed using the Get-RoleGroup cmdlet. Enclose the description in quotation marks

Members.d: The Members parameter specifies the mailboxes or mail-enabled USGs to add as a member of the role group. You can identify the user or group by the name, DN, or primary SMTP address value. You can specify multiple members separated by commas (Value1,Value2,...ValueN). If the value contains spaces, enclose the value in quotation marks

Name.d: The Name parameter specifies the name of the role. The maximum length of the name is 64 characters.

SCSecurityFilter.d: This resource configures a Security Filter in Purview.

Action.d: The Action parameter filters the results by the type of search action that a filter is applied to.

Description.d: The Description parameter specifies a description for the compliance security filter. The maximum length is 256 characters. If the value contains spaces, enclose the value in quotation marks (").

FilterName.d: The FilterName parameter specifies the name of the compliance security filter that you want to view. If the value contains spaces, enclose the value in quotation marks (").

Filters.d: The Filters parameter specifies the search criteria for the compliance security filter. The filters are applied to the users specified by the Users parameter. You can create three different types of filters: Mailbox filter, Mailbox content filter or Site and site content filter

Region.d: The Region parameter specifies the satellite location for multi-geo tenants to conduct eDiscovery searches in.

Users.d: The User parameter filters the results by the user who gets a filter applied to their searches. Acceptable values are : The alias or email address of a user, All or The name of a role group

SCSensitivityLabel.d: This resource configures a Sensitivity Label in Purview.

AdvancedSettings.d: The AdvancedSettings parameter enables client-specific features and capabilities on the sensitivity label. The settings that you configure with this parameter only affect apps that are designed for the setting.

ApplyContentMarkingFooterAlignment.d: The ApplyContentMarkingFooterAlignment parameter specifies the footer alignment.

ApplyContentMarkingFooterEnabled.d: The ApplyContentMarkingFooterEnabled parameter specifies whether to enable or disable the sensitivity label.

ApplyContentMarkingFooterFontColor.d: The ApplyContentMarkingFooterFontColor parameter specifies the color of the footer text. This parameter accepts a hexadecimal color code value in the format #xxxxxx. The default value is #000000.

ApplyContentMarkingFooterFontSize.d: The ApplyContentMarkingFooterFontSize parameter specifies the font size (in points) of the footer text.

ApplyContentMarkingFooterMargin.d: The ApplyContentMarkingFooterMargin parameter specifies the size (in points) of the footer margin.

ApplyContentMarkingFooterText.d: The ApplyContentMarkingFooterText parameter specifies the footer text. If the value contains spaces, enclose the value in quotation marks.

ApplyContentMarkingHeaderAlignment.d: The ApplyContentMarkingHeaderAlignment parameter specifies the header alignment.

ApplyContentMarkingHeaderEnabled.d: The ApplyContentMarkingHeaderEnabled parameter enables or disables the Apply Content Marking Header action for the label.

ApplyContentMarkingHeaderFontColor.d: The ApplyContentMarkingHeaderFontColor parameter specifies the color of the header text. This parameter accepts a hexadecimal color code value in the format #xxxxxx. The default value is #000000.

ApplyContentMarkingHeaderFontSize.d: The ApplyContentMarkingHeaderFontSize parameter specifies the font size (in points) of the header text.

ApplyContentMarkingHeaderMargin.d: The ApplyContentMarkingHeaderMargin parameter specifies the size (in points) of the header margin.

ApplyContentMarkingHeaderText.d: The ApplyContentMarkingHeaderText parameter specifies the header text. If the value contains spaces, enclose the value in quotation marks.

ApplyWaterMarkingEnabled.d: The ApplyWaterMarkingEnabled parameter enables or disables the Apply Watermarking Header action for the label.

ApplyWaterMarkingFontColor.d: The ApplyWaterMarkingFontColor parameter specifies the color of the watermark text. This parameter accepts a hexadecimal color code value in the format #xxxxxx.

ApplyWaterMarkingFontSize.d: The ApplyWaterMarkingFontSize parameter specifies the font size (in points) of the watermark text.

ApplyWaterMarkingLayout.d: The ApplyWaterMarkingAlignment parameter specifies the watermark alignment.

ApplyWaterMarkingText.d: The ApplyWaterMarkingText parameter specifies the watermark text. If the value contains spaces, enclose the value in quotation marks.

AutoLabelingSettings.d: The AutoLabelingSettings parameter specifies the conditions for label to be automatically applied to files and emails.

Comment.d: The Comment parameter specifies an optional comment.

ContentType.d: The ContentType parameter specifies where the sensitivity label can be applied.

DisplayName.d: The DisplayName parameter specifies the display name for the sensitivity label. The display name appears in the Microsoft Office and is used by Outlook users to select the appropriate sensitivity label before they send a message.

EncryptionContentExpiredOnDateInDaysOrNe.d: The EncryptionContentExpiredOnDateInDaysOrNever parameter specifies when the encrypted content expires. Valid values are integer or never.

EncryptionDoNotForward.d: The EncryptionDoNotForward parameter specifies whether the Do Not Forward template is applied.

EncryptionEnabled.d: The EncryptionEnabled parameter specifies whether encryption in enabled.

EncryptionEncryptOnly.d: The EncryptionEncryptOnly parameter specifies whether the encrypt-only template is applied.

EncryptionOfflineAccessDays.d: The EncryptionOfflineAccessDays parameter specifies the number of days that offline access is allowed.

EncryptionPromptUser.d: The EncryptionPromptUser parameter specifies whether to set the label with user defined permission in Word, Excel, and PowerPoint.

EncryptionProtectionType.d: The EncryptionProtectionType parameter specifies the protection type for encryption.

EncryptionRightsDefinitions.d: The EncryptionRightsDefinitions parameter specifies the rights users have when accessing protected. This parameter uses the syntax Identity1:Rights1,Rights2;Identity2:Rights3,Rights4. For example, john@contoso.com:VIEW,EDIT;microsoft.com:VIEW.

EncryptionRightsUrl.d: The EncryptionRightsUrl parameter specifies the URL for hold your own key (HYOK) protection.

LocaleSettings.d: The LocaleSettings parameter specifies one or more localized label name or label Tooltips in different languages. Regions include all region codes supported in Office Client applications.

Name.d: The Name parameter specifies the unique name for the sensitivity label. The maximum length is 64 characters. If the value contains spaces, enclose the value in quotation marks.

ParentId.d: The ParentId parameter specifies the parent label that you want this label to be under (a sublabel). You can use any value that uniquely identifies the parent sensitivity label for example name.

Priority.d: The Priority parameter specifies a priority value for the sensitivity label that determines the order of label processing. A lower integer value indicates a higher priority.

SiteAndGroupExternalSharingControlType.d: The SiteAndGroupExternalSharingControlType parameter specifies the external user sharing setting for the label.

SiteAndGroupProtectionAllowAccessToGuest.d: The SiteAndGroupProtectionAllowAccessToGuestUsers parameter enables or disables access to guest users.

SiteAndGroupProtectionAllowEmailFromGues.d: The SiteAndGroupProtectionAllowEmailFromGuestUsers parameter enables or disables email from guest users.

SiteAndGroupProtectionAllowFullAccess.d: The SiteAndGroupProtectionAllowFullAccess parameter enables or disables full access.

SiteAndGroupProtectionAllowLimitedAccess.d: The SiteAndGroupProtectionAllowLimitedAccess parameter enables or disables limited access.

SiteAndGroupProtectionEnabled.d: The SiteAndGroupProtectionEnabled parameter enables or disables the Site and Group Protection action for the labels.

SiteAndGroupProtectionPrivacy.d: The SiteAndGroupProtectionPrivacy parameter specifies the privacy level for the label.

Tooltip.d: The ToolTip parameter specifies the default tooltip and sensitivity label description that's seen by users. It the value contains spaces, enclose the value in quotation marks.

SCSupervisoryReviewPolicy.d: This resource configures a Supervisory Review Policy in Purview.

Comment.d: The Comment parameter specifies an optional comment. If you specify a value that contains spaces, enclose the value in quotation marks.

Name.d: The Name parameter specifies the unique name for the supervisory review policy. The name can't exceed 64 characters. If the value contains spaces, enclose the value in quotation marks.

Reviewers.d: The Reviewers parameter specifies the SMTP addresses of the reviewers for the supervisory review policy. You can specify multiple email addresses separated by commas.

SCSupervisoryReviewRule.d: This resource configures a Supervisory Review Rule in Purview.

Condition.d: The Condition parameter specifies the conditions and exceptions for the rule.

Name.d: The Name parameter specifies the unique name for the supervisory review policy. The name can't exceed 64 characters. If the value contains spaces, enclose the value in quotation marks.

Policy.d: The Policy parameter specifies the supervisory review policy that's assigned to the rule. You can use any value that uniquely identifies the policy.

SamplingRate.d: The SamplingRate parameter specifies the percentage of communications for review. If you want reviewers to review all detected items, use the value 100.

SCUnifiedAuditLogRetentionPolicy.d: This resource configures a Unified Audit Log Retention Policy in Purview.

Description.d: The description for the audit log retention policy

Name.d: Unique name for the audit log retention policy

Operations.d: Specifies the audit log operations that are retained by the policy

Priority.d: Priority value for the policy that determines the order of policy processing.

RecordTypes.d: Specifies the audit logs of a specific record type that are retained by the policy.

RetentionDuration.d: How long audit log records are kept

UserIds.d: Specifies the audit logs that are retained by the policy based on the ID of the user who performed the action

SHSpaceGroup.d: Represents a Services Hub space group.

Roles.d: List of roles associated with the group. Accepted values are: CustomerActivityPagePermissionRole, HealthPermissionRole, InviteUsersPermissionRole, PlansPermissionRole, SharedFilesPermissionRole, SupportCasePermissionRole, TrainingManager, TrainingPermissionRole, WorkspaceAdministratorRole. Role Account manager,IncidentManagerUnified,CSMAdministrator, ContractSupportUser are read-only and inherited from the upstream system and cannot be modified.

SpaceName.d: Name of the associated Services Hub space.

SHSpaceUser.d: Represents a Services Hub space user.

Roles.d: List of roles associated with the user. Accepted values are: CustomerActivityPagePermissionRole, HealthPermissionRole, InviteUsersPermissionRole, PlansPermissionRole, SharedFilesPermissionRole, SupportCasePermissionRole, TrainingManager, TrainingPermissionRole, WorkspaceAdministratorRole. Role Account manager,IncidentManagerUnified,CSMAdministrator, ContractSupportUser are read-only and inherited from the upstream system and cannot be modified.

SpaceName.d: Name of the associated Services Hub space.

SPOAccessControlSettings.d: This resource allows users to configure and monitor the access control settings for your SPO tenant sharing settings.

ConditionalAccessPolicy.d: Blocks or limits access to SharePoint and OneDrive content from un-managed devices.

DisallowInfectedFileDownload.d: Prevents the Download button from being displayed on the Virus Found warning page.

DisplayStartASiteOption.d: Determines whether tenant users see the Start a Site menu option

EnableRestrictedAccessControl.d: Enables or disables the restricted access control.

ExternalServicesEnabled.d: Enables external services for a tenant. External services are defined as services that are not in the Office 365 datacenters.

IPAddressAllowList.d: Configures multiple IP addresses or IP address ranges (IPv4 or IPv6). Use commas to separate multiple IP addresses or IP address ranges.

IPAddressEnforcement.d: Allows access from network locations that are defined by an administrator.

IsSingleInstance.d: Specifies the resource is a single instance, the value must be 'Yes'

StartASiteFormUrl.d: Specifies URL of the form to load in the Start a Site dialog. The valid values are:<emptyString> (default) - Blank by default, this will also remove or clear any value that has been set.Full URL - Example: https://contoso.sharepoint.com/path/to/form

SPOApp.d: This resource allows users to deploy App instances in the App Catalog.

Identity.d: The name of the App.

Overwrite.d: Overwrites the existing app package if it already exists.

Path.d: The path the the app package on disk.

Publish.d: This will deploy/trust an app into the app catalog.

SPOBrowserIdleSignout.d: This resource configures an SharePoint Online Idle session sign-out policy.

Enabled.d: Enables the browser idle sign-out policy

IsSingleInstance.d: Specifies the resource is a single instance, the value must be 'Yes'

SignOutAfter.d: Specifies a time interval of inactivity before the user gets signed out

WarnAfter.d: Specifies a time interval of inactivity before the user gets a warning about being signed out

SPOHomeSite.d: This resource allows administrators to configure a Site Collection as the Home Site of the tenant. The menu of this SharePoint Site Collection will be shown on SharePoint Home.

IsSingleInstance.d: Specifies the resource is a single instance, the value must be 'Yes'

Url.d: The URL of the home site collection

SPOHubSite.d: This resource allows users to configure a Site Collection as Hub Site Collection and configure its properties.

AllowedToJoin.d: The users or mail-enabled security groups which are allowed to associate their site with a hub site

Description.d: The description of the hub site

LogoUrl.d: The url to the logo of the hub site

RequiresJoinApproval.d: Does the hub site require approval to join

SiteDesignId.d: The guid of the site design to link to the hub site

Title.d: The title of the hub site

Url.d: The URL of the site collection

SPOOrgAssetsLibrary.d: This resource configures an SharePoint Online Org site assets library.

CdnType.d: Specifies the CDN type. The valid values are public or private.

LibraryUrl.d: Indicates the absolute URL of the library to be designated as a central location for organization assets.

ThumbnailUrl.d: Indicates the absolute URL of the library to be designated as a central location for organization Indicates the URL of the background image used when the library is publicly displayed. If no thumbnail URL is indicated, the card will have a gray background.

SPOPropertyBag.d: Configures a value in a site's property bag.

Url.d: Url of the site where to configure the PropertyBag property.

SPORetentionLabelsSettings.d: Configures the retention label settings. This setting is accessible via the Purview Record Management settings screen.

AdvancedRecordVersioningDisabled.d: Set to enable or disable the advanced record versioning.

AllowFilesWithKeepLabelToBeDeletedODB.d: Set whether files with Keep Label can be deleted in OneDrive for Business.

AllowFilesWithKeepLabelToBeDeletedSPO.d: Set whether files with Keep Label can be deleted in SharePoint Online.

IsSingleInstance.d: Specifies the resource is a single instance, the value must be 'Yes'

MetadataEditBlockingEnabled.d: Set metadata edit blocking enabled setting.

SPOSearchManagedProperty.d: This resource allows users to create and monitor SharePoint Online Search Managed Properties.

Aliases.d: Define an alias for a managed property if you want to use the alias instead of the managed property name in queries and in search results. Use the original managed property and not the alias to map to a crawled property. Use an alias if you don't want to or don't have permission to create a new managed property.

AllowMultipleValues.d: Allow multiple values of the same type in this managed property. For example, if this is the 'author' managed property, and a document has multiple authors, each author name will be stored as a separate value in this managed property.

CompanyNameExtraction.d: Enables the system to extract company name entities from the managed property when crawling new or updated items. Afterwards, the extracted entities can be used to set up refiners in the web part.

CompleteMatching.d: By default, search returns partial matches between queries against this managed property and its content. Select Complete Matching for search to return exact matches instead. If a managed property 'Title' contains 'Contoso Sites', only the query Title: 'Contoso Sites' will give a result.

Description.d: Description of the Managed Property

FinerQueryTokenization.d: By default, search tokenizes queries coarser than content. If a managed property 'ID' contains the string '1-23-456#7', and you query ID:'1-23', you might not get a partial match because search didn't break the query into small enough parts. Consider selecting finer query tokenization if the content of this managed property contains separators such as dots and dashes. Finer query tokenization makes queries against this managed property slower.

FullTextContext.d: Defines the context of a managed property within its full-text index.

FullTextIndex.d: Defines which full-text index the Managed Property is stored in.

LanguageNeutralTokenization.d: By default, search depends on language when it breaks queries and content into parts (tokenization). Select language neutral tokenization if you have multilingual content and this managed property contains tags that are based on metadata term sets or other identifiers.

MappedCrawledProperties.d: Names of the crawled properties that are mapped to this managed property

Name.d: The Name of the Managed Property

Queryable.d: Enables querying against the specific Managed Property. The Managed Property field name must be included in the query, either specified in the query itself or included in the query programmatically. If the Managed Property is 'author', the query must contain 'author:Smith'.

Refinable.d: Yes: Enables using the property as a refiner for search results in the front end. You must manually configure the refiner in the web part. Yes - latent: Enables switching refinable to active later, without having to do a full re-crawl when you switch. Both options require a full crawl to take effect.

Retrievable.d: Enables the content of this managed property to be returned in search results. Enable this setting for managed properties that are relevant to present in search results.

Safe.d: Enables this managed property to be returned for queries executed by anonymous users. Enable this setting for managed properties that do not contain sensitive information and are appropriate for anonymous users to view.

Searchable.d: Enables querying against the content of the managed property. The content of this managed property is included in the full-text index. For example, if the property is 'author', a simple query for 'Smith' returns items containing the word 'Smith' and items whose author property contains 'Smith'.

Sortable.d: Yes: Enables sorting the result set based on the property before the result set is returned. Use for example for large result sets that cannot be sorted and retrieved at the same time. Yes - latent: Enables switching sortable to active later, without having to do a full re-crawl when you switch. Both options require a full crawl to take effect.

TokenNormalization.d: Enable to return results independent of letter casing and diacritics(for example accented characters) used in the query.

Type.d: The Type of the Managed Property

SPOSearchResultSource.d: This resource allows users to create and monitor SharePoint Online Search Result Sources.

Description.d: Description of the Result Source.

Name.d: The Name of the Result Source.

Protocol.d: The protocol of the Result Source.

QueryTransform.d: Change incoming queries to use this new query text instead. Include the incoming query in the new text by using the query variable '{searchTerms}'.

SourceURL.d: Address of the root site collection of the remote SharePoint farm or Exchange server.

Type.d: Select SharePoint Search Results to search over the entire index. Select People Search Results to enable query processing specific to People Search, such as phonetic name matching or nickname matching. Only people profiles will be returned from a People Search source.

UseAutoDiscover.d: Specifies if AutoDiscover should be used for the Exchange Source URL

SPOSharingSettings.d: This resource allows users to configure and monitor the sharing settings for your SPO tenant sharing settings

BccExternalSharingInvitations.d: When the feature is enabled, all external sharing invitations that are sent will blind copy the e-mail messages listed in the BccExternalSharingsInvitationList.

BccExternalSharingInvitationsList.d: Specifies a list of e-mail addresses to be BCC'd when the BCC for External Sharing feature is enabled.Multiple addresses can be specified by creating a comma separated list with no spaces.

DefaultLinkPermission.d: Specifies the link permission on the tenant level. Valid values to set are View and Edit. A value of None will be set to Edit as its the default value.

DefaultSharingLinkType.d: Lets administrators choose what type of link appears is selected in the 'Get a link' sharing dialog box in OneDrive for Business and SharePoint Online

EnableGuestSignInAcceleration.d: Accelerates guest-enabled site collections as well as member-only site collections when the SignInAccelerationDomain parameter is set.

ExternalUserExpirationRequired.d: Enable Guest access to a site or Onedrive to expire after

ExternalUserExpireInDays.d: Specifies Number of days for Guest Access links to expire.

FileAnonymousLinkType.d: Configures anonymous link types for files

FolderAnonymousLinkType.d: Configures anonymous link types for folders

IsSingleInstance.d: Specifies the resource is a single instance, the value must be 'Yes'

NotifyOwnersWhenItemsReshared.d: When this parameter is set to $true and another user re-shares a document from a user’s OneDrive for Business, the OneDrive for Business owner is notified by e-mail.

PreventExternalUsersFromResharing.d: Allow or deny external users re-sharing

ProvisionSharedWithEveryoneFolder.d: Creates a Shared with Everyone folder in every user's new OneDrive for Business document library.

RequireAnonymousLinksExpireInDays.d: Specifies all anonymous links that have been created (or will be created) will expire after the set number of days.

SharingAllowedDomainList.d: Specifies a list of email domains that is allowed for sharing with the external collaborators. Entry values as an array of domains.

SharingBlockedDomainList.d: Specifies a list of email domains that is blocked or prohibited for sharing with the external collaborators. Entry values as an array of domains.

SharingDomainRestrictionMode.d: Specifies the external sharing mode for domains.

ShowAllUsersClaim.d: Enables the administrator to hide the All Users claim groups in People Picker.

ShowEveryoneClaim.d: Enables the administrator to hide the Everyone claim in the People Picker.

ShowEveryoneExceptExternalUsersClaim.d: Enables the administrator to hide the Everyone except external users claim in the People Picker.

ShowPeoplePickerSuggestionsForGuestUsers.d: Enables the administrator to hide the guest users claim in the People Picker.

SPOSite.d: This resource allows users to create and monitor SharePoint Online Site Collections.

AllowSelfServiceUpgrade.d: Specifies if the site administrator can upgrade the site collection.

AnonymousLinkExpirationInDays.d: Specifies that all anonymous/anyone links that have been created (or will be created) will expire after the set number of days. Only applies if OverrideTenantAnonymousLinkExpirationPolicy is set to true. To remove the expiration requirement, set the value to zero (0)

CommentsOnSitePagesDisabled.d: Specifies if comments on site pages are enabled or disabled.

DefaultLinkPermission.d: Specifies the default link permission for the site collection. None - Respect the organization default link permission. View - Sets the default link permission for the site to 'view' permissions. Edit - Sets the default link permission for the site to 'edit' permissions.

DefaultSharingLinkType.d: Specifies the default link type for the site collection. None - Respect the organization default sharing link type. AnonymousAccess - Sets the default sharing link for this site to an Anonymous Access or Anyone link. Internal - Sets the default sharing link for this site to the 'organization' link or company shareable link. Direct - Sets the default sharing link for this site to the 'Specific people' link.

DenyAddAndCustomizePages.d: Determines whether the Add And Customize Pages right is denied on the site collection. For more information about permission levels, see User permissions and permission levels in SharePoint.

DisableFlows.d: Disables Microsoft Flow for this site.

HubUrl.d: The URL of the Hub site the site collection needs to get connected to.

LocaleId.d: Specifies the language of the new site collection. Defaults to the current language of the web connected to.

OverrideTenantAnonymousLinkExpirationPol.d: False - Respect the organization-level policy for anonymous or anyone link expiration. True - Override the organization-level policy for anonymous or anyone link expiration (can be more or less restrictive)

Owner.d: Specifies the owner of the site.

RestrictedToRegion.d: Defines geo-restriction settings for this site

SharingAllowedDomainList.d: Specifies a list of email domains that is allowed for sharing with the external collaborators. Use the space character as the delimiter.

SharingBlockedDomainList.d: Specifies a list of email domains that is blocked for sharing with the external collaborators.

SharingCapability.d: Specifies what the sharing capabilities are for the site. Possible values: Disabled, ExternalUserSharingOnly, ExternalUserAndGuestSharing, ExistingExternalUserSharingOnly.

SharingDomainRestrictionMode.d: Specifies the external sharing mode for domains.

ShowPeoplePickerSuggestionsForGuestUsers.d: To enable the option to search for existing guest users at Site Collection Level, set this parameter to $true.

SocialBarOnSitePagesDisabled.d: Disables or enables the Social Bar for Site Collection.

StorageMaximumLevel.d: Specifies the storage quota for this site collection in megabytes. This value must not exceed the company's available quota.

StorageWarningLevel.d: Specifies the warning level for the storage quota in megabytes. This value must not exceed the values set for the StorageMaximumLevel parameter.

Template.d: Specifies with template of site to create.

TimeZoneId.d: TimeZone ID of the site collection.

Title.d: The title of the site collection.

Url.d: The URL of the site collection.

SPOSiteAuditSettings.d: Set Audit settings for a site.

AuditFlags.d: Audit flag for the site collection. Can be 'All' or 'None'.

Url.d: URL of the site collection to configure.

Description.d: Description of site design.

IsDefault.d: Is site design applied by default to web templates.

PreviewImageAltText.d: Site design alternate preview image text.

SiteScriptNames.d: The names of the site design scripts.

Title.d: The title of the site design.

WebTemplate.d: Web template to which the site design is applied to when invoked.

SPOSiteDesignRights.d: This resource configures rights on Site Designs.

Rights.d: Rights to grant user principals on site design rights.

SiteDesignTitle.d: The title of the site design

UserPrincipals.d: List of user principals with separated by commas to site design rights.

SPOSiteGroup.d: Configure groups for a SharePoint Online site.

Identity.d: The name of the site group

Owner.d: The owner (email address) of the site group

PermissionLevels.d: The permission level of the site group

SPOSiteScript.d: This resource allows users to manage JSON files that specify an ordered list of actions to run when creating the new sites.

SPOStorageEntity.d: This resource configures Storage Entity for SharePoint Online.

Comment.d: Comment for the storage entity.

Description.d: Description of storage entity.

Key.d: The key of the storage entity.

SiteUrl.d: The url of site collection or tenant.

SPOTenantCdnEnabled.d: This resource enables / disables SharePoint online CDN

CdnType.d: Specifies the CDN type. The valid values are public or private.

Enable.d: Specify to enable or disable tenant CDN.

SPOTenantCdnPolicy.d: This resource configures Content Delivery Network policies for SharePoint Online.

CDNType.d: Type of Content Delivery Network. Can be 'Private' or 'Public'.

ExcludeRestrictedSiteClassifications.d: List of site classifications to exclude.

IncludeFileExtensions.d: List of file extensions to include in the Policy.

SPOTenantSettings.d: This resource allows users to configure and monitor the tenant settings for their SPO tenant settings.

AllowDownloadingNonWebViewableFiles.d: Gets or sets a value to specify the advanced setting of the conditional access policy.

AllowEditing.d: Prevents users from editing Office files in the browser and copying and pasting Office file contents out of the browser window.

AllowSelectSGsInODBListInTenant.d: List of security groups to include in OneDrive access restrictions

AllowSelectSecurityGroupsInSPSitesList.d: List of security groups to include in SharePoint access restrictions.

ApplyAppEnforcedRestrictionsToAdHocRecip.d: When the feature is enabled, all guest users are subject to conditional access policy. By default guest users who are accessing SharePoint Online files with pass code are exempt from the conditional access policy.

CommentsOnSitePagesDisabled.d: Set to false to enable a comment section on all site pages, users who have access to the pages can leave comments. Set to true to disable this feature.

DenySelectSGsInODBListInTenant.d: List of security groups to exclude in OneDrive access restrictions

DenySelectSecurityGroupsInSPSitesList.d: List of security groups to exclude in SharePoint access restrictions

DisableCustomAppAuthentication.d: Configure if ACS-based app-only authentication should be disabled or not.

DisablePersonalListCreation.d: Allows configuring whether personal lists created within the OneDrive for Business site of the user is enabled or disabled in the tenant. If set to $false, personal lists will be allowed to be created in the tenant. If set to $true, it will be disabled in the tenant.

DisabledModernListTemplateIds.d: Guids of out of the box modern list templates to hide when creating a new list.

DisabledWebPartIds.d: Provide GUID for the Web Parts that are to be disabled on the Sharepoint Site

DisplayNamesOfFileViewersInSpo.d: Allows configuring whether display name of people who view the file are visible in the property pane of the site in SharePoint site collection.

EnableAIPIntegration.d: Boolean indicating if Azure Information Protection (AIP) should be enabled on the tenant.

EnableAzureADB2BIntegration.d: Enables OneDrive and SharePoint integration with Microsoft Entra B2B.

ExemptNativeUsersFromTenantLevelRestrice.d: Determines whether or not we need to include external participants in shared channels for SharePoint access restriction.

FilePickerExternalImageSearchEnabled.d: Sets whether webparts that support inserting images, like for example Image or Hero webpart, the Web search (Powered by Bing) should allow choosing external images.

HideDefaultThemes.d: Defines if the default themes are visible or hidden

HideSyncButtonOnODB.d: Set whether to hide the sync button on OneDrive for Business sites.

HideSyncButtonOnTeamSite.d: To enable or disable Sync button on Team sites

IsFluidEnabled.d: Allows configuration on whether Fluid components are enabled or disabled in the tenant. If set to $true, then this feature will be enabled on all sites in the tenant. If set to $false, it will be disabled on all sites in the tenant.

IsLoopEnabled.d: Allows configuring whether loop components are enabled or disabled in the tenant. If set to $true, loop components will be allowed to be created in the tenant. If set to $false, it will be disabled in the tenant.

IsSharePointNewsfeedEnabled.d: Indicates whether the newsfeed is allowed on the modern site pages in SharePoint.

IsSingleInstance.d: Specifies the resource is a single instance, the value must be 'Yes'

IsSiteCreationEnabled.d: Indicates whether users are allowed to create sites.

IsSiteCreationUiEnabled.d: Indicates whether the UI commands for creating sites are shown.

IsSitePagesCreationEnabled.d: Indicates whether creating new modern pages is allowed on SharePoint sites.

LegacyAuthProtocolsEnabled.d: Setting this parameter prevents Office clients using non-modern authentication protocols from accessing SharePoint Online resources.

MarkNewFilesSensitiveByDefault.d: Allow or block external sharing until at least one Office DLP policy scans the content of the file.

MaxCompatibilityLevel.d: Specifies the upper bound on the compatibility level for new sites.

MinCompatibilityLevel.d: Specifies the lower bound on the compatibility level for new sites.

MobileFriendlyUrlEnabledInTenant.d: Gets or sets a value to specify if user checks handle mobile friendly url.

NoAccessRedirectUrl.d: Specifies the URL of the redirected site for those site collections which have the locked state 'NoAccess'. The valid values are: '' (default) - Blank by default, this will also remove or clear any value that has been set. Full URL - Example: https://contoso.sharepoint.com/Pages/Locked.aspx

NotificationsInSharePointEnabled.d: When set to $true, users aren't able to share with security groups or SharePoint groups.

OfficeClientADALDisabled.d: When set to true this will disable the ability to use Modern Authentication that leverages ADAL across the tenant.

OneDriveSharingCapability.d: DEPRECATED - Determines what level of sharing is available for OneDrive sites. It corresponds to the SharingCapabilities for OneDrive sites.

OwnerAnonymousNotification.d: Specifies whether an email notification should be sent to the OneDrive for Business owners when an anonymous links are created or changed.

RequireAcceptingAccountMatchInvitedAccou.d: Ensures that an external user can only accept an external sharing invitation with an account matching the invited email address. Note, this only applies to new external users accepting new sharing invitations. Also, the resource owner must share with an organizational or Microsoft account or the external user will be unable to access the resource.

SearchResolveExactEmailOrUPN.d: Removes the search capability from People Picker. Note, recently resolved names will still appear in the list until browser cache is cleared or expired.

SignInAccelerationDomain.d: Specifies the home realm discovery value to be sent to Azure Active Directory (AAD) during the user sign-in process.

SocialBarOnSitePagesDisabled.d: Disables or enables the Social Bar. It will give users the ability to like a page, see the number of views, likes, and comments on a page, and see the people who have liked a page.

SpecialCharactersStateInFileFolderNames.d: Permits the use of special characters in file and folder names in SharePoint Online and OneDrive for Business document libraries. The only two characters that can be managed at this time are the # and % characters.

TenantDefaultTimezone.d: The default timezone of a tenant for newly created sites.

UseFindPeopleInPeoplePicker.d: When set to $true, users aren't able to share with security groups or SharePoint groups.

UsePersistentCookiesForExplorerView.d: Lets SharePoint issue a special cookie that will allow this feature to work even when Keep Me Signed In is not selected.

SPOTheme.d: This resource allows users to configure and monitor the themes for their SPO tenant.

IsInverted.d: This value should be false for light themes and true for dark themes; it controls whether SharePoint uses dark or light theme colors to render text on colored backgrounds.

Name.d: The name of the theme, which appears in the theme picker UI and is also used by administrators and developers to refer to the theme in PowerShell cmdlets or calls to the SharePoint REST API.

Palette.d: Specifies the color scheme which composes your theme.

SPOUserProfileProperty.d: This resource allows users to configure and monitor the profile properties of a user.

Properties.d: Array of MSFT_SPOUserProfilePropertyInstance representing the profile properties to set.

UserName.d: Username of the user to configure the profile properties for. E.g. John.Smith@contoso.com

SentinelAlertRule.d: Configures alert rules in Azure Sentinel.

AlertRuleTemplateName.d: The Name of the alert rule template used to create this rule.

CustomDetails.d: Dictionary of string key-value pairs of columns to be attached to the alert

Description.d: The name of the workspace.

DisplayName.d: The display name of the indicator

DisplayNamesExcludeFilter.d: The alerts' displayNames on which the cases will not be generated.

DisplayNamesFilter.d: The alerts' displayNames on which the cases will be generated.

Enabled.d: Determines whether this alert rule is enabled or disabled.

EntityMappings.d: Array of the entity mappings of the alert rule

Id.d: The unique id of the indicator.

IncidentConfiguration.d: The settings of the incidents that created from alerts triggered by this analytics rule

Kind.d: The kind of the alert rule

ProductFilter.d: The alerts' productName on which the cases will be generated

Query.d: The query that creates alerts for this rule.

QueryFrequency.d: The frequency (in ISO 8601 duration format) for this alert rule to run.

QueryPeriod.d: The period (in ISO 8601 duration format) that this alert rule looks at.

ResourceGroupName.d: The name of the resource group. The name is case insensitive.

SeveritiesFilter.d: The alerts' severities on which the cases will be generated

Severity.d: The severity for alerts created by this alert rule.

SubTechniques.d: The sub-techniques of the alert rule

SubscriptionId.d: The name of the resource group. The name is case insensitive.

SuppressionDuration.d: The suppression (in ISO 8601 duration format) to wait since last time this alert rule been triggered.

SuppressionEnabled.d: Determines whether the suppression for this alert rule is enabled or disabled.

Tactics.d: The tactics of the alert rule

Techniques.d: The techniques of the alert rule

TriggerOperator.d: The operation against the threshold that triggers alert rule.

TriggerThreshold.d: The threshold triggers this alert rule.

SentinelSetting.d: Configures settings for a Sentinel instance.

AnomaliesIsEnabled.d: Specififies if Anomaly detection should be enabled or not.

EntityAnalyticsIsEnabled.d: Specififies if Entity Analyticsshould be enabled or not.

EyesOnIsEnabled.d: Specififies if Auditing and Health Monitoring should be enabled or not.

SubscriptionId.d: Gets subscription credentials which uniquely identify Microsoft Azure subscription. The subscription ID forms part of the URI for every service call.

UebaDataSource.d: The list of Data sources associated with the UEBA.

SentinelThreatIntelligenceIndicator.d: Configures threat intelligence indicators in Azure Sentinel.

Description.d: The name of the workspace.

DisplayName.d: The display name of the indicator

Id.d: The unique id of the indicator.

Pattern.d: Pattern of a threat intelligence entity

PatternType.d: Pattern type of a threat intelligence entity

ResourceGroupName.d: The name of the resource group. The name is case insensitive.

SubscriptionId.d: The name of the resource group. The name is case insensitive.

DefaultDuration.d: The default duration of a watchlist (in ISO 8601 duration format)

Description.d: A description of the watchlist

DisplayName.d: The display name of the watchlist.

Id.d: The id (a Guid) of the watchlist

ItemsSearchKey.d: The search key is used to optimize query performance when using watchlists for joins with other data. For example, enable a column with IP addresses to be the designated SearchKey field, then use this field as the key field when joining to other event data by IP address.

Name.d: Tha name of the watchlist.

NumberOfLinesToSkip.d: The number of lines in a csv content to skip before the header

RawContent.d: The raw content that represents to watchlist items to create. Example : This line will be skipped header1,header2 value1,value2

ResourceGroupName.d: The name of the resource group. The name is case insensitive.

SourceType.d: The source of the watchlist. Only accepts 'Local file' and 'Remote storage'. And it must included in the request.

SubscriptionId.d: The name of the resource group. The name is case insensitive.

TeamsAIPolicy.d: This resource implements Teams AI Policies.

Description.d: Provides explanatory text about the Teams AI policy.

EnrollFace.d: EnrollFace controls user access to user face enrollment in the Teams app settings.

EnrollVoice.d: EnrollVoice controls user access to user voice enrollment in the Teams app settings.

Identity.d: Unique identity of the AI Policy.

PassiveVoiceEnrollment.d: Policy value of the Teams AI PassiveVoiceEnrollment policy. PassiveVoiceEnrollment controls user access to express voice enrollment in the Teams app settings.

SpeakerAttributionForBYOD.d: Setting to Enabled turns on speaker attribution in BYOD scenarios while Disabled will turn off the function.

TeamsAppPermissionPolicy.d: Manages the Teams App Permission Policies.

DefaultCatalogApps.d: The list of apps for the Default Catalog.

DefaultCatalogAppsType.d: The types of apps for the Default Catalog.

Description.d: Enables administrators to provide explanatory text to accompany a Teams app permission policy.

GlobalCatalogApps.d: The list of apps for the Global Catalog.

GlobalCatalogAppsType.d: The types of apps for the Global Catalog.

Identity.d: Unique identifier to be assigned to the new Teams app permission policy. Use the 'Global' Identity if you wish to assign this policy to the entire tenant.

PrivateCatalogApps.d: The list of apps for the Private Catalog.

PrivateCatalogAppsType.d: The types of apps for the Private Catalog.

TeamsAppSetupPolicy.d: Manages Teams app setup policies in your tenant.

AllowSideLoading.d: This is also known as side loading. This setting determines if a user can upload a custom app package in the Teams app. Turning it on lets you create or develop a custom app to be used personally or across your organization without having to submit it to the Teams app store.
