WatermarkForCameraVideoOpacity.d: Allows the transparency of watermark to be customizable. Must be between 1 and 100 inclusive.

WatermarkForCameraVideoPattern.d: Allows the pattern design of watermark to be customizable.

WatermarkForScreenSharingOpacity.d: Allows the transparency of watermark to be customizable. Must be between 1 and 100 inclusive.

WatermarkForScreenSharingPattern.d: Allows the pattern design of watermark to be customizable.

WhoCanRegister.d: Specifies who can attend and register for webinars.

TeamsMessagingConfiguration.d: The TeamsMessagingConfiguration determines the messaging settings for users in your tenant.

ContentBasedPhishingCheck.d: This setting enables content-based phishing detection for Teams messages in the tenant.

CustomEmojis.d: This setting enables/disables the use of custom emojis and reactions across the whole tenant. Upon enablement, admins and/or users can define a user group that is allowed. Possible Values: True, False

EnableInOrganizationChatControl.d: This setting determines if chat regulation for internal communication in tenant is allowed. Possible Values: True, False

EnableVideoMessageCaptions.d: This setting determines if closed captions will be displayed, for Teams Video Clips, during playback. Possible values: True, False

FileTypeCheck.d: This setting enables weaponizable file detection in Teams messages in the tenant. Possible Values: Enabled, Disabled

MessagingNotes.d: This setting enables/disables MessagingNotes integration across the whole tenant. Possible Values: Disabled, Enabled

ReportIncorrectSecurityDetections.d: This setting enables the end users to Report incorrect security detections in Teams messages in the tenant. Possible Values: Enabled, Disabled

Storyline.d: This setting enables/disables the availability of Viva Engage storylines in Teams chats across the whole tenant.

UrlReputationCheck.d: This setting enables malicious URL detection in Teams messages in the tenant. Possible Values: Enabled, Disabled

TeamsMessagingPolicy.d: This resource is used to configure the Teams messaging policy.

AllowChatWithGroup.d: This setting determines if users can chat with groups (Distribution, M365 and Security groups). Possible values: True, False

AllowCustomGroupChatAvatars.d: These settings enables, disables updating or fetching custom group chat avatars for the users included in the messaging policy. Possible values: True, False

AllowExtendedWorkInfoInSearch.d: This setting enables/disables showing company name and department name in search results for MTO users. Possible values: True, False

AllowFluidCollaborate.d: Determines is Fluid Collaboration should be enabled or not.

AllowFullChatPermissionUserToDeleteAnyMe.d: This setting determines if users with the 'Full permissions' role can delete any group or meeting chat message within their tenant. Possible values: True, False

AllowGiphy.d: Determines whether a user is allowed to access and post Giphys. Set this to TRUE to allow. Set this FALSE to prohibit.

AllowGiphyDisplay.d: Determines if Giphy images should be displayed that had been already sent or received in chat. Possible values: True, False

AllowGroupChatJoinLinks.d: This setting determines if users in a group chat can create and share join links for other users within the organization to join that chat. Possible values: True, False

AllowImmersiveReader.d: Determines whether a user is allowed to use Immersive Reader for reading conversation messages. Set this to TRUE to allow. Set this FALSE to prohibit.

AllowMemes.d: Determines whether a user is allowed to access and post memes. Set this to TRUE to allow. Set this FALSE to prohibit.

AllowOwnerDeleteMessage.d: Determines whether owners are allowed to delete all the messages in their team. Set this to TRUE to allow. Set this to FALSE to prohibit.

AllowPasteInternetImage.d: Determines if a user is allowed to paste internet-based images in compose. Possible values: True, False

AllowPriorityMessages.d: Determines whether a user is allowed to send priorities messages. Set this to TRUE to allow. Set this FALSE to prohibit.

AllowRemoveUser.d: Determines whether a user is allowed to remove a user from a conversation. Set this to TRUE to allow. Set this FALSE to prohibit.

AllowSmartCompose.d: Turn on this setting to let a user get text predictions for chat messages.

AllowSmartReply.d: Turn this setting on to enable suggested replies for chat messages. Set this to TRUE to allow. Set this to FALSE to prohibit.

AllowStickers.d: Determines whether a user is allowed to access and post stickers. Set this to TRUE to allow. Set this FALSE to prohibit.

AllowUrlPreviews.d: Use this setting to turn automatic URL previewing on or off in messages. Set this to TRUE to turn on. Set this to FALSE to turn off.

AllowUserChat.d: Determines whether a user is allowed to chat. Set this to TRUE to allow a user to chat across private chat, group chat and in meetings. Set this to FALSE to prohibit all chat.

AllowUserDeleteChat.d: Turn this setting on to allow users to permanently delete their 1:1, group chat, and meeting chat as participants (this deletes the chat only for them, not other users in the chat).

AllowUserDeleteMessage.d: Determines whether a user is allowed to delete their own messages. Set this to TRUE to allow. Set this to FALSE to prohibit.

AllowUserEditMessage.d: Determines whether a user is allowed to edit their own messages. Set this to TRUE to allow. Set this to FALSE to prohibit.

AllowUserTranslation.d: Determines whether a user is allowed to translate messages to their client languages. Set this to TRUE to allow. Set this to FALSE to prohibit.

AllowVideoMessages.d: Determines whether a user is allowed to send video messages in Chat. Set this to TRUE to allow a user to send video messages. Set this to FALSE to prohibit sending video messages.

AudioMessageEnabledType.d: Determines whether a user is allowed to send audio messages. Possible values are: ChatsAndChannels,ChatsOnly,Disabled.

AutoShareFilesInExternalChats.d: Determines whether files are automatically shared in external chats. Possible values: Enabled: Files are automatically shared in external chats. Disabled: Files are not automatically shared in external chats.

ChatPermissionRole.d: Determines the Supervised Chat role of the user. Set this to Full to allow the user to supervise chats. Supervisors have the ability to initiate chats with and invite any user within the environment. Set this to Limited to allow the user to initiate conversations with Full and Limited permissioned users, but not Restricted. Set this to Restricted to block chat creation with anyone other than Full permissioned users.

CreateCustomEmojis.d: This setting enables the creation of custom emojis and reactions within an organization for the specified policy users.

DeleteCustomEmojis.d: These settings enable and disable the editing and deletion of custom emojis and reactions for the users included in the messaging policy.

Description.d: Provide a description of your policy to identify purpose of creating it.

DesignerForBackgroundsAndImages.d: This setting determines whether a user is allowed to create custom AI-powered backgrounds and images with MS Designer.Possible values are: Enabled, Disabled

GiphyRatingType.d: Determines the Giphy content restrictions applicable to a user. Set this to STRICT, MODERATE or NORESTRICTION.

Identity.d: Identity for the teams messaging policy you're modifying. To modify the global policy, use this syntax: -Identity global. To modify a per-user policy, use syntax similar to this: -Identity TeamsMessagingPolicy.

InOrganizationChatControl.d: This setting determines if chat regulation for internal communication in the tenant is allowed. Possible values: BlockingAllowed, BlockingDisallowed

ReadReceiptsEnabledType.d: Use this setting to specify whether read receipts are user controlled, enabled for everyone, or disabled. Set this to UserPreference, Everyone or None.

Tenant.d: Globally unique identifier (GUID) of the tenant account whose external user communication policy are being created.

UseB2BInvitesToAddExternalUsers.d: Indicates whether B2B invites should be used to add external users when necessary. Possible values: Enabled: External users will be added using B2B invites. Disabled: External users will not be added using B2B invites.

UsersCanDeleteBotMessages.d: Determines whether a user is allowed to delete messages sent by bots. Possible values are: True, False

TeamsMobilityPolicy.d: This resource configures an The TeamsMobilityPolicy allows Admins to control Teams mobile usage for users.

Description.d: Enables administrators to provide explanatory text about the policy. For example, the Description might indicate the users the policy should be assigned to.

IPAudioMobileMode.d: When set to WifiOnly, prohibits the user from making and receiving calls or joining meetings using VoIP calls on the mobile device while on a cellular data connection. Possible values are: WifiOnly, AllNetworks.

IPVideoMobileMode.d: When set to WifiOnly, prohibits the user from making and receiving video calls or enabling video in meetings using VoIP calls on the mobile device while on a cellular data connection. Possible values are: WifiOnly, AllNetworks.

Identity.d: Specify the name of the Teams Mobility Policy.

LinksInTeams.d: Controls the availability of browser choice options in Teams Mobile. When set to OfferBrowserOptions, users get two browser choice experiences: (1) browser options in the Teams Mobile settings page for configuring default preferences, and (2) browser options in the upsell card when tapping links. When set to UseUserDefaults, links open using the user's system default browser settings. Possible values are: OfferBrowserOptions, UseUserDefaults.

MobileDialerPreference.d: Determines the mobile dialer preference, possible values are: Teams, Native, UserOverride.

TeamsNetworkRoamingPolicy.d: This resource configures an New-CsTeamsNetworkRoamingPolicy allows IT Admins to create policies for Network Roaming and Bandwidth Control experiences in Microsoft Teams.

AllowIPVideo.d: Determines whether video is enabled in a user's meetings or calls. Set this to TRUE to allow the user to share their video. Set this to FALSE to prohibit the user from sharing their video.

Description.d: Description of the new policy to be created.

Identity.d: Specify the name of the Teams Network Roaming Policy.

MediaBitRateKb.d: Determines the media bit rate for audio/video/app sharing transmissions in meetings.

TeamsNotificationAndFeedsPolicy.d: This resource configures a Teams Notifications and Feeds Policy.

Description.d: Free format text

SuggestedFeedsEnabledType.d: The SuggestedFeedsEnabledType parameter in the Microsoft Teams notifications and feeds policy controls whether users receive notifications about suggested activities and content within their Teams environment. When enabled, this parameter ensures that users are notified about recommended or relevant activities, helping them stay informed and engaged with important updates and interactions.

TrendingFeedsEnabledType.d: The TrendingFeedsEnabledType parameter in the Microsoft Teams notifications and feeds policy controls whether users receive notifications about trending activities within their Teams environment. When enabled, this parameter ensures that users are notified about popular or important activities, helping them stay informed about significant updates and interactions.

TeamsOnlineVoiceUser.d: This resource configures the Teams Online Voice User.

TeamsOnlineVoicemailPolicy.d: This resource configures the Teams Online Voicemail Policies.

EnableEditingCallAnswerRulesSetting.d: Controls if editing call answer rule settings are enabled or disabled for a user. Possible values are $true or $false.

EnableTranscription.d: Allows you to disable or enable voicemail transcription. Possible values are $true or $false.

EnableTranscriptionProfanityMasking.d: Allows you to disable or enable profanity masking for the voicemail transcriptions. Possible values are $true or $false.

EnableTranscriptionTranslation.d: Allows you to disable or enable translation for the voicemail transcriptions. Possible values are $true or $false.

Identity.d: Identity of the Teams Online Voicemail Policy.

MaximumRecordingLength.d: A duration of voicemail maximum recording length. The length should be between 30 seconds to 600 seconds.

PostambleAudioFile.d: The audio file to play to the caller after the user's voicemail greeting has played and before the caller is allowed to leave a voicemail message.

PreambleAudioFile.d: The audio file to play to the caller before the user's voicemail greeting is played.

PreamblePostambleMandatory.d: Is playing the Pre- or Post-amble mandatory before the caller can leave a message. Possible values are $true or $false.

PrimarySystemPromptLanguage.d: The primary (or first) language that voicemail system prompts will be presented in. Must also set SecondarySystemPromptLanguage. When set, this overrides the user language choice.

SecondarySystemPromptLanguage.d: The secondary language that voicemail system prompts will be presented in. Must also set PrimarySystemPromptLanguage and may not be the same value as PrimarySystemPromptanguage. When set, this overrides the user language choice.

ShareData.d: Specifies whether voicemail and transcription data are shared with the service for training and improving accuracy. Possible values are Defer and Deny.

TeamsOnlineVoicemailUserSettings.d: This resource configures a Teams User's Online Voicemail Settings.

CallAnswerRule.d: The CallAnswerRule parameter represents the value of the call answer rule, which can be any of the following: DeclineCall, PromptOnly, PromptOnlyWithTransfer, RegularVoicemail, VoicemailWithTransferOption.

DefaultGreetingPromptOverwrite.d: The DefaultGreetingPromptOverwrite parameter represents the contents that overwrite the default normal greeting prompt. If the user's normal custom greeting is not set and DefaultGreetingPromptOverwrite is not empty, the voicemail service will play this overwrite greeting instead of the default normal greeting in the voicemail deposit scenario.

DefaultOofGreetingPromptOverwrite.d: The DefaultOofGreetingPromptOverwrite parameter represents the contents that overwrite the default out-of-office greeting prompt. If the user's out-of-office custom greeting is not set and DefaultOofGreetingPromptOverwrite is not empty, the voicemail service will play this overwrite greeting instead of the default out-of-office greeting in the voicemail deposit scenario.

Identity.d: The Identity parameter represents the ID of the specific user in your organization; this can be either a SIP URI or an Object ID.

OofGreetingEnabled.d: The OofGreetingEnabled parameter represents whether to play out-of-office greeting in voicemail deposit scenario.

OofGreetingFollowAutomaticRepliesEnabled.d: The OofGreetingFollowAutomaticRepliesEnabled parameter represents whether to play out-of-office greeting in voicemail deposit scenario when user set automatic replies in Outlook.

OofGreetingFollowCalendarEnabled.d: The OofGreetingFollowCalendarEnabled parameter represents whether to play out-of-office greeting in voicemail deposit scenario when user set out-of-office in calendar.

PromptLanguage.d: The PromptLanguage parameter represents the language that is used to play voicemail prompts.

ShareData.d: Specifies whether voicemail and transcription data is shared with the service for training and improving accuracy.

TransferTarget.d: The TransferTarget parameter represents the target to transfer the call when call answer rule set to PromptOnlyWithTransfer or VoicemailWithTransferOption. Value of this parameter should be a SIP URI of another user in your organization. For user with Enterprise Voice enabled, a valid telephone number could also be accepted as TransferTarget.

VoicemailEnabled.d: The VoicemailEnabled parameter represents whether to enable voicemail service. If set to $false, the user has no voicemail service.

TeamsOrgWideAppSettings.d: This resource configures Org-Wide App Settings for Teams.

IsSideloadedAppsInteractionEnabled.d: Determines whether or not to allow interaction with custom apps.

IsSingleInstance.d: Specifies the resource is a single instance, the value must be 'Yes'

TeamsPstnUsage.d: This resource configures a Teams PSTN Usage.

Usage.d: An online PSTN usage (such as Local or Long Distance) that can be used in conjunction with voice routes and voice routing policies.

TeamsShiftsPolicy.d: This resource configures a TeamsShiftPolicy instance and sets its properties.

AccessGracePeriodMinutes.d: Determines the grace period time in minutes between when the first shift starts or last shift ends and when access is blocked

AccessType.d: Determines the Teams access type granted to the user. Today, only unrestricted access to Teams app is supported.

EnableScheduleOwnerPermissions.d: Determines whether a user can manage a Shifts schedule as a team member.

Identity.d: Specifies the policy instance name

ShiftNoticeFrequency.d: Determines the frequency of warning dialog displayed when user opens Teams.

ShiftNoticeMessageCustom.d: Specifies a custom message. Must set ShiftNoticeMessageType to 'CustomMessage' to enforce this

ShiftNoticeMessageType.d: Specifies the warning message is shown in the blocking dialog when a user access Teams off shift hours. Select one of 7 Microsoft provided messages, a default message or a custom message.

TeamsTargetingPolicy.d: This resource configures a Teams Targeting Policy.

CustomTagsMode.d: Determine whether Teams users can create tags in team. Set this to Enabled to allow users to create new tags. Set this to Disabled to prohibit them from creating new tags.

Description.d: Pass in a new description if that field needs to be updated.

ManageTagsPermissionMode.d: Determine whether team users can manage tag settings in Teams. Set this to EnabledTeamOwner to only allow Teams owners to manage tag settings in current Teams. Set this to EnabledTeamOwnerMember to allow Teams owners and Teams members to manage tag settings in current Teams. Set this to EnabledTeamOwnerMemberGuest to allow Teams owners, Teams members and guest users to manage tag settings in current Teams. Set this to MicrosoftDefault to user default setting in current Teams, which will be the same as EnabledTeamOwner. Set this to Disabled to prohibit all users from managing tag settings in current Teams.

ShiftBackedTagsMode.d: Determine whether Teams can have tags created by Shift App. Set this to Enabled to allow tags created by Shift App. Set this to Disabled to prohibit tags from Shift App.

TeamOwnersEditWhoCanManageTagsMode.d: Determine whether Teams owners can change Tenant tag settings. Set this to Enabled to allow Teams owners to change Tenant tag settings for current Teams. Set this to Disabled to prohibit them from changing Tenant tag settings.

TeamsTeam.d: This resource configures or creates a new Team.

AllowAddRemoveApps.d: Allow add or remove apps from the Team.

AllowCreatePrivateChannels.d: Determines whether private channel creation is allowed for the team.

AllowCreateUpdateChannels.d: Allow members to create and update channels within Team.

AllowCreateUpdateRemoveConnectors.d: Allow members to manage connectors within Team.

AllowCreateUpdateRemoveTabs.d: Allow members to manage tabs within Team.

AllowDeleteChannels.d: Allow members to delete channels within Team.

AllowGuestCreateUpdateChannels.d: Allow guests to create and update channels in Team.

AllowGuestDeleteChannels.d: Allow guests to delete channel in Team.

AllowOwnerDeleteMessages.d: Allow owners to delete messages within Team.

AllowStickersAndMemes.d: Allow stickers and mimes in the Team.

AllowUserDeleteMessages.d: Allow members to delete messages within Team.

AllowUserEditMessages.d: Allow members to edit messages within Team.

Description.d: Description of Team.

DisplayName.d: Display Name of the Team

GiphyContentRating.d: Giphy content rating of the Team.

GroupID.d: Team group ID, only used to target a Team when duplicated display names occurs.

MailNickName.d: MailNickName of O365 Group associated with Team

Owner.d: Owners of the Team. At least one entry is required.

ShowInTeamsSearchAndSuggestions.d: determines whether or not private teams should be searchable from Teams clients for users who do not belong to that team. Set to $false to make those teams not discoverable from Teams clients.

TeamsTemplatesPolicy.d: This resource configures a Teams Templates Policy.

Description.d: Description of the Teams Templates Policy.

HiddenTemplates.d: The list of Teams templates to hide.

Identity.d: Identity of the Teams Templates Policy.

TeamsTenantDialPlan.d: This resource is used to configure the tenant-wide dial plans for Microsoft Teams.

Description.d: The Description parameter describes the tenant dial plan - what it's for, what type of user it applies to and any other information that helps to identify the purpose of the tenant dial plan. Maximum characters: 512.

Identity.d: The Identity parameter is a unique identifier that designates the name of the tenant dial plan. Identity is an alphanumeric string that cannot exceed 49 characters. Valid characters are alphabetic or numeric characters, hyphen (-) and dot (.). The value should not begin with a (.).

NormalizationRules.d: List of normalization rules that are applied to this dial plan.

SimpleName.d: The SimpleName parameter is a display name for the tenant dial plan. This name must be unique among all tenant dial plans within the Skype for Business Server deployment.This string can be up to 49 characters long. Valid characters are alphabetic or numeric characters, hyphen (-), dot (.) and parentheses (()).

TeamsTenantNetworkRegion.d: As an Admin, you can use the Windows PowerShell command, New-CsTenantNetworkRegion to define network regions. A network region interconnects various parts of a network across multiple geographic areas. The RegionID parameter is a logical name that represents the geography of the region, and has no dependencies or restrictions. Tenant network region is used for Location Based Routing.

CentralSite.d: Name of the associated Central Site.

Description.d: Provide a description of the network region to identify purpose of creating it.

Identity.d: Unique identifier for the network region to be created.

TeamsTenantNetworkSite.d: As an Admin, you can use the Windows PowerShell command, New-CsTenantNetworkSite to define network sites. Network sites are defined as a collection of IP subnets. Each network site must be associated with a network region. Tenant network site is used for Location Based Routing.

Description.d: Provide a description of the network site to identify purpose of creating it.

EmergencyCallRoutingPolicy.d: This parameter is used to assign a custom emergency call routing policy to a network site

EmergencyCallingPolicy.d: This parameter is used to assign a custom emergency calling policy to a network site

EnableLocationBasedRouting.d: This parameter determines whether the current site is enabled for location based routing.

Identity.d: Unique identifier for the network site to be created.

LocationPolicy.d: LocationPolicy is the identifier for the location policy which the current network site is associating to.

NetworkRegionID.d: NetworkRegionID is the identifier for the network region which the current network site is associating to.

NetworkRoamingPolicy.d: NetworkRoamingPolicy is the identifier for the network roaming policy to which the network site will associate to.

SiteAddress.d: The address of current network site.

TeamsTenantNetworkSubnet.d: As an Admin, you can use the Windows PowerShell command, New-CsTenantNetworkSubnet to define network subnets and assign them to network sites. Each internal subnet may only be associated with one site. Tenant network subnet is used for Location Based Routing.

Description.d: Provide a description of the network subnet to identify purpose of creating it.

Identity.d: Unique identifier for the network subnet to be created.

MaskBits.d: This parameter determines the length of bits to mask to the subnet. IPv4 format subnet accepts maskbits from 0 to 32 inclusive. IPv6 format subnet accepts maskbits from 0 to 128 inclusive.

NetworkSiteID.d: NetworkSiteID is the identifier for the network site which the current network subnet is associating to.

TeamsTenantTrustedIPAddress.d: As an Admin, you can use the Windows PowerShell command, New-CsTenantTrustedIPAddress to define external subnets and assign them to the tenant. You can define an unlimited number of external subnets for a tenant.

Description.d: Provide a description of the trusted IP address to identify purpose of creating it.

Identity.d: Unique identifier for the IP address to be created.

MaskBits.d: This parameter determines the length of bits to mask to the subnet.

TeamsTranslationRule.d: This resource configures an Cmdlet to create a new telephone number manipulation rule.

Description.d: A friendly description of the normalization rule.

Identity.d: The Identifier of the rule. This parameter is required and later used to assign the rule to the Inbound or Outbound Trunk Normalization policy.

Pattern.d: A regular expression that caller or callee number must match in order for this rule to be applied.

Translation.d: The regular expression pattern that will be applied to the number to convert it.

TeamsUnassignedNumberTreatment.d: This resource configures an Creates a new treatment for how calls to an unassigned number range should be routed. The call can be routed to a user, an application or to an announcement service where a custom message will be played to the caller.

Description.d: Free format description of this treatment.

Identity.d: The Id of the treatment.

Pattern.d: A regular expression that the called number must match in order for the treatment to take effect. It is best practice to start the regular expression with the hat character and end it with the dollar character. You can use various regular expression test sites on the Internet to validate the expression.

Target.d: The identity of the destination the call should be routed to. Depending on the TargetType it should either be the ObjectId of the user or application instance/resource account or the AudioFileId of the uploaded audio file.

TargetType.d: The type of target used for the treatment. Allowed values are User, ResourceAccount and Announcement.

TreatmentPriority.d: The priority of the treatment. Used to distinguish identical patterns. The lower the priority the higher preference. The priority needs to be unique.

TeamsUpdateManagementPolicy.d: This resource configures the Teams Update policies. For additional information, please refer to <https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/MicrosoftTeams/public-preview-doc-updates#set-the-update-policy/>

AllowManagedUpdates.d: Determines if managed updates should be allowed or not.

AllowPreview.d: Determines if preview builds should be allowed or not.

AllowPublicPreview.d: Determines the ring of public previews to subscribes to.

BlockLegacyAuthorization.d: This setting will force Teams clients to enforce session revocation for core Messaging and Calling/Meeting scenarios. If turned ON, session revocation will be enforced for calls, chats and meetings for opted-in users. If turned OFF, session revocation will not be enforced for calls, chats and meetings for opted-in users.

Description.d: The description of the Teams Update Management Policy.

DisabledInProductMessages.d: List of IDs of the categories of the in-product messages that will be disabled. You can choose one of the categories from this table: 91382d07-8b89-444c-bbcb-cfe43133af33 = What's New. edf2633e-9827-44de-b34c-8b8b9717e84c = Conferences

Identity.d: Identity of the Teams Update Management Policy.

UpdateDayOfWeek.d: Determines the day of week to perform the updates. Value should be between 0 and 6.

UpdateTime.d: Determines the time of day to perform the updates. Must be a valid HH:MM format string with leading 0. For instance 08:30.

UpdateTimeOfDay.d: Determines the time of day to perform the updates. Accepts a DateTime as string. Only the time will be considered.

UseNewTeamsClient.d: Determines whether or not users will use the new Teams client.

TeamsUpgradeConfiguration.d: This resource configures the Teams Upgrade settings.

DownloadTeams.d: The DownloadTeams property allows admins to control whether the Skype for Business client should automatically download Teams in the background. This Boolean setting is only honored on Windows clients, and only for certain values of the user's TeamsUpgradePolicy. If NotifySfbUser=true or if Mode=TeamsOnly in TeamsUpgradePolicy, this setting is honored. Otherwise it is ignored.

IsSingleInstance.d: Specifies the resource is a single instance, the value must be 'Yes'

SfBMeetingJoinUx.d: The SfBMeetingJoinUx property allows admins to specify which app is used to join Skype for Business meetings, even after the user has been upgraded to Teams. Allowed values are: 'SkypeMeetingsApp' and 'NativeLimitedClient'. 'NativeLimitedClient' means the existing Skype for Business rich client will be used, but since the user is upgraded, only meeting functionality is available. Calling and Messaging are done via Teams. 'SkypeMeetingsApp' means use the web-downloadable app. This setting can be useful for organizations that have upgraded to Teams and no longer want to install Skype for Business on their users' computers.

TeamsUpgradePolicy.d: This resource configures the Teams Upgrade policies.

Identity.d: Identity of the Teams Upgrade Policy.

MigrateMeetingsToTeams.d: Specifies whether to move existing Skype for Business meetings organized by the user to Teams. This parameter can only be true if the mode of the specified policy instance is either TeamsOnly or SfBWithTeamsCollabAndMeetings, and if the policy instance is being granted to a specific user. It not possible to trigger meeting migration when granting TeamsUpgradePolicy to the entire tenant.

Users.d: DEPRECATED. Use the TeamsUserPolicyAssignment resource instead.

TeamsUser.d: This resource is used to add new users to a team

User.d: UPN of user to add to Team

TeamsUserCallingSettings.d: This resource configures a Teams User's Calling Settings.

CallGroupOrder.d: The order in which to call members of the Call Group. The supported values are Simultaneous and InOrder.

CallGroupTargets.d: The members of the Call Group. You need to always specify the full set of members as the parameter value. What you set here will overwrite the current call group membership.

ForwardingTarget.d: The forwarding target. Supported types of values are ObjectId's, SIP addresses and phone numbers. For phone numbers we support the following types of formats: E.164 (+12065551234 or +1206555000;ext=1234) or non-E.164 like 1234.

ForwardingTargetType.d: The forwarding target type. Supported values are Voicemail, SingleTarget, MyDelegates and Group. Voicemail is only supported for Immediate forwarding.

ForwardingType.d: The type of forwarding to set. Supported values are Immediate and Simultaneous

GroupNotificationOverride.d: The group notification override that will be set on the specified user. The supported values are Ring, Mute and Banner.

Identity.d: The Identity of the user to set call forwarding, simultaneous ringing and call group settings for. Can be specified using the ObjectId or the SIP address.

IsForwardingEnabled.d: This parameter controls whether forwarding is enabled or not.

IsUnansweredEnabled.d: This parameter controls whether forwarding for unasnwered calls is enabled or not.

UnansweredDelay.d: The time the call will ring the user before it is forwarded to the unanswered target. The supported format is hh:mm:ss and the delay range needs to be between 10 and 60 seconds in 10 seconds increments, i.e. 00:00:10, 00:00:20, 00:00:30, 00:00:40, 00:00:50 and 00:01:00. The default value is 20 seconds.

UnansweredTarget.d: The unanswered target. Supported type of values are ObjectId, SIP address and phone number. For phone numbers we support the following types of formats: E.164 (+12065551234 or +1206555000;ext=1234) or non-E.164 like 1234.

UnansweredTargetType.d: The unanswered target type. Supported values are Voicemail, SingleTarget, MyDelegates and Group.

TeamsUserPolicyAssignment.d: This resource is used to assign Teams policy to a specified user.

CallingLineIdentity.d: Name of the Calling Line Policy.

ExternalAccessPolicy.d: Name of the External Access Policy.

OnlineVoiceRoutingPolicy.d: Name of the Online VOice Routing Policy.

OnlineVoicemailPolicy.d: Name of the Online Voicemail Policy.

TeamsAppPermissionPolicy.d: Name of the Teams App Permission Policy.

TeamsAppSetupPolicy.d: Name of the Teams App Setup Policy.

TeamsAudioConferencingPolicy.d: Name of the Teams Audio Conferencing Policy.

TeamsCallHoldPolicy.d: Name of the Teams Call Hold Policy.

TeamsCallParkPolicy.d: Name of the Teams Call Park Policy.

TeamsCallingPolicy.d: Name of the Teams Calling Policy.

TeamsChannelsPolicy.d: Name of the Teams Channel Policy.

TeamsEmergencyCallRoutingPolicy.d: Name of the Teams Emergency Call Routing Policy.

TeamsEmergencyCallingPolicy.d: Name of the Teams Emergency Calling Policy.

TeamsEnhancedEncryptionPolicy.d: Name of the Teams Enhanced Encryption Policy.

TeamsEventsPolicy.d: Name of the Teams Events Policy.

TeamsMeetingBroadcastPolicy.d: Name of the Teams Meeting Broadcast Policy.

TeamsMeetingPolicy.d: Name of the Teams Meeting Policy.

TeamsMessagingPolicy.d: Name of the Teams Messaging Policy.

TeamsMobilityPolicy.d: Name of the Teams Mobility Policy.

TeamsUpdateManagementPolicy.d: Name of the Teams Update Management Policy.

TeamsUpgradePolicy.d: Name of the Teams Upgrade Policy.

TenantDialPlan.d: Name of the Tenant Dial Plan Policy.

User.d: User Principal Name of the user representing the policy assignments.

TeamsVdiPolicy.d: This resource implements Teams VDI Policies.

DisableAudioVideoInCallsAndMeetings.d: Disables Audio and Video in Calls and Meeting.

Identity.d: Unique identity of the VDI Policy.

VDI2Optimization.d: Determines whether a user can be VDI 2.0 optimized.

TeamsVoiceRoute.d: This resource configures a Teams Voice Route.

BridgeSourcePhoneNumber.d: BridgeSourcePhoneNumber is an E.164 formatted Operator Connect Conferencing phone number assigned to your Audio Conferencing Bridge. Using BridgeSourcePhoneNumber in an online voice route is mutually exclusive with using OnlinePstnGatewayList in the same online voice route. When using BridgeSourcePhoneNumber in an online voice route, the OnlinePstnUsages used in the online voice route should only be used in a corresponding OnlineAudioConferencingRoutingPolicy. The same OnlinePstnUsages should not be used in online voice routes that are not using BridgeSourcePhoneNumber.

Description.d: A description of what this online voice route is for.

Identity.d: Identity of the Teams Voice Route.

NumberPattern.d: A regular expression that specifies the phone numbers to which this route applies. Numbers matching this pattern will be routed according to the rest of the routing settings.

OnlinePstnGatewayList.d: This parameter contains a list of online gateways associated with this online voice route. Each member of this list must be the service Identity of the online PSTN gateway.

OnlinePstnUsages.d: A list of online PSTN usages (such as Local, Long Distance, etc.) that can be applied to this online voice route. The PSTN usage must be an existing usage (PSTN usages can be retrieved by calling the Get-CsOnlinePstnUsage cmdlet).

Priority.d: A number could resolve to multiple online voice routes. The priority determines the order in which the routes will be applied if more than one route is possible.

TeamsVoiceRoutingPolicy.d: This resource configures a Teams Voice Routing Policy.

Description.d: Enables administrators to provide explanatory text to accompany an online voice routing policy. For example, the Description might include information about the users the policy should be assigned to.

Identity.d: Identity of the Teams Voice Routing Policy.

OnlinePstnUsages.d: A list of online PSTN usages (such as Local or Long Distance) that can be applied to this online voice routing policy. The online PSTN usage must be an existing usage (PSTN usages can be retrieved by calling the Get-CsOnlinePstnUsage cmdlet).

TeamsWorkloadPolicy.d: This resources implements a Teams workload policy.

Description.d: Description of the policy.

Identity.d: Unique identity for the Teams workload policy

VivaEngagementRoleMember.d: Manage a Viva Engage role assigned to a user.

Members.d: User principal names of the users to assign to the role.
